Description: This domain is found in Mitochondrial dynamics protein MID51 from humans and similar proteins predominantly found in vertebrates. MID51 promotes the recruitment and association of the fission mediator dynamin-related protein 1 (DNM1L) to the mitochondrial surface to drive mitochondrial fission. This entry represents the C-terminal domain of this protein, which adopts a nucleotidyltransferase fold with a mixed alpha-beta topology configured into two lobes separated by a deep cleft [[PMID:24515348],[PMID:24508339],[PMID:25581164],[PMID:30703167]].

Name: Grass antifreeze beta roll
Description: The grass Lolium perenne produces an ice-binding protein (LpIBP) that helps this perennial tolerate freezing by inhibiting the recrystallization of ice [[PMID:22306740]]. Ice-binding proteins (IBPs) are also produced by freeze-avoiding organisms to halt the growth of ice and are better known as antifreeze proteins (AFPs) [[PMID:22306740]]. The ice-binding site (IBS) is formed by a flat beta-sheet on one surface of the beta-roll [[PMID:22306740]]. This region folds as a left-handed beta-helix. Each of the eight beta-helical coils is composed of two seven-residue tandem repeats with a consensus sequence XXNXVXG where X is a solvent-exposed residue, typically with a polar side chain [[PMID:22306740]].

Name: Complement component 3, CUB domain, second segment
Description: Complement C3 is major component in the both the classical and alternative complement activation pathways. C3 is cleaved into two chains (alpha and beta) by C3 convertases. This entry represents the second segment of the CUB domain, which is completed by [pfam:PF21406] [[PMID:16831446]]. The CUB domain is interrupted by the TE domain. The CUB, TE and MG8 domains form a massive, tightly packed super domain. This entry also includes venom factors, also known as Complement C3 homologues. These are complement-activating proteins with structural and functional similarities to complement component C3b, the activated form of C3 [[PMID:17412383]].

Name: Phage 5-bladed beta propeller receptor binding platform domain
Description: This entry represents the receptor binding domain of a variety of bacteriophage. The cell wall of S. aureus typically contains poly-ribitol phosphate type wall teichoic acid (WTA), which is modified with D-alanine and N-acetyl-glucosamine (GlcNAc). It has been shown that phage psi11 requires GlcNAc residues on WTA for adsorption8. Gp45 of phage psi11 was identified and characterized as the receptor-binding protein (RBP) [[PMID:27212064]]. This entry represents the 5 bladed beta propeller platform domain that has been suggested to bind to the GlcNac substrate [[PMID:27282779]].

Name: E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase SHPRH, first helical domain
Description: This entry represents the first helical domain found towards the C-terminal end of the E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase SHPRH, a protein involved in  DNA repair [[PMID:18719106]]. Helices in this domain adopt an arrangement similar to TPRs.

Name: Fungal epsilon subunit of F1F0-ATP synthase C-terminal domain
Description: F-type ATPases consist of two structural domains, F1 containing the extramembraneous catalytic core, and F0 which contains the membrane proton channel. This entry includes ATP synthase subunit delta from fungi (called epsilon in bacteria or chloroplast ATPase), which interacts with subunit C from F0 which is important for connecting the rotor of F1 (gamma subunit) to the rotor of F0 (C subunit). This entry represents the C-terminal alpha-helical domain which is likely to act as an inhibitor of ATPase activity [[PMID:17082766],[PMID:10576729],[PMID:20843806],[PMID:22864911],[PMID:27373333]].

Name: Metazoan delta subunit of F1F0-ATP synthase, C-terminal domain
Description: F-type ATPases consist of two structural domains, F1 containing the extramembraneous catalytic core, and F0 which contains the membrane proton channel. This entry includes ATP synthase subunit delta from animals (called epsilon in bacteria or chloroplast ATPase), which interacts with subunit C from F0 which is important for connecting the rotor of F1 (gamma subunit) to the rotor of F0 C subunit). This entry represents the C-terminal alpha-helical domain which is likely to act as an inhibitor of ATPase activity [[PMID:11062563],[PMID:19966409],[PMID:20847295],[PMID:31197009],[PMID:32929284]].

Name: PcRGLX-like protein C-terminal alpha/alpha toroid domain
Description: This family of proteins includes Exo-rhamnogalacturonan lyase from Penicillium chrysogenum 31B (PcRGLX). The family also includes YetA from B. subtilis is functionally uncharacterised. This family of proteins is found in Actinobacteria, Firmicutes, Proteobacteria and fungi. Proteins in this family are typically between 863 and 928 amino acids in length.  This entry represents the C-terminal alpha/alpha-toroid domain. Members of this family share a 3 domain architecture [[PMID:29574769]].

Name: Rhamnogalacturonan lyase family 11, C-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found at the C-terminal end of Rhamnogalacturonan endolyase YesW and Rhamnogalacturonan exolyase YesX from Bacillus subtilis and other bacterial polysaccharide lyases from family 11 (RGL11). These proteins are responsible for an initial cleavage of the RG type I (RG-I) region of plant cell wall pectin. They consist of an N-terminal domain [pfam:PF18370] and a C-terminal eight-bladed beta-propeller domain (this entry) [[PMID:17947240],[PMID:19193638],[PMID:24419797]].

Name: NLR family member X1, C-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found at the C-terminal end of NLR family member X1 from humans (NLRX1) and similar proteins from vertebrates. NLRX1 is both a proinflammatory activator that stimulates the production of reactive oxygen species (ROS) via TNFalpha activation and is able to down-regulate inflammatory responses through interaction with the mitochondrial antiviral signaling adaptor and TRAF6-IKK. This domain is organised into a three subdomains: an N-terminal helical domain, a central LRR motifs and a C-terminal three-helix bundle. The interaction of this domain with RNA ligands plays a key role in NLRX1-mediated ROS activation [[PMID:22386589]].

Name: Complement component 3, CUB domain, first segment
Description: This entry represents the first segment of the CUB domain from complement component C3, which is completed but a second segment represented in [pfam:PF21308]. The CUB domain is interrupted by the TE domain. The CUB domain forms a massive, tightly packed super domain with the TE and  MG8 domains [[PMID:16177781],[PMID:16831446],[PMID:17051160],[PMID:17051150],[PMID:21205667]]. These entry also includes venom factors, also known as Complement C3 homologues. These are complement-activating proteins with structural and functional similarities to complement component C3b, the activated form of C3 [[PMID:17412383]].

Name: Avirulence protein ATR1, WY-domain
Description: Avirulence protein ATR1 is a secreted effector  that acts as an elicitor of hypersensitive response (HR) specifically on plants. It activates the N-terminal Toll-interleukin-1 receptor (TIR) domain of Arabidopsis NLR RPP1 [[PMID:33273071]]. This entry represents the C-terminal helical domain, referred to as the WY-domain.

Name: Long-tail fiber proximal subunit, C-terminal, trimerization domain
Description: The long tail fibers of bacteriophage T4 consist of rigid proximal and distal parts, connected by a hinge region. The proximal half- fiber is formed by a parallel homo-trimer of the Long-tail fiber proximal subunit Gp34. This entry represents a region of the C-terminal domain, which forms a long triple beta-helix made up of parallel beta-strands [[PMID:28665339],[PMID:31209305]].

Name: Reelin subrepeat B
Description: Reelin is an extracellular matrix serine protease that plays a role in layering of neurons in the cerebral cortex and cerebellum. It regulates microtubule function in neurons and neuronal migration. It binds to the extracellular domains of lipoprotein receptors VLDLR and LRP8/APOER2 which induces tyrosine phosphorylation of DAB1 and modulation of TAU phosphorylation. Reelin consists of a signal sequence, an F-spondin-like region and nine sequentially concatenated repeat units. Each complete reelin repeat contains a central epidermal growth factor (EGF) module flanked by two subrepeats of 150-190 amino acids (subrepeats A and B) [[PMID:16858396],[PMID:17548821],[PMID:20223215]]. This entry represents subrepeat B (also referred to R3B) which adopts a beta jelly-roll fold similar to bacterial xylanase. They contain the signature Asp-box motif [[PMID:16858396],[PMID:17548821],[PMID:20223215]].

Name: PaaA-like, N-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found at the N-terminal end of PaaA from Enterobacter agglomerans, an enzyme involved in post-translational modification and host immunity. PaaA is composed of two domains: [pfam:PF00899] and the domains represented by this entry, which folds into a globular domain and has similarity to RiPP precursor peptide recognition elements or RREs [[PMID:27088303]].

Name: Toxin CdiA-like, helical domain
Description: This entry represents the helical domain of  EC869 CdiA toxin, a toxin of the Contact-dependent growth inhibition (CDI) system [[PMID:23236156]]. EC869 appears to function as a Zn2+-dependent DNAse degrading the genome of target cells.

Name: Nucleoporin NUP120, helical domain
Description: This entry represents the helical domain of Nup120 from S. pombe referred to as the irregular helical bundle (IHB) [[PMID:22955883],[PMID:23019579]], which makes extensive contacts with the propeller and helical bundle at the N-terminal. Nup120 functions as a component of the nuclear pore complex (NPC). Deletion of this gene causes clustering of NPCs at one side of the nuclear envelope, moderate nucleolar fragmentation and slower cell growth.

Name: Ribosomal protein L31p, N-terminal
Description: Ribosomal protein L31p (bL31, also known as 54S ribosomal protein L36), is a component of the mitochondrial ribosome. Overexpression of bL31m suppresses mutations in the COX2 leader peptide-encoding and initiation codon regions [[PMID:11259585],[PMID:15166137]]. This entry represents the N-terminal domain of yeast bL31 [[PMID:24675956]].

Name: Protein LANA1-like, DNA-binding domain
Description: Latency-associated nuclear antigen (LANA) is a multifunctional protein that plays a role in the replication and long-term persistence of the viral episomal genome in dividing cells. It binds to mitotic chromosomes via its N-terminal region and to a 16-bp imperfect palindrome within the origin of replication (oriP) located in the viral terminal repeat (TR) through its C-terminal. It also plays a role in the repression of host NF-kappa-B activity upon TNF-alpha stimulation by promoting the proteasomal degradation of host RELA [[PMID:21697472]]. This entry represents the DNA-binding domain of LANA from murine gammaherpesvirus 68, which is homologous to that of Kaposi's sarcoma-associated herpesvirus (KSHV) [[PMID:24146614],[PMID:24146618],[PMID:24146617],[PMID:25947153],[PMID:26424851],[PMID:21697472]].

Name: 26S proteasome subunit RPN2, N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal helical domain of 26S proteasome regulatory subunit RPN2 and 26S proteasome non-ATPase regulatory subunit 1 (PSMD1). It adopts a rod-like structure [[PMID:22405010],[PMID:24706844],[PMID:27428775],[PMID:28689658],[PMID:29398115]].

Name: Transposable element Tc3 transposase, HTH
Description: Tc3 is transposase with a specific DNA-binding domain. It binds specifically to the terminal nucleotides of the TC3 inverted repeat and acts by making double strand breaks at the ends of TC3 element. It has two DNA-binding domains at the N-terminal, the first one represented in [pfam:PF11427] and the second one in this entry [[PMID:15304566]]. They both bind to the major groove on a single DNA oligomer, separated by a linker  that interacts closely with the minor groove [[PMID:15304566]].

Name: PGRS repeats
Description: PE_PGRSs are a large family of Mtb proteins implicated in tuberculosis (TB) pathogenesis that show a modular structure: the N-terminal PE domain, highly homologous to the domain found in tens of others PE proteins and whose structure has been already solved by crystallography; a C-terminal domain that is unique for each protein; the polymorphic glycine-rich domain (PGRS) that varies in length from few tens to more than 1,000 amino acids [[PMID:36048802]].

Name: DNA helicase Pif1, 2B domain
Description: Pif1 is a DNA helicase conserved from bacteria to humans. It suppresses both G-quadruplex-associated DNA damage and telomere lengthening. In budding yeast, it exists in two forms, nuclear and mitochondrial forms. Its nuclear form inhibits telomerase, while its mitochondrial is involved in repair and recombination of mitochondrial DNA. These proteins belong to the helicase superfamily 1 (SF1) and contain RecA-like domains (1A and 2A) which, together with two additional domains, 1B and 2B, form the helicase core [[PMID:30698796],[PMID:29202194]]. This entry represents domain 2B, which adopts a SH3-like fold.

Name: Choline-binding repeat
Description: This entry represents probable choline-binding cell wall repeats.

Name: TSS9, PorZ, N-terminal beta-propeller domain
Description: This domain is found at the N-terminal end of PorZ (also known as Immunoreactive 84kD antigen PG93) from Porphyromonas gingivalis and in similar proteins predominantly found in Bacteroidetes. PorZ is an essential component of the type-IX secretion system (T9SS) machinery. It consists of three domains. This entry represents the first domain, which folds into a seven-stranded beta-propeller [[PMID:27883039]].

Name: T7SS, ESX-1 secretion system protein EccA1, N-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found at the N-terminal end of ESX-1 secretion system protein EccA1 from Mycobacterium tuberculosis and similar proteins predominantly from Actinobacteria. EccA1 is part of the ESX-1 specialised secretion system (a cluster of the type VII secretion system, T7SS). EccA1 contains a C-terminal ATPase domain ([pfam:PF00004] and [pfam:PF17866]) and a tetratricopeptide repeat (TPR) containing N-terminal domain (this entry). The six tandem TPR motifs of this domain packed in parallel create a series of repeating antiparallel alpha-helices that give rise to an overall superhelix structure [[PMID:21784982],[PMID:23818233]].

Name: TELO2-interacting protein 1
Description: This entry includes TELO2-interacting protein 1 from yeast and its homologues from mammals. Budding yeast Tti1 is a subunit of the ASTRA complex, which is involved in chromatin remodelling [[PMID:19040720]]. Human TTI1 is a regulator of the DNA damage response (DDR). It is part of the TTT complex that is required to stabilize protein levels of the phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase-related protein kinase (PIKK) family proteins. The TTT complex is involved in the cellular resistance to DNA damage stresses [[PMID:34233195],[PMID:20427287]]. Together with the TTT complex and HSP90, participates in the proper folding of newly synthesized PIKKs [[PMID:20427287]]. Members of this entry are entirely composed of HEAT repeats [[PMID:34233195]].

Name: CATSPER subunit beta protein 2nd domain
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller domain from the Cation channel sperm-associated protein subunit beta protein. The cation channel of sperm (CatSper) is essential for sperm motility and fertility. The auxiliary subunit beta contains a single transmembrane segment and a large extracellular region.

Name: G7C, Tail fiber protein, domain 3
Description: This domain is found in Tail fiber protein from Escherichia virus G7C (Gp63) and similar proteins from tailed bacteriophages and bacterial prophages. Gp63 is a receptor-binding proteins (RBP) that emanate from the phage particle in the form of fibres for the virus to recognise and bind to their host. It deacetylates surface polysaccharides of E. coli leaving the backbone of the polysaccharide intact. This entry represents domain 3 of Ggp63, which shows structural similarity to a small domain that connects the sialidase domain with the N-terminal particle-binding domain in phage tailspikes with sialidase activity ([pfam:PF12218]). It shows two beta-helical rungs capped by an N-terminal alpha-helix oriented perpendicular to the rungs' axis [[PMID:28513100]].

Name: Stromalin conservative domain
Description: This entry represents a conserved domain found in stromal antigen or stromalin proteins, also known as cohesin subunit SA-1/2/3 (STAG1-3 or SCC3 homologue 1-3) that were originally identified from a murine bone marrow stromal cell line and are represented in several organisms from yeast to humans. The homologues from yeast are cohesin subunit SCC3 and meiotic recombination protein Rec11. STAG1-3 and SCC3 are components of the cohesin complex, which is required for the cohesion of sister chromatids after DNA replication [[PMID:16043696],[PMID:10698974],[PMID:25171859]]. This domain consists of HEAT-like repeated helices [[PMID:25171859]]. Mutations in STAG3 have been associated with male infertility [[PMID:31682730]].

Name: AP-5 complex subunit beta-1, N-terminal
Description: AP5B1 is part of the AP-5 adaptor protein complex, which may be involved in endosomal transport [[PMID:22022230],[PMID:29381698]]. This entry represents the N-terminal domain which adopts an alpha-solenoid structure.

Name: AP5B1, middle domain
Description: AP5B1 is part of the AP-5 adaptor protein complex, which may be involved in endosomal transport [[PMID:22022230],[PMID:29381698]]. This entry represents the central domain which consists of a repeated all-helical arrangement.

Name: Bride of doubletime-like, TPR domain
Description: This domain is found at the C-terminal end of Bride of doubletime from Drosophila melanogaster (Bdbt) and similar proteins predominantly from arthropods. Bdbt is a mediator of the effects of the kinase Doubletime on the key circadian transcriptional regulator Period. This entry represents the entirely alpha-helical TPR domain [[PMID:24210908]]. It is normally found associated with [pfam:PF18023].

Name: SDA1, C-terminal
Description: This entry includes SDA1 protein and its homologues. SDA1 is a Saccharomyces cerevisiae protein which is involved in the control of the actin cytoskeleton. The protein is essential for cell viability [[PMID:15112237]]. It is a HEAT repeat-containing protein [[PMID:16541108]], required for 60S pre-ribosomal subunits export to the cytoplasm and may also be required for 60S ribosomal subunit maturation and accumulation [[PMID:10704371],[PMID:11160833]]. This entry represents the C-terminal region of SDA1 proteins.

Name: YDR124W, N-terminal
Description: This entry includes mitochondrial proteins of unknown function found in yeast. Null mutant showed severe fragmentation of mitochondria and its expression is strongly induced by alpha factor. Members of this entry may have a role in maintenance of mitochondrial morphology [[PMID:31945465]]. This entry represents the N-terminal domain which may have an alpha/beta fold.

Name: WipA, alpha-helical hairpin
Description: This entry represents the alpha-helical hairpin of WipA protein from Legionella pneumophila [[PMID:28389563]] which adopts a coiled-coil structure. WipA is one of the effector proteins that is involved in its pathogenicity. This domain interacts with the phosphatase domain of another WipA molecule to form a dimer. It has been suggested that it mediates protein-protein interactions with a tyrosine- phosphorylated host target [[PMID:28389563]].

Name: Csf1 N-terminal region
Description: This entry includes Csf1 protein from yeast, which is the orthologue of human BLTP1. Csf1 also known as Cold sensitive for fermentation protein 1 is a fungal membrane protein required for fermentation at low temperature [[PMID:10781556]]. Csf1, similar to BLTP1, is a tube-forming lipid transport protein which provides phosphatidylethanolamine for glycosylphosphatidylinositol (GPI) anchor synthesis in the endoplasmic reticulum [[PMID:35015055]]. These proteins belong to the repeating beta-groove (RBG) superfamily which are conserved lipid transfer proteins containing long hydrophobic grooves consisting of multiple repeating modules made of five beta-sheets followed by a loop [[PMID:35491307]].

Name: tRNA modifying enzyme MnmG/GidA C-terminal helical domain
Description: The GidA associated domain is a domain that has been identified at the C-terminus of protein GidA. It consists of several helices, the last three being rather short and forming small bundle. GidA is an tRNA modification enzyme found in bacteria and mitochondrial. Based on mutational analysis this domain has been suggested to be implicated in binding of the D-stem of tRNA [[PMID:19446527]] and, in particular the small bundle, to be responsible for the interaction with protein MnmE [[PMID:18565343]]. Structures of GidA in complex with either tRNA or MnmE are missing. Reported to bind to Pfam family MnmE, [pfam:PF12631]. This entry represents the first helices of the GidA associated domain. The last three helices are covered in [pfam:PF13932].

Name: Emaravirus ABC_AB protein
Description: This family corresponds to the ABC proteins of clades A and B of emaraviruses (plant-infecting bunyaviruses). They are involved in pathogenicity, and may act as silencing suppressors in some species [1, https://doi.org/10.3390/f13111868]. This family contains a hydrophobic region bordered by two highly conserved positions R/K and G. They may consist of three helices according to structure predictions.

Name: Emaravirus ABC_C protein
Description: This family corresponds to the ABC proteins of clade C of emaraviruses (plant-infecting bunyaviruses). They are involved in pathogenicity, and may act as silencing suppressors in some species [1,https://doi.org/10.3390/f13111868]. These proteins may adopt an all-alpha topology.

Name: Dynactin subunit 5
Description: This entry includes dynactin 5 (DCTN5, also known as subunit p25). Dynactin is a major component of the activator complex that stimulates dynein-mediated vesicle transport. DCTN5 is part of the pointed-end subcomplex in dynactin that also includes p26, p27 (also known as dynactin 6), and Arp11 [[PMID:30143531],[PMID:28602352]]. This subcomplex interacts with membranous cargoes. DCTN5 plays a dual role in cargo binding and dynactin regulation, being critical for this process. DCTN5 contains imperfect tandem repeats of a hexapeptide repeat motif (X-[STAV]-X-[LIV]-[GAED]-X) [[PMID:15043994]].

Name: MIOS, alpha-solenoid
Description: GATOR complex protein MIOS, a component of the GATOR subcomplex GATOR2 which consists of MIOS, SEC13, SEH1L, WDR24 and WDR59, and it activates mTORC1 and the TORC1 signaling pathway through the inhibition of the GATOR1 subcomplex [[PMID:25263562]]. Sea4 is the counterpart from yeast, a component of the SEA complex which coats the vacuolar membrane and is involved in intracellular trafficking, autophagy, response to nitrogen starvation, and amino acid biogenesis [[PMID:21454883]]. This entry represents a region between the WD40 and the zinc ribbon-like domains, which may have an alpha-solenoid structure.

Name: MIOS, WD40 repeat
Description: GATOR complex protein MIOS, a component of the GATOR subcomplex GATOR2 which consists of MIOS, SEC13, SEH1L, WDR24 and WDR59, and it activates mTORC1 and the TORC1 signaling pathway through the inhibition of the GATOR1 subcomplex [[PMID:25263562]]. Sea4 is the counterpart from yeast, a component of the SEA complex which coats the vacuolar membrane and is involved in intracellular trafficking, autophagy, response to nitrogen starvation, and amino acid biogenesis [[PMID:21454883]]. This entry represents the N-terminal WD40 repeat domain of these proteins.

Name: 10-bladed beta propeller domain
Description: This entry represents a family of beta propeller repeats identified in metagenomics data. They have 10 blades in an arrangement similar to the sortilin protein.

Name: C-terminal repeat from RTX toxins
Description: This entry represents a short repeat found near the C-terminus of some RTX toxins such as [swissprot:Q9KS12] [[PMID:17646359]]. AlphaFold models suggest this repeat forms a beta solenoid structure. These repeats contain a GXGXD motif in each repeat which is similar to that found in the RTX calcium-binding nonapeptide repeat [pfam:PF00353]. This suggests that these repeats may also be needed for translocation via the type I secretion system [[PMID:17646359]].

Name: PMI1/PMIR1-2, C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents a presumed domain found towards the C-terminal of plant proteins, such as PMI1 (PLASTID MOVEMENT IMPAIRED 1 and PMIR1/2 (PMI1 related 1 and 2) from Arabidopsis. PMI1 and PMIR1 are required for efficient chloroplast photorelocation movement, while PMIR2 seems not necessary for this [[PMID:26324877]]. PMI1 is a mobile signal that initiates coordinated changes in protein-protein and protein-plasma membrane interactions for chloroplast movement in response to light, which determines where in the cell chloroplasts are able to anchor o the plasma membrane [[PMID:35477788]]. PMI1 has been found to mediate ABA sensitivity during germination [[PMID:25154696]].

Name: Cohesin subunit SCC3, C-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found at the C-terminal end of Cohesin subunit SCC3 from Saccharomyces cerevisiae and similar proteins from yeasts. SCC3 is a component of cohesin complex, a complex required for the cohesion of sister chromatids after DNA replication. This domain is is composed of a continuously twisted tandem array of antiparallel alpha-helices resembling tandem HEAT repeats. It binds to two small sections within Scc1, the other component of the complex [[PMID:25171859],[PMID:30109982]].

Name: TetR/AcrR family transcriptional repressors, C-terminal domain
Description: TetR/AcrR family transcriptional repressors regulate a wide range of cellular activities, including multidrug resistance, virulence and pathogenicity of bacteria. Members of this family are two domain proteins consisting of N-terminal HTH DNA-binding domain and C-terminal ligand recognition domain. This entry represents the C-terminal domain that besides the ligand recognition, is involved in dimerisation. In contrast to the N-terminal domain, this domain is more diverse allowing different regulators in the TetR/AcrR family to accommodate specific sets of inducing ligands.

Name: Family of unknown function (DUF6873)
Description: This entry represents a family of uncharacterised proteins that have a five fold symmetric alpha/beta propeller fold belonging to the GME (guanidino-group modifying enzymes) superfamily [[PMID:11504612],[PMID:16779844]].

Name: YNCE-like beta-propeller
Description: This entry includes bacterial YNCE proteins which consist of a beta-propeller structure (the so-called YVTN beta-propeller) [[PMID:22120742],[PMID:18931447]] which binds DNA [[PMID:18931447]].

Name: 4-fold beta flower protein
Description: This family of proteins have a repetitive structure composed of pairs of beta strands. In this family the proteins usually have four copies of the beta hairpin repeat.

Name: 5-fold beta-flower protein
Description: This family of proteins have a repetitive structure composed of pairs of beta strands. In this family the proteins usually have five copies of the beta hairpin repeat.

Name: 6-fold beta flower protein
Description: This family of proteins have a repetitive structure composed of pairs of beta strands. In this family the proteins usually have six copies of the beta hairpin repeat with a central alpha helix in the barrel.

Name: Family of unknown function (DUF6880)
Description: This entry represents a family of proteins that form an alpha-helical solenoid structure of HEAT-like repeats. Proteins in this family are found in various bacterial species, but most commonly in proteobacteria.

Name: Mucin-20 protein
Description: This entry represents a short repeat from mucin-20 protein that forms a beta solenoid structure in the AlphaFold model.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF6895)
Description: This entry contains a set of proteins that have a predicted 6-helical hairpin fold found in a wide range of glycosyl hydrolase enzymes, suggesting a potential function for these proteins. These proteins are largely found in actinobacteria and Alphaproteobacteria.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF6902)
Description: This entry represents a family of bacterial proteins that have an alpha/alpha(6) toroid structure. This suggests that these proteins are likely to be an as yet uncharacterised family of glycosyl hydrolases.

Name: Family of unknown function (DUF6910)
Description: The function of this family of proteins is unknown. They are predicted to adopt a five-bladed beta-propeller and are probably distantly related to apyrase.

Name: GP180, GOLD domain
Description: This entry represents the GOLD domain from GPR180, which is associated with TGF-beta signaling, as it regulates the activity of the TGF-beta receptor complex through SMAD3 phosphorylation [[PMID:36373655]]. Furthermore, GPR180 is also required for the action of Collagen triple helix repeat containing 1 (CTHRC1) to regulate brown and beige adipocyte activity and glucose homeostasis [[PMID:34880217]]. GPR180 was first to be an orphan Rhodopsin-like GPCR given structural similarities to GPCR transmembrane region, however, it was then confirmed not to signal as a a GPCR [[PMID:36373655],[PMID:34880217]]. GPR180 is homologous to TMEM145, TMEM181 and WLS, which have been described as GOST proteins (for GOLD domain seven-transmembrane helix proteins) together with TMEM87A/B, GPR107 and GPR108, given they share the same domain architecture consisting of a GOLD domain atop of a membrane-spanning seven-transmembrane helix domain [[PMID:36373655]].

Name: BRF2-like, C-terminal cyclin repeat
Description: This domain is found in human Transcription factor IIIB 50 kDa subunit (BRF2) and similar sequences mainly found in eukaryotes. BRF2 is a general activator of RNA polymerase III transcription. This protein consists of an N-terminal zinc ribbon ([pfam:PF08271]), a core domain consisting of two cyclin fold imperfect repeats and a C-terminal domain. This entry represents the second or C-terminal cyclin repeat [[PMID:26638071],[PMID:28743884],[PMID:29345637]].

Name: Gp138, beta helical trimerization domain
Description: This domain is found in Gp138 from Escherichia phage phi92 ([swissprot:I7HXF9]) and similar sequences found in tailed bacteriophages and prophages from proteobacteria. Gp138 forms an spike-shaped trimer with three highly intertwined chains. Each polypeptide has an oligonucleotide/oligosaccharide-binding (OB-fold) domain (Pfam: PF18352) and a long beta-helical C-terminal domain (this entry), which initiates the contact with the host cell membrane. The trimeric beta-helix is composed of three antiparallel beta-sheets, which swap three C-terminal beta-strands with each other around the threefold axis [[PMID:22325780]].

Name: Mitochondrial-derived peptide MOTS-c
Description: This protein family represents human Mitochondrial-derived peptide MOTS-c (MT-RNR1), a 16-residue peptide that regulates insulin sensitivity and metabolic homeostasis [[PMID:25738459]].

Name: Repeat of unknown function (DUF6923)
Description: This entry represents a beta propeller domain composed of five blades. It is often found in large extracellular proteins in one or more copies. It is uncertain whether this domain might have an enzymatic function or a ligand binding function. Structurally this domain appears to have a sixfold symmetry, but one of the blades takes up the space of normal repeats. This unusual blade is composed of three strands of the first repeat and a final C-terminal strand from the last repeat.

Name: TR61B FKBP-like domain
Description: This is the FKBP-like domain found towards the N-terminal of TR61B (tRNA (adenine(58)-N(1))-methyltransferase from human and similar proteins. This mitochondrion-specific protein catalyses the formation of N1-methyladenine at position 58 (m1A58) in various tRNAs [[PMID:23097428]] and the formation of 1-methyladenosine at position 947 of mitochondrial 16S ribosomal RNA which is most likely important for mitoribosomal structure and function [[PMID:27631568]]. TR61B is most similar to bacterial TrmI than to eukaryotic Trmt61A, according to phylogenetic tree analysis [[PMID:23097428]]. The function of this domain is not clear but it may be necessary for overall folding and full enzymatic activity.

Name: Family of unknown function (DUF6929)
Description: This is a family of uncharacterised bacterial proteins. They are predicted to fold into five-bladed beta-propeller that has similarity to apyrase but it is circularly permuted. The C-terminal two beta-strands of this domain occupy the position of the two N-terminal beta-strands in apyrase.

Name: PTEN upstream open reading frame MP31
Description: This protein family represents human PTEN upstream open reading frame MP31, a micropeptide that inhibits lactate dehydrogenase (LDH)-mediated conversion of lactate to pyruvate in mitochondria by competing with mitochondrial LDH for binding to NAD+ and also inhibits cellular lactate utilisation [[PMID:33406399]].

Name: Mitoregulin
Description: This family represents human Mitoregulin (MTLN) and similar proteins predominantly found in vertebrates. MTLN has a regulatory role in adipocyte metabolism. This microprotein interacts directly with the beta subunit of the mitochondrial trifunctional protein, an enzyme critical in the beta-oxidation of long-chain fatty acids. It also increases mitochondrial membrane potential while decreasing mitochondrial reactive oxygen species [[PMID:32243843],[PMID:29949756]].

Name: PSD13 N-terminal repeats
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal helical repeats of PSD13 from human and its orthologues from yeast, RPN9 [[PMID:25631053],[PMID:24706844],[PMID:27428775],[PMID:29636472]]. These proteins are a component of the 26S proteasome, a multiprotein complex involved in the ATP-dependent degradation of ubiquitinated proteins. This domain mediates interaction with the ubiquitin receptor Rpn10 (also known as PSMD4 in human) [[PMID:25631053]].

Name: CSN7 helical bundle subdomain
Description: This entry represents the helical bundle subdomain (HB) found at the N-terminal of CSN7 (COP9 signalosome complex subunit 7) proteins. This HB constitutes a functional subdomain that, with the C-terminal winged helix subdomain, constitutes the PCI domain, which mediate interactions with other COP9 signalosome components. The COP9 signalosome complex (CSN) that regulates the ubiquitin (Ubl) conjugation pathway by mediating the deneddylation of the cullin subunits of SCF-type E3 ligase complexes which leads to a decrease in Ubl ligase activity of SCF-type complexes such as SCF, CSA or DDB2. This complex is involved in various cellular and developmental processes [[PMID:18854373],[PMID:25043011],[PMID:31444342]].

Name: Pdc1 Ge1 domain
Description: This entry represents the Ge1 domain of Pdc1 from S. pombe. Pdc1 is known to be related to metazoan Edc4 (also known asGe-1) family, although the sequence similarity is very low. EDC4 (enhancer of mRNA-decapping protein 4) is a regulator of mRNA decapping in cytoplasmic processing bodies (P-bodies) [[PMID:24862735]].

Name: Large ribosomal subunit protein bL9m C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents a presumed domain found at the C-terminal of the large ribosomal subunit protein bL9m (mitochondrial) from animals. This domain may adopt a similar fold than prokaryotic large ribosomal subunit protein bL9.

Name: CT0912-like, C-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found at the C-terminal end of [swissprot:Q8KDY2] from Chlorobaculum tepidum (CT0912), a functionally uncharacterised protein with a ferredoxin-like domain repeat. This domain shows an alpha-beta configuration.

Name: Magnesium transporter MRS2-like
Description: This entry represents eukaryotic membrane transporters distantly related to prokaryotic CorA transporters, including the inner mitochondrial membrane Mg2+ transporters Mfm1p/Lpe10p and Mrs2p from yeast, MRS2/MRSL from human and the homologues from plants [[PMID:23999289]].

Name: ElaD/SseL N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal helical domain found in a group of CE proteases from human pathogens, including ElaD from E.coli, SseL from Salmonella typhimurium and ShiCE from Shigella [[PMID:32109687],[PMID:27425412]], which are dedicated deubiquitinases (DUBs), proteases that reverse the addition of ubiquitin to substrates, and effectively hijacks the host's ubiquitination processes [[PMID:27425412]]. This domain is structurally similar to VHS domains and it serves to localise the catalytic domain ([pfam:PF22102]) through an additional Ub binding site [[PMID:32109687]].

Name: Nucleoporin NUP120, helical domain
Description: This domain is found in Nucleoporin NUP120 from Saccharomyces cerevisiae and similar fungal sequences. NUP120 is a a component of the nuclear pore complex (NPC) involved in nuclear poly(A)+ RNA and pre-ribosome export, in GSP1 nuclear import, in NPC assembly and distribution, as well as in nuclear envelope organisation. This entry represents the alpha-helical domain of NUP120, which packs against the side of the beta-propeller [pfam:PF11715], located N-terminal of the protein [[PMID:19706512],[PMID:19576787],[PMID:25745173],[PMID:33268786]].

Name: T6SS, Peptidoglycan muramidase Tse3, N-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found in Peptidoglycan muramidase Tse3 from Pseudomonas aeruginosa and similar sequences from bacteria. Tse3 is a toxin secreted by the H1 type VI (H1-T6SS) secretion system into the periplasm of recipient cells. This protein degrades peptidoglycan via muramidase activity. It is organised into a small N-terminal domain (this entry), which contains seven alpha-helices packed in a rod-like helical repeat motif, and a C-terminal catalytic domain ([pfam:PF22115]) [[PMID:24100309],[PMID:24025333],[PMID:24724564]].

Name: Magnetotaxis protein MtxA, C-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found at the C-terminal end of Magnetotaxis protein MtxA from Magnetospirillum gryphiswaldense ([swissprot:A4TUL6]) and similar sequences from magnetotactic aquatic proteobacteria. Magnetotaxis is believed to direct the swimming of cells toward growth-favoring microoxic zones in natural waters. This domain shows five anti-parallel alpha-helices-and-turn motifs folded as tetra-trico-peptide repeat (TPR) motifs [[PMID:26052516]].

Name: Glycosyl hydrolase family 95 catalytic domain
Description: The founding member of the GH95 family of glycosyl hydrolases is alpha-1,2-fucosidase that catalyses the hydrolysis of an alpha-1,2-linked fucose. A member of this family, FucOB from A. muciniphila was shown to hydrolyse all three types of H antigen structures to obtain the afucosylated Bombay phenotype [[PMID:36997505]]. Members of this family are multidomain proteins and contain a central catalytic domain with an (alpha/alpha)6 helical barrel topology, represented by this entry.

Name: GtfA extended beta-sheet domain
Description: This entry represents the extended beta-sheet domain (EBD, also known as DUF1975) of UDP-N-acetylglucosamine--peptide N-acetylglucosaminyltransferase GtfA subunit [[PMID:26884191],[PMID:24936067]]. GtfA is the core enzyme of the OGT complex, which also includes the co-activator GtfB, to glycosylate the serine-rich repeat (SRR) of adhesin PsrP in Streptococcus pneumoniae, a protein involved in the infection and pathogenesis [[PMID:26884191],[PMID:24936067]]. The function of this domain is not clear.

Name: SSO1393-like, winged-helix domain
Description: This entry represents a WH-like domain present in SSO1393-like proteins. SSO1393 is an archaeal protein from Sulfolobus solfataricus (PDB:3qyf). This protein has been described as a component of the CRISPR system [[PMID:30444997]]. CRISPR (clustered regularly interspaced short palindromic repeat) is an adaptive immune system that provides protection against mobile genetic elements (viruses, transposable elements and conjugative plasmids) [[PMID:30444997],[PMID:30232454]].

Name: Acylamino-acid-releasing enzyme-like, N-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found at the N-terminal end of Acylamino-acid -releasing enzyme from Aeropyrum pernix (APH) and similar archaeal sequences. APH catalyses the removal of an N-acylated amino acid from peptides. This domain folds as a regular seven-bladed beta-propeller [[PMID:15296741],[PMID:17350041]].

Name: Bacteriophage T4 gp5 C-terminal trimerisation domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal trimerisation domain of bacteriophage T4 gp5. This domain associates to form a triple-stranded beta-helix that forms an equilateral triangular prism, which acts as a membrane-puncturing needle [[PMID:11823865],[PMID:12923574]]. The interior of the beta-helix has an increased hydrophobicity toward the C terminus. The middle part of the helix has a pore, enriched with polar and charged side chains.

Name: CFP-like, C-terminal thrombospondin type 1 domain
Description: This domain is found at the C-terminal end of human Properdin (CFP, for Complement factor P) and similar animal proteins. CFP is a positive regulator of the alternate pathway (AP) of complement. It contains six thrombospondin repeat type I (TSP 1-6 domains and an N-terminal domain ([pfam:PF18487]). This entry represents the last TSP type I domain, which binds to the first TSP type I domain ([pfam:PF00090]), contributing to multimerisation [[PMID:12391027],[PMID:15491616],[PMID:21821127],[PMID:31507604],[PMID:31552043]].

Name: Surface cell antigen Sca2 helical repeat
Description: Sca2 (surface cell antigen 2) is the only bacterial protein known to promote both actin filament nucleation and profilin-dependent elongation, mimicking eukaryotic formins to assemble actin comet tails for Rickettsia motility. We show that Sca2's functional mimicry of formins is achieved through a unique mechanism. Unlike formins, Sca2 is monomeric, but has N- and C-terminal repeat domains (NRD and CRD) that interact with each other for processive barbed-end elongation. The crystal structure of NRD reveals a previously undescribed fold, consisting of helix-loop-helix repeats arranged into an overall crescent shape [[PMID:23818602]].

Name: Quiescin sulphydryl oxidase pseudo-Erv domain
Description: Quiescin sulphydryl oxidase (QSOX) family of enzymes is the only known example of conserved concatenation of disulphide-generating and disulphide-transferring modules within a single polypeptide. Formation and transfer of disulphide bonds in QSOX are mediated by redox-active cysteine pairs in a canonical Cys-X-X-Cys pattern. One of these pair is in the N-terminal thioredoxin-like domain (Trx1). Another is in the C-terminal flavin adenine dinucleotide (FAD)-binding domain (Erv) related to the mitochondrial enzyme Erv1. The middle domain, the so-called pseudo-Erv domain, bridges the Trx1 and Erv domains and is structurally similar to the Erv domain [[PMID:22801504]].

Name: CofB beta-repeat domain
Description: CofB is a minor pilin involved in type IV pili assembly. It consists of a pilin domain at the N-terminal, followed by a beta-repeat hinge-like domain and a C-terminal beta-sheet-rich lectin domain [[PMID:26324721],[PMID:26876601]]. This entry represents the central beta-repeat domain which has two three-stranded anti-parallel beta-sheets [[PMID:26324721],[PMID:26876601]].

Name: CofB C-terminal domain
Description: CofB is a minor pilin involved in type IV pili assembly. It consists of a pilin domain at the N-terminal, followed by a beta-repeat hinge-like domain and a C-terminal beta-sheet-rich domain, similar to H-type lectins [[PMID:26876601],[PMID:26324721]]. This entry represents the C-terminal domain which is required for CofB to initiate pilus assembly [[PMID:26876601],[PMID:26324721]].

Name: PSMD12/CSN4, N-terminal
Description: The 26S proteasome consists of a 20S proteolytic core particle (CP) and a 19S regulatory particle (RP). The RP can be further divided into two subcomplexes, a base and a peripheral lid. The RP lid is structurally similar to the COP9 signalosome (CSN). The core CSN subunits (CSN4/5/7/6) show a remarkable one-to-one sequence correspondence with those of the RP lid (RPN5/11/9/8), suggesting a common ancestry and architecture [[PMID:11742986]]. This entry represents the N-terminal region of PSMD12/RPN5) and CSN4 subunits, which consists of helical repeats [[PMID:26744777],[PMID:24706844],[PMID:25043011],[PMID:27428775],[PMID:30177392]].

Name: BDI_1685-like, C-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found at the C-terminal end of BDI_1685 from Parabacteroides distasonis ([swissprot:A6LCM3]) and similar bacterial sequences. This domain is usually found associated with [pfam:PF17128].

Name: DGF-1 beta-sheet domain
Description: This entry represents a beta-sheet domain present in DGF-1 from Typanosoma proteins, one of the most abundant proteins in T. cruzi genome [[PMID:36839564]]. This domain adopts a pectin lyase-like fold. The function of this protein is not yet clear, however, it is likely to play a role in cell-to-cell interactions or signal transduction.

Name: Annexin E1, fourth repeat
Description: This domain is found at the C-terminal end of Annexin E1 from Giardia intestinalis (ANXE1) and similar sequences predominantly found in lower eukaryotes. ANXE1 may function as a calcium -regulated structural element linking phospholipid bilayer and underlying axoneme. It shows a core domain that consists of four tandem repeats and a a C-terminal extension. This entry represents the fourth repeat, which shows five alpha-helices and forms a module with the first repeat [[PMID:19046974],[PMID:22796298]].

Name: Aldos-2-ulose dehydratase, beta-propeller domain
Description: This domain is found in Aldos-2-ulose dehydratase from Phanerodontia chrysosporium (AUDH) and similar sequences from invertebrates and fungi. AUDH is a bifunctional enzyme which catalyses the dehydration of anhydrofructose into ascopyrone M, and the isomerisation of ascopyrone M into microthecin. It is organised into three domains: an N-terminal domain with a beta-propeller fold (this entry), a middle domain containing two cupin folds ([pfam:PF18637]) and a C-terminal lectin domain. This domain folds into a seven-bladed beta-propeller, where each blade is made up of a four-stranded, antiparallel beta-sheet [[PMID:22330145]].

Name: SgrA, immunoglobulin-like
Description: This entry represents a Immunoglobulin-like domain present in the Serine-glutamate repeat protein A (SgrA), also known as LPXTG family cell surface protein Fms2 [[PMID:27334767]].

Name: A/B hydrolase-like, N-terminal domain
Description: This is the N-terminal domain found in a group of uncharacterised bacterial proteins, which have a Phospholipase/carboxylesterase/thioesterase domain ([pfam:PF02230]) at the C-terminal. This domain consists of ARM repeats.

Name: Negative regulator of ofd1, C-terminal
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal region of fission yeast Negative regulator of ofd1 (Nro1) whose alpha-helices are grouped in six helical hairpins [[PMID:21481773],[PMID:21610214]].

Name: Small ribosomal subunit protein mS39, PPR
Description: This entry represents the a PPR section found at the N-terminal of Small ribosomal subunit protein mS39 (also known as PPR domain protein 3 (PTCD3)), a mitochondrial RNA-binding protein that has a role in mitochondrial translation [[PMID:24799711],[PMID:24162847],[PMID:25231995],[PMID:25664731],[PMID:25517350]].

Name: RAP1 binding motif of TAZ1
Description: This entry represents a short helical motif in Telomere length regulator TAZ1 found in S.pombe. TAZ1 recruits the spRAP1 protein to telomeres. The TAZ1 RAP1-binding motif (RBM) binds the RAP1 C-terminal domain (RCT), which structurally resembles the first 3-helix bundle found in yeast and human RAP1 RCT. TAZ1, an ortholog of TRF1 and TRF2, has a TRF homology (TRFH) domain ([pfam:PF08558]), the RBM domain, a dimerization domain, and a myb-like C-terminal ([pfam:PF00249]). RAP1, identified in budding yeast as repressor/activator protein 1, is a well-conserved telomere binding protein and is also found in fission yeast and mammals. In Saccharomyces cerevisiae, RAP1 directly binds DNA and is involved in transcriptional activation and mating type information gene silencing, as well as in binding to numerous binding sites at each telomere, where it functions in telomere length regulation, telomeric position effect gene silencing, and telomere end protection. Like S. pombe RAP1, human RAP1 does not bind telomeric DNA directly, but binds telomere repeat binding factor 2 (TRF2) through the RAP C-terminal domain (RCT) [[PMID:21217703],[PMID:22244753],[PMID:10850490]].

Name: Phage tail fiber repeats
Description: This entry represents a small repeat found in phage tail proteins from tailed bacteriophages and prophages mainly from flavobacteria.

Name: ADP-ribosylating toxin CARDS, C-terminal beta-trefoil domain
Description: This domain is found at the C-terminal end of ADP-ribosylating toxin CARDS from Mycoplasma pneumoniae, the main virulence factor for this bacteria. CARDS (for Community-Acquired Respiratory Distress Syndrome Toxin) is organised into three domains, an N-terminal [pfam:PF02917] and tandem C-terminal beta -trefoil domain. This entry represents the last domain (named D3) [[PMID:25848012]].

Name: Avirulence protein AvrPiz-t
Description: This entry represents avirulence protein AvrPiz-t from Magnaporthe oryzae [[PMID:23334361]]. It is predicted to be secreted and it can suppress programmed cell death (PCD) induced by BAX in tobacco which suggests that it might contribute to the pathogenicity of M.oryzae.

Name: Colicin-A N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal translocation domain of colicin A, an endonuclease active on both single- and double-stranded DNA but with undefined specificity [[PMID:19627502],[PMID:22493500]]. This domain interacts directly with TolAIII (C-terminal domain of TolA) which facilitates translocation through the cell envelope to reach their cytotoxic site of action [[PMID:22493500]].

Name: Hydrophobin
Description: This protein family includes Hydrophobin from Neurospora crassa (Eas) and similar fungal sequences. Eas contributes to surface hydrophobicity, which is important for processes such as association of hyphae in reproductive structures. Eas adopts a beta-barrel structure with several disordered regions, displaying a complete segregation of charged and hydrophobic residues on its surface, which is consistent with its ability to form an amphipathic polymer [[PMID:16537446]].

Name: SbsC,spectrin-like repeat
Description: This entry represents a Spectrin-like domain present in the bacterial S-layer protein SbsC from Geobacillus stearothermophilus [[PMID:18682224]]. This  domain shows structural similarity to the Spectrin domain ([pfam:PF00435]).

Name: Cmr7b-like, BtrG-like domain
Description: This entry represents a BtrG-like domain present in Cmr7b protein from Sulfolobus solfataricus. Cmr7b is involved in the CRISPR/Cas pathway [[PMID:22227115]]. CRISPR (clustered regularly interspaced short palindromic repeat) is an adaptive immune system that provides protection against mobile genetic elements (viruses, transposable elements and conjugative plasmids).

Name: ToxT, N-terminal cupin-like domain
Description: This entry represents a cupin-like domain present in ToxT from Vibrio Cholerae [[PMID:20133655],[PMID:27599865]]. ToxT serves as the culminating factor in the  regulatory pathway, ensuring the coordinated production of two virulence factors: the toxin-coregulated pilus (TCP) and cholera toxin (CT), which are crucial for the pathogenicity of V. cholerae in causing cholera. ToxT possesses dimerization and regulatory elements in its cupin-like N-terminal domain, while its C-terminus harbors two HTH AraC-like DNA binding domains [[PMID:31815195],[PMID:21673111]].

Name: Macrophage Locus Protein A, C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the Macrophage Locus Protein A (MglA) C-terminal domain from Francisella tularensis. MglA interact with both RNA polymerase (RNAP) and the pathogenicity island (FPI) gene regulator to activate FPI transcription [[PMID:26121147],[PMID:28864445],[PMID:33217319]].

Name: Mannosylglycerate hydrolase MGH1-like glycoside hydrolase domain
Description: This entry represents the glycoside hydrolase domain found at the C-terminal of Mannosylglycerate hydrolase MGH1 and similar sequences.

Name: MRM3-like substrate binding domain
Description: MRM3 catalyses the formation of 2'-O-methylguanosine at position 1370 Gm1370) in the 16S mitochondrial large subunit ribosomal RNA (mtLSU rRNA), a conserved modification in the peptidyl transferase domain of the mtLSU rRNA [[PMID:12077432],[PMID:15581897],[PMID:34315873]]. This entry represents the substrate binding domain of MRM3 from animals and related sequences from bacteria.

Name: Gp49, pectin lyase-like
Description: This entry represents the beta-helix repeats from the catalytic domain of tailspike Gp49 from phage LKA1 [[PMID:29176754]]. Gp49 binds and cleaves B-band LPS of Pseudomonas aeruginosa, reducing its virulence [[PMID:29176754]].

Name: Pheromone cCF10 receptor, C-terminal TPR region
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal tetratrico peptide repeat region (TPR) of the pheromone cCF10 receptor from Enterococcus faecalis (PrgX), which serves as a molecular switch controlling expression of conjugation and virulence genes encoded by the conjugative plasmid pCF10. This region includes two domains: a large central dimerisation (and pheromone-binding) domain, and a C-terminal regulatory domain that is important for structural integrity [[PMID:16339309],[PMID:17038121]].

Name: NOP9-like PUF repeat domain
Description: This entry represents the PUF repeat domain found in the NOP9 protein.ED   L9JDG0.1/603-752; L9JDG0.1/603-716;

Name: Choice-of-anchor I domain
Description: This entry represents the choice-of-anchor I domain.

Name: PreP C-terminal domain
Description: PreP is a protease that degrades targeting peptides as well as other unstructured peptides in both mitochondria and chloroplasts. This entry represents the C-terminal domain of PreP and related proteins.

Name: Virilizer, yeast
Description: This entry includes virilizer (Vir1p), the yeast homologue of human virilizer (VIRMA) [[PMID:36930734]]. It is a component of the MIS complex, a complex that mediates N6-methyladenosine (m6A) methylation of meiotic mRNAs and is required for initiation of meiosis, progression through the meiotic divisions and sporulation. In the complex, it plays a scaffolding role, stabilizing the other complex members [[PMID:36930734]].

Name: eIF3a, PCI domain, TPR-like region
Description: This entry represents the TPR-like region of the PCI domain of the Eukaryotic translation initiation factor 3 subunit A from Saccharomyces cerevisiae (eIF3a), the RNA-binding component of the eukaryotic translation initiation factor 3 (eIF-3) complex, which specifically targets and initiates translation of a subset of mRNAs involved in cell proliferation. This region folds into flatly arranged helical repeats [[PMID:25171412]].

Name: Poly(A) RNA polymerase, mitochondrial-like, central palm domain
Description: This domain is found in human Poly(A) RNA polymerase, mitochondrial (MTPAP) and similar proteins from eukaryotes. MTPAP is a noncanonical polymerase that creates the 3' poly(A) tail of mitochondrial transcripts. It contains three domains: a N-terminal domain RL ([pfam:PF17797]) and the canonical palm (this entry) and fingers domains. This domain shows a five -stranded beta-sheet with the three catalytic aspartic acid residues located on its surface [[PMID:21292163]], together with several alpha- helices, forming an alpha-beta configuration.

Name: PPP2R1A-like HEAT repeat
Description: This entry represents HEAT repeats found in protein phosphatase 2A PR65/A subunit (PPP2R1A) [[PMID:9989501],[PMID:10353245],[PMID:16901787],[PMID:17086192],[PMID:17055435]].

Name: Histone H3 K4-specific methyltransferase SET7 N-terminal
Description: This entry includes histone-lysine N-methyltransferase SETD7 (also known as SET7/9) which specifically monomethylate Lys-4 of histone H3, creating a specific tag for epigenetic transcriptional activation [[PMID:12372304],[PMID:12389038],[PMID:12514135],[PMID:15525938],[PMID:16415881]]. This entry represents the N-terminal region, which consists of MORN repeats.

Name: Large ribosomal subunit protein uL24m
Description: This entry represents a domain found in the homologues of large ribosomal subunit protein uL24m. This protein is a component of the mitochondrial ribosome (mitoribosome) [[PMID:24675956]].

Name: Fatty acid synthase central AT domain
Description: This entry includes a domain found in the central region of the acyltransferase domain (AT) from fungal type I fatty acid synthase (FAS, such as FAS1 from yeast) and mycobacteria, which have secondary-structure elements and sequence homology [[PMID:17448991],[PMID:17431182],[PMID:31509190],[PMID:31506493],[PMID:32471977]]. Mycobacterial fatty acid synthase (FAS) complex is an homohexameric multifunctional enzyme that catalyses the synthesis of fatty acid precursors of mycolic acids, the major components of the cell wall in Mycobacteria and play an important role in pathogenicity [[PMID:23291528]].

Name: EST1-like DNA-binding domain
Description: This entry represents a DNA/RNA binding domain found in a group of worm sequences, such as [swissprot:G5ECF1] from C.elegans [[PMID:23348841]], which are related to Est1A from human (SMG6), defined as 14-3-3-like proteins involved in nonsense-mediated mRNA decay (NMD) pathway.

Name: Mal s 1 allergenic protein-like
Description: This protein family includes Mal s 1 allergenic protein from the yeast Malassezia sympodialis (Mala s 1), which forms a relatively compact cup-like beta-propeller consisting of six blades each with four antiparallel beta-strands [[PMID:17481656]].

Name: S1/CSD-like domain 2 of the TOTE conflict systems
Description: This entry represents a presumed ribonucleoprotein complex-forming domain of the TOTE ((TPR, OB, TBP, Effector) conflict systems, potentially binding ssRNAs derived from hybrid duplexes bound by the systems. It is typically found in a two-domain tandem repeat arrangement, this is the second domain of the two [[PMID:35609893]].

Name: S1/CSD-like domain 1 of the TOTE conflict systems
Description: This entry represents a presumed ribonucleoprotein complex-forming domain of the TOTE systems, potentially binding ssRNAs derived from hybrid duplexes bound by the systems. It is typically found in a two-domain tandem repeat arrangement, this is the first domain of the two.

Name: NACHT C-terminal Cysteine and Histidine-containing
Description: This is a domain discovered C-terminal to HEAT repeat modules, observed in some bacterial NACHT conflict systems. These domains feature highly conserved cysteine and histidine residues [[PMID:37160116]].

Name: ToastRack found in some PspC
Description: This entry represents the ToastRack domain found fused to some PspC [[PMID:38809013]].

Name: Ant venom allergen Sol i 2 family
Description: Sol i 2, one of four known potent allergens from the venom of red imported fire ant, is a powerful trigger of anaphylaxis. It causes production of IgE antibody in many individuals stung by fire ants. The closest structure homolog of Sol I 2 is the odorant binding protein and pheromone binding protein LUSH of the fruit fly Drosophila ([pfam:PF01395]), suggesting a possible similar biological function [[PMID:22100449]].

Name: Plant bHLH transcription factor, ACT-like domain
Description: This entry represents an ACT-like domain found at the C-terminal of plant transcription factors, such as transcription factor GLABRA 3 from Arabidopsis and the bHLH transcription factor (swissprot:A0A060CUZ2) from maize, which is associated with bHLH domains. This domain association is unique to the plantae kingdom [[PMID:37126718]]. Phylogenetic analysis show that the association of ACT-like and bHLH domains occurred early in Plantae by recruitment of an ACT-like domain in a common ancestor with widely distributed ACT DOMAIN REPEAT (ACR) genes by an ancestral bHLH gene. The functional significance of this association relies on the homodimer formation of ACT-like domains that negatively affects DNA binding of the associated bHLH domains [[PMID:37126718]]. For some bHLH transcription factors with the C-terminal ACT-like domain, such as Arabidopsis SPEECHLESS and MUTE, the ACT-like domain serves as heterodimerisation selectivity interface.

Name: ZW10 C-terminal helical domain
Description: This entry represents the last of the alpha-helical repeat domains of Zw10 protein. Zw10 protein, in the context of the conserved Rod-Zwilch-Zw10 (RZZ) complex, is required for correct metaphase check-pointing during mitosis [[PMID:34061181],[PMID:11146659]] and it is also important in vesicle transport between Golgi and ER as part of a second complex together with the conserved conserved RINT1/Tip20p and NAG/Sec39p proteins [[PMID:11146660]]. Zw10 is the metazoan homologue of yeast Dsl1, both included in the complexes associated with tethering containing helical rods (CATCHR) family, sharing structural features [[PMID:11146660],[PMID:22685323]].

Name: Dermonecrotic toxin, barrel-like domain
Description: Dermonecrotic toxin (ToxA), also known as Pasteurella multocida toxin (PMT), inhibits osteoblastic differentiation in mammals and birds. It consists of an N-terminal domain that binds to target cells, and a large C-terminal domain. This entry represents a presumed barrel-like domain found between the N- and C-terminal domains.

Name: DNAJC11, beta-barrel domain
Description: This entry represents a beta-barrel domain found in the central region of DNAJC11 from humans and its orthologues. DNAJC11 is a mitochondrial outer membrane protein involved in mitochondrial biogenesis and in the response to microenvironment changes and requirements. The J-domain mediates its mitochondrial localization, while the C-terminal domain is critical for protein-protein interactions [[PMID:31550165]]. The function of this domain is believed to be involved in protein-protein interactions. However, its exact function is unknown [[PMID:31550165]].

Name: Polymorphic toxin system, DSP-PTPase phosphatase
Description: This domain occupies the toxin tip position in a subset of polymorphic toxin domains [[PMID:36968430],[PMID:22731697]]. This domain is found at the extreme C-terminal of certain polymorphic toxin-related Tc-like toxins deployed by bacteria against eukaryotic host cells. This position is where the toxin module delivered into the target cells host resides [[PMID:36968430]].

Name: COP9 signalosome complex subunit 3, N-terminal helical repeats
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal helical repeat domain from COP9 signalosome complex subunit 3 [[PMID:25043011],[PMID:31444342]], a component of the eight-subunit COP9 signalosome complex (CSN), which inactivate Cullin-RING E3 ubiquitin ligases (CRLs) by removing their covalently attached activator, NEDD8. The CSN complex has two organisational centres, one consisting of an open ring formed by association of the WH subdomains from the PCI proteins (CSN1-4 and CSN7-8) and a bundle comprising the C-terminal alpha-helices from each subunit. The N-terminal helical repeat domains of the PCI proteins radiate from the PCI ring at the base of the complex [[PMID:25043011]].

Name: RsiG-like
Description: This entry represents a domain found in homologues of RsiG protein from Streptomyces. This domain adopts an alpha-helix and in Streptomyces self associates to form a dimer. This dimer binds c-di-GMP via two E(X)3-S(X)2-R(X)3-Q(X)-D repeat motifs, one on each helix. The complex RsiG-(c-di-GMP) binds to the sporulation-specific sigma factor, WhiG. Some homologues of RsiG contain a second helix and hence two c-di-GMP binding motifs.

Name: F1F0 ATP synthase subunit ASA5
Description: This entry represents the homologues of mitochondrial F1F0 ATP synthase subunit ASA5. F0 has six transmembrane helices and four long, membrane-intrinsic helices, which form the two helical hairpins of the conserved alpha subunit. Of the six transmembrane helices, two belong to ASA6 and one each to ASA5, ASA8, ASA9, and ASA10.

Name: Protein M, smaller domain
Description: This domain is found in proteins from Mycoplasmaceae, including Uncharacterized protein MG281 from Mycoplasma genitalium, also known as protein M. This virulence protein consists of two domains. The larger one ([pfam:PF20757]) binds the IgG light chain to block the binding of antibody to antigen and includes a leucine-rich repeat (LRR)-like subdomain. This domain folds into two alpha-helices [[PMID:24503852]].

Name: TrAA12-like
Description: This entry represents homologues of the AA12 enzyme from Trichoderma reesei ([swissprot:G0RPY6]). This enzyme adopts six-bladed beta-propeller structure. AA12 shows similarities to the global active-site architecture of bacterial glucose dehydrogenases, suggesting their common evolutionary origin.

Name: Plant cysteine-rich protein domain
Description: This domain is found in a group of plant cysteine-rich proteins, including Cysteine-rich repeat secretory protein 1 from Arabidopsis thaliana. This domain is usually found C-terminal to two copies of [pfam:PF01657]. It is predicted to adopt an alpha -helical structure.

Name: Alpha-1,3-glucanase catalytic domain D2
Description: This entry represents a domain found in the homologues of Alpha-1,3-glucanase. This domain is found C-terminal in the catalytic domain (CatAgl-FH1). It folds into a right-handed beta-helix fold [[PMID:31788942]].

Name: PsaO transmembrane domain
Description: PsaO is one of the nine membrane-embedded subunits of photosystem I. This entry represents the transmembrane domain that was shown to consists of three transmembrane helices arranged in an L-shape [[PMID:36951548]].

Name: Pel9A-like, right handed beta helix region
Description: This region is found in Pectate lyase L from Erwinia chrysanthemi (Pel9A) and similar bacterial and fungal proteins. Pel9A shows endo-cleaving activity on polygalacturonate or partially methylated pectin and has an important role in soft -rot disease. This region forms a parallel beta helix domain [[PMID:14670977]].

Name: CATSPERE beta-propeller domain
Description: The CATSPER (cation channel of sperm) complex is a tetrameric complex consisting of CATSPER1, CATSPER2, CATSPER3 and CATSPER4, it functions as an alkalinisation-activated calcium channel. This complex is involved in sperm cell hyperactivation, a process needed for sperm motility, which is essential late in the preparation of sperm for fertilisation [[PMID:34225353]]. This complex requires four auxiliary subunits (beta, gamma, delta and epsilon), which play a role in channel assembly [[PMID:34225353]]. This entry includes CATSPERE, the auxiliary subunit epsilon of the CatSper complex. This is a multi-domain protein which has a similar domain organisation as the other auxiliary subunits, beta, gamma and delta. The epsilon subunit has an additional N-terminal domain (NTD2) which is followed by a beta-propeller domain, represented in this entry.

Name: Probable glycosyl hydrolase domain
Description: This domain is closely related to domains members of glycosyl hydrolase family 43. It is predicted to fold into a five bladed beta-propeller.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7017)
Description: This entry represents a domain consisting of TPR repeats found at the N-terminal of of group of uncharacterised sequences predominantly from bacteria, which seem to be related to TOTE conflict systems as they are associated with [pfam:PF22708].

Name: AimR transcriptional regulator-like
Description: This entry includes AimR transcriptional regulator from Bacillus phage and related sequences found in tailed bacteriophages and prophages. AimR (arbitrium receptor), which is a part of the so-called phage arbitrium system, is a DNA-binding phage regulatory protein that acts as a receptor for a 6-amino acid signaling peptide cleaved rom the C terminus of AimP. AimR controls expression of AimX, the third member of the quorum sensing-like arbitrium system and an inhibitor of phage lysogeny [[PMID:28099413]]. AimR assembles in an alpha-alpha superhelix fold and accommodates the signaling peptide encircled by its tetratricopeptide repeats, which is reminiscent of RRNPP family members from the quorum-sensing system [[PMID:30224798],[PMID:30745087]].

Name: EMC2 TPR-like repeat domain
Description: ER membrane protein complex subunit 2 (EMC2, also known as tetratricopeptide repeat protein 35) is a tetratricopeptide repeat-containing protein, and a component of the ER membrane protein complex (EMC), which is required for efficient folding of proteins in the endoplasmic reticulum (ER). EMC2 is a cytosolic component of the complex [[PMID:32459176]]. Purified recombinant EMC2 formed a stable complex with either EMC8 or EMC9 [[PMID:32459176]].

Name: Integrator complex subunit 1, R3 domain
Description: Integrator (INT) complex is involved in the small nuclear RNAs (snRNA) U1 and U2 transcription and in their 3'-box-dependent processing. It binds the C-terminal domain (CTD) of RNA polymerase II (Pol II) and functions as an RNA endonuclease to cleave different classes of RNAs, regulating the transcription of both protein-coding genes and noncoding elements [[PMID:33243860]]. Integrator also associates with protein phosphatase 2A (PP2A) core enzyme to form the INTAC complex [[PMID:33243860]] which regulates transcription through dephosphorylation of Pol II CTD. This entry includes the Integrator complex subunit 1 (INTS1) which forms the backbone module of the complex (it consists of four modules: backbone, shoulder, phosphatase and endonuclease) through the association with INTS2 and INTS7 to form a rigid scaffold. The backbone and shoulder modules form a central cruciform scaffold. INTS1 contains four repetitive helix hairpin domains towards the C-terminal (R1-R4); this entry represents R3 [[PMID:33243860]].

Name: Integrator complex subunit 1, R4 domain
Description: Integrator (INT) complex is involved in the small nuclear RNAs (snRNA) U1 and U2 transcription and in their 3'-box-dependent processing. It binds the C-terminal domain (CTD) of RNA polymerase II (Pol II) and functions as an RNA endonuclease to cleave different classes of RNAs, regulating the transcription of both protein-coding genes and noncoding elements [[PMID:33243860]]. Integrator also associates with protein phosphatase 2A (PP2A) core enzyme to form the INTAC complex [[PMID:33243860]] which regulates transcription through dephosphorylation of Pol II CTD. This entry includes the Integrator complex subunit 1 (INTS1) which forms the backbone module of the complex (it consists of four modules: backbone, shoulder, phosphatase and endonuclease) through the association with INTS2 and INTS7 to form a rigid scaffold. The backbone and shoulder modules form a central cruciform scaffold. INTS1 contains four repetitive helix hairpin domains towards the C-terminal (R1-R4); this entry represents R4 domain [[PMID:33243860]].

Name: Integrator complex subunit 1, INTS2-binding domain
Description: Integrator (INT) complex is involved in the small nuclear RNAs (snRNA) U1 and U2 transcription and in their 3'-box-dependent processing. It binds the C-terminal domain (CTD) of RNA polymerase II (Pol II) and functions as an RNA endonuclease to cleave different classes of RNAs, regulating the transcription of both protein-coding genes and noncoding elements [[PMID:33243860],[PMID:34762484]]. Integrator also associates with protein phosphatase 2A (PP2A) core enzyme to form the INTAC complex [[PMID:33243860]] which regulates transcription through dephosphorylation of Pol II CTD. This entry includes the Integrator complex subunit 1 (INTS1) which forms the backbone module of the complex (it consists of four modules: backbone, shoulder, phosphatase and endonuclease) through the association with INTS2 and INTS7 to form a rigid scaffold. The backbone and shoulder modules form a central cruciform scaffold. INTS1 is an all-alpha helical protein with four repetitive helix hairpin domains towards the C-terminal (R1-R4) [[PMID:33243860]]. This entry represents a domain found at the N-terminal region of INTS1, after [pfam:PF12432], which interacts with the INTS2 subunit [[PMID:34762484]].

Name: Large ribosomal subunit protein mL44, endonuclease domain
Description: Large ribosomal subunit protein mL44 (RM44) is a component of the 39S subunit of mitochondrial ribosome. It may play a role in the assembly/stability of nascent mitochondrial polypeptides exiting the ribosome. This entry represents the endonuclease (RNase III-like) domain [[PMID:25278503],[PMID:24362565],[PMID:25271403]].

Name: CCR4-NOT transcription complex subunit 1, N-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found at the N-terminal end of human CCR4-NOT transcription complex subunit 1 (CNOT1) and similar sequences from animals. CNOT1 is a scaffolding component of the CCR4-NOT complex which is one of the major cellular mRNA deadenylases and is linked to various cellular processes including bulk mRNA degradation, miRNA-mediated repression, translational repression during translational initiation and general transcription regulation. This domain adopts the structure of an helical HEAT-repeat [[PMID:36586408]].

Name: LEM-3-like GIY-YIG domain
Description: This domain is found in the metazoan homologues of LEM-3 DNA nuclease. LEM-3 processes chromatin bridges that arise from incomplete DNA replication, unresolved recombination intermediates, or the perturbance of chromosome structure [[PMID:29463814]]. It localizes to the midbody and this localization depends on factors needed for midbody assembly. LEM-3 is composed of three domains: an ankyrin-repeat domain at the N-terminal, followed by a LEM domain and a C-terminal GIY-YIG nuclease domain represented by this entry.

Name: PIK3R4-like, middle domain
Description: This entry represents a TPR-like domain found in diverse eukaryotic kinases and phosphatases. The domain is present in phosphoinositide 3-kinase regulatory subunit 4 (PIK3R4), serine/threonine-protein kinase VPS15, protein phosphatase 2A regulatory subunits, and protein phosphatase 4 regulatory subunit 1. The domain shows strong conservation across mammals, plants, fungi and other eukaryotes. VPS15 is involved in autophagy and vacuolar protein sorting pathways. Protein phosphatase 2A regulatory subunits are essential components of the PP2A holoenzyme complex. The specific function of this TPR-like domain remains to be determined [[PMID:9078365],[PMID:22361507]].

Name: E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase listerin, N-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found at the N-terminal end of E3 ubiquitin -protein ligase listerin from Saccharomyces cerevisiae (Ltn1) and similar eukaryotic proteins. Ltn1 is a component of the ribosome quality control complex (RQC), a ribosome-associated complex that mediates ubiquitination and extraction of incompletely synthesised nascent chains for proteasomal degradation. This domain is predominantly composed of alpha -helices that are arranged into HEAT repeats, each consisting of two antiparallel alpha-helices that pack against each other in a repeating array [[PMID:27385828]].

Name: Protein zer-1 homolog-like, C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of human Protein zer-1 homolog (ZER1) and similar eukaryotic proteins. ZER1 serves as substrate adapter subunit in the E3 ubiquitin ligase complex ZYG11B-CUL2-Elongin BC. This protein functions to target substrates bearing N-terminal degrons for proteasomal degradation with the first four residues of substrates being the key recognition elements. This domain consists of armadillo repeats [[PMID:34214466]].

Name: Mediator of RNA polymerase II transcription subunit 14, RM2
Description: This entry represents an alpha/beta motif that is found tandemly repeated in the homologues of human Mediator of RNA polymerase II transcription subunit 14 (Med14). Med14 is a component of the Mediator complex, a coactivator involved in the regulated transcription of nearly all RNA polymerase II-dependent genes. Med14 along with Med17 are scaffold subunits in nucleating Mediator [[PMID:31402173],[PMID:33958484]]. The major portion of Med14 exists in the Head-Shoulder module of the Mediator and consists of nine tandem repeats (RM1 to RM9), which are arranged in an atypical right-handed helix [[PMID:33958484]]. This entry represents repeat RM2.

Name: Mediator of RNA polymerase II transcription subunit 14, RM8
Description: This entry represents an alpha/beta motif that is found tandemly repeated in the homologues of human Mediator of RNA polymerase II transcription subunit 14 (Med14). Med14 is a component of the Mediator complex, a coactivator involved in the regulated transcription of nearly all RNA polymerase II-dependent genes. Med14 along with Med17 are scaffold subunits in nucleating Mediator [[PMID:31402173],[PMID:33958484]]. The major portion of Med14 exists in the Head-Shoulder module of the Mediator and consists of nine tandem repeats (RM1 to RM9), which are arranged in an atypical right-handed helix [[PMID:33958484]]. This entry represents repeat RM8.

Name: Mediator of RNA polymerase II transcription subunit 14, RM6
Description: This entry represents an alpha/beta motif that is found tandemly repeated in the homologues of human Mediator of RNA polymerase II transcription subunit 14 (Med14). Med14 is a component of the Mediator complex, a coactivator involved in the regulated transcription of nearly all RNA polymerase II-dependent genes. Med14 along with Med17 are scaffold subunits in nucleating Mediator [[PMID:31402173],[PMID:33958484]]. The major portion of Med14 exists in the Head-Shoulder module of the Mediator and consists of nine tandem repeats (RM1 to RM9), which are arranged in an atypical right-handed helix [[PMID:33958484]]. This entry represents repeat RM6.

Name: LTN1 family HEAT repeat region
Description: This entry represents a series of HEAT repeats from the LTN1 family of proteins.  The LTN1 family consists of E3 ubiquitin-protein ligases that are integral components of the ribosome quality control complex (RQC). The RQC is a ribosome-associated complex responsible for the ubiquitination and subsequent extraction of incompletely synthesized nascent polypeptides for proteasomal degradation. Members of the LTN1 family are recruited to stalled 60S ribosomal subunits, often by NEMF, to mediate the ubiquitination of stalled nascent chains. This ubiquitination event is crucial for the recruitment of VCP/p97 or CDC48, which facilitates the extraction and degradation of the incomplete translation products. In some species, LTN1 proteins also ubiquitinate proteins derived from mRNAs lacking stop codons or translation arrest products induced by specific sequences. Additionally, they may play a role in chromatin function and transcription regulation indirectly.

Name: Fibronectin type I domain
Description: This entry represents a domain that structurally resembles the fibronectin type 1 repeat.
