Name: Transcription elongation factor SPT5, KOWx domain
Description: This domain is found in human Transcription elongation factor SPT5 and related proteins from eukaryotes. SPT5 is a component of the elongation factor DRB-sensitivity inducing factor (DSIF) that plays multiple roles during transcription. SPT5 is a multidomain protein consisting of an N-terminal NusG domain, a tandem of KOW domains and and a mobile C-terminal-repeat region. This entry represents the KOWx domain that is located between domains KOW3 and KOW4. The KOWx domain along with KOW4 and KOW5 forms an RNA clamp [[PMID:28892040]]. The tandem KOWx-4 contacts the wall, stalk, and dock domains of Pol II [[PMID:28892040]]. This only includes Spt5 proteins from eukaryotes.

Name: Transcription elongation factor SPT5 homolog 1/2, KOW6 domain
Description: This domain is found in Transcription elongation factor SPT5 homolog 1 (SPT51) from Arabidopsis thaliana and related plant proteins which are homologues of SPT5 from animals. SPT5 is a component of the elongation factor DRB-sensitivity inducing factor (DSIF) that plays multiple roles during transcription. SPT5 is a multidomain protein consisting of an N-terminal NusG domain, a tandem of KOW domains and and a mobile C-terminal-repeat region. This entry represents the KOW6 domain that is located between domains KOW5 and KOW7. It folds into a beta-barrel with an SH3-like topology.

Name: Transcription elongation factor SPT5, KOW1 domain
Description: This domain is found in human Transcription elongation factor SPT5 and related proteins from eukaryotes. SPT5 is a component of the elongation factor DRB-sensitivity inducing factor (DSIF) that plays multiple roles during transcription [[PMID:26217010],[PMID:28892040],[PMID:28775211],[PMID:30733384],[PMID:33846633]]. SPT5 is a multidomain protein consisting of an N-terminal NusG domain, a tandem of KOW domains and and a mobile C-terminal-repeat region. This entry represents the KOW1 domain that is located between the N-terminal NusG domain and KOW2. In the RNA polymerase II (Pol II)-DSIF elongation complex, KOW1 and NGN-SPT4 domains together form a 'DNA clamp' [[PMID:28892040]]. In the Pol II-DSIF-SPT6-PAF1c-TFIIS-nucleosome complex, the rewrapped DNA displaces SPT4 and the SPT5 NGN and KOW1 domains of DSIF from their previously observed binding positions on Pol II [[PMID:35709268]].

Name: Proteasome activator complex subunit 4-like, HEAT repeat-like
Description: This domain is found in Proteasome activator complex subunit 4 (PSME4) and in similar sequences from animals and fungi. PSME4 is an associated component of the proteasome that specifically recognises acetylated histones and promotes ATP- and ubiquitin -independent degradation of core histones during spermatogenesis and DNA damage response. This protein adopts a dome-like structure composed of HEAT repeat-like modules interconected through three long loops. This entry represents a region of HEAT repeats that contains a long loop [[PMID:32134919]].

Name: Nucleolar protein 10-like, N-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found at the N-terminal end of human Nucleolar protein 10 (NOL10) and similar eukaryotic sequences. This domain is normally found N-terminal to [pfam:PF23097]. This entry includes a entire beta-propeller structure and two alpha -helices.

Name: Small subunit processome component 20 homolog, C-terminal
Description: This domain is found at the C-terminal end of human Small subunit processome component 20 homolog (UTP20), which is involved in the 18S rRNA processing. UTP20 is a huge alpha -solenoid that functions as a scaffold. This domain is often found C-terminal to [pfam:PF20416].

Name: ITFG1, C2 domain
Description: This entry represents the C2 domain present in integrin alpha FG-GAP repeat-containing 1 (ITFG1) proteins. ITFG1 promotes the adhesion between neighbouring cells through its extracellular domain and regulates microtubule dynamics through its intracellular domain [[PMID:25437307]].

Name: WDR72, alpha-solenoid domain
Description: This entry represents an alpha-solenoid domain found in WD repeat-containing protein 72 (WDR72), a protein that plays a major role in formation of tooth enamel [[PMID:19853237],[PMID:25008349]]. It is specifically required during the maturation phase of amelogenesis for normal formation of the enamel matrix and clearance of enamel proteins. These proteins have a particular domain architecture comprising one or two beta-propellers followed by an alpha-solenoid, a highly conserved architecture among proteins that form membrane coat complexes, dating back to the early eukaryotic endomembrane system [[PMID:25008349]]. This entry also includes WDR7, whose function is currently unknown.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7048)
Description: This entry represents a domain found in uncharacterised proteins mainly from Trypanosoma sp. According to structural predictions, this domain is likely to adopt a beta-propeller fold.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7051)
Description: This is a domain of unknown function mostly found in organisms within the Trypanosoma genus. This domain adopt a multiple helical bundle fold.

Name: P5A-ATPase, transmembrane helical hairpin
Description: This domain is found in the homologues of endoplasmic reticulum P5A-ATPase (Spf1 in yeast; ATP13A1 in humans). The P5A-ATPase acts as a dislocase that mediates the ATP-dependent extraction of mislocalized mitochondrial transmembrane proteins from the endoplasmic reticulum membrane [[PMID:32973005]]. It interacts directly with the TM of the mitochondrial tail-anchored proteins. The domain represented by this entry consists of two transmembrane helices that form a helical hairpin. This domain seems to be loosely associated with the core transmembrane domain [[PMID:32973005]].

Name: Periplakin spectrin-like repeat
Description: This spectrin-like repeat is found N-terminal in human Periplakin and related proteins including Envoplakin-like protein. Periplakin is a component of the cornified envelope of keratinocytes. It coimmunoprecipitates with Envoplakin and both proteins form a network radiating from desmosomes. These two proteins probably provide a scaffolding onto which the cornified envelope is assembled [[PMID:9412476]]. Envoplakin and periplakin are the two proteins that are most commonly targeted by pathogenic autoantibodies.

Name: Alpha-glucan water dikinase 1 Ig-like N-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found N-terminal in the Alpha-glucan water dikinase 1 (CWD1) from Arabidopsis thaliana and related plant proteins including Alpha-amylase 3 (AMY3). CWD1 is involved in the incorporation of phosphate into starch-like alpha-glucan, with preferred C-6 position of glucose units. It acts as an overall regulator of starch mobilization and it is required for starch degradation [[PMID:11487701]]. AMY3 is involved in the determination of the final structure of glucans by shortening long linear phospho-oligosaccharides in the chloroplast stroma. Can act on both soluble and insoluble glucan substrates to release small linear and branched malto-oligosaccharides [[PMID:24089528]]. The domain represented by this entry is predicted to adopt a globular structure with an Ig-like topology. This domain is distantly related to CBM98 carbohydrate-binding family.

Name: CFAP43, WD40 domain
Description: This entry represents a WD40 domain found in CFAP43 protein from Trypanosoma brucei brucei and related sequences from Kinetoplastids. This protein is located between the doublet microtubules 5 and 6 and associated with the axoneme along the paraflagellar rod [[PMID:29449551]].

Name: CFAP43 N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents a beta-propeller domain found at N-terminal region of CFAP43 from Trypanosoma sp, and similar sequences from lower eukaryotes, according to structural prediction. These proteins are essential for flagellar function and, consequently, cell proliferation in T. brucei [[PMID:29449551]].

Name: ZNF598 PAH domain
Description: This PAH domain is found in human E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase ZNF598 and related eukaryotic proteins. ZNF598 plays a key role in the ribosome quality control (RQC), a pathway that takes place when the ribosome has stalled during translation, leading to degradation of nascent peptide chains. ZNF598 is activated when ribosomes are stalled following translation of prematurely polyadenylated mRNAs [[PMID:28065601]]. Independently of its role in RQC, this protein may also act as a negative regulator of interferon-stimulated gene expression [[PMID:29719242]].

Name: MROH2B-like, HEAT repeats
Description: This entry represents HEAT repeats from Maestro heat-like repeat -containing protein family member 2B from mouse (MROH2B), Protein SHOOT GRAVITROPISM 6 from Arabidopsis thaliana (SGR6) and similar sequences mainly found in animals and plants. SPIF (also known as MRO2B) may play a role in the process of sperm capacitation [[PMID:27105888]]. SGR6 is involved in inflorescence stems gravitropism, by modulating vacuolar membrane (VMs) dynamics in gravity-sensing cells (e.g. endodermal cells) during the amyloplast sedimentation process [[PMID:24486761]].

Name: Leucine-rich repeat and WD repeat-containing protein 1, LRR
Description: This entry represents the Leucine-rich repeat domain found at the N-terminal of Leucine-rich repeat and WD repeat-containing protein 1 (LRWD1) from animals and related sequences. LRWD1 (also known as CENP-33 and ORCA) is required for for G1/S transition. It recruits and stabilizes the origin recognition complex (ORC) onto chromatin during G1 to establish pre-replication complex (preRC) and to heterochromatic sites in pre-replicated cells [[PMID:20932478],[PMID:22427655],[PMID:22645314]]. This proteins consist of a LRR domain at the N-terminal and a WD (beta-propeller) domain at the C-terminal.

Name: Mitochondrial protein cyt-4 SH3 domain
Description: This entry represents a SH3 domain found in mitochondrial protein CYT4 from Neurospora crassa and related fungal sequences. CYT4 is required for RNA 5'- and 3'-end processing and splicing.

Name: Leucine-rich repeat and WD repeat-containing protein 1, WD domain
Description: This entry represents the WD (beta-propeller) domain found at the C-terminal of Leucine-rich repeat and WD repeat-containing protein 1 (LRWD1) from animals and related sequences. LRWD1 (also known as CENP-33 and ORCA) is required for for G1/S transition. It recruits and stabilizes the origin recognition complex (ORC) onto chromatin during G1 to establish pre-replication complex (preRC) and to heterochromatic sites in pre-replicated cells [[PMID:20932478],[PMID:22427655],[PMID:22645314]]. This proteins consist of a LRR domain at the N-terminal and a WD domain at the C-terminal. This domain is required for chromatin association of LRWD1 and for its binding to ORC [[PMID:22427655]].

Name: Mitochondrial protein cyt-4, winged helix
Description: This entry represents a winged helix domain found in mitochondrial protein CYT4 from Neurospora crassa and related fungal sequences. CYT4 is required for RNA 5'- and 3'-end processing and splicing.

Name: Pre-mRNA-splicing factor SYF1 middle HAT repeat
Description: Syf1 is a component of the spliceosome and is involved in pre-RNA splicing. It has HAT (Half A TPR) repeats which have a repetitive pattern characterised by three aromatic residues with a conserved spacing. They are structurally and sequentially similar to TPRs [[PMID:27459055],[PMID:28076345],[PMID:28076346],[PMID:28919079],[PMID:29146871]]. This entry represents HAT repeats from the middle region of Syf1.

Name: MROH2B-like, HEAT repeats
Description: This entry represents HEAT repeats from maestro heat-like repeat-containing proteins, including Maestro heat-like repeat -containing protein family member 2B from mouse (MROH2B, also known as SPIF), which may play a role in the process of sperm capacitation [[PMID:27105888]].

Name: MROH2B-like, HEAT repeats
Description: This entry represents HEAT repeats from maestro heat-like repeat-containing proteins, including Maestro heat-like repeat -containing protein family member 2B from mouse (MROH2B, also known as SPIF), which may play a role in the process of sperm capacitation [[PMID:27105888]]. This region is found at the C-terminal end of MROH2B.

Name: Pre-mRNA-splicing factor Syf1/CRNKL1 C-terminal HAT repeat
Description: This entry includes a group of pre-mRNA-processing factors that are components of the spliceosome, involved in pre-RNA splicing, including Syf1 from human and its homologues from fungi, crooked neck from Drosophila and its homologues from human and fungi Crooked neck-like protein 1 (CRNKL1/SYF-3) and CFL1, respectively. These proteins contain HAT (Half A TPR) repeats which have a repetitive pattern characterised by three aromatic residues with a conserved spacing. They are structurally and sequentially similar to TPRs [[PMID:28076346],[PMID:27459055],[PMID:35705093]]. The HAT repeats in these proteins can be divided in three segments, N-terminal, central and C-terminal. This entry represents the C-terminal HAT repeats of these proteins. The C-terminal HAT repeats make contacts with other proteins in the complex, to stabilise it.

Name: Pre-mRNA-splicing factor Syf1/CNRKL1 N-terminal HAT repeat
Description: This entry includes a group of pre-mRNA-processing factors that are components of the spliceosome, involved in pre-RNA splicing, including Syf1 from human and its homologues from fungi, crooked neck from Drosophila and its homologues from human and fungi Crooked neck-like protein 1 (CRNKL1/SYF-3) and CFL1, respectively. These proteins contain HAT (Half A TPR) repeats which have a repetitive pattern characterised by three aromatic residues with a conserved spacing. They are structurally and sequentially similar to TPRs [[PMID:28076346],[PMID:27459055],[PMID:35705093]]. The HAT repeats in these proteins can be divided in three segments, N-terminal, central and C-terminal. This entry represents the N-terminal HAT repeats of these proteins. The N-terminal HAT repeats of Syf1 and Syf3 contact the U2 core domain via the U2' protein [[PMID:28076346]].

Name: PRP39 N-terminal HAT repeat
Description: This entry includes HAT (Half A TPR) repeat proteins including Pre-mRNA-processing factor 39, SART3, PRP42 and similar proteins. PRP39 is a component of the spliceosome that functions prior to stable branch point recognition by the U1 snRNP particle to facilitate or stabilize the U1 snRNP/5'-splice site interaction and has a direct role in spliceosome assembly. PRP42 from yeast is an essential component of the U1 snRNP particle, which recognises and binds the 5'-splice site of pre-mRNA. This entry represents the N-terminal segment of the HAT repeats.

Name: PRP39 C-terminal HAT repeat
Description: This entry includes HAT (Half A TPR) repeat proteins including Pre-mRNA-processing factor 39, SART3, PRP42 and similar proteins. PRP39 is a component of the spliceosome that functions prior to stable branch point recognition by the U1 snRNP particle to facilitate or stabilize the U1 snRNP/5'-splice site interaction and has a direct role in spliceosome assembly. PRP42 from yeast is an essential component of the U1 snRNP particle, which recognises and binds the 5'-splice site of pre-mRNA. This entry represents the C-terminal segment of the HAT repeats, which are structurally similar to TPRs.

Name: HEATR1-like, HEAT repeats
Description: This entry represents HEAT repeats found in human HEAT repeat -containing protein 1 (HEATR1) and similar eukaryotic proteins. HEATR1 is a ribosome biogenesis factor involved in nucleolar processing of pre-18S ribosomal RNA.

Name: Plant disease resistance protein RPS2-like, leucine-rich repeats
Description: This entry represents a region of leucine-rich repeats found in Disease resistance protein RPS2 from Arabidopsis thaliana and similar proteins predominantly from plants. RPS2 specifically recognises the AvrRpt2 type III effector avirulence protein from Pseudomonas syringae [[PMID:12581527],[PMID:8091210]].

Name: VPS11-like, N-terminal beta-propeller
Description: This is the N-terminal beta-propeller domain of human Vacuolar protein sorting-associated protein 11 homolog (VPS11), which plays a role in vesicle-mediated protein trafficking to lysosomal compartments including the endocytic membrane transport and autophagic pathways.

Name: Stalled ribosome sensor GCN1-like, HEAT repeats
Description: This HEAT repeats are found several times found in human Stalled ribosome sensor GCN1 and similar eukaryotic proteins. GCN1 is a ribosome collision sensor that plays a key role in the RNF14 -RNF25 translation quality control pathway, which occurs when a ribosome has stalled during translation, promoting ubiquitination and degradation of translation factors on stalled ribosomes [[PMID:32610081],[PMID:36638793]]. The structure of this protein is predicted to be entirely formed by HEAT repeats.

Name: TPR repeat domain
Description: These predicted tetratrico peptide repeats (TPR) are found in uncharacterised proteins from actinomycetes. This entry is found C-terminal to [pfam:PF22905] in some sequences.

Name: Pentatricopeptide repeat-containing protein
Description: The Pentatricopeptide Repeat (PPR) domain is involved in various RNA-related processes, primarily within organelles such as mitochondria and chloroplasts. Proteins containing this domain play crucial roles in RNA stabilisation, processing, splicing, editing, and translation. They are essential for the biogenesis of ribosomes and the translation machinery, influencing the expression of genes encoded by organelle genomes. These proteins also regulate photosynthesis-related processes and mitochondrial functions, including tRNA homeostasis and the activity of respiratory complexes. Some PPR-containing proteins participate in retrograde signalling from plastids to the nucleus, thereby affecting nuclear gene expression. The domain is characterised by its RNA-binding capacity, often recognising specific RNA sequences or structures, and can act as a barrier to RNA degradation or a facilitator of RNA processing events.

Name: Fungal tetratrico peptide repeats
Description: These predicted tetratrico peptide repeats (TPR) are found in a group of fungal proteins. This domain is often found associated with [pfam:PF23276].

Name: Fungal tetratrico peptide repeats
Description: These predicted tetratrico peptide repeats (TPR) are found in E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase listerin from Neurospora crassa (Rkr-1) and similar fungal proteins. Rkr-1 component of the ribosome quality control complex (RQC), a ribosome-associated complex that mediates ubiquitination and extraction of incompletely synthesized nascent chains for proteasomal degradation [[PMID:27385828]].

Name: Transcription elongation factor SPT5, second KOW domain
Description: This entry includes Transcription elongation factor SPT5, a component of the elongation factor DRB-sensitivity inducing factor (DSIF) that plays multiple roles during transcription [[PMID:26217010],[PMID:28892040],[PMID:30733384],[PMID:33846633]]. SPT5 is a multidomain protein consisting of an N-terminal NusG domain ([pfam:PF03439]), tandem of KOW domains and and a mobile C-terminal-repeat region. This entry represents the second KOW domain.

Name: Disease resistance protein RPS4B-like, leucine-rich repeats
Description: This entry represents a region of leucine-rich repeat found in Disease resistance protein RPS4B from Arabidopsis thaliana (RPS4B) and similar proteins found in plants and some sp. RPS4B specifically recognizes the AvrRps4 type III effector avirulence protein from P.syringae.

Name: Transcription elongation factor SPT5, seventh KOW domain
Description: This entry includes Transcription elongation factor SPT5, a component of the elongation factor DRB-sensitivity inducing factor (DSIF) that plays multiple roles during transcription [[PMID:26217010],[PMID:28892040],[PMID:30733384],[PMID:33846633]]. SPT5 is a multidomain protein consisting of an N-terminal NusG domain ([pfam:PF03439]), tandem of KOW domains and and a mobile C-terminal-repeat region. The KOW domains differ in number between yeast, which has five, and animals, which have seven. This entry represents the seventh KOW domain of Spt5 from animals, which is located at the very C-terminal of the sequences [[PMID:28892040]]. The KOW domains of these proteins play critical roles in recruitment of multiple other eukaryotic transcription elongation and RNA biogenesis factors and they are also involved in the binding of the eukaryotic Spt5 proteins to RNA polymerases.

Name: Transcription elongation factor SPT5, sixth KOW domain
Description: This entry includes Transcription elongation factor SPT5, a component of the elongation factor DRB-sensitivity inducing factor (DSIF) that plays multiple roles during transcription [[PMID:28892040],[PMID:26217010],[PMID:30733384],[PMID:33846633]]. SPT5 is a multidomain protein consisting of an N-terminal NusG domain ([pfam:PF03439]), tandem of KOW domains and and a mobile C-terminal-repeat region. The KOW domains differ in number between yeast, which has five, and animals, which have seven. This entry represents the sixth KOW domain of SPT5 from animals [[PMID:28892040]]. The KOW domains of these proteins play critical roles in recruitment of multiple other eukaryotic transcription elongation and RNA biogenesis factors, and they are also involved in the binding of the eukaryotic SPT5 proteins to RNA polymerases [[PMID:28892040],[PMID:26217010],[PMID:30733384],[PMID:33846633]].

Name: Guanine nucleotide exchange factor DBS-like, spectrin-like
Description: This spectrin-like domain is found in human Guanine nucleotide exchange factor DBS (MCF2L) and similar sequences mainly found in animals. MCF2L catalyses guanine nucleotide exchange on RHOA and CDC42, and thereby contributes to the regulation of RHOA and CDC42 signalling pathways. The spectrin domain-repeat forms a three helix bundle.

Name: Transcription elongation factor SPT5, fifth KOW domain
Description: This entry includes Transcription elongation factor SPT5, a component of the elongation factor DRB-sensitivity inducing factor (DSIF) that plays multiple roles during transcription [[PMID:28892040],[PMID:26217010],[PMID:30733384],[PMID:33846633]]. SPT5 is a multidomain protein consisting of an N-terminal NusG domain ([pfam:PF03439]), tandem of KOW domains and and a mobile C-terminal-repeat region. The KOW domains differ in number between yeast, which has five, and animals, which have seven. This entry represents the fifth KOW domain of SPT5[[PMID:28892040]]. The KOW domains of these proteins play critical roles in recruitment of multiple other eukaryotic transcription elongation and RNA biogenesis factors, and they are also involved in the binding of the eukaryotic SPT5 proteins to RNA polymerases [[PMID:28892040],[PMID:26217010],[PMID:30733384],[PMID:33846633]].

Name: Transcription elongation factor SPT5, fourth KOW domain
Description: This entry includes Transcription elongation factor SPT5, a component of the elongation factor DRB-sensitivity inducing factor (DSIF) that plays multiple roles during transcription [[PMID:26217010],[PMID:28892040],[PMID:30733384],[PMID:33846633]]. SPT5 is a multidomain protein consisting of an N-terminal NusG domain ([pfam:PF03439]), tandem of KOW domains and and a mobile C-terminal-repeat region. The KOW domains differ in number between yeast, which has five, and animals, which have seven. This entry represents the fourth KOW domain of SPT5 [[PMID:26217010]]. The KOW domains of these proteins play critical roles in recruitment of multiple other eukaryotic transcription elongation and RNA biogenesis factors, and they are also involved in the binding of the eukaryotic SPT5 proteins to RNA polymerases [[PMID:26217010],[PMID:28892040],[PMID:30733384],[PMID:33846633]].

Name: Armadillo-like repeats
Description: This entry represents a region of armadillo-like repeats found in human WD repeat and FYVE domain-containing protein 3 (WDFY3) and similar eukaryotic sequences. WDFY3 is required for selective macroautophagy (aggrephagy). It is important for normal brain development.

Name: Nucleoporin Nup120, HEAT repeats
Description: These HEAT repeats form an elongated alpha-helical stack at the C-terminal of Nucleoporin nup120 from Schizosaccharomyces pombe and similar eukaryotic sequences. Nup120 functions as a component of the nuclear pore complex (NPC).

Name: Plant tetratrico peptide repeats
Description: This entry represents the predicted tetratrico peptide repeats (TPR) found in F-box protein At2g35280 from Arabidopsis thaliana and similar plant proteins. This entry is found associated with [pfam:PF01753] in some members of this family.

Name: GBF1-like, tetratrico peptide repeat region
Description: These predicted tetratrico peptide repeats (TPR) are found at the C-terminal end of human name Golgi-specific brefeldin A-resistance guanine nucleotide exchange factor 1 (GBF1) and similar eukaryotic proteins. GBF1 is involved in trafficking in the early secretory pathway and required for the assembly of the Golgi apparatus. This entry is often found associated with [pfam:PF12783] and [pfam:PF01369].

Name: IFT80 second beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the second beta-propeller of the Intraflagellar transport protein 80 homolog (IFT80) from human and related proteins. IFT80 is a component of the intraflagellar transport (IFT) complex B, which is essential for the development and maintenance of motile and sensory cilia. It is required for initiation of the ciliary axoneme [[PMID:29658880]]. It has an unique architecture consisting of two beta-propellers followed by an alpha-solenoid structure [[PMID:29658880]]. This beta-propeller is involved in dimerization together with the alpha-solenoid[[PMID:29658880]].

Name: PEP5/VPS11 N-terminal
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller domain found at the N-terminal of the Pep5-like zinc finger protein from Schizosaccharomyces pombe and its orthologues from human, mouse and A. thaliana Vacuolar protein sorting-associated protein 11. Vps11 aggregates with other Vps proteins to form the homotypic fusion and protein sorting complex (HOPS), which promotes the fusion of late endosomes and autophagosomes with lysosomes [[PMID:33452836]]. Pep5 (also known as Vps11) was initially isolated as a protease deficient mutant and shown to be involved in protein trafficking and vacuole biogenesis pathways [[PMID:22570702]].

Name: WDR90, 4th beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the fourth beta-propeller found in WD repeat-containing protein 90 from mouse and human, and the orthologue POC16 from Chlamydomonas reinhardtii. WDR90 proteins constitute an evolutionary conserved central core microtubule triplet component that is essential for maintaining the inner centriolar scaffold components in centrioles [[PMID:32946374]]. POC16 is important for flagellum assembly as two poc16 mutants display shorter flagella and cannot swim [[PMID:28781053]].

Name: Nucleoporin NUP160, helical domain
Description: This entry represents the central helical domain of NUP160, which is found in animals, including humans, mice, and Drosophila melanogaster. NUP160 is one of up to 60 proteins that constitute the Nuclear Pore Complex (NPC) as a component of the NUP107-160 subcomplex [[PMID:11684705]]. NUP160, in conjunction with NUP133, interacts with NUP98 and NUP153, mediating mRNA export [[PMID:11684705]]. NUP160 mutations have been associated with Steroid-Resistant Nephrotic Syndrome (SRNS). Silencing Drosophila NUP160 specifically in nephrocytes results in functional abnormalities, reduced cell size and nuclear volume, as well as a disorganised nuclear membrane structure [[PMID:30910934]].

Name: Nup160 C-terminal TPR
Description: This entry represents the tetratricopeptide-like repeat found in the C-terminal region of nucleoporin Nup160 from human, mouse, Drosophila and Arabidopsis thaliana. NUP160 depletion could restore autophagy and inhibit fibrosis and inflammatory response in high glucose-treated [[PMID:34533106]]. The Arabidopsis Nup160, a scaffold Nup, is important for regulating the floral transition, a key developmental phase change in flowering plants, through modulating the subcellular localization of HOS1 at NPCs [[PMID:33367234]].

Name: RDM3 beta-solenoid
Description: This entry represents a beta-solenoid domain found at C-terminal region of Protein RNA-directed DNA methylation 3 (RDM3) from Arabidopsis thaliana and similar proteins from plants. RNA-directed DNA methylation (RdDM) initiates and re-establishes silencing of transposable elements through de novo methylation of cytosines [[PMID:31274236]].

Name: NUP160/120 middle TPR
Description: This entry represents a segment of TPRs found in the middle region of nucleoporin Nup160 from human, mouse, Drosophila and Arabidopsis thaliana, as well as in the homologues from yeast, Nucleoporin 120 (Nup120). NUP160 depletion could restore autophagy and inhibit fibrosis and inflammatory response in high glucose-treated [[PMID:34533106]]. The Arabidopsis Nup160, a scaffold Nup, is important for regulating the floral transition, a key developmental phase change in flowering plants, through modulating the subcellular localization of HOS1 at NPCs [[PMID:33367234]]. Nup120 is part of a Nuclear Pore Complex (NPC) as a component of the ctNup84 complex [[PMID:21784248]].

Name: PEP5/VPS11 Clathrin repeat
Description: This entry represents the clathrin repeat found in Pep5-like zinc finger protein from Schizosaccharomyces pombe (also known as Vps11) and its orthologues from human, mouse and A. thaliana Vacuolar protein sorting-associated protein 11 (VPS11). Vps11 aggregates with other Vps proteins to form the homotypic fusion and protein sorting complex (HOPS), which promotes the fusion of late endosomes and autophagosomes with lysosomes [[PMID:33452836]]. Pep5 (also known as Vps11) was initially isolated as a protease deficient mutant, and has function in protein trafficking and vacuole biogenesis pathways [[PMID:22570702]].

Name: HVO_0234-like, beta propeller
Description: This family represents a group of proteins mainly from halobacteria, including HVO_0234 from Haloferax volcanii ([swissprot:D4GZH9]). Members are predicted to adopt a beta -propeller configuration.

Name: IFT122 second beta-propeller
Description: IFT122 is one of the core components (together with WDR19/IFT144 and IFT140) of the IFT-A complex which is required for retrograde ciliary transport and entry into cilia of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs), being involved in cilia function and/or assembly [[PMID:36462505],[PMID:29220510]]. IFT-A polymerises interacts with IFT-B, and uses an array of beta-propeller and TPR domains to create 'carriages' of the IFT train that engage TULP adaptor proteins [[PMID:29220510]]. This entry represents the second beta-propeller of IFT122, which is a crucial subunit for IFT-A integrity [[PMID:36462505]].

Name: IFT122 first beta-propeller
Description: IFT122 is one of the core components (together with WDR19/IFT144 and IFT140) of the IFT-A complex which is required for retrograde ciliary transport and entry into cilia of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs), being involved in cilia function and/or assembly [[PMID:36462505],[PMID:29220510]]. IFT-A polymerises interacts with IFT-B, and uses an array of beta-propeller and TPR domains to create 'carriages' of the IFT train that engage TULP adaptor proteins [[PMID:29220510]]. This entry represents the first beta-propeller of IFT122, which is a crucial subunit for IFT-A integrity [[PMID:36462505]].

Name: IFT140 first beta-propeller
Description: IFT140 is one of the core components (together with WDR19/IFT144 and IFT122) of the IFT-A complex which is required for retrograde ciliary transport and entry into cilia of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs), being involved in cilia function and/or assembly [[PMID:36462505],[PMID:20889716]]. IFT-A polymerises interacts with IFT-B, and uses an array of beta-propeller and TPR domains to create 'carriages' of the IFT train that engage TULP adaptor proteins [[PMID:20889716]]. The beta-propeller domains of IFT140 contact the beta-propeller domains of IFT121 from the adjacent complex [[PMID:36462505]]. This entry represents the first beta-propeller of IFT140.

Name: IFT140 second beta-propeller
Description: IFT140 is one of the core components (together with WDR19/IFT144 and IFT122) of the IFT-A complex which is required for retrograde ciliary transport and entry into cilia of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs), being involved in cilia function and/or assembly [[PMID:20889716],[PMID:36462505]]. IFT-A polymerises interacts with IFT-B, and uses an array of beta-propeller and TPR domains to create 'carriages' of the IFT train that engage TULP adaptor proteins [[PMID:36462505]]. The beta-propeller domains of IFT140 contact the beta-propeller domains of IFT121 from the adjacent complex [[PMID:20889716]]. This entry represents the second beta-propeller of IFT140.

Name: IFT80/172/WDR35/WDR19 TPR domain
Description: This entry describes the tetratricopeptide-like repeat (TPR) domain found in proteins comprising the intraflagellar transport (IFT) complexes B2 and A, which are found in nematodes and mammals, including IFT80, IF172, WDR35, WDR19, and DYF2. The IFT complex is required for retrograde ciliary transport and entry of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs) into cilia, and it is involved in ciliogenesis [[PMID:29658880],[PMID:28479320],[PMID:36593313],[PMID:33175651]].

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7099)
Description: This is a beta-propeller domain found in a group on uncharacterised proteins from fungi. The function of this domain is unknown.

Name: WDR19 first beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the first beta-propeller of WRD19 (also known as IFT144) from animals and the orthologue from C.elegans DYF2. WDR19 is a core component of the IFT complex A (IFT-A, together with IFT140 and IFT122), a complex required for retrograde ciliary transport and entry into cilia of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs), being involved in cilia function and/or assembly [[PMID:20889716],[PMID:36462505]]. IFT-A polymerises interacts with IFT-B, and uses an array of beta-propeller and TPR domains to create 'carriages' of the IFT train that engage TULP adaptor proteins [[PMID:36462505]].

Name: WDR35 second beta-propeller
Description: WDR35 (also known as IFT121) is a component of the IFT-A complex, which is required for retrograde ciliary transport and entry into cilia of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs), being involved in cilia function and/or assembly [[PMID:36462505],[PMID:29220510]]. IFT-A polymerises interacts with IFT-B, and uses an array of beta-propeller and TPR domains to create 'carriages' of the IFT train that engage TULP adaptor proteins [[PMID:29220510]]. Proteins in this entry are part of the non-core subcomplex, together with IFT43 and IFT139 [[PMID:36462505],[PMID:29220510]]. This entry represents the second beta-propeller of WDR35. Knock-out WDR35 cells showed defective retrograde trafficking within cilia [[PMID:29220510]].

Name: WDR90/POC16, second beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the second WD40 beta-propeller found in WD repeat-containing protein 90, identified in animals, and the homologue protein POC16 found in Chlamydomonas reinhardtii. WDR90 is proposed to be localised in the centriole and required for ciliogenesis [[PMID:28781053]]. The similarities between WDR90 and the flagellar inner B-microtubule protein FAP52/WDR16 lead to the proposal that the WD40 domains of WDR90 might be located within the B-microtubule of the centriole triplet [[PMID:32946374]].

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7104)
Description: This entry includes uncharacterised fungal sequences which consist of repeats that may adopt an alpha-solenoid structure related to ankirin repeats. The overall arrangement of these repeats may have a horseshoe conformation similar to that in leucine-rich repeats.

Name: LTI65/LTI78 PGEED repeat
Description: This entry represents a disordered region found in LTI78/LTI65 from Arabidopsis thaliana and related plant proteins. LTI78/LTI65 are involved in responses to abiotic stresses [[PMID:19470100],[PMID:21374086],[PMID:9536054]]. This region consists of repeats containing the conserved PGEED sequence and is located C-terminal of the CAP160 repeat ([pfam:PF07918]).

Name: LTI65/LTI78 NYQTKV repeat
Description: This entry represents a disordered region found in LTI78 and LTI65 from Arabidopsis and related plant proteins. LTI78 and LTI65 are involved in responses to abiotic stress [[PMID:8437577],[PMID:21374086],[PMID:9536054]]. This region consists of repeats containing the conserved sequence NYQTKV. This region is found N-terminal of the CAP160 repeat ([pfam:PF07918]).

Name: FAZ1, C-terminal
Description: The entry represents a repeats-containing region found at the C-terminal of FAZ1 from Trypanosoma brucei brucei and related proteins from typanosomatids. FAZ1 is a component of FAZ filament that is required for correct FAZ assembly and attachment [[PMID:17945531]]. These repeats consist of 36x14aa repeats in T.brucei and T.cruzi while in the Leishmania major LmjF34.0690 orthologue there are 70x14aa repeats [[PMID:17945531]].

Name: APC4, WD40 domain C-terminal half
Description: Apc4 is a component of the anaphase promoting complex/cyclosome (APC/C), a cell cycle-regulated E3 ubiquitin ligase that controls progression through mitosis and the G1 phase of the cell cycle. This complex acts by ubiquitination and degradation of target proteins. Apc4 consists of a beta-propeller that is split by      a long alpha-helical domain ([pfam:PF12816]) which generate an L-shaped molecule [[PMID:26343760]]. APC4 interacts with APC5; the N-terminal of APC1  serves to stabilise the union between APC4 and APC5, both of which lie towards the bottom-front of the APC [[PMID:12049731],[PMID:16896351],[PMID:19584054],[PMID:26343760]]. This entry represents the C-terminal half of the beta-propeller of ACP4 from animals [[PMID:26343760]].

Name: Apc4 WD40 domain C-terminal half, fungal
Description: Apc4 is a component of the anaphase promoting complex/cyclosome (APC/C), a cell cycle-regulated E3 ubiquitin ligase that controls progression through mitosis and the G1 phase of the cell cycle. This complex acts by ubiquitination and degradation of target proteins. Apc4 consists of a beta-propeller that is split by a long alpha-helical domain ([pfam:PF12816]) which generate an L-shaped molecule [[PMID:26343760]]. APC4 interacts with APC5; the N-terminal of APC1  serves to stabilise the union between APC4 and APC5, both of which lie towards the bottom-front of the APC [[PMID:19584054],[PMID:26343760]]. This entry represents the C-terminal half of the beta-propeller of Apc4 from S.pombe and related fungal sequences, that are not recognised in [pfam:PF23405].

Name: Echinoderm microtubule-associated protein first beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents beta-propeller domains of echinoderm microtubule-associated protein (EMAP)-like (EML) proteins [[PMID:24706829]], a family of proteins that contribute to formation of the mitotic spindle and interphase microtubule network [[PMID:24706829]]. In these proteins, the beta-propellers are intimately associated as a pair which was defined as tandem atypical propeller in EMLs (TAPE) domain. There is a hydrophobic core between the propellers which corresponds the HELP motif ([pfam:PF03451]) that contributes blades 1 and 14 buried at the interface and joints them [[PMID:24706829]]. This model completes the first beta-propeller of the TAPE pair in EMLs. In some members, such as EML5 and EML6, there are three TAPE pairs.

Name: Vps8 beta-propeller
Description: Vps8 is one of the Golgi complex components necessary for vacuolar sorting. It is the core component of the endosomal tethering complex CORVET (class C core vacuole/endosome tethering). Vps8 co-operates with Vps21-GTP to mediate endosomal clustering in a reaction that is dependent on Vps3. Vps8 is the only CORVET subunit that is enriched on late endosomes, suggesting that it is a marker for the maturation of late endosomes [[PMID:25266290],[PMID:19828734]]. These proteins consist of a beta-propeller at the N-terminal followed by a long, central TPR domain ([pfam:PF12816]) and a RING zinc finger at the C-terminal; this entry represents the beta-propeller domain.

Name: Vps42 beta-propeller
Description: Vps41 is a subunit of the homotypic vacuole fusion and vacuole protein sorting (HOPS) complex. It is essential or membrane docking and fusion at the Golgi-to-endosome and endosome-to-vacuole stages of protein transport [[PMID:36098503],[PMID:25908847]]. It consists of a beta-propeller at the N-terminal, represented in this entry, followed by an alpha-solenoid (TPR-like) domain, a CHCR domain and a RING zinc finger [[PMID:36098503],[PMID:25908847]].

Name: Echinoderm microtubule-associated protein second beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents beta-propeller domains of echinoderm microtubule-associated protein (EMAP)-like (EML) proteins [[PMID:24706829]], a family of proteins that contribute to formation of the mitotic spindle and interphase microtubule network [[PMID:24706829]]. In these proteins, the beta-propellers are intimately associated as a pair which was defined as tandem atypical propeller in EMLs (TAPE) domain. There is a hydrophobic core between the propellers which corresponds the HELP motif ([pfam:PF03451]) that contributes blades 1 and 14 buried at the interface and joints them [[PMID:24706829]]. This model completes the second beta-propeller of the TAPE pair in EMLs. In some members, such as EML5 and EML6, there are three TAPE pairs. The first beta-propeller of  the pair is represented in [pfam:PF23409].

Name: E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase RFWD3 WD40 domain
Description: This entry represents the WD40-repeat (WDR) of the E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase RFWD3, a protein required for the repair of DNA interstrand cross-links (ICL) in response to DNA damage [[PMID:33321094]]. This domain is responsible for the functional interactions that allow RFWD3 to maintain genomic stability [[PMID:33321094]].

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7131)
Description: This entry is a domain of unknown function found in some PA-PLA1 family members, which consists of mitochondrial proteins that are probable phospholipases with a specific function to hydrolyze phosphatidic acid. Members of this family are involved in the metabolism of phospholipids, which are critical components of cell membranes. By hydrolyzing phosphatidic acid, these enzymes may play a role in the regulation of mitochondrial membrane composition and dynamics, as well as in signaling pathways related to lipid metabolism.

Name: Plastid division regulator alpha solenoid domain
Description: This entry represents the alpha helical solenoid domain of the Plastid Division Regulators family that is involved in the coordination and regulation of plastid division in plants. Members of this family function as key components of the plastid division machinery, which is responsible for the constriction and division of plastids, including chloroplasts and non-green plastids. They play roles in the placement of division sites, formation of FtsZ rings, and regulation of FtsZ assembly dynamics. The family includes proteins that act as antagonistic regulators, influencing the interaction between FtsZ proteins and other division components, such as ARC3 and ARC6. They are also involved in stromule biogenesis and may contribute to plant gravitropism.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7133)
Description: This domain is found in a number of uncharacterised bacterial proteins. It is predicted to form a a six-bladed beta-propeller with significant similarity to glucose/sorbosone dehydrogenases.

Name: NELF-A N-terminal domain
Description: The N-terminal domain of the NELF-A protein forms a novel helical fold, comprising five alpha-helices (residues 6-110). This domain shows structural similarity to the HIV integrase-binding domain present in the human PSIP1 protein. The N-terminal domain is highly conserved among metazoans and is crucial for NELF-A's interaction with the NELF-C subunit, forming a stable heterodimer interface. Two invariant tryptophan residues (W24 and W89) in this domain insert into hydrophobic pockets on NELF-C, mediating this interaction. NELF-A is a subunit of the Negative Elongation Factor (NELF) complex, which plays a key role in regulating transcription elongation by RNA Polymerase II. The NELF complex induces promoter-proximal pausing of Pol II, a rate-limiting step in transcription regulation that allows integration of additional signals before productive elongation.

Name: Dedicator of cytokinesis (DOCK) TPR region
Description: This entry corresponds to a TPR-like alpha solenoid region in DOCK family proteins.  The DOCK family proteins function as guanine nucleotide exchange factors (GEFs) that activate small GTPases by exchanging bound GDP for free GTP, thus converting them into their active GTP-bound form. These proteins are involved in various cellular processes, including cytoskeletal rearrangements, cell migration, phagocytosis of apoptotic cells, and regulation of adherens junctions between cells. Some family members may also play a role in disease processes such as Alzheimer's disease by interacting with presenilin proteins and affecting Tau phosphorylation. The DOCK family is characterized by the presence of a conserved DOCKER domain, which is necessary for their GEF activity and interaction with small GTPases like RAC1 and RAC2.

Name: Vps41 TPR-like region
Description: This entry represents the central TPR-like region of Vps41 proteins. The VPS41 family is involved in vesicle-mediated protein trafficking to lysosomal compartments, including endocytic membrane transport and autophagic pathways. Members of the VPS41 family are believed to act as core components of the putative HOPS and CORVET endosomal tethering complexes, which are essential for the conversion of early endosomes to late endosomes, implicating specific Rab GTPases in the process. They are involved in tethering and docking events during SNARE-mediated membrane fusion, and regulate endocytic, phagocytic, and autophagic traffic towards lysosomes. The family plays a role in the fusion of autophagosomes with lysosomes and may be involved in sorting to the regulated secretory pathway. Additionally, VPS41 proteins contribute to the normal development of certain cellular structures in some organisms and may have a role in the negative regulation of apoptosis. They are also required for the uptake of exogenous dsRNA in experimental RNA silencing.

Name: Leprecan family TPR-like repeat region
Description: This entry represents the alpha-helical N-terminal alpha helical domain of members of the leprecan family. Members of this family often have an associated C-terminal enzymatic domain.  The Leprecan family proteins are involved in the hydroxylation of proline residues in collagen, an essential post-translational modification for the stability and assembly of collagen fibrils. Members of this family form complexes with other enzymes to hydroxylate lysine residues in collagen alpha chains, which is crucial for the proper assembly and cross-linking of collagen fibrils in various tissues, including skin, bone, tendon, aorta, and cornea. Some family members also exhibit growth suppressive activity and may play a role in the secretory pathway of cells. The family's enzymatic activity is particularly important for the structural integrity of type I, IV, and V collagens, contributing to normal bone density and skin stability.

Name: Prolyl 4-hydroxylase peptide-substrate-binding domain
Description: This entry represents the prolyl 4-hydroxylase peptide substrate binding domain, which is formed by two and a half TPR-like repeats.  The P4HA family is responsible for the post-translational hydroxylation of proline residues in -Xaa-Pro-Gly- sequences, which is a critical step in the biosynthesis of collagens and other proteins. This modification by the P4HA enzymes enhances the stability of collagen by facilitating the formation of hydrogen bonds within the triple helical structure of collagen fibers. The hydroxylation of proline residues to 4-hydroxyproline is essential for maintaining the structural integrity of collagen and ensuring proper function of the extracellular matrix where these proteins are predominantly found.

Name: Transport and Golgi organization protein 6 homolog, ARM-like repeat
Description: This region is found in human Transport and Golgi organization protein 6 homolog (TANGO6) and similar proteins predominantly found in animals. This region, which is predicted to show ARM-like repeats, is often found N-terminal to [pfam:PF10363] and [pfam:PF10304].

Name: E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase LIN-like, ARM repeat
Description: This region is found in a group of plant proteins, including soybean E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase LIN. It is predicted to fold into ARM-like repeats.

Name: Tubulin-specific chaperone D-like, ARM repeat
Description: This region is found at the N-terminal end of human Tubulin -specific chaperone D (TBCD), which is required for tubulin complex assembly and involved in the regulation of microtubule polymerisation or depolymerisation. It is predicted to fold into ARM-like repeats.

Name: NWD2 C-terminal beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller found at the C-terminal of NACHT and WD repeat domain-containing protein 2 (NWD2) from human and similar eukaryotic proteins. The function of these proteins is not yet known.

Name: CCR4-NOT transcription complex subunit 1, NOT1_connector
Description: This entry represents a region of human CCR4-NOT transcription complex subunit 1 (CNOT1), an scaffolding component of the CCR4-NOT complex, which is one of the major cellular mRNA deadenylases. This region shows several repeats of the HEAT type [[PMID:24121232],[PMID:36586408]].

Name: Serine/threonine-protein kinase ATR-like, HEAT repeats
Description: This region of HEAT repeats is found in human Serine/threonine -protein kinase ATR and similar eukaryotic proteins. ATR acts as a DNA damage sensor and is associated with severe disorders such as Seckel syndrome 1. This protein is organised into a large N-terminal alpha solenoid containing HEAT repeats, a FAT (FRAP, ATM, TRRAP) domain, a kinase domain ([pfam:PF00454]), and a C-terminal short segment referred to as FATC ([pfam:PF02260]). The FAT domain contains TPR (tetratricopeptide) repeats ([pfam:PF02259]) followed by a HEAT repeat domain (this entry) [[PMID:29271416]].

Name: Leucine-rich repeat region
Description: This entry represents a region containing leucine-rich repeats found in a variety of plant resistance proteins.

Name: Telomere repeat-binding protein 1-like, ubiquitin-like domain
Description: This domain is found in Telomere repeat-binding protein 1 from Arabidopsis thaliana (TPR1) and similar plant proteins. TPR1 binds specifically to the plant telomeric double-stranded DNA sequences 5'-GGTTTAG-3'. This domain is predicted to adopt a ubiquitin-like fold.

Name: Serine/threonine-protein kinase ULK4-like, HEAT repeats
Description: This region of HEAT repeats is found in human Serine/threonine-protein kinase ULK4 and similar sequences mainly found in animals and plants. ULK4 may be involved in the remodeling of cytoskeletal components and in the regulation of neurite branching and elongation, as well as cell motility.

Name: EIPR1 beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller found in eukaryotic EARP and GARP complex-interacting protein 1 (EIPR1). EIPR1 is a component of the endosomal retrieval machinery, binding to both the Endosome- Associated Recycling Protein (EARP) and Golgi-Associated Retrograde Protein (GARP) tethering complexes. GARP and EARP participate in SNARE -dependent fusion of endosome-derived carriers with the trans-Golgi network and recycling endosomes, respectively [[PMID:27440922]]. EIPR1 is required for the stability of the EARP complex subunits and for the  localization of EARP and its association with membranes, but does not affect localization or function of the related GARP complex [[PMID:31721635]]. WD repeat-containing protein (DWA2) from Arabidopsis thaliana is also a member of this family. DWA2 is a component of an E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase complex that acts as negative regulator in abscisic acid signaling [[PMID:20525848]].

Name: At1g61320/AtMIF1, LRR domain
Description: This entry represents the leucine-rich repeats (LRR) found in plant proteins, such as At1g61320 and At1g61330  from Arabidopsis thaliana, F-box/LRR-repeat containing proteins involved in the ubiquitin-proteasome pathway. They function as components of SCF (SKP1-CUL1-F-box protein) E3 ubiquitin ligase complexes [[PMID:23166809]]. They have a F-box domain at the N-terminal ([pfam:PF00646]). AtMIF1 (also known as F-box protein AT3G62230) from Arabidopsis, physically interacts with MYB domain protein 5 (MYB5), which results in MYB5 degradation representing a new regulatory pathway, 26S proteasome-AtMIF1-MYB5 that regulates oil content of seeds, increasing their oil content without affecting plant growth [[PMID:33098089]].

Name: WDR26 Lis-homology domain
Description: This entry represents the LisH motif present towards the N-terminal of WD repeat-containing protein 26 homolog (WDR26) from Arabidopsis thaliana and other eukaryotic proteins. WDR26 is involved in cellular signaling control of high light acclimation, defense response to biotic and abiotic stress and implicated in the regulation of hormones [[PMID:26706055]]. This motif is often associated to CTLH and WD domains.

Name: Putative E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase LIN, ARM-like domain
Description: This entry represents a domain found in E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase LIN, a putative E3 ubiquitin ligase involved in the rhizobial infection process [[PMID:19776163]]. It plays an important role in the early steps of bacterial symbiont thread formation in roots, and in growth, differentiation and maintenance of nodules [[PMID:19776163],[PMID:15516512]]. This domain may adopt an ARM-repeat fold.

Name: F-box protein AT5G49610-like, beta-propeller
Description: This entry includes a group of plant proteins containing a F-box domain at the N-terminal, followed by a beta-propeller-like domain.  The F-box motif is a key component of the SCF (SKP1-cullin-F-box) complex, which functions as an E3 ligase [[PMID:23166809]]. Members of this family are likely to play a role in the ubiquitination pathway, where they are responsible for targeting specific substrates for proteasomal degradation. The F-box domain facilitates the recognition and binding of substrate proteins, while the FBD (F-box associated domain), may contribute to the specificity and functionality of the SCF complex.

Name: E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase LIN-like, ARM repeat
Description: This region of armadillo-like repeats is found in a group of plant proteins, including E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase LIN from soybean. It is often found C-terminal to [pfam:PF23568].

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7151)
Description: This entry represents a domain of unknown function found in bacterial proteins. Some of the members of this family might contain an EF-hand calcium binding site. The predicted structure of this domain consists of three beta sheets.

Name: SHMOOSE
Description: This protein family includes human Protein SHMOOSE, a 58-residues long micropeptide that increases neural cell metabolic activity and mitochondrial oxygen consumption rate rate. It shows increased expression in Alzheimer disease patient brains[[PMID:36658336]].

Name: Nucleoporin pom152-like, immunoglobulin-like domain
Description: This domain is found in Nucleoporin pom152 from Schizosaccharomyces pombe and similar fungal proteins. Pom152 functions as a component of the nuclear pore complex (NPC). This domain is predicted to adopt an immunoglobulin-like structure.

Name: ECM29, ARM-like repeats
Description: This is a region of armadillo-like repeats found in human Proteasome adapter and scaffold protein ECM29 and similar protein sequences predominantly found in vertebrates. ECM29 binds to the 26S proteasome, motor proteins and other compartment specific proteins. This entry is often found C-terminal to [pfam:PF13001].

Name: DExH-box ATP-dependent RNA helicase DExH18, N-terminal
Description: This domain is found at the N-terminal end of DExH-box ATP-dependent RNA helicase DExH18, mitochondrial from Arabidopsis thaliana and similar sequences from plants. DExH18 is a major helicase player in mitochondrial RNA metabolism. It plays a role in the RNA surveillance system in mitochondria; regulates the stability of mature mRNAs, the removal of aberrantly formed mRNAs and the rapid degradation of non coding processing intermediates. This domain is predicted to adopt an all alpha-helical configuration.

Name: Erythroid differentiation-related factor 1-like, TPR repeats
Description: This region of tetratricopeptide-like repeats (TPR) is found at the C-terminal end of human Erythroid differentiation-related factor 1 (EDRF1) and similar proteins predominantly found in vertebrates. EDRF1 is a transcription factor involved in erythroid differentiation.

Name: RSE1/DDB1/CPSF1 second beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the second beta-propeller found in RSE1/SF3B3/ DDB1/CPSF1/CFT1 proteins in eukaryotes, which share a domain architecture consisting of three beta-propellers. Members of the family have diverse functions, primarily related to RNA/DNA binding. Pre-mRNA-splicing factor RSE1 plays an important role in the spliceosome, a complex that removes introns from pre-mRNAs [[PMID:16940174]]. Its orthologue, splicing factor 3B subunit 3 (SF3B3) also forms part of the U2 small nuclear ribonucleoprotein particle (snRNP) and has been identified as part of the SAGA transcriptional coactivator complex in Drosophila melanogaster [[PMID:27185460]]. Also included in this entry is DNA damage -binding protein 1 (DDB1), which is both involved in the recognition and repair of UV-induced DNA damage, as part of the UV-DDB complex, and in protein ubiquitination, as part of the DCX (DDB1-CUL4-X-box) complex [[PMID:16407252],[PMID:11421366]]. Additionally, this family includes cleavage and polyadenylation specificity factor subunit 1 (CPSF1) and its orthologue protein CFT1, both involved in the cleavage and polyadenylation of pre-mRNAs [[PMID:9819400],[PMID:8929410]], for which this entry is a partial match.

Name: FAM234A/B beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller domain found in FAM234A/B proteins from mammals, whose function is unknown.

Name: Dystrophin-1-like, spectrin repeat
Description: This domain is found in Dystrophin-1 from Caenorhabditis elegans (Dys-1) and similar eukaryotic sequences. Dys-1 plays a role in cholinergic transmission and as a functional partner of dystrobrevin (Dyb-1), necessary for muscle maintenance. It is required for neuronal positioning.

Name: ECM29, C-terminal ARM-like repeats
Description: This is a region of armadillo-like repeats found at the C-terminal end of human Proteasome adapter and scaffold protein ECM29 and similar eukaryotic sequences. ECM29 binds to the 26S proteasome, motor proteins and other compartment specific proteins.

Name: BBS7 beta-propeller
Description: BBS7 is one of seven proteins that form the BBSome complex, localised at ciliary bases in the cytoplasm [[PMID:23572516]]. The BBS7 beta-propeller interacts with the beta-propeller domain of another BBSome protein, BBS1, in order to control access of the membrane-recruiting protein ARL6 [[PMID:31303482]]. Mutations on or lack of BB7 (or any BBSome protein) causes Bardet-Biedl syndrome (BBS), characterized by cilium dysfunction [[PMID:16909204]].

Name: LRRK2 ARM repeat
Description: This entry represents the armadillo repeat (ARM) found at the N-terminal of Leucine-rich repeat serine/threonine-protein kinase 2 (LRRK2) from eukaryotes. The domain adopts an ARM-like fold, consisting of a right-handed superhelix formed by two curved layers of repeated helical structural units [[PMID:10361086]]. LRRK2 is a serine/threonine-protein kinase which phosphorylates a broad range of proteins involved in multiple processes such as neuronal plasticity, primary ciliogenesis, autophagy, and vesicle trafficking [[PMID:20949042],[PMID:29125462],[PMID:22012985],[PMID:29212815]]. Mutations in LRRK2 are the most common genetic cause of Parkinson's disease (PD)[[PMID:20949042]]. LRRK2 phosphorylates a group of Rab GTPase proteins, including Rab32. A patch of negatively charged residues within the ARM domain has been pointed to contribute to complex formation by binding a positive residue in Rab switch 1 region [[PMID:29212815],[PMID:34107286],[PMID:31552791]]. ARM domain is also suggested to form connections between adjacent microtubules [[PMID:32783917]]. Also included in this entry is the armadillo repeat domain of Protein aardvark from Dictyostelium discoideum, a component of the junctional complex, both involved in cell adhesion and signaling [[PMID:11130075]].

Name: LRRK2 ANK repeat
Description: This entry represents the ankyrin repeat (ANK) found towards the N-terminal in Leucine-rich repeat serine/threonine-protein kinase 2 (LRRK2) from eukaryotes, between the ARM repeat domain ([pfam:PF23744]) and the LRR domain ([pfam:PF13855]). LRRK2 is a serine/threonine-protein kinase which phosphorylates a broad range of proteins involved in multiple processes such as neuronal plasticity, primary ciliogenesis, autophagy, and vesicle trafficking [[PMID:20949042],[PMID:29125462],[PMID:22012985],[PMID:29212815]]. Mutations in LRRK2 are the most common genetic cause of Parkinson's disease [[PMID:20949042]]. LRRK2 phosphorylates a group of Rab GTPase proteins including Rab29. The ANK domain plays a key role in this process, as conserved leucine residues within the ANK domain are required for Rab29 binding, which mediates Golgi recruitment and kinase activation [[PMID:29212815],[PMID:34107286],[PMID:32783917]].

Name: LRRK2 beta propeller
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller found at the C-terminal in Leucine-rich repeat serine/threonine-protein kinase 2 (LRRK2) from eukaryotes. LRRK2 is a serine/threonine-protein kinase which phosphorylates a broad range of proteins involved in multiple processes such as neuronal plasticity, primary ciliogenesis, autophagy, and vesicle trafficking [[PMID:20949042],[PMID:29125462],[PMID:22012985],[PMID:29212815]]. Mutations in LRRK2 are the most common genetic cause of Parkinson's disease (PD)[[PMID:20949042]].The beta-propeller domain is suggested to mediate LRRK2 dimerization and autophosphorylation, and plays an important role in neurotoxicity associated to LRRK2 PD mutations [[PMID:20041156],[PMID:34107286],[PMID:32783917]].

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7165)
Description: This entry represents a domain of unknown function found towards the N-terminal of fungal proteins. This domain adopts a beta-propeller like fold and may be found in association with an N-terminal F-box motif.

Name: WDR11 first beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the first beta-propeller found at the N-terminal of WD repeat-containing protein 11 (WDR11) from animals and similar eukaryotic proteins. Members of this family share a domain architecture consisting of two beta-propellers followed by a TPR domain. WDR11 has been implicated in human developmental genetic disorders characterised by delayed puberty and infertility. WDR11 modulates the Hedgehog (Hh) signalling pathway and is essential for adequate ciliogenesis [[PMID:29263200]]. The beta-propellers in WDR11 are involved in protein-protein interaction. This are essential for proper interaction with EMX1, a homeodomain transcription factor involved in the development of olfactory neurons. Impaired pubertal development is suggested to result from a deficiency of productive WDR11 protein interaction [[PMID:20887964]]. WDR11 is also involved in vesicle traffic as it binds TBC1D23, a vesicle-golgin adaptor required for endosome-to-Golgi trafficking [[PMID:29084197]].

Name: WDR11 second beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the second beta-propeller found in WD repeat-containing protein 11 (WDR11) from animals and similar eukaryotic proteins. Members of this family share a domain architecture consisting of two beta-propellers followed by a TPR domain. WDR11 has been implicated in human developmental genetic disorders characterised by delayed puberty and infertility. WDR11 modulates the Hedgehog (Hh) signalling pathway and is essential for adequate ciliogenesis [[PMID:29263200]]. The beta-propellers in WDR11 are involved in protein-protein interaction. This are essential for proper interaction with EMX1, a homeodomain transcription factor involved in the development of olfactory neurons. Impaired pubertal development is suggested to result from a deficiency of productive WDR11 protein interaction [[PMID:20887964]]. WDR11 is also involved in vesicle traffic as it binds TBC1D23, a vesicle-golgin adaptor required for endosome-to-Golgi trafficking [[PMID:29084197]].

Name: WDR11 TPR domain
Description: This entry represents the TPR domain found at the C-terminal of WD repeat-containing protein 11 (WDR11) from animals and similar eukaryotic proteins. Members of this family share a domain architecture consisting of two beta-propellers followed by a TPR domain. WDR11 has been implicated in human developmental genetic disorders characterised by delayed puberty and infertility. WDR11 modulates the Hedgehog (Hh) signalling pathway and is essential for adequate ciliogenesis [[PMID:29263200]]. WDR11 also interacts with EMX1, a homeodomain transcription factor involved in the development of olfactory neurons [[PMID:20887964]]; and is involved in vesicle traffic as it binds TBC1D23, a vesicle-golgin adaptor required for endosome-to-Golgi trafficking [[PMID:29084197]].

Name: IP5P C-F beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller domain found mainly in plant inositol polyphosphate 5-phosphatases (IP5P), such as Type I IP5P12/IP5PC and IP5P13/IP5PD and Type II IP5P14/IP5PE and IP5P15/IP5PF from Arabidopsis thaliana. IP5Ps have different functions, including the degradation of phosphoinositides in the signalling pathways of various cellular processes such as Ca2+ release, abscisic acid signalling and vesicle trafficking, among others [[PMID:31426386]]. In the case of IP5P13, the beta propeller domain interacts with the sucrose non-fermenting-1-related kinase (SnRK1.1) in order to achieve a regulatory role linking inositol, sugar and stress signalling [[PMID:18931139]].

Name: HPS5 beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller domain found in HPS5, a component of the biogenesis of lysosome-related organelles complex-2 (BLOC-2) [[PMID:15030569]] and which has a role in the endo-lysosomal dynamics of skin fibroblasts [[PMID:28296950]]. Its mutation or absence has been linked to eye-colour mutations in insects [[PMID:9714595]] and Hermansky-Pudlak type 5 syndrome (HPS-5) in mammals, a disease characterized by defects in lysosome-related organelles such as melanosomes and platelet dense granules [[PMID:18544035]]. This entry also represents the beta-propeller domain found in the tectonin beta-propeller repeat-containing protein 2 (TCPR2), which is a paralogue of HPS5. TCPR2 acts as a molecular scaffold linking early secretion pathway and autophagy [[PMID:26431026]].

Name: Insect HPS5 TPR domain
Description: This entry represents the TPR domain found at the C-terminal of HPS5 protein from insects, a component of the biogenesis of lysosome-related organelles complex-2 (BLOC-2) [[PMID:15030569]]. Its mutation or absence has been linked to eye-colour mutations [[PMID:9714595]]. The function of this domain is unknown.

Name: HPS5 TPR domain
Description: This entry represents the TPR domain found at the C-terminal of HPS5 protein from vertebrates, a component of the biogenesis of lysosome-related organelles complex-2 (BLOC-2) [[PMID:15030569]] and which has a role in the endo-lysosomal dynamics of skin fibroblasts [[PMID:28296950]]. Its mutation or absence has been linked to Hermansky-Pudlak type 5 syndrome (HPS-5) in mammals, a disease characterized by defects in lysosome-related organelles such as melanosomes and platelet dense granules [[PMID:18544035]]. The function of this domain is unknown.

Name: DDB1- and CUL4-associated factor 12 beta propeller
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller domain found in DDB1- and CUL4-associated factor 12 proteins (DCAF12) as well as DCAF12-like proteins 1 and 2 (DCAF12L1 and DCAF12L2) from animals. DCAF proteins have been hypothesised to play important roles during spermatogenesis due to their high level of conservation in mammals and testis enrichment [[PMID:32867693]]. Mutations in DCAF12L1 has only been associated to male infertility in humans but not in mice [[PMID:35850260]]. In insects, mutations in the DCAF12 homolog impairs larval development [[PMID:30670470]]. The function of this domain is unknown.

Name: DDB1- and CUL4-associated factor 4 beta-propeller domain
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller domain found in DDB1- and CUL4-associated factor 4 (DCAF4) and DCAF4-like proteins 1 and 2 (DCAF4L1 and DCAF4L2) from mammals. These proteins are substrate receptors for the Cullin-DDB1 ubiquitin ligase complexes [[PMID:16964240]]. DCAF4 has been identified as a key protein in proper proteostasis of Cu, Zn superoxide dismutase (SOD1) [[PMID:32014991]] and has been associated to Leukocyte telomere length (LTL) in humans, which in turn has been link to human diseases, including sporadic melanoma [[PMID:25624462]]. The function of this domain is unknown.

Name: WD repeat-containing protein 75 second beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the second beta-propeller found in WD repeat-containing protein 75 (WDR75) from animals. WDR75 has a crucial positive role in ribosome biogenesis and its malfunction activates p53, leading to cellular senescence [[PMID:34611297]]. The function of this domain is unknown. This entry also represents the second beta-propeller domain of U3 small nucleolar RNA-associated protein 13 and 17 (UTP13 and UTP17), which are WDR75 orthologs from yeast.

Name: RIG first beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the first beta-propeller found at the N-terminal of Protein rigor mortis (RIG) from Drosophila melanogaster. RIG is a nuclear receptor-interacting protein required for the response to steroid hormone ecdysone during larval development [[PMID:14645129]]. This domain acts as a specific protein-protein interaction domain. Also included in this entry as partial matches are orthologues Gem-associated protein 5 (GEMI5) from animals. GEMI5 is a component of the survival of motor neurons (SMN) complex, that binds small nuclear RNAs (snRNAs). The SMN complex assembles the heptameric Sm protein core on snRNAs and plays a critical role in the biogenesis of small nuclear ribonucleoproteins (snRNPs) [[PMID:16857593],[PMID:35987821]].

Name: GEMI5 TPR domain
Description: This entry represents the TPR domain found towards the C-terminal of Gem-associated protein 5 (GEMI5) from animals and related eukaryotic sequences. GEMI5 is a component of the survival of motor neurons (SMN) complex, that binds small nuclear RNAs (snRNAs). The SMN complex assembles the heptameric Sm protein core on snRNAs playing a critical role in the biogenesis of small nuclear ribonucleoproteins (snRNPs) [[PMID:16857593]]. GEMI5 is also implicated in translation control, and gene expression reprogramming [[PMID:34424823]]. The TPR domain in GEMI5 mediates the dimerization of the protein [[PMID:32485878]]. This is possible thanks to the helical repeats stretched in an alpha-solenoid rod that provides an extensive flat surface for self-dimerization [[PMID:31799608]]. Mutations in this domain have been shown to cause a neurodevelopmental disorder characterised by cerebellar atrophy, intellectual disability (ID), and motor dysfunction [[PMID:36980979]]. Also included in this entry is Protein rigor mortis (RIG) TPR domain from Drosophila melanogaster. RIG is a nuclear receptor-interacting protein required for the response to steroid hormone ecdysone during larval development [[PMID:14645129]].

Name: RIG second beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the second beta-propeller found in Protein rigor mortis (RIG) from Drosophila melanogaster. RIG is a nuclear receptor-interacting protein required for the response to steroid hormone ecdysone during larval development. This domain acts as a specific protein-protein interaction domain [[PMID:14645129]]. Also included in this entry are orthologues Gem-associated protein 5 (GEMI5) from animals. GEMI5 is a component of the survival of motor neurons (SMN) complex. The beta-propellers in GEMI5 bind small nuclear RNAs (snRNAs) playing a critical role in the assembly of the heptameric Sm protein core on snRNAs and the biogenesis of small nuclear ribonucleoproteins (snRNPs) [[PMID:16857593],[PMID:31799608]].

Name: ELP1 N-terminal second beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents second out of two N-terminal beta-propeller domains, found in Elongator complex protein 1 (ELP1) from eukaryotes.  Elongator is a conserved protein complex implicated in multiple processes including regulation of transcription, alpha-tubulin acetylation, and tRNA modification. Mutations in the ELP1 gene have been shown to cause the neurodegenerative disease familial dysautonomia [[PMID:29332244]]. ELP1 binds members of the Elongator complex ELP2 and ELP3; and the beta-propellers are suggested to act as scaffold for this protein-protein interaction [[PMID:11818576]]. Also known as IKAP, ELP1 acts as a regulator of IkB kinase complex involved in proinflammatory cytokine signaling. Residues within this second beta propeller have been shown to be required for interaction with upstream regulatory kinase NIK, acting as a scaffold that assembles IKKs with NIK [[PMID:9751059]].

Name: SPT8 beta propeller
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller found in Transcription factor SPT8 (SPT8) from fungi. SPT8 is a member of the transcription regulatory histone acetylation (HAT) complex SAGA. SPT8 interacts with TATA-binding protein (TBP) acting as a suppressor of transcription initiation defects caused by promoter insertions of the transposable element Ty [[PMID:10026213]].

Name: Mmc1 N-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found N-terminal in Mmc1 from Schizosaccharomyces pombe and related proteins. Mmc1 functions in concert with the mitochondrial contact site and cristae organizing system (MICOS) complex to promote normal mitochondrial morphology and respiratory function [[PMID:38692277]]. This domain is predicted to adopt a globular structure with significant similarity to dynamin-like GTPases, in particular, to mitofusin 1. However, Mmc1 lacks key residues required for GTP binding and hydrolysis. It was proposed that Mmc1 stabilizes cristae architecture by acting as a scaffold to support cristae ultrastructure on the matrix side of the inner membrane [[PMID:38692277]].

Name: Mmc1 C-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found C-terminal in Mmc1 from Schizosaccharomyces pombe and related proteins. Mmc1 functions in concert with the mitochondrial contact site and cristae organizing system (MICOS) complex to promote normal mitochondrial morphology and respiratory function [[PMID:38692277]]. This domain is predicted to adopt a globular structure composed mainly of alpha helices. Some of are predicted to be transmembrane and as such it is likely that this domain may associates with membrane. It was proposed that Mmc1 stabilizes cristae architecture by acting as a scaffold to support cristae ultrastructure on the matrix side of the inner membrane [[PMID:38692277]].

Name: WD repeat-containing protein 75 first beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the first beta-propeller found in WD repeat-containing protein 75 (WDR75) from animals. WDR75 has a crucial positive role in ribosome biogenesis and its malfunction activates p53, leading to cellular senescence [[PMID:34611297]]. This domain forms a composite binding site with UTP4 protein in the SSU processome for the 5' external transcribed spacer [[PMID:34516797]].

Name: ELP1 TPR domain
Description: This entry represents the TPR domain found within the C-terminal dimerization domain of Elongator complex protein 1 (ELP1), from eukaryotes. Elongator is a conserved protein complex implicated in multiple processes including regulation of transcription, alpha-tubulin acetylation and tRNA modification. Mutations in the ELP1 gene have been shown to cause the neurodegenerative disease familial dysautonomia [[PMID:29332244]]. The C-terminal dimerization domain consists of 28 alpha-helices organised in three domains: an elongated alpha-solenoid domain (helices 1 to 14); the TPR domain, formed by six double-helix repeats (helices 15 to 26) that act as an interface for protein dimerization; and a three-helix bundle, C-terminal cap (helices 26 to 28) [[PMID:26261306]]. ELP1 self-association contributes to its stability and is important for Elongator assembly [[PMID:26261306]]. ELP1 is considered to function both as a scaffold, for Elongator complex ELP2 and ELP3 binding, and as a docking site for various factors that regulate Elongator activity [[PMID:26261306],[PMID:11818576],[PMID:23510783]]. Also known as IKAP, ELP1 acts as a regulator of IkB kinase complex involved in proinflammatory cytokine signalling [[PMID:9751059]].

Name: SV2A/B/C luminal domain
Description: This entry represents the luminal domain found in synaptic vesicle protein 2 A, B and C (SV2A, SV2B and SV2C) from animals. SV2s are specific to vesicles that undergo calcium-regulated secretion and are essential for normal nervous system function, particularly for neurotransmission [[PMID:30826548]]. SV2s have also been identified as targets for botulinum neurotoxins (BoNTs) serotypes A, D, E and F [[PMID:19476346]]. In the case of VS2C, this domain interacts directly and allows BoNT/A transport into host cells [[PMID:24240280]].

Name: Cytochrome c-type biogenesis protein H TPR domain
Description: This entry represents the TPR domain found in cytochrome c-type biogenesis proteins CcmH and CycH from bacteria. These proteins are membrane-anchored thiol-oxidoreductases that are essential in the maturation of c-type cytochromes [[PMID:7635817]]. In the case of CycH, it has been hypothesised that this TPR domain interacts with other proteins in order to mediate the assembly of c-type cytochromes [[PMID:14758542]]. This entry also represents a TPR domain found in other proteins such as NRFG, NRFF and RNA polymerase-associated protein CDC27.

Name: ELP1 alpha-solenoid
Description: This entry represents the alpha-solenoid domain found within the C-terminal dimerization domain of Elongator complex protein 1 (ELP1), from eukaryotes. Elongator is a conserved protein complex implicated in multiple processes including regulation of transcription, alpha-tubulin acetylation and tRNA modification. Mutations in the ELP1 gene have been shown to cause the neurodegenerative disease familial dysautonomia [[PMID:29332244]].
