The C-terminal dimerization domain consists of 28 alpha-helices organised in three domains: an elongated alpha-solenoid domain (helices 1 to 14); a TPR-like domain, formed by six double-helix repeats (helices 15 to 26); and a three-helix bundle, C-terminal cap (helices 26 to 28) [[PMID:26261306]]. Residues within the alpha-14-helix are highly conserved from yeast to human and act as a kink to maintain the TPR-like domain and alpha-solenoid domain at nearly a 90-degree angle. ELP1 self-association contributes to its stability and is important for Elongator assembly [[PMID:26261306]]. ELP1 is considered to function both as a scaffold, for Elongator complex ELP2 and ELP3 binding, and as a docking site for various factors that regulate Elongator activity [[PMID:26261306],[PMID:11818576],[PMID:23510783]]. Also known as IKAP, ELP1 acts as a regulator of IkB kinase complex involved in proinflammatory cytokine signalling [[PMID:9751059]].

Name: ARM-like repeat
Description: This entry represents an array of alpha solenoid repeats found in a large family of fungal proteins. This domain is generally found at the N-terminus of the proteins followed by a NACHT domain [pfam:PF05729].

Name: Endo-beta-N-acetylglucosaminidase EndoS LRR domain
Description: This entry represents the leucine-rich repeat domain found in Endo-beta-N-acetylglucosaminidase EndoS from Streptococcus spp. EndoS is a secreted protein that catalyzes the hydrolysis of the N-linked oligosaccharides on opsonizing immunoglobulin G [[PMID:11406581]], thus contributing to immune response evasion and increased survival of the bacteria in patients [[PMID:12438337]]. The function of this domain is unknown.

Name: COPA/B TPR domain
Description: This entry represents the TPR domain found in Coatomer subunits alpha and beta (COPA1-3, COPB2 and COB21-3), from eukaryotes. The domain adopts an alpha-solenoid fold. Coatomer complexes function in the sorting and trafficking of proteins between subcellular organelles [[PMID:34450031]].Coat protein I (COPI)-coated vesicles mediate retrograde transport from the Golgi to the endoplasmic reticulum (ER), as well as transport within the Golgi [[PMID:31712447]]. The Coatomer protein complex subunit beta 2 (COPB2) is involved in the formation of the COPI coatomer protein complex. COPB2 is a vital oncogene in many cancer types that regulates multiple biological behaviours of tumor cells, including proliferation, survival, tumourigenesis, invasion, and metastasis [[PMID:34101128]]. COPA constitutes part of COPI, playing a role in the retrograde transport of cargo proteins between the Golgi and the ER and the movement of vesicles within the Golgi [[PMID:32725128]].

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7330)
Description: This entry represents a domain found in uncharacterised proteins, mainly from fungi. This domain may consist of beta-solenoid repeats. This entry shows distant similarity to [pfam:PF13349].

Name: SCAP Beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller domain found at the C-terminal of SCAP proteins, key regulators of lipid homeostasis in cells. It acts as a cholesterol sensor and escort for SREBP, therefore, when cholesterol levels are low, SCAP binds to SREBP and facilitates its transport from the ER to the Golgi, where it undergoes proteolytic cleavage and activates it, leading to its activation and translocation to the nucleus to activate the transcription of genes involved in cholesterol synthesis. This domain is involved in the interaction with SREBP [[PMID:34192549],[PMID:33446483]].

Name: NXF1/2/3/5 leucine-rich repeat domain
Description: This entry represents the leucine-rich repeat domain found in nuclear RNA export factor 1, 2, 3 and 5 (NXF1, NXF2, NXF3 and NXF5, respectively) from animals and their yeast orthologues mRNA export factor 67 (MEX67). These proteins are involved in the nuclear export of mRNA from the nucleus to the cytoplasm [[PMID:10924507],[PMID:10228171]]. NXFs are used by type D retroviruses to export their unspliced genomic RNA to the cytoplasm through this LRR domain, which binds to constitutive transport element of retroviral RNAs [[PMID:11060011]]. Additionally, cellular mRNA transport is affected when this domain is mutated [[PMID:10202158]]. In the case of MEX67, this domain is found within a region of the protein that allows MEX67 to act as an accessory factor in Rae1p-dependent mRNA export and is devoid of RNA-binding [[PMID:11073978]].

Name: DNA polymerase zeta catalytic subunit, N-terminal
Description: This domain is found at the N-terminal end of DNA polymerase zeta catalytic subunit (REV3) from Saccharomyces cerevisiae and similar eukaryotic sequences. REV3 is a nonessential DNA polymerase involved in DNA repair, mitochondrial DNA repair and translesion synthesis. This domain shows a mainly beta- configuration [[PMID:32111820],[PMID:32807989],[PMID:34174285]]. It is often found N-terminal to [pfam:PF24055].

Name: BT_1020-like, N-terminal beta propeller
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal region of BT_1020 from Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron, which covers part of its 5-bladed beta propeller domain. It is usually found associated with [pfam:PF22585].

Name: PIC/HAP1/IgA0 second beta-solenoid repeat region
Description: This entry represents the second solenoid-repeat region found in some serine peptidase autotransporters from bacteria, including IgA0, HAP1, Pic, SepA and EspC. These proteins have been associated to virulent strains [[PMID:9695914],[PMID:8932311]]. Pic and other bacterial autotransporters confer serum resistance but do not degrade immunoglobulin [[PMID:9695914],[PMID:10531204]], as in the case of IgA0 [[PMID:4199558]]. This region counts with insertions between beta-solenoid repeats which have been suggested to be involved in forming a unique structure for recognition and binding of protein substrate [[PMID:19393662]].

Name: Nucleoporin POM152, N-terminal transmembrane domain
Description: This domain is found at the N-terminal end of Nucleoporin POM152 from Saccharomyces cerevisiae and similar fungal proteins. POM152, a component of the nuclear pore complex (NPC), is important for the de novo assembly of NPCs. This protein consists of an N-terminal transmembrane domain (TMD, this entry) and an extended C-terminal domain (CTD) with tandem repeats of Ig-like domains [[PMID:37738963]].

Name: F-box protein At5g52880 ARM repeat
Description: This domain is found N-terminal in Arabidopsis thaliana F-box protein At5g52880 and related proteins. It usually precedes F-box [pfam:PF12937].

Name: CAF1B/HIR1 beta-propeller domain
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller domain found in chromatin assembly factor 1 subunit CAF1B (also known as p60 subunit) from animals, and its homologues Cac2 from yeasts and FAS from plants. These proteins are involved in DNA synthesis-coupled chromatic assembly in S-phase and chromatin assembly following DNA repair alongside other CHAF proteins in what is known as CAF1 complex [[PMID:9813080],[PMID:16980972]]. In plants, the loss of activity of the CAF1 pathway causes delay and arrest of the cell cycle during pollen development [[PMID:18045841]]. The CAF1 complex is also required for stable maintenance of telomeres and 45S rDNA in Arabidopsis [[PMID:20699390]]. This domain is involved in CAF1B interactions with other CAF1 complex subunits [[PMID:27221973]]. This entry also includes histone cell cycle regulator HIRA from animals and their yeast orthologues HIR1, which are required in replication-independent chromatic assembly [[PMID:15121850]]. The beta-propeller of HIRA proteins is involved in the protein interactions with Ubinuclein-1 (UBN1) to achieve chromatic-remodelling pathway in senescent cells [[PMID:19029251]].

Name: EDC4-like pdc1 beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller domain of the enhancer of mRNA-decapping protein 4-like protein pdc1 from yeast. This protein is required for the assembly of processing bodies (P-bodies), cytoplasmic RNA granules where mRNA decay occurs [[PMID:23319050]]. While evidence suggests pdc1 interacts with proteins Edc3 and Dcp2 through its terminal domains to achieve functional P-bodies, the function of this domain remains unknown [[PMID:24862735]]. This entry also includes the beta-propeller domain of pdc1's paralogue, the uncharacterised protein YAG9 (or eti1) from yeast, which localises to P-bodies and interacts with exonuclease Exo2 but whose function remains unknown [[PMID:24755092]].

Name: Amanitin toxin
Description: Members of this family are ribosomally produced precursors of toxins produced by several mushrooms. These precursors undergo extensive post-translational modification to become amatoxins (e.g. alpha-amanitin) [[PMID:18025465]]. They are members of the MSDIN fungal toxin family [[PMID:24613547]]. Amanitin is a major toxin belonging to the bicyclic octapeptides amatoxins that acts by binding non-competitively to RNA polymerase II and greatly slowing the elongation of transcripts from target promoters [[PMID:22202811]]. Amanitin complex with dual function macrocyclase-peptidase enzyme was structurally characterised [[PMID:29051530]].

Name: Single-strand DNA deaminase toxin A, C-terminal
Description: This domain is found at the C-terminal end of Single-strand DNA deaminase toxin A from Pseudomonas syringae (SsdA) and similar bacterial and fungal sequences. SsdA shows a typical alpha-beta deaminase configuration [[PMID:33448264]].

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7400)
Description: This entry represents a domain of unknown function found towards the N-terminal of some PRiA4b ORF-3-like proteins from bacteria, such as SwissProt:A0A1I7K0M5. This domain may adopt a TPR-like fold and is generally associated to PriA4_ORF3 ([pfam:PF07929]) domain.

Name: Transportin-3/Importin-13 second TPR domain
Description: This entry represents the second TPR domain found in Transportin-3 (TNPO3) from animals, as well as orthologues MTR10 and uncharacterised protein YNR7 from yeast and MOS14 from plant. This entry also includes Importin-13 (IPO3) from animals. These proteins are involved in transport of target proteins into the nucleus. TNPO3 mediates the nuclear import of splicing factor serine/arginine (SR) proteins, such as RBM4, SFRS1 and SFRS2, by recognizing phosphorylated SR domains [[PMID:12628928],[PMID:24449914]]. This domain is involved in the binding to the globular Ran protein in TNOP3 [[PMID:24915079]]. In IPO13, this domain is involved in the protein interaction with Ubc9 for nuclear import [[PMID:21139563]] and with eIF1A for nuclear import [[PMID:23435562]].

Name: Transportin-3/Importin-13 fourth TPR domain
Description: This entry represents the third TPR domain found in Transportin-3 (TNPO3) from animals, as well as orthologues MTR10 and uncharacterised protein YNR7 from yeast and MOS14 from plants. This entry also includes Importin-13 (IPO3) from animals. These proteins are involved in transport of target proteins into the nucleus. TNPO3 mediates the nuclear import of splicing factor serine/arginine (SR) proteins, such as RBM4, SFRS1 and SFRS2, by recognizing phosphorylated SR domains [[PMID:12628928],[PMID:24449914]]. This domain interacts with the RS region of the ASF protein for nuclear transport [[PMID:24449914]].

Name: Transportin-3/Importin-13 third TPR domain
Description: This entry represents the third TPR domain found in Transportin-3 (TNPO3) from animals, as well as orthologues MTR10 and uncharacterised protein YNR7 from yeast and MOS14 from plants. This entry also includes Importin-13 (IPO3) from animals. These proteins are involved in transport of target proteins into the nucleus. TNPO3 mediates the nuclear import of splicing factor serine/arginine (SR) proteins, such as RBM4, SFRS1 and SFRS2, by recognizing phosphorylated SR domains [[PMID:12628928],[PMID:24449914]]. This domain is part of TPR structure that binds proteins for nuclear transport [[PMID:21139563],[PMID:24915079]].

Name: EGF-like domain-containing protein ComC LRR domain
Description: This entry represents the LRR domain found in N-terminal of EGF-like domain containing protein ComC from amoebas. This protein regulates aggregation, inhibiting lagC and activating lagD expression [[PMID:14651934]]. The function of this domain is unknown.

Name: TTI1 N-terminal TPR domain
Description: This entry represents the TPR domain found at the N-terminal of TELO2-interacting protein 1 homologue (TTI1) from animals and yeast. TTI1 binds to TELO2 and TTI2 proteins to form the TTT complex, which plays a role in the assembly and stability of all phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase-related protein kinases (PIKKs) [[PMID:20810650]], pivotal proteins in various pathways related to cell metabolism, proliferation and differentiation [[PMID:25460276]]. This domain interacts with the FAT domain of the PIKK ATM [[PMID:34838521]].

Name: TELO2-interacting protein 1 homologue second TPR domain
Description: This entry represents the second TPR domain found in TELO2-interacting protein 1 homologue (TTI1) from animals. TTI1 binds to TELO2 and TTI2 proteins to form the TTT complex, which plays a role in the assembly and stability of all phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase-related protein kinases (PIKKs) [[PMID:20810650]], pivotal proteins in various pathways related to cell metabolism, proliferation and differentiation [[PMID:25460276]]. This domain interacts with TELO2 [[PMID:34838521],[PMID:34233195]].

Name: TTI1 C-terminal TPR domain
Description: This entry represents the TPR domain found at the C-terminal of TELO2-interacting protein 1 homologue (TTI1) from animals and yeast. TTI1 binds to TELO2 and TTI2 proteins to form the TTT complex, which plays a role in the assembly and stability of all phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase-related protein kinases (PIKKs) [[PMID:20810650]], pivotal proteins in various pathways related to cell metabolism, proliferation and differentiation [[PMID:25460276]]. This domain binds to HEAT repeats of the ATM PIKK [[PMID:34838521]].

Name: Staphylococcus phage terminal repeat-encoded protein TreK
Description: This entry represents a terminal repeat-encoded protein (TreK) found in Twort-like Staphylococcus phages. Twort-like viruses are members of the Myoviridae family and are characterized by large genomes (>125 kb) with a low G+C content (30-31%) and terminally redundant ends. These terminal repeats are several thousand base pairs long and may influence the host range. The TreK protein is encoded within these terminal repeat regions. Twort-like phages are known for their broad host range and strictly lytic life cycle, making them suitable candidates for phage therapy against Staphylococcus aureus infections. This domain is often found in various Staphylococcus phages, particularly those belonging to the Herelleviridae family. The protein is typically around 90-95 amino acids in length. While its exact function remains unknown, its conservation among Twort-like phages suggests it plays an important role in phage biology, possibly related to host range determination or genome packaging.  Structure prediction shows these proteins form a globular domain that includes a 5 stranded highly curved beta sheet with a C-terminal helix.

Name: Repeat of unknown function (DUF7450)
Description: This entry represents a group of uncharacterised proteins from bacteria that, according to structure predictions (SwissProt:A0A7C3HQW7 or SwissProt:A0A5C6D6K5), they may contain novel repeats that fold into twisted long beta-hairpins different from typical beta-barrel hairpins and they do not exhibit homology to any beta-barrel.

Name: Family of unknown function (DUF7453)
Description: This entry represents a group of uncharacterised sequences from bacteria. According to structure predictions, these proteins may comprise novel repeats consisting of an arrangement of four beta-strands in which the second one is longer and curved. This arrangement is repeated eight times in SwissProt:A0A5B9QB27, which form a closed repeat.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7477)
Description: This entry represents a domain consisting of a novel repeat fold which containing five repeats, each of them comprising three antiparallel beta-strands and a short alpha-helix, according to structure predictions (e.g SwissProt:S8CFV9). This domain is found in Casein kinase 1-like protein HD16 from rice, a protein involved in the regulation of flowering time through gibberellin (GA) signaling and independently of photoperiod [[PMID:20400938]].

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7478)
Description: This entry represents a domain containing a novel repeat fold, according to structure predictions. It may comprise six repeats, each of them consisting of two antiparallel beta-strands and a short alpha-helix.

Name: Edg1, TPR repeats
Description: This entry represents a region of tetratricopeptide-like repeats found in a group of animal proteins, including protein edg-1 from Caenorhabditis elegans, which plays a role in regulating deps-1 cluster formation in the germline.

Name: Membrane-bound transcription factor PTM, chromo domain-like
Description: This is the putative chromo domain-like domain found in the membrane-bound transcription factor PTM (also known as DDP1) and similar plant sequences. PTM is required for the plastid-to-nucleus retrograde signalling, which plays an important role in the coordinated expression of both nuclear- and chloroplast-localized genes that encode photosynthesis-related proteins.

Name: Family of unknown function (DUF7481)
Description: This entry represents a group of uncharacterised sequences from bacteria. They may consist on repeats with a novel fold which is organised in three units with twisted beta-strands each.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7482)
Description: This entry includes a group of uncharacterised sequences from bacteria and archaea, which, according to structure predictions, they may have a novel repeat fold. They may consist of 5-6 antiparallel beta-strands decorated with short alpha-helices.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7483)
Description: This entry includes uncharacterised bacterial sequences that may adopt a novel fold consisting on three repeat units, each of them are type of beta-sandwich with three beta-strands on one side and four or five on the other.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7485)
Description: This entry includes uncharacterised sequences mainly from actinobacteria. According to structure predictions, they may form a beta-propeller-like fold consisting of four repeated units containing three (in one of them) and four (on the other three units) beta-strands. There is an additional unit which is slightly different that resembles a seven stranded beta-sandwich.

Name: Family of unknown function (DUF7486)
Description: This entry represents a group of uncharacterised sequences that, according to structure predictions. they may consist on a novel repeat fold which comprise beta-hairpin-beta units organised in four and five units, forming two domains.

Name: Family of unknown function (DUF7487)
Description: This entry represents a group of uncharacterised sequences from viruses. They may have a novel repeat fold consisting of two domains at the N- and C-terminal with beta-hairpin-beta-alpha configuration that are connected by nine alpha-helices linked by loops.

Name: Nucleoporin POM152, Ig-like domain
Description: This entry includes the Ig-like domains found in Nucleoporin POM152 from yeast, a component of the nuclear pore complex (NPC) [[PMID:8682855]]. POM152 is a glycoprotein subunit of transmembrane ring of NPC and is important for the assembly of the NPC [[PMID:11352933]].

Name: PYCO1 Rubisco-binding repeat
Description: This entry represents a repeat found in the pyrenoid component 1 (PYCO1) protein from diatoms. PYCO1 is an intrinsically disordered repeat protein that plays a crucial role in the formation of the pyrenoid, a microcompartment within chloroplasts that concentrates CO2 for more efficient carbon fixation. This entry represents a repeat that binds to the Rubisco small subunit. Four KWSPR/Q motifs can form a tetrad that binds to the small subunits lining Rubisco's central solvent channel.  The repeats enable PYCO1 to undergo liquid-liquid phase separation, forming condensates that specifically partition diatom Rubisco. This phase separation is crucial for the formation and function of the pyrenoid in diatoms, which are responsible for a significant portion of marine carbon fixation.

Name: CSLinker motif
Description: The CSLinker motif is found in the pyrenoid linker protein from Chlorella sorokiniana. It binds to the large subunit of Rubisco (RbcL) and is involved in phase separation of Rubisco in the pyrenoid, a sub-compartment of the chloroplast that is part of the CO2-concentrating mechanism in algae.  The motif consists of an alpha-helical region of approximately 10 residues that interacts with the N-terminal region of RbcL via two salt bridges and hydrophobic interactions. The key residues involved in binding are highly conserved.  CSLinker is able to phase separate Rubisco from various species across the green lineage, including some plants, demonstrating its potential for engineering pyrenoids in crop plants to enhance CO2 fixation.

Name: Integrator complex subunit 7, N-terminal
Description: Integrator complex subunit 7 (INTS7) is a component of the integrator complex, a complex that is recruited to the U1 and U2 snRNA genes and mediates the snRNAs' 3' end processing. The integrator complex interacts with the C-terminal tail of the largest subunit of RNA-polymerase II to promote 3'-snRNA processing [[PMID:16239144]]. This entry represents the superhelical alpha-solenoid found at the N-terminal of INTS7. INTS7 interacts with INTS1 and INTS2 making extensive contacts with each other, nucleating the assembly of the whole INTAC complex [[PMID:33243860]].

Name: y05I-like putative transcription factor C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of family of uncharacterised phage protein that includes T4 y05I protein [swissprot:P39240]. The family also includes homologues from T2 and T6 phage among others. These proteins contain two domains. These domains appear to be related to those found in the eukaryotic PurA transcription factor. This N-terminal presumably single stranded DNA binding domain contains two copies of a PurA-like repeat. The C-terminal domain contains a single copy that may mediate dimerisation in an analogous fashion to PurA.

Name: y05I-like putative transcription factor N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of family of uncharacterised phage protein that includes T4 y05I protein [swissprot:P39240]. The family also includes homologues from T2 and T6 phage among others. These proteins contain two domains. These domains appear to be related to those found in the eukaryotic PurA transcription factor. This N-terminal presumably single stranded DNA binding domain contains two copies of a PurA-like repeat. The C-terminal domain contains a single copy that may mediate dimerisation in an analogous fashion to PurA.

Name: FBXO47, ARM repeats
Description: This is the C-terminal region of human F-box only protein 47 (FBXO47) and similar animal sequences. This region is predicted to consist of armadillo (ARM)-like repeats.

Name: KDM8-like, N-terminal ARM repeats
Description: This region of armadillo (ARM)-like repeats is found at the N-terminal end of human Bifunctional peptidase and arginyl -hydroxylase JMJD5 (also known as KDM8) and similar animal proteins. KDM8 functions both as an endopeptidase and 2-oxoglutarate-dependent monooxygenase.

Name: At3g06530-like, ARM repeats
Description: This is a region of armadillo-like repeats found in the uncharacterised protein At3g06530 from Arabidopsis thaliana and similar plant sequences. This region is normally found associated with [pfam:PF12397].

Name: Proteasome adapter and scaffold protein ECM29, HEAT-repeat domain
Description: This entry represents a HEAT-repeat domain found in ECM29, an adapter/scaffolding protein that binds to the 26S proteasome, motor proteins and other compartment specific proteins [[PMID:20682791]]. This domain is found in the C-terminal half of ECM29, which binds kinesin and myosin [[PMID:20682791]].

Name: Rod, N-terminal
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller domain found at the N-terminal of Rod (SwissProt:Q9V9W7) protein from Drosophila and relates sequences from arthropods, which is the orthologue of human KNTC1. Rod is the major subunit of the Rod-Zw10-Zwilch (RZZ) complex. RZZ is an essential component of the outer kinetochore during mitosis and helps monitor and correct kinetochore-microtubule attachments during pro-metaphase, thus, regulating mitotic progression.

Name: KNTC1, N-terminal
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller domain found at the N-terminal of KNTC1 (also known as ROD) from vertebrates. KNTC1 is the major subunit of the RZZ (ROD-Zwilch-ZW10) complex [[PMID:35373361]], which plays a significant role in spindle assembly, kinetochore-microtubule attachment, and spindle pole separation. KNTC1 is required for the assembly of essential complexes onto kinetochores, including dynein-dynactin and MAD1-MAD2 [[PMID:11146660],[PMID:11590237],[PMID:15824131]]. This domain mediates extensive interactions with the other subunits of the complex, forming a RZZ module that binds Spindly, a DD adaptor that can activate dynein motility [[PMID:35373361]].

Name: KNTC1 third ARM-repeats domain
Description: This entry represents the third segment of ARM repeats present in KNTC1 (also known as ROD), following the beta-propeller domain. KNTC1 is a crucial component of the mitotic checkpoint, ensuring cells do not exit mitosis prematurely [[PMID:11146660],[PMID:11590237],[PMID:15824131]]. KNTC1 is required for the assembly of essential complexes onto kinetochores, such as dynein-dynactin and MAD1-MAD2. KNTC1 is the major subunit of the RZZ (ROD-Zwilch-ZW10) complex, which plays a significant role in spindle assembly, kinetochore-microtubule attachment, and spindle pole separation [[PMID:35373361]].

Name: KNTC1 second ARM-repeats domain
Description: This entry represents the second segment of ARM repeats present in KNTC1 (also known as ROD), following the beta-propeller domain. KNTC1 is a crucial component of the mitotic checkpoint, ensuring cells do not exit mitosis prematurely [[PMID:11146660],[PMID:11590237],[PMID:15824131]]. KNTC1 is required for the assembly of essential complexes onto kinetochores, such as dynein-dynactin and MAD1-MAD2. KNTC1 is the major subunit of the RZZ (ROD-Zwilch-ZW10) complex, which plays a significant role in spindle assembly, kinetochore-microtubule attachment, and spindle pole separation [[PMID:35373361]].

Name: Nucleoporin pom152-like, first immunoglobulin-like domain
Description: This is the first immunoglobulin-like (Ig-like) domain found in Nucleoporin pom152 from Schizosaccharomyces pombe and similar fungal proteins. Pom152 functions as a component of the nuclear pore complex (NPC).

Name: KNTC1 first ARM-repeats domain
Description: This entry represents the first segment of ARM repeats present in KNTC1 (also known as ROD), following the beta-propeller domain. KNTC1 is a crucial component of the mitotic checkpoint, ensuring cells do not exit mitosis prematurely [[PMID:11146660],[PMID:11590237],[PMID:15824131]]. KNTC1 is required for the assembly of essential complexes onto kinetochores, such as dynein-dynactin and MAD1-MAD2. KNTC1 is the major subunit of the RZZ (ROD-Zwilch-ZW10) complex, which plays a significant role in spindle assembly, kinetochore-microtubule attachment, and spindle pole separation [[PMID:35373361]].

Name: KRIT1 ankyrin-repeats domain
Description: KRIT1, also known as CCM1, a Rap1-binding protein, is expressed in endothelial cells where it is present in cell-cell junctions and associated with junctional proteins [[PMID:17954608]]. Together with CCM2/MGC4607 and CCM3/PDCD10, KRIT1 constitutes a set of proteins whose mutations are found in cerebral cavernous malformations, characterised by cerebral hemorrhages and vascular malformations in the central nervous system. KRIT-1 localization is mediated by its FERM domain [[PMID:22577140]]. This entry represents an ankyrin-repeats domain found in the central region of KRIT1 [[PMID:23317506]].

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7595)
Description: This entry represents a domain of unknown function found in uncharacterised plant sequences. This domain may have a beta-propeller structure.

Name: Nucleoporin pom152-like, ninth immunoglobulin-like domain
Description: This domain is found in Nucleoporin pom152 from Schizosaccharomyces pombe and similar fungal proteins. Pom152 functions as a component of the nuclear pore complex (NPC). This domain is predicted to adopt an immunoglobulin-like structure.

Name: Nuclear anchorage protein 1, spectrin repeat
Description: This entry represents spectrin repeats found in Nuclear anchorage protein 1 from C. elegans, a protein that plays a central role in nuclear and mitochondrial anchoring [[PMID:12169658],[PMID:22298703]]. It may connect nuclei to the cytoskeleton by interacting with unc-84 at the nuclear envelope and with F-actin in the cytoplasm, creating a bridge across the nuclear envelope between the cytoskeleton and the nucleus [[PMID:15773756]].

Name: Dynamin-like GTPase MGM1-like, lipid interacting stalk
Description: This is the lipid-interacting stalk (LIS) domain of Dynamin-like GTPase MGM1, mitochondrial from Saccharomyces cerevisiae and similar fungal sequences. MGM1 is essential for normal mitochondrial morphology by mediating fusion of the mitochondrial inner membranes. This domain has been implicated in lipid binding with several key lysine residues [[PMID:32041880],[PMID:31292547]].

Name: Defensin-like
Description: This entry includes defensin-like proteins from plants which may adopt a scorpion toxin-like fold.

Name: Ints3-like, C-terminal HEAT repeats
Description: This is the C-terminal domain of human Integrator complex subunit 3 (Ints3) and similar animal proteins. Ints3 is a component of the Integrator (INT) complex, which is involved in the small nuclear RNAs (snRNA) U1 and U2 transcription and in their 3'-box-dependent processing. This domain forms a dimer, which binds single-stranded RNA/DNA [[PMID:33434574],[PMID:34400606]].

Name: ANKLE2 third alpha/beta domain
Description: This domain is found in the human Ankyrin repeat and LEM domain-containing protein 2 (ANKLE2) and related proteins. Human ANKLE2 is involved in mitotic nuclear envelope reassembly by promoting dephosphorylation of BAF/BANF1 during mitotic exit [[PMID:22770216]]. The domain represented by this entry is predicted to adopt globular alpha/beta structure consisting of a five-stranded antiparallel beta-sheet and helices packed on one side of it.

Name: Cohesin subunit SCC3/SA, HEAT-repeats domain
Description: This entry includes subunits of the cohesin complex - a protein complex required for sister chromatid cohesion in eukaryotes, including SA-1, SA-2 and SA-3 from vertebrates (STAG1-3), the orthologue from S.pombe Psc3 and the homologue from Arabidopsis, SCC3 [[PMID:10698974],[PMID:25171859],[PMID:16043696],[PMID:31682730]]. This entry represents a central region of these proteins containing HEAT repeats, which may be involved in binding Scc1 subunit and nucleic acids. In STAG3, mutations in this region cause male infertility due to meiotic arrest [[PMID:31682730]].

Name: Dynein axonemal assembly factor 5, HEAT-repeat domain
Description: This entry represents a domain found in the central region of Dynein axonemal assembly factor 5 (DAAF5) from animals, which consists of HEAT repeats. DAAF5 is a cytoplasmic protein involved in the delivery of the dynein machinery to the motile cilium. It is required for the assembly of the axonemal dynein inner and outer arms, two structures attached to the peripheral outer doublet A microtubule of the axoneme, that play a crucial role in cilium motility [[PMID:25232951],[PMID:23040496]].

Name: Surface lipoprotein assembly modifier, N-terminal TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide repeats (TPR) found at the N-terminal of Surface lipoprotein assembly modifier from the proteobacteria Pasteurella multocida (Slam) and similar sequences. Slam plays a role in the export to the cell surface of some cell outer membrane lipoproteins [[PMID:28620585]].

Name: DOP1 middle TPR domain
Description: DOP1 is the founding member of the Dopey family and is required for correct cell morphology and spatiotemporal organisation of multicellular structures in the filamentous fungus Emericella nidulans (Aspergillus nidulans) [[PMID:10931277]]. Dop1 homologues are found in mammals, where they may play a role in regulating membrane trafficking of cargo proteins. Together with ATP9A and MON2, it regulates SNX3 retromer-mediated endosomal sorting of WLS away from lysosomal degradation [[PMID:30213940]]. In S. cerevisiae, it is required for normal cellular morphogenesis [[PMID:10931277]]. Loss of Dop1 activity affects endosome to Golgi transport and causes a severe perturbation of the organization of the endoplasmic reticulum; Mon2 a relative of large Arf exchange factors, recruits Dop1 to the Golgi apparatus [[PMID:16301316]]. These are long proteins consisting of TPRs; this entry represents a domain found at the middle region of DOP1.

Name: DOP1 C-terminal
Description: DOP1 is the founding member of the Dopey family and is required for correct cell morphology and spatiotemporal organisation of multicellular structures in the filamentous fungus Emericella nidulans (Aspergillus nidulans) [[PMID:10931277]]. In S. cerevisiae, it is required for normal cellular morphogenesis [[PMID:10931277]]. Loss of Dop1 activity affects endosome to Golgi transport and causes a severe perturbation of the organization of the endoplasmic reticulum; Mon2 a relative of large Arf exchange factors, recruits Dop1 to the Golgi apparatus [[PMID:16301316]]. Dop1 homologues are found in mammals, where they may play a role in regulating membrane trafficking of cargo proteins. Together with ATP9A and MON2, it regulates SNX3 retromer-mediated endosomal sorting of WLS away from lysosomal degradation [[PMID:16301316]]. This entry represents the C-terminal domain of DOP1, which consists of TPRs.

Name: DOP1 TPR domain
Description: DOP1 is the founding member of the Dopey family and is required for correct cell morphology and spatiotemporal organisation of multicellular structures in the filamentous fungus Emericella nidulans (Aspergillus nidulans) [[PMID:10931277]]. Dop1 homologues are found in mammals, where they may play a role in regulating membrane trafficking of cargo proteins. Together with ATP9A and MON2, it regulates SNX3 retromer-mediated endosomal sorting of WLS away from lysosomal degradation [[PMID:30213940]]. In S. cerevisiae, it is required for normal cellular morphogenesis [[PMID:10931277]]. Loss of Dop1 activity affects endosome to Golgi transport and causes a severe perturbation of the organization of the endoplasmic reticulum; Mon2 a relative of large Arf exchange factors, recruits Dop1 to the Golgi apparatus [[PMID:16301316]]. These are long proteins consisting of TPRs; this entry represents a domain found near the near the C-terminal of DOP1.

Name: Tetratricopeptide repeat
Description: This region of tetratricopeptide-like (TPR) repeats is found in a group of uncharacterised fungal proteins.

Name: CEMIP beta-helix
Description: This entry represents the beta-helix domain of CEMIP, CEMIP2 and related sequences [[PMID:37234743]]. Proteins in this entry proteins are involved in the metabolism of hyaluronic acid (HA) and mediate the depolymerization of high-molecular-weight HA into intermediate-sized fragments [[PMID:37527776]], a process crucial for various physiological functions, including the regulation of angiogenesis, heart development, and cell migration [[PMID:33647313],[PMID:34624311]]. This domain has the common structural motif of enzymes involved in polysaccharide degradation, encompassing both hydrolases and lyases, however, biochemical experiments failed to detect any hyaluronidase activity in CEMIP2 and the putative active site remains to be elucidated [[PMID:37234743]]. This entry also includes Fibrocystin (PKHD), a protein that promotes ciliogenesis within renal epithelial cells, contributing to the formation of kidney tubules and maintaining the lumen architecture [[PMID:18235088],[PMID:20875407]]. It may participate in the regulation of the cholangiocytes proliferation and the CCN2 production in an CXCL8-dependent manner [[PMID:30898581]].

Name: Nuclear anchorage protein 1, spectrin-like repeat
Description: This entry represents spectrin-like repeats found in Nuclear anchorage protein 1 from Caenorhabditis elegans, which plays a central role in nuclear and mitochondrial anchoring [[PMID:22298703]].

Name: Nuclear anchorage protein 1, spectrin repeat
Description: This entry represents spectrin-like repeats found in Nuclear anchorage protein 1 from Caenorhabditis elegans, which plays a central role in nuclear and mitochondrial anchoring [[PMID:22298703]].

Name: Listerin HEAT repeat region
Description: This entry represents a putative HEAT repeat from the LTN1 family of proteins.  The LTN1 family consists of E3 ubiquitin-protein ligases that are integral components of the ribosome quality control complex (RQC) [[PMID:23685075],[PMID:25132172],[PMID:28757607]]. The RQC is a ribosome-associated complex responsible for the ubiquitination and subsequent extraction of incompletely synthesized nascent polypeptides for proteasomal degradation. Members of the LTN1 family are recruited to stalled 60S ribosomal subunits, often by NEMF, to mediate the ubiquitination of stalled nascent chains. This ubiquitination event is crucial for the recruitment of VCP/p97 or CDC48, which facilitates the extraction and degradation of the incomplete translation products. In some species, LTN1 proteins also ubiquitinate proteins derived from mRNAs lacking stop codons or translation arrest products induced by specific sequences. Additionally, they may play a role in chromatin function and transcription regulation indirectly.

Name: Repeat of unknown function (DUF7627)
Description: This entry represents a domain found in the uncharacterised protein B0303.14 from C.elegans and related sequences from nematodes.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7635)
Description: This is a presumed domain found in uncharacterised sequences from plasmodium falciparum (SwissProt:Q8IBR8) and similar sequences from Apicomplexa. It may adopt a beta-propeller-like fold, which, from structure predictions, it seems incomplete.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7640)
Description: This is a presumed domain found in a group of uncharacterised sequences from diatoms. This domain may adopt a beta-solenoid fold and in some members is repeated twice or three times.

Name: Dynein regulatory complex subunit 7 MORN domain
Description: Dynein regulatory complex subunit 7 (DRC7) is a component of the nexin-dynein regulatory complex (N-DRC), a key regulator of ciliary/flagellar motility which maintains the alignment and integrity of the distal axoneme and regulates microtubule sliding in motile axonemes [[PMID:37258679]]. In humans, defective ciliary motility can lead to male infertility and a congenital disorder called primary ciliary dyskinesia (PCD), in which impaired clearance of mucus by the cilia causes chronic respiratory infections [[PMID:37258679]]. This entry represents the MORN-repeats domain, found in the central region of human DRC7.

Name: KLHDC2/KLHL20/DRC7 Kelch-repeats domain
Description: This entry represents a Kelch-repeats-like domain found in a diverse group of proteins, including KLHDC2, Kelch-like protein 20 and FBX42 from human, which are the substrate-recognition component of E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase complexes. KLHDC2 is the substrate-recognition component of Cul2-RING (CRL2) E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase complex of the DesCEND (destruction via C-end degrons) pathway, which recognises a C-degron located at the extreme C-terminus of target proteins, leading to their ubiquitination and degradation [[PMID:29775578],[PMID:30526872],[PMID:38177675]]. KLHL20 and its orthologue from Drosophila diablo, are the substrate-specific adapter of a BCR (BTB-CUL3-RBX1) E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase complex, involved in interferon response and anterograde Golgi to endosome transport [[PMID:31279627]]. This domain can be found in DRC7 from C. reinhardtii and related sequences [[PMID:37258679]].

Name: Virginiamycin B lyase family
Description: Virginiamycin B lyase enzymes inactivate type B streptogramin antibiotics by linearizing the lactone ring at the ester linkage. This reaction generates a free phenylglycine carboxylate and converts the threonyl moiety into 2-amino-butenoic acid. These enzymes play a crucial role in antibiotic resistance by neutralizing the antibacterial activity of streptogramins.

Name: TreTu toxin, C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of TreTu from Salmonella enterica and related proteins. TreTu is a part of the TreTu/TriTu toxin/antitoxin system and is involved in the ADP-ribosylation of the EF-Tu GTP-bound form. The C-terminal domain of TreTu adopts a typical ADP-ribosyltransferase fold. The TreTu activity is neutralised by TriTu immunity protein which acts like a lid that closes the TreTu catalytic site and traps the NAD+ [[PMID:36484105]].

Name: TORTIFOLIA1 C-terminal
Description: This entry includes a group of plant microtubule-associated proteins, including TORTIFOLIA1/SPIRAL2 and SPIRAL2-like (SP2L) from Arabidopsis. They regulate the orientation of cortical microtubules and the direction of organ growth [[PMID:18577573]]. TORTIFOLIA1-like protein 1 (also known as SPIRAL2) may regulate katanin-based microtubule (MT) severing [[PMID:24055158]]. During virus infection, it may stabilize MT crossovers to support the formation and intercellular spread of the viral RNA complexes [[PMID:25133612]]. This entry represents a domain found at the C-terminal of TORTIFOLIA1/SPIRAL2 and related proteins. This domain may consist of TPRs.

Name: TORTIFOLIA1 N-terminal
Description: This entry includes a group of plant microtubule-associated proteins, including TORTIFOLIA1/SPIRAL2 and SPIRAL2-like (SP2L) from Arabidopsis. They regulate the orientation of cortical microtubules and the direction of organ growth [[PMID:18577573]]. TORTIFOLIA1-like protein 1 (also known as SPIRAL2) may regulate katanin-based microtubule (MT) severing [[PMID:24055158]]. During virus infection, it may stabilize MT crossovers to support the formation and intercellular spread of the viral RNA complexes [[PMID:25133612]]. This entry represents a domain found at the N-terminal of TORTIFOLIA1 and related sequences such as SINE1/2, which function during stomatal dynamics induced by light-dark transitions and abscisic acid (ABA), that requires F-actin reorganisation [[PMID:37819623]].

Name: CUL7/CUL9 ARM repeat domain
Description: This domain is found in human Cillin-7 (CUL7), Cullin-9 (CUL9) and related proteins. CUL7 is a core component of the 3M and Cul7-RING(FBXW8) complexes, which mediates the ubiquitination of target proteins [[PMID:24793695]]. CUL9, a core component of the Cul9-RING ubiquitin-protein ligase complex, acts downstream of the 3M complex, which inhibits the CUL9 activity, leading to prevent ubiquitination of BIRC5 [[PMID:24793696]]. This domain is composed of ARM repeats.

Name: Galaxin-like repeats
Description: These entry represents a repetitive region found in galaxins and related proteins. These proteins share common structural features such as the presence of repetitive motifs containing di-cysteine residues. These pairs of cysteines are predicted to reside on beta-hairpins and to form a disulfide bonds each with the pair located downstream and upstream of them, forming a continuum. This probably is essential for the function of galaxins which have a role in the formation of calcium carbonate skeletons in marine organisms, such as corals [[PMID:19638240]].

Name: F-box protein At3g26010-like, beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal beta propeller domain of F-box protein At3g26010 from Arabidopsis thaliana and similar plant proteins. Members of this family contain an F-box domain ([pfam:PF00646]).

Name: FBD-associated F-box protein At5g56370, LRR repeats
Description: This entry represents the leucine-rich repeats (LRR) found in FBD-associated F-box protein At5g56370 from Arabidopsis thaliana and similar plant proteins.

Name: IF140 C-terminal TPR domain
Description: IFT140 is one of the core components (together with WDR19/IFT144 and IFT122) of the IFT-A complex which is required for retrograde ciliary transport and entry into cilia of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs), being involved in cilia function and/or assembly [[PMID:36462505],[PMID:20889716]]. IFT-A polymerises interacts with IFT-B, and uses an array of beta-propeller and TPR domains to create 'carriages' of the IFT train that engage TULP adaptor proteins [[PMID:20889716]]. The beta-propeller domains of IFT140 contact the beta-propeller domains of IFT121 from the adjacent complex [[PMID:36462505]]. This entry represents the TPR domain found at the C-terminal of IFT140.

Name: IF140/IFT172 TPR domain
Description: IFT140 is one of the core components (together with WDR19/IFT144 and IFT122) of the IFT-A complex which is required for retrograde ciliary transport and entry into cilia of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs), being involved in cilia function and/or assembly [[PMID:36462505],[PMID:20889716]]. IFT-A polymerises interacts with IFT-B, and uses an array of beta-propeller and TPR domains to create 'carriages' of the IFT train that engage TULP adaptor proteins [[PMID:20889716]]. The beta-propeller domains of IFT140 contact the beta-propeller domains of IFT121 from the adjacent complex [[PMID:36462505]]. IFT172 is a component of the IFT-B complex which interacts with IFT-A complex. This entry represents the TPR region of IFT140/IFT172 and similar proteins. This domain of IFT140 forms an interface with IFT144 [[PMID:36462505]].

Name: CGL160, C-terminal
Description: This domain is found in Protein CONSERVED ONLY IN THE GREEN LINEAGE 160 from Arabidopsis thaliana (CGL160) and similar proteins from plants and cyanobacteria. CGL160 facilitates the assembly of the membrane proton channel of the chloroplastic F-type ATPase [[PMID:24664203]].

Name: ARMC5
Description: This entry represents the ARM-repeats domain of ARMC5 from animal, a protein involved in fetal development, T-cell function and adrenal gland growth homeostasis. It negatively regulates adrenal cells survival. It plays a role in steroidogenesis, modulates steroidogenic enzymes expression and cortisol production [[PMID:24283224],[PMID:28676429]].

Name: MABP1/WDR62 first WD40 domain
Description: This entry represents the second WD40 domain of MABP1 and WDR62. MABP1 (Mitogen-activated protein kinase-binding protein 1) is a negative regulator of NOD2 function, leading to the down-regulation of NOD2-induced processes such as activation of NF-kappa-B signalling, IL8 secretion and antibacterial response [[PMID:22700971]]. WDR62 is a microcephaly-related, microtubule (MT)-associated protein (MAP) that localises to the spindle pole and regulates spindle organisation by recruiting katanin to the spindle pole by forming a complex with TPX2/Aurora A [[PMID:34137789],[PMID:34137788]]. The microtubule-binding affinity of WDR62 is autoinhibited through JNK phosphorylation-induced intramolecular interaction [[PMID:34137788]]. Additionally, WRD62 is implicated in the development of the cerebral cortex influencing both the proliferation and migration of neurons [[PMID:20890278],[PMID:26297806]]. The WD40 domains of WDR62 mediate the interaction with Aurora A and MT-binding [[PMID:34137789]].

Name: MABP1/WDR62 second WD40 domain
Description: This entry represents the second WD40 domain of MABP1 and WDR62. MABP1 (Mitogen-activated protein kinase-binding protein 1) is a negative regulator of NOD2 function, leading to the down-regulation of NOD2-induced processes such as activation of NF-kappa-B signalling, IL8 secretion and antibacterial response [[PMID:22700971]]. WDR62 is a microcephaly-related, microtubule (MT)-associated protein (MAP) that localises to the spindle pole and regulates spindle organisation by recruiting katanin to the spindle pole by forming a complex with TPX2/Aurora A [[PMID:34137789],[PMID:20890278]]. The microtubule-binding (MT) affinity of WDR62 is autoinhibited through JNK phosphorylation-induced intramolecular interaction [[PMID:20890278]]. Additionally, WRD62 is implicated in the development of the cerebral cortex influencing both the proliferation and migration of neurons [[PMID:26297806],[PMID:20729831]]. The WD40 domains of WDR62 mediate the interaction with Aurora A and (MT binding) [[PMID:34137789]].

Name: FANCA arcN subdomain
Description: Fanconi anemia (FA) is a human disorder characterised by cancer susceptibility and cellular sensitivity to DNA crosslinks and other damages. The FA complex repairs the interstrand cross-linking (ICL) lesions and coordinates activities of the downstream DNA repair pathway including nucleotide excision repair, translesion synthesis, and homologous recombination. The FA core complex consists of FANCA, FANCB, FANCC, FANCE, FANCF, FANCG, FANCL, FANCM, FANCT (UBET2), FAAP100 and FAAP24 [[PMID:32002546],[PMID:29017571]]. This entry represents the N-terminal half of the arc-shaped C-terminal domain of FANCA (arcN) which interacts with the C-terminal half of a second FANCA molecule which interact in a head-to-tail manner to form a pseudo-symmetric dimeric interface [[PMID:32002546]]. The C-terminal domain of FANCA (arcN and arcC halves) consists of HEAT repeats [[PMID:32002546],[PMID:33686268]].

Name: Glucosamine inositolphosphorylceramide transferase 1, N-terminal
Description: This is the N-terminal domain of Glucosamine inositolphosphorylceramide transferase 1 from Arabidopsis thaliana (GINT1), a member of the Glycosyltransferase Family 64 that is involved in glycosylinositolphosphorylceramide and sphingolipid glycosylation. This domain is predicted to adopt a five-bladed beta-propeller fold.

Name: WDR55
Description: This entry includes WDR55 proteins and its homologues. WDR55 are nucleolar proteins that modulate rRNA synthesis and play a central role in organogenesis [[PMID:18769712]]. They are implicated in the development of the thymus and other organs.

Name: WDR35/TULP4 N-terminal
Description: This entry represents a beta-propeller domain found at N-terminal of WDR35 (in which corresponds to the first one) and TULP4 proteins. TULP4 may be a substrate-recognition component of a SCF-like ECS (Elongin-Cullin-SOCS-box protein) E3 ubiquitin ligase complex. WDR35 (also known as IFT121) is a component of the IFT-A complex, which is required for retrograde ciliary transport and entry into cilia of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs), being involved in cilia function and/or assembly [[PMID:36462505],[PMID:29220510]]. IFT-A polymerises interacts with IFT-B, and uses an array of beta-propeller and TPR domains to create 'carriages' of the IFT train that engage TULP adaptor proteins [[PMID:29220510]]. Proteins in this entry are part of the non-core subcomplex, together with IFT43 and IFT139 [[PMID:36462505],[PMID:29220510]]. This entry represents the second beta-propeller of WDR35. Knock-out WDR35 cells showed defective retrograde trafficking within cilia [[PMID:29220510]].

Name: EIF3I
Description: This entry includes EIF3I, a component of the eukaryotic translation initiation factor 3 (eIF-3) complex, which is required for several steps in the initiation of protein synthesis [[PMID:27462815],[PMID:17581632]]. The eIF-3 complex specifically targets and initiates translation of a subset of mRNAs involved in cell proliferation including cell cycling, differentiation and apoptosis, and uses different modes of RNA stem-loop binding to exert either translational activation or repression [[PMID:25849773]].

Name: CDC20/Fizzy WD40 domain
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller found in WD40 protein Cdc20/Fizzy, a protein that associates and phosphorylates anaphase promoting complex/cyclosome (APC/C) at the end of mitosis [[PMID:27030811],[PMID:26083744]]. It is required for full ubiquitin ligase activity of the APC/C and confers substrate specificity upon the complex [[PMID:27030811]].

Name: Prp19 WD40 domain
Description: This entry represents the WD40 domain of Prp19 from animals [[PMID:28962858]], which consists of a regular beta-propeller architecture. Prp19 is essential for spliceosome assembly and stability, a core component of the NCT complex, part of the spliceosome. Prp19 self-associates through this domain and a central coiled-coil which is key for the foundation of NTC complex assembly [[PMID:28962858]].

Name: WDHD1 first WD40 domain
Description: This is the WD40 domain found at the N-terminal of WDHD1 (also known as AND-1) [[PMID:28381552],[PMID:34694004]]. WDHD1 is an adaptor molecule crucial for DNA replication [[PMID:28381552],[PMID:34694004]]. It is a core replisome component that acts as a replication initiation factor. It binds directly to the CMG complex and functions as a hub to recruit additional proteins to the replication fork [[PMID:28381552],[PMID:35585232]].

Name: Nephrocystin 3, N-terminal
Description: This entry represents a P-loop like domain found at the N-terminal of nephrocystin-3, a protein required for normal ciliary development and function. It inhibits disheveled-1-induced canonical Wnt-signaling activity and may also play a role in the control of non-canonical Wnt signaling which regulates planar cell polarity [[PMID:18371931]]. This entry represents the N-terminal domain of nephrocystin-3 (NPHP3) which belongs to NACHT NTPases clade defined by a residue (glycine, alanine or serine) directly C-terminal  of the Mg2+-coordinating aspartate, and two additional acidic residues one position downstream, with a consensus sequence hhhhD[GAS]hDE [[PMID:15381417]]. This domain is also found in Ankyrin repeat domain-containing protein 50 from human.

Name: Nephrocystin-3 TPR domain
Description: This entry represents the TPR domain found at the C-terminal of nephrocystin-3 (NPHP3), a protein required for normal ciliary development and function. It inhibits disheveled-1-induced canonical Wnt-signaling activity and may also play a role in the control of non-canonical Wnt signaling which regulates planar cell polarity [[PMID:18371931]].

Name: U3 small nucleolar RNA-associated protein 6 C-terminal
Description: U3 small nucleolar RNA-associated protein 6 (UTP6) is a component of the small subunit (SSU) processome containing the U3 snoRNA that is involved in processing of pre-18S rRNA [[PMID:12068309]]. These proteins consist of TPRs; this entry represents the C-terminal region of UTP6 from animals.

Name: GlmU-like, C-terminal hexapeptide
Description: This domain is found at the C-terminal end of Bifunctional protein GlmU from Streptococcus pneumoniae and similar sequences. This domain is responsible for the transfer of acetyl group from acetyl coenzyme A to glucosamine-1-phosphate (GlcN-1-P) to produce N-acetylglucosamine-1-phosphate (GlcNAc-1-P). This domain consists of hexapeptide repeats that form a left-handed beta-helix [[PMID:11118459]].

Name: TNRC18-like,(4.1.1) SH3
Description: This domain is found immediately N-terminal to the Tudor domain ([pfam:PF21744]) in human Trinucleotide repeat-containing gene 18 protein (TNRC18), a chromatin regulator that recognises histone H3 lysine 9 to mediate the silencing of endogenous retroviruses class I [[PMID:37938770]].

Name: SESTD1-like, spectrin repeat
Description: This region of spectrin repeats is found in human SEC14 domain and spectrin repeat-containing protein 1 (SESTD1) and similar animal proteins. SESTD1 is a regulator of the transient receptor potential channels TRPC4 and TRPC5.

Name: Fungal GCN1-like, HEAT repeats
Description: This region of HEAT repeats is found in eIF-2-alpha kinase activator GCN1 from Saccharomyces cerevisiae and similar fungal sequences. GCN1 is a ribosome collision sensor that activates a translation quality control pathway when a ribosome has stalled during translation [[PMID:33790014]].

Name: Bridge-like lipid transfer protein family member 3A/B
Description: This entry represents the human Bridge-like lipid transfer protein family member 3A and 3B and related proteins. These proteins are tube-forming lipid transport protein which mediate the transfer of lipids between membranes at organelle contact sites [[PMID:35499567]]. They are required for retrograde traffic of vesicle clusters in the early endocytic pathway to the Golgi complex [[PMID:20163565]].

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7758)
Description: This domain is found in uncharacterised proteins mainly from nematodes. It is composed of repetitive alpha-helical hairpins and has detectable similarity to 14-3-3 proteins.

Name: Mechanosensitive ion channel protein 2/3 C-terminal
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of Mechanosensitive ion channel proteins 2 and 3 from Arabidopsis and related plant sequences. These channels open in response to stretch forces in the membrane lipid bilayer and control plastid size, shape, and perhaps division during normal plant development by altering ion flux in response to changes in membrane tension [[PMID:16401419]]. They act as a component of the chloroplast division machinery [[PMID:21810996]]. These channels are closely related to MscS from bacteria.

Name: FHOD1/3 FH3 domain
Description: This is the FH3 domain of formin proteins, such as FH1/FH2 domain-containing proteins 1 and 3 from human [[PMID:18786395],[PMID:33472039]]. FHOD1 is required for the assembly of F-actin structures, such as stress fibers and depends on the Rho-ROCK cascade for its activity. It contributes to the coordination of microtubules with actin fibers and plays a role in elongation [[PMID:15878344]]. This domain consists of armadillo repeats and mediates the interactions with spectrin repeat 12 (SR12) from Nesprin [[PMID:33472039]].

Name: TR130 NTS domain
Description: This domain is found in the yeast Trafficking protein particle complex II-specific subunit 130 (TR130) and related proteins. TRS130 plays a role in the YPT GEF activity of TRAPP II in concert with the two other TRAPP II-specific subunits TRS65 and TRS120. This protein contains an N-terminal alpha-solenoid domain, known as NTS domain, followed by four Ig-like domains arranged in tandem [[PMID:35080977]]. This entry represents the NTS domain.

Name: Leucine-rich repeat domain
Description: This domain is found in uncharacterised fungal proteins. It is composed of leucine-rich repeats.

Name: RUNKEL ARM-repeat domain
Description: This is the C-terminal domain of Serine/threonine-protein kinase RUNKEL from Arabidopsis thaliana and related plant sequences. It consists of ARM repeats. RUNKEL is an essential protein that regulates phragmoplast microtubule organization during cell plate expansion in cytokinesis during cell division, both somatic and syncytial [[PMID:19268593],[PMID:23451828]].

Name: Attractin/LZTR1 beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller domain found in attracting (ATRN), LZTR1, among others. ATRN is involved in the initial immune cell clustering during inflammatory response and may regulate chemotactic activity of chemokines. It may play a role in melanocortin signaling pathways that regulate energy homeostasis and hair color [[PMID:9736737]]. It was thought to have dipeptidase activity but it was shown later to lack that activity [[PMID:17261078]]. LZTR1 is a substrate-specific adapter of a BCR (BTB-CUL3-RBX1) E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase complex that mediates ubiquitination of Ras (K-Ras/KRAS, N-Ras/NRAS and H-Ras/HRAS). This domain can be found in a number of other proteins, such as KLHDC3, a substrate-recognition component of a Cul2-RING (CRL2) E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase complex of the DesCEND (destruction via C-end degrons) pathway.

Name: Elongation factor 3/GNC1 HEAT repeat
Description: This entry represents a block of three HEAT repeats found at the N-terminal of elongation factor 3 (EF3) from yeast, after the 5 HEAT repeats block [[PMID:16929303]]. EF3 is a ribosome-dependent ATPase that functions in cytoplasmic translation elongation. It is required for the ATP-dependent release of deacylated tRNA from the ribosomal E-site during protein biosynthesis [[PMID:16929303],[PMID:29300771]]. This block is also found in stalled ribosome sensor GCN1, a ribosome collision sensor that plays a key role in the RNF14-RNF25 translation quality control pathway, a pathway that takes place when a ribosome has stalled during translation, and which promotes ubiquitination and degradation of translation factors on stalled ribosomes [[PMID:32610081]].

Name: EF3 N-terminal HEAT repeat
Description: This entry represents the first five HEAT repeats found at the N-terminal of elongation factor 3 (EF3) from yeast, which is followed by a block of three HEAT repeats ([pfam:PF24984]) [[PMID:16929303]]. EF3 is a ribosome-dependent ATPase that functions in cytoplasmic translation elongation. It is required for the ATP-dependent release of deacylated tRNA from the ribosomal E-site during protein biosynthesis [[PMID:16929303],[PMID:29300771]]. This block is also found twice in the central region of the stalled ribosome sensor GCN1, a ribosome collision sensor that plays a key role in the RNF14-RNF25 translation quality control pathway, a pathway that takes place when a ribosome has stalled during translation, and which promotes ubiquitination and degradation of translation factors on stalled ribosomes. This entry also includes NEW1 from S. cerevisiae, a translation termination and ribosome biogenesis factor. It is an ATP-binding cassette protein that cosediments with polysomes and is required for biogenesis of the small ribosomal subunit [[PMID:31299085]].

Name: Stalled ribosome sensor GCN1 N-terminal
Description: This entry represents a set of four HEAT repeats found towards the N-terminal of the stalled ribosome sensor GCN1, a ribosome collision sensor that plays a key role in the RNF14-RNF25 translation quality control pathway, a pathway that takes place when a ribosome has stalled during translation, and which promotes ubiquitination and degradation of translation factors on stalled ribosomes [[PMID:32610081]]. This entry also includes ILA from Arabidopsis, a protein involved in immunity against bacterial infection and in non-host resistance [[PMID:20360018]].

Name: N-acetylneuraminate epimerase
Description: This entry includes N-acetylneuraminate epimerase (NANM) from Haemophilus influenzae, E. coli and its homologues. It converts alpha-N-acetylneuranimic acid (Neu5Ac) to the beta-anomer, accelerating the equilibrium between the alpha- and beta-anomers [[PMID:18063573],[PMID:33895133]].

Name: Dynein axonemal heavy chain 2/5/8 coiled-coil
Description: This entry represents a coiled-coil region found in Dynein axonemal heavy chains 2/5/8 which is similar to spectrin repeats. These proteins are involved in sperm motility. DYH2 is part of the axonemal inner dynein arm complex plays a central role in ciliary beat [[PMID:30811583]].

Name: UBP24/USP9X/USP9Y ARM repeat
Description: This entry represents an ARM-repeats domain found in ubiquitin carboxyl-terminal hydrolases (UCHs), such as UBP24, UBP34, USP9X and USP9Y and the orthologues from Drosophila. UCHs are thiol proteases that recognise and hydrolyse the peptide bond at the C-terminal glycine of ubiquitin [[PMID:23845989]]. UBP24 regulates cell survival in various contexts through modulating the protein stability of some of its substrates including DDB2, MCL1 or TP53 [[PMID:23159851]]. USP9X and USP9Y are involved in deubiquitination of target proteins [[PMID:12895410]].

Name: Zer-1-like, Leucine-rich repeats
Description: This region of leucine-rich repeats (LRR) is found in Zyg eleven-related protein 1 from Caenorhabditis elegans (Zer-1) and similar eukaryotic proteins. Zer-1 functions as a target recruitment subunit in the E3 ubiquitin ligase complex zer-1-cul-2-elc-1.

Name: Importin-9 central HEAT repeats
Description: Importin-9 is a nuclear transport receptor that mediates nuclear import of proteins, such as histones, proteasome and actin [[PMID:30855230],[PMID:34711951]]. It consists of 20 tandem HEAT repeats [[PMID:30855230],[PMID:34711951]]. This entry represents a central segment of HEAT repeats which includes repeats 7-8 that are part of interface 2 that interacts with a five-residue segment of the H2B N-terminal tail described in [[PMID:34711951]].

Name: R13L1/DRL21 LRRs
Description: This entry represents Leucine-rich repeats (LRRs) found in R13L1, DRL21 from Arabidopsis thaliana and related plant proteins. R13L1 and DRL21 are likely disease resistance proteins. DRL21 has adenylate cyclase activity [[PMID:31850369]].

Name: Teneurin NHL domain
Description: Teneurins are ancient cell-cell adhesion receptors that are vital for brain development and synapse organisation [[PMID:29540701]]. This entry represents the NHL six-bladed beta-propeller domain which packs against the TTR, FN and shell domains with the bottom face and it is held in place by the FN domain which acts as a wedge to position the NHL at an angle perpendicular to the YD-shell domain [[PMID:29540701]]. The top face of the NHL propeller contains extended loops, stabilised by five disulphide bonds, which are highly conserved among vertebrates members [[PMID:29540701]].

Name: Teneurin YD-shell
Description: Teneurins are ancient cell-cell adhesion receptors that are vital for brain development and synapse organisation [[PMID:29540701]]. This entry represents the YD-shell domain which forms a hollow tube, consisting of a single up-and-down spiralling beta-sheet with 78 strands [[PMID:29540701]] and shows similarity to structures of bacterial toxin complexes TcB-TcC. This domain is the most most structurally conserved region of teneurins and the TcB-TcC toxin complexes [[PMID:29540701]]. This big domain comprises YD or rearrangement hotspot (Rhs) repeats.

Name: Elongation factor Ts, mitochondrial, N-terminal
Description: This domain is found at the N-terminal end of the mitochondrial Elongation factor Ts from Bos taurus (EF-Ts), which associates with the EF-Tu. GDP complex and induces the exchange of GDP to GTP. This domain consists of helical segments [[PMID:15557323]].

Name: Serine/threonine-protein kinase ATR, M-HEAT region
Description: This region of HEAT repeats is found in human Serine/threonine -protein kinase ATR and similar eukaryotic proteins. ATR acts as a DNA damage sensor and is associated with severe disorders such as Seckel syndrome 1. This protein is organised into a large N-terminal alpha solenoid containing HEAT repeats, a FAT (FRAP, ATM, TRRAP) domain ([pfam:PF02259]), a kinase domain ([pfam:PF00454]), and a C-terminal short segment referred to as FATC ([pfam:PF02260]). The N-terminal part has two continuous HEAT repeat regions, the N-HEAT and the M-HEAT [[PMID:29271416]]. This entry represents the M-HEAT region.

Name: Serine/threonine-protein kinase ATR, N-HEAT region
Description: This region of HEAT repeats is found in human Serine/threonine -protein kinase ATR and similar proteins from vertebrates. ATR acts as a DNA damage sensor and is associated with severe disorders such as Seckel syndrome 1. This protein is organised into a large N-terminal alpha solenoid containing HEAT repeats, a FAT (FRAP, ATM, TRRAP) domain ([pfam:PF02259]), a kinase domain ([pfam:PF00454]), and a C-terminal short segment referred to as FATC ([pfam:PF02260]). N-terminal part has two continuous HEAT repeat regions, the N-HEAT and the M-HEAT [[PMID:29271416]]. This entry represents the N-HEAT region.

Name: VPS13-like family middle region
Description: VPS13 proteins have been implicated in processes including vesicle fusion, autophagy, and actin regulation. They bind phospholipids and act as channels that mediate the transfer of lipids between membranes at organelle contact sites [[PMID:34830155],[PMID:30741634],[PMID:34216812],[PMID:30018089]]. It has been proposed that members of this entry have the capacity to bind and likely transfer tens of glycerolipids at once. Yeast VPS13 acts at multiple cellular sites, namely the interface between mitochondria and the vacuole, on endosomes, on the nuclear-vacuole junction and the vacuole, depending on  the carbon source and metabolic state. VPS13A, VPS13C and VPS13D from mammals localise at contacts at contacts between the ER and other organelles, i.e. VPS13A and VPS13D bridge the ER to mitochondria, VPS13C bridges the ER to late endosomes and lysosomes and VPS13B may localise to endosome-endosome contacts [[PMID:30741634],[PMID:34216812],[PMID:30018089]]. Mutations in human VPS13 proteins cause different diseases such as Chorea-acanthocytosis, Cohen syndrome, Parkinson's disease, and spastic ataxia [[PMID:17196930]].

Name: Nesprin-1, spectrin repeats
Description: This entry represents a region of spectrin repeats found in human Nesprin-1 (SYNE1), a multi-isomeric modular protein which forms a linking network between organelles and the actin cytoskeleton to maintain the subcellular spatial organisation. This protein has been associated with several severe disorders.

Name: Vacuolar-sorting associated protein 13, adaptor binding domain
Description: This entry represents the VPS13 adaptor binding (VAB) domain, previously known as SHR-BD, found in vacuolar protein sorting-associated protein 13 (VPS13) [[PMID:30018089]]. Proteins containing this domain are involved in vesicle fusion, autophagy, and actin regulation [[PMID:30018089]] and, through membrane-specific adaptor proteins such as Ypt35, Spo71 and the mitochondrial membrane protein Mcp1, VPS13 proteins are recruited to different membranes. This domain interacts with Ypt35 which recruits VPS13 to endosomal and vacuolar membranes, and with Mcp1 to target VPS13 at mitochondria. Defects in the human protein VPS13A cause chorea-acanthocytosis, an autosomal recessive neurodegenerative disorder [[PMID:30018089],[PMID:11381253]]. In plants, this domain is found to be the region which interacts with SHR or the SHORT-ROOT transcription factor, a regulator of root-growth and asymmetric cell division that separates ground tissue into endodermis and cortex. The plant protein containing the SHR-BD is named SHRUBBY or SHBY UniProt:Q9FT44 [[PMID:23444357]].

Name: Csf1 family C-terminal region
Description: This entry includes Csf1 protein from yeast, which is the orthologue of human BLTP1. Csf1 also known as Cold sensitive for fermentation protein 1 is a fungal membrane protein required for fermentation at low temperature [[PMID:10781556]]. Csf1, similar to BLTP1, is a tube-forming lipid transport protein which provides phosphatidylethanolamine for glycosylphosphatidylinositol (GPI) anchor synthesis in the endoplasmic reticulum [[PMID:35015055]]. These proteins belong to the repeating beta-groove (RBG) superfamily which are conserved lipid transfer proteins containing long hydrophobic grooves consisting of multiple repeating modules made of five beta-sheets followed by a loop [[PMID:35491307]].

Name: BLTP1-like family middle region
Description: This entry represents the middle region of a group of very long eukaryotic proteins, including the human BLTP1 (also known as KIAA1109) protein. These proteins are tube-forming lipid transport proteins that belong to the RBG (repeating beta-groove) superfamily. They consist of long hydrophobic grooves made of multiple repeating modules of five beta-sheets followed by a loop [[PMID:35491307],[PMID:35015055]]. BLTP1 is a tube-forming lipid transport protein which provides phosphatidylethanolamine for glycosylphosphatidylinositol (GPI) anchor synthesis in the endoplasmic reticulum. It plays a role in endosomal trafficking and endosome recycling and it is also involved in the actin cytoskeleton and cilia structural dynamics [[PMID:35491307],[PMID:30906834]]. BLTP1 also acts as a regulator of phagocytosis [[PMID:31540829]]. In humans, it has been associated with rheumatoid arthritis [[PMID:26917059]]. Mutations of the BLTP1 gene has been associated with Alkuraya-Kucinskas syndrome (MIM 617822), a severe disorder of brain development and arthrogryposis [[PMID:29290337]].

Name: BLTP1-like family C-terminal region
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal region of a group of very long eukaryotic proteins, including the human BLTP1 (also known as KIAA1109) protein. These proteins are tube-forming lipid transport proteins that belong to theRBG (repeating beta-groove) superfamily. They consist of long hydrophobic grooves made of multiple repeating modules of five beta-sheets followed by a loop [[PMID:35491307],[PMID:35015055]]. BLTP1 is a tube-forming lipid transport protein which provides phosphatidylethanolamine for glycosylphosphatidylinositol (GPI) anchor synthesis in the endoplasmic reticulum. It plays a role in endosomal trafficking and endosome recycling and it is also involved in the actin cytoskeleton and cilia structural dynamics [[PMID:35491307],[PMID:30906834]]. BLTP1 also acts as a regulator of phagocytosis [[PMID:31540829]]. In humans, it has been associated with rheumatoid arthritis [[PMID:26917059]]. Mutations of the BLTP1 gene has been associated with Alkuraya-Kucinskas syndrome (MIM 617822), a severe disorder of brain development and arthrogryposis [[PMID:29290337]].

Name: TEP-1 second beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the second of three presumed beta-propeller domains found at the C-terminal of Telomerase protein component 1 (TEP1), a component of the telomerase ribonucleoprotein complex that is essential for the replication of chromosome termini [[PMID:19179534]]. It is also a component of the ribonucleoprotein vaults particle, a multi-subunit structure involved in nucleo-cytoplasmic transport [[PMID:11149928]].

Name: TEP-1 C-terminal beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller found at the C-terminal of Telomerase protein component 1 (TEP1), a component of the telomerase ribonucleoprotein complex that is essential for the replication of chromosome termini [[PMID:19179534]]. It is also a component of the ribonucleoprotein vaults particle, a multi-subunit structure involved in nucleo-cytoplasmic transport [[PMID:11149928]].

Name: Defensin-like family
Description: This entry represents defensin-like proteins from Arabidopsis thaliana and related plant proteins.

Name: Tetratricopeptide repeat protein 21 ARM repeat
Description: This entry represents an ARM-repeats domain found in the middle region of Tetratricopeptide repeat protein 21 (TT21) from human and its homologues. TT21 is an intraflagellar transport (IFT)-associated protein required for spermatogenesis. It is involved in sperm flagellar formation and intraflagellar transport [[PMID:30929735]].

Name: Tetratricopeptide repeat protein 21 second ARM domain
Description: This entry represents the second ARM-repeats domain found towards the N-terminal of Tetratricopeptide repeat protein 21 (TT21) from human and its homologues. TT21 is an intraflagellar transport (IFT)-associated protein required for spermatogenesis. It is involved in sperm flagellar formation and intraflagellar transport [[PMID:30929735]].

Name: Tetratricopeptide repeat protein 21 N-terminal ARM repeat
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal segment of ARM repeats of Tetratricopeptide repeat protein 21 (TT21) from human and its homologues. TT21 is an intraflagellar transport (IFT)-associated protein required for spermatogenesis. It is involved in sperm flagellar formation and intraflagellar transport [[PMID:30929735]].

Name: Tetratricopeptide repeat protein 21 C-terminal ARM domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal ARM-repeats domain of Tetratricopeptide repeat protein 21 (TT21) from human and its homologues. TT21 is an intraflagellar transport (IFT)-associated protein required for spermatogenesis. It is involved in sperm flagellar formation and intraflagellar transport [[PMID:30929735]].

Name: TT21 fifth ARM repeats domain
Description: This is the fifth ARM-repeats domain found towards the C-terminal of Tetratricopeptide repeat protein 21 (TT21) from human and its homologues. TT21 is an intraflagellar transport (IFT)-associated protein required for spermatogenesis. It is involved in sperm flagellar formation and intraflagellar transport [[PMID:30929735]].

Name: Med14 RM domain
Description: This entry represents a domain found in the human Mediator of RNA polymerase II transcription subunit 14 (Med14) and related proteins. Med14 is a component of the Mediator complex, a coactivator involved in the regulated transcription of nearly all RNA polymerase II-dependent genes. Mediator functions as a bridge to convey information from gene-specific regulatory proteins to the basal RNA polymerase II transcription machinery. Mediator is recruited to promoters by direct interactions with regulatory proteins and serves as a scaffold for the assembly of a functional preinitiation complex with RNA polymerase II and the general transcription factors [[PMID:16595664]]. The major portion of Med14 exists in the Head-Shoulder module of the Mediator and consists of nine tandem repeats (RM1 to RM9), which are arranged in an atypical right-handed helix [[PMID:33958484]]. This entry represents repeat RM3.

Name: VPS8-like, TPR-like repeats
Description: This region of TPR-like repeats is found towards the C-terminal of Vacuolar protein sorting-associated protein 8 from Saccharomyces cerevisiae (VPS8) and similar eukaryotic proteins. VPS8 is involved in the retention of proteins to the late-Golgi and plays an integral role in the complex vacuolar protein sorting process.

Name: Med14 RM domain
Description: This entry represents a domain found in the human Mediator of RNA polymerase II transcription subunit 14 (Med14) and related proteins. Med14 is a component of the Mediator complex, a coactivator involved in the regulated transcription of nearly all RNA polymerase II-dependent genes. Mediator functions as a bridge to convey information from gene-specific regulatory proteins to the basal RNA polymerase II transcription machinery. Mediator is recruited to promoters by direct interactions with regulatory proteins and serves as a scaffold for the assembly of a functional preinitiation complex with RNA polymerase II and the general transcription factors [[PMID:16595664]]. The major portion of Med14 exists in the Head-Shoulder module of the Mediator and consists of nine tandem repeats (RM1 to RM9), which are arranged in an atypical right-handed helix [[PMID:33958484]]. This entry represents repeat RM5.

Name: Tetratricopeptide repeat protein 21 forth ARM domain
Description: This entry represents the forth ARM-repeats domain found around the middle of Tetratricopeptide repeat protein 21 (TT21) from human and its homologues. TT21 is an intraflagellar transport (IFT)-associated protein required for spermatogenesis. It is involved in sperm flagellar formation and intraflagellar transport [[PMID:30929735]].

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7799)
