Description: This entry represents a presumed domain found towards the N-terminal of a group of uncharacterised sequences from C. elegans and related proteins from nematodes. This domain may consist of spectrin repeats.

Name: EIF2B subunit epsilon LbH domain
Description: This entry represents the central Left-handed parallel beta-Helix (LbH) domain of Translation initiation factor eIF-2B subunit epsilon (EIF2B), a guanine nucleotide-exchange factor that mediates the exchange of GDP (bound to initiation factor eIF2) for GTP, generating active eIF2-GTP complex. EIF2B is a complex multimeric protein consisting of five subunits, alpha, beta, gamma, delta, epsilon [[PMID:25858979],[PMID:31048492]].

Name: GMPPB C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of GMPPB (Mannose-1-phosphate guanylyltransferase catalytic subunit beta), which consists of a left-handed parallel beta-helix composed of tandem repeats of a [LIV]-G-X(4) hexapeptide [[PMID:33986552]]. This protein catalyses the formation of GDP-mannose, an essential precursor of glycan moieties of glycoproteins and glycolipids [[PMID:33986552]]. In S. pombe, it is required for proper septum structure and is involved cell cycle progression through cell-size checkpoint [[PMID:16049679]]. In budding yeasts Mgp1 (also known as Psa1) plays an important part in maintenance of cell-wall integrity [[PMID:11055399]].

Name: Cnot1 ARM repeat
Description: This ARM domain is found toward the C terminus of  human CCR4-NOT transcription complex subunit 1 (CNOT1) and similar sequences from animals. CNOT1 is a scaffolding component of the CCR4-NOT complex which is one of the major cellular mRNA deadenylases and is linked to various cellular processes including bulk mRNA degradation, miRNA-mediated repression, translational repression during translational initiation and general transcription regulation.

Name: Animal spectrin repeats
Description: This region of spectrin repeats is found in a group of uncharacterised proteins from animals.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7812)
Description: This domain is found in uncharacterised plant proteins. It is predicted to fold into an alpha-helical structure with similarity to alpha-solenoid domains.

Name: Separase-like, TPR repeats
Description: This region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats is found in Separase from Arabidopsis thaliana (ESP1) and similar plant proteins. ESP1 is essential for embryo and endosperm development and may play a role in centromeric heterochromatin structure /formation during early meiosis, non-homologous centromere association and radial microtubule system.

Name: AtTam37 zinc finger region
Description: This domain is found in the AtTam37 from Arabidopsis thaliana and related proteins. AtTam37 is an essential component of the chloroplast import motor. It serves as a  molecular chaperone within the AtYcf2-FtsHi complex [[PMID:39197452]]. The domain represented by this entry contains four highly conserved pairs of cysteine residues which are involved in the coordination of two zinc ions.

Name: Separase-like, TPR repeats
Description: This region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats is found in Separase from Arabidopsis thaliana (ESP1) and similar plant proteins. ESP1 is essential for embryo and endosperm development and may play a role in centromeric heterochromatin structure /formation during early meiosis, non-homologous centromere association and radial microtubule system.

Name: AtTam38 family
Description: This entry represents the AtTam38 protein from Arabidopsis thaliana and related plant proteins. AtTam38 is a component of the AtYcf2-FtsHi complex. AtTam38 serves as an anchor of the stromal motor module to the IEM (inner envelope membrane of the chloroplast) [[PMID:39197452]]. It plays a key role in AtYcf2-FtsHi assembly and maintenance of the tilted posture of the motor module [[PMID:39197452]].

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7824)
Description: This is a presumed domain found in the middle region of bacterial sequences, which have a [pfam:PF20103] at the N-terminal.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7825)
Description: This is a presumed domain found at the C-terminal of a group of uncharacterised sequences from bacteria. It may adopt a TPR-like fold. It is found associated with [pfam:PF20103] at the N-terminal and [pfam:PF25148] in the middle.

Name: Trm732, TPR repeats
Description: This region of predicted of tetratricopeptide-like repeats is found in tRNA (32-2'-O)-methyltransferase regulator trm732 from Schizosaccharomyces pombe, which together with methyltransferase trm7, methylates the 2'-O-ribose of nucleotides at position 32 of the anticodon loop of substrate tRNAs [[PMID:31831667]]. This entry is often found associated with [pfam:PF10350].

Name: Trm732, C-terminal TPR repeats
Description: This region of predicted of tetratricopeptide-like repeats is found in tRNA (32-2'-O)-methyltransferase regulator trm732 from Schizosaccharomyces pombe, which together with methyltransferase trm7, methylates the 2'-O-ribose of nucleotides at position 32 of the anticodon loop of substrate tRNAs [[PMID:31831667]]. This entry is often found associated with [pfam:PF10350].

Name: CD59 glycoprotein
Description: This entry represents the human CD59 glycoprotein and related proteins. CD59 is a cell-surface glycoprotein that protects host cells from complement-mediated lysis. It binds to and prevents the normal functioning of the complement proteins C8 and C9 which form part of a membrane penetrating assembly called the membrane attack complex. CD59 folds into a compact domain with a central three-stranded antiparallel beta-sheet and a two-stranded antiparallel beta-sheet located on one side of it. This structure is stabilised by disulfide bonds and has a significant similarity to the snake venom neurotoxins and other members of the uPAR/Ly6/CD59/snake toxin-receptor superfamily [[PMID:7520819]]. CD59 binds the pore-forming beta-hairpins of C8 to form an intermolecular beta-sheet that prevents membrane perforation. While bound to C8, CD59 deflects the cascading C9 beta-hairpins, rerouting their trajectory into the membrane [[PMID:36797260]].

Name: AP-5 complex subunit zeta-1, TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found at the N-terminal end of human AP-5 complex subunit zeta-1 (AP5Z1, also known as SPG48), which is part of AP-5, a probable fifth adaptor protein complex that may be involved in endosomal transport. This protein has been associated with hereditary spastic paraplegia [[PMID:20613862],[PMID:22022230]].

Name: AP-5 complex subunit zeta-1, C-terminal TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found at the C-terminal end of human AP-5 complex subunit zeta-1 (AP5Z1, also known as SPG48), which is part of AP-5, a probable fifth adaptor protein complex that may be involved in endosomal transport. This protein has been associated with hereditary spastic paraplegia [[PMID:20613862],[PMID:22022230]].

Name: Repeat of unknown function (DUF7826)
Description: This entry represents a family of small uncharacterised bacterial alpha solenoid proteins.

Name: WDR36/Utp21 second beta-propeller domain
Description: This entry includes WDR36 from human and its orthologue from yeasts, Utp21, which are part of the small subunit (SSU) processome, the first precursor of the small eukaryotic ribosomal subunit. They are involved in the nucleolar processing of SSU 18S rRNA [[PMID:21051332],[PMID:34516797]]. This entry represents the second beta-propeller domain, found in the middle region of these proteins.

Name: WD repeat-containing protein 35, TPR repeats
Description: This region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats is found in human WD repeat-containing protein 35 (WDR35), a component of the IFT complex A (IFT-A), which is involved in ciliogenesis and ciliary protein trafficking. Members of this family are mainly found in animals.

Name: WDR36/Utp21 first beta-propeller
Description: This entry includes WDR36 from human and its orthologue from yeasts, Utp21, which are part of the small subunit (SSU) processome, the first precursor of the small eukaryotic ribosomal subunit. They are involved in the nucleolar processing of SSU 18S rRNA [[PMID:21051332],[PMID:34516797]]. This entry represents the first beta-propeller domain, found at the N-terminal of these proteins.

Name: WDR3 second beta-propeller domain
Description: This is the second beta-propeller domain of WDR3 from animals and its homologues. It is part of the small subunit (SSU) processome, the first precursor of the small eukaryotic ribosomal subunit [[PMID:34516797]].

Name: WDR3 first beta-propeller domain
Description: This is the first beta-propeller domain of WDR3 from animals and its homologues. It is part of the small subunit (SSU) processome, the first precursor of the small eukaryotic ribosomal subunit [[PMID:34516797]].

Name: THOC3 beta-propeller domain
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller domain of THO complex subunit 3 (THOC3) from animals and its homologues from plants and yeasts. It is a component of the THO subcomplex of the TREX complex which is involved in mRNA export and specifically associates with spliced mRNA and not with unspliced pre-mRNA [[PMID:15833825],[PMID:15998806],[PMID:17190602]]. The THOC1-THOC2-THOC3 core complex alone is sufficient to bind export factor NXF1-NXT1 and promote ATPase activity of DDX39B [[PMID:33191911]].

Name: WDR5 beta-propeller domain
Description: This is the beta-propeller domain of WDR5, a protein that contributes to histone modification; it may position the N-terminal of histone H3 for efficient trimethylation at 'Lys-4'. It is part of the MLL1/MLL complex, being involved in methylation and dimethylation at 'Lys-4' of histone H3 [[PMID:16829959],[PMID:23210835],[PMID:19556245]]. It also part of the NSL complex, in which it may be involved in acetylation of nucleosomal histone [[PMID:20018852]].

Name: WDR41
Description: This entry includes WDR41, the non-catalytic component of the C9orf72-SMCR8 complex which has guanine nucleotide exchange factor (GEF) activity and regulates autophagy [[PMID:27617292],[PMID:28195531]]. This complex also acts as a negative regulator of autophagy initiation by interacting with the ULK1/ATG1 kinase complex and inhibiting its protein kinase activity [[PMID:27103069],[PMID:27617292]]. WDR41 plays an essential role in C9orf72 lysosome recruitment, which is important for lysosomal responses to changes in amino acid availability [[PMID:29995611]]. WDR41 also interacts with PQLC2, a lysosomal cationic amino acid transporter that serves as a sensor that responds to scarcity of its substrates by recruiting C9orf72-SMCR8-WDR41 complex to the surface of lysosomes [[PMID:33597295]].

Name: FKB95, Kelch-repeats domain
Description: This entry represents a domain consisting of Kelch repeats found in plant proteins that are also associated with a F-box domain, such as FKB95 from Arabidopsis, a protein that is a component of SCF(ASK-cullin-F-box) E3 ubiquitin ligase complexes, which mediate the ubiquitination and subsequent proteasomal degradation of target proteins.

Name: At1g68980, TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found at the N-terminal end of At1g68980 from Arabidopsis thaliana and similar plant sequences.

Name: GLGC left-handed parallel beta-helix domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal left-handed parallel beta helix (LbH) domain of glucose-1-phosphate adenylyltransferases (GLGC). GLGC catalyse the first committed and rate-limiting step in starch biosynthesis in plants and glycogen biosynthesis in bacteria. It is the enzymatic site for regulation of storage polysaccharide accumulation in plants and bacteria. The enzyme is a homotetramer. This domain contains the imperfect tandem repeats of a hexapeptide repeat motif (X-[STAV]-X-[LIV]-[GAED]-X). The LbH domain is involved in cooperative allosteric regulation and oligomerisation.

Name: Ribonuclease II, winged helix domain
Description: This entry represents a winged helix domain found in ribonuclease II (RNR1) from Arabidopsis thaliana and similar sequences from plants and cyanobacteria. RNR1 is a 3'-5' exoribonuclease that catalyses 3' maturation of chloroplast and mitochondrion ribosomal RNAs. It degrades short nucleotidic extensions to generate the mature 3'-ends [[PMID:15891117],[PMID:23061883]]. It is involved in the maturation of 23S, 16S and 5S rRNAs.

Name: At2g24240-like, C-terminal beta propeller
Description: This is the beta propeller found at the C-terminal end of BTB/POZ domain-containing protein At2g24240 from Arabidopsis thaliana and similar plant proteins. At2g24240 may act as a substrate-specific adapter of an E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase complex (CUL3-RBX1-BTB) which mediates the ubiquitination and subsequent proteasomal degradation of target proteins.

Name: Toc75-like, second POTRA domain
Description: This is the second POTRA domain of Toc75 from Arabidopsis thaliana and similar plant proteins. Toc75 is an essential protein required for the import of protein precursors into chloroplasts. Toc75 has three POTRA domains, which together bent L-shaped conformation. The second POTRA domain contains a 40-residue insertion that folds into an elongated extra alpha-helix and loop, producing an overall beta-alpha-alpha -alpha-beta-beta fold [[PMID:28559331]].

Name: Toc75-like, POTRA domain
Description: This entry represents the first and third POTRA domains of Toc75 from Arabidopsis thaliana and similar plant proteins. Toc75 is an essential protein required for the import of protein precursors into chloroplasts. Toc75 has three POTRA domains, represented by this entry and [pfam:PF25280], which together adopt a bent L-shaped conformation. The first and third POTRA domains fold into one another, each containing the conserved core beta-alpha-alpha-beta-beta fold seen in other POTRA domains [[PMID:28559331]].

Name: Lambda-carrageenase beta-propeller domain
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller domain found at the N-terminal of Lambda-carrageenases (CGLA), a protein that hydrolyses lambda-carrageenan with inversion of anomeric configuration [[PMID:16926183]].

Name: EMC1 N-terminal beta-propeller domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal beta-propeller domain of EMC1 (ER membrane protein complex subunit 1), a protein part of the endoplasmic reticulum membrane protein complex (EMC) that enables the energy-independent insertion into endoplasmic reticulum membranes of newly synthesized membrane proteins [[PMID:32439656],[PMID:32459176],[PMID:30415835]]. This complex preferentially accommodates proteins with transmembrane domains that are weakly hydrophobic or contain destabilising features such as charged and aromatic residues [[PMID:30415835]]. EMC1 contains two eight-bladed beta-propellers and stabilises the entire complex, contacting six of the remaining eight subunits [[PMID:32439656]]. The beta-propellers are in the lumen and may serve as a platform for recruitment of co-factors at the site of nascent protein insertion [[PMID:32439656]].

Name: IFT122, TPR domain
Description: IFT122 is one of the core components (together with WDR19/IFT144 and IFT140) of the IFT-A complex which is required for retrograde ciliary transport and entry into cilia of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs), being involved in cilia function and/or assembly [[PMID:36462505],[PMID:29220510]]. IFT-A polymerises interacts with IFT-B, and uses an array of beta-propeller and TPR domains to create 'carriages' of the IFT train that engage TULP adaptor proteins [[PMID:29220510]]. This entry represents the TPR repeat domain that follows the two beta-propeller domains.

Name: Decapeptide repeat region
Description: This entry corresponds to a region composed of a 10 residue repeat that forms an alpha helical solenoid structure.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7878)
Description: This domain is found in uncharacterised bacterial proteins. It is predicted to contain two structural beta(3)-alpha repeats that swap their alpha-helix in a similar way as observed in the known ssDNA-binding transcriptional regulator domains. It is likely that this domain is distantly related to them and probably represents the minimal two repeat containing fold.

Name: BRWD/PHIP ancillary domain-like domain
Description: This entry represents a conserved domain found in bromodomain and WD repeat-containing proteins (BRWD) and PH-interacting protein (PHIP). The domain shows structural similarity to the ancillary domain of Auxin Response Factors ([pfam:PF06507]). It is found in BRWD1, BRWD3 and PHIP proteins, which are involved in transcriptional regulation. BRWD1 and BRWD3 proteins contain multiple bromodomains and WD40 repeats, while PHIP contains WD40 repeats and a PH domain.

Name: TELO2 ARM repeat domain
Description: This entry represents an ARM repeat domain found in TELO2 proteins that forms part of the TTT (TELO2-TTI1-TTI2) complex. Recent cryo-EM structures show that this domain adopts an elongated alpha-helical solenoid structure typical of ARM repeats. The domain mediates key interactions with TTI1 within the TTT complex, which functions as an adaptor complex for the R2TP chaperone system in the stabilization of phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase-related protein kinases (PIKKs). The TELO2 ARM domain provides a binding platform for TTI1 while remaining flexibly connected to the C-terminal domain via an intrinsically disordered linker.

Name: SRB8 ARM-like domain
Description: This entry represents an ARM-like domain found in the Mediator of RNA polymerase II transcription subunit 12/SRB8 proteins from fungi. These proteins are components of the Mediator complex, which is a coactivator involved in the regulated transcription of nearly all RNA polymerase II- dependent genes. ARM-like domains typically form a right-handed superhelix of alpha helices and are often involved in protein-protein interactions.

Name: STK11-interacting protein PH domain
Description: This entry represents a pleckstrin homology (PH) domain found in STK11-interacting proteins (S11IP). The domain is found between an N-terminal LIP1 domain and a C-terminal PH_PLEKHM2 domain. Some members of the family also contain LRR (leucine-rich repeat) domains. The protein is found in vertebrates including human, mouse, chicken and frog.

Name: ATM-like, TPR repeats
Description: This region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats is found in Serine/threonine-protein kinase ATM from Arabidopsis thaliana and similar plant proteins. ATM activates checkpoint signalling upon genotoxic stresses such as ionizing radiation (IR) or DNA replication stalling.

Name: F-box/LRR-repeat protein 15, leucine rich repeat domain
Description: This domain represents a set of tandem leucine rich repeats found in human F-box/LRR-repeat protein 15 (FBXL15) and similar eukaryotic proteins. FBXL15 is the substrate recognition component of a SCF (SKP1-CUL1-F-box protein), an E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase complex which mediates the ubiquitination and subsequent proteasomal degradation of SMURF1, thereby acting as a positive regulator of the BMP signalling pathway. It is required for dorsal/ventral pattern formation and bone mass maintenance [[PMID:21572392]].

Name: Alsin RCC1-like domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal RCC1-like domain (RLD) of the alsin protein and its homologues. The RLD is found at the N-terminus of alsin and contains six repeats. While the specific function is unknown, it may be involved in guanine nucleotide exchange activity. A deletion mutation in the first RLD repeat causes juvenile amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS2), suggesting its importance in motor neuron function.

Name: MEC1-like, HEAT repeats
Description: This region of HEAT repeats is found at the N-terminal end of the yeast Serine/threonine-protein kinase MEC1 and similar fungal proteins. MEC1 activates checkpoint signalling upon genotoxic stresses such as ionising radiation (IR), ultraviolet light (UV), or DNA replication stalling, thereby acting as a DNA damage sensor [[PMID:33169019],[PMID:36175395]].

Name: RCC1-like domain
Description: This entry represents the RCC1-like domain (RLD) of a number of proteins including RCC1. These proteins are typically composed of 7 blades that form a propeller structure.

Name: G protein beta WD-40 repeat protein
Description: This entry represents the WD-40 repeat domain found in G protein beta subunits. G beta subunits are essential components of heterotrimeric G proteins, acting as molecular scaffolds that facilitate interactions between G alpha subunits and various downstream effector proteins. The structure consists of a beta-propeller formed by seven WD-40 repeats.

Name: ZNFX1 domain
Description: This domain is found in RNA-binding proteins involved in antiviral responses and immunity against certain bacteria. Members of this family act as double-stranded RNA sensors, recognising viral RNA and interacting with mitochondrial antiviral-signaling protein (MAVS) to initiate the type I interferon response. This domain is often found associated with [pfam:PF13086] and is predicted to adopt a mainly beta configuration.

Name: PIFI-like, immunoglobulin-like domain
Description: This predicted immunoglobulin-like domain is found in Protein POST-ILLUMINATION CHLOROPHYLL FLUORESCENCE INCREASE, chloroplastic from Arabidopsis thaliana (PIFI) and similar plant proteins. PIFI is required during nonphotochemical quenching and is involved in NAD(P)H dehydrogenase complex-mediated chlororespiratory electron transport.

Name: Mitochondrial chaperone BCS1-like, ATPase lid domain
Description: This ATPase lid domain is found at the C-terminal end of human Mitochondrial chaperone BCS1 and similar eukaryotic proteins. BCS1 is a chaperone necessary for the incorporation of Rieske iron-sulfur protein UQCRFS1 into the mitochondrial respiratory chain complex III. It plays an important role in the maintenance of mitochondrial tubular networks, respiratory chain assembly and formation of the LETM1 complex. This domain is normally found immediately C-terminal to [pfam:PF00004] and also associated with [pfam:PF08740].

Name: BSD2 cysteine rich domain
Description: This domain is found in the chloroplastic Protein BUNDLE SHEATH DEFECTIVE 2 (BSD2) from Arabidopsis thaliana and related proteins. BSD2 is a chloroplast chaperone required for RuBisCo biogenesis and translational regulation of the RuBisCo large subunit [[PMID:29217567]]. It has a role in stabilizing an end-state assembly intermediate of eight RuBisCo large subunits until the small subunits become available. The structure of this domain has been determined and it has  a hairpin architecture arranged around two Zn atoms, each coordinated by four cysteines [[PMID:29217567]]. Members with this domain contain a highly conserved CxxCxGxG sequence motif.

Name: BRWD1-like, N-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found at the N-terminal end of human Bromodomain and WD repeat-containing protein 1 (BRWD1) and similar eukaryotic proteins probably involved in the regulation of cell morphology and cytoskeletal organization. Members of this family may function as transcriptional activators and are potentially involved in chromatin remodeling. This domain is predicted to adopt an all-alpha configuration.

Name: CFAP46, N-terminal TPR repeats
Description: This region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats is found at the N-terminal end of human Cilia- and flagella-associated protein 46 (CFAP46) and similar sequences. CFAP46 plays a role in cilium movement as part of the central apparatus of the cilium axoneme.

Name: RIC1 second beta propeller domain
Description: This entry represents the second beta-propeller domain found in RIC1 (Ribosome control protein 1) proteins. RIC1 proteins form a complex with RGP1 that functions as a guanine nucleotide exchange factor (GEF) for RAB6A, exchanging bound GDP for free GTP. The RIC1-RGP1 complex is required for efficient fusion of endosome-derived vesicles with the Golgi compartment and participates in the recycling of mannose-6-phosphate receptors. RIC1 is also required for phosphorylation and localization of GJA1 and plays a role in procollagen transport and secretion. It is essential for correct cartilage morphogenesis and development of the craniofacial skeleton. This domain is consistently found C-terminal to another beta-propeller domain in RIC1 proteins from species ranging from yeast to humans.

Name: UGP3-like, C-terminal hexapeptide repeats
Description: This region of hexapeptide repeats is found at the C-terminal end of UTP--glucose-1-phosphate uridylyltransferase 3, chloroplastic from Arabidopsis thaliana (UGP3) and similar plant proteins. UGP3 is involved in the biosynthesis of sulfolipids in the chloroplast. It catalyses the first committed step in sulfolipid biosynthesis.

Name: ISE2, SH3 domain
Description: This predicted SH3 domain is found in DExH-box ATP-dependent RNA helicase DExH15 chloroplastic from Arabidopsis thaliana (ISE2) and similar plant proteins. ISE2 is involved in group II intron splicing. It is required during embryogenesis and is involved in post-transcriptional gene silencing.

Name: CAF17, C-terminal beta-barrel domain
Description: This domain is found at the C-terminal end of the human putative transferase CAF17, mitochondrial and similar eukaryotic proteins. CAF17 is involved in the maturation of mitochondrial 4Fe-4S proteins functioning late in the iron-sulfur cluster assembly pathway [[PMID:30269484],[PMID:30950402]]. CAF17 part of the COG0354 family which includes folate-dependent proteins such as YgfZ which shares structural similarity [[PMID:20489182]]. This entry also includes putative aminomethyltransferase folate-binding proteins in bacteria.

Name: AIM3/BBC1 Cysteine proteinase-fold domain
Description: This entry represents a C-terminal domain found in fungal proteins that contains a cysteine peptidase-like fold but appears to lack the conserved catalytic residues. The domain is found in proteins that also contain an N-terminal SH3 domain. BBC1 is involved in the regulation of actin patch assembly and AIM3 is involved in mitochondrial inheritance, though the specific role of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: Aladin seven-bladed propeller
Description: This entry represents the seven-bladed beta-propeller of Aladin and related proteins. Aladin, also known as adracalin, is a nuclear envelope protein encoded by the AAAS gene in humans. Aladin is a component of the nuclear pore complex, where it forms a hetero-dimer with nucleoporin NDC1 [[PMID:35301439]]. There are two ALADIN-NDC1 dimers in each inner ring subunit, one on the cytoplasmic side and the other on the nuclear side [[PMID:35301439]]. The ALADIN beta-propellers are located in close proximity to the nuclear envelope. One side of the ALADIN beta-propeller is targeted by 15 achalasia-addisonianism-alacrima syndrome (AAAS)-derived mutations. These mutations either alter the structure or damage the interaction of ALADIN with other proteins.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7899)
Description: This domain is found in uncharacterised plant proteins. It is predicted to adopt an eight-bladed beta-propeller.

Name: At4g14310 8-bladed propeller
Description: This domain is found in the KIN14B-interacting protein At4g14310 from Arabidopsis thaliana and related proteins. At4g14310 is probably involved in division plane determination, which is crucial for proper cell division in plants [[PMID:20706207]]. This domain is predicted to fold into an eight-bladed beta-propeller.

Name: HEATR5A-like, HEAT repeats
Description: This is a region of HEAT repeats found in human HEAT repeat-containing protein 5A (HEATR5A) and similar animal sequences. The function of these proteins is unknown.

Name: NWD1-like, winged helix-turn-helix domain
Description: This domain is found in human NACHT domain- and WD repeat -containing protein 1 (NWD1) and similar animal proteins. NWD1 may play a role in the control of androgen receptor protein steady-state levels. This domain is predicted to adopt a winged helix-turn-helix fold.

Name: TmcB, TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found in Tiny macrocysts protein B from Dictyostelium discoideum (TmcB) and similar eukaryotic proteins. TmcB is a regulator of the cAMP signalling pathway specific to sexual development. It controls the levels of external cAMP by regulating the expression of phosphodiesterase PdsA and its inhibitor PdiA.

Name: RESC1/2 CYTH-like domain
Description: This domain is found in the RNA-editing substrate-binding complex protein 1 and 2 (RESC1 and RESC2) from Trypanosoma brucei and related proteins. In Trypanosoma, the editosome, composed of RNA-editing substrate-binding complex (RESC) and RNA-editing catalytic complex (RECC) is involved in guide RNA(gRNA)-programmed editing to recode cryptic mitochondrial transcripts into messenger RNAs. RESC1 and RESC2 are paralogs that are essential components of the RESC complex and the RESC1/2 dimer stabilizes gRNAs and most other proteins bind mRNAs [[PMID:37410820],[PMID:36999600]]. This domain has detectable similarity to members of the CYTH-like phosphatase superfamily and adopts similar structure [[PMID:37410820],[PMID:36999600]].

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7911)
Description: This domain is found in uncharacterised nematode proteins. It is predicted to fold into a five-bladed beta-propeller.

Name: Rol-3 five-bladed beta-propeller
Description: This domain is found in the Protein roller-3 (Rol-3) from Caenorhabditis elegans and related proteins. Rol-3 plays a crucial role in the development and locomotion in nematodes. Rol-3 has an essential function during early to mid-larval development [[PMID:8138151]]. The domain represented by this entry is predicted to fold into a five bladed beta-propeller.

Name: F-box protein pof12 beta-propeller
Description: This domain is found in the F-box protein pof12 from Schizosaccharomyces pombe and related proteins. Pof12 contains an F-box motif, which is a structural domain typically involved in protein-protein interactions. The exact function of this protein is currently elusive. The domain represented by this entry is predicted to fold into a seven-bladed beta-propeller and located upstream the F-box domain.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7914)
Description: This domain is found in uncharacterised proteins mainly from Trypanosomes. It is predicted to fold into a seven-bladed beta-propeller.

Name: CHK1-like, TPR repeats
Description: This region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats is found in Histidine protein kinase 1 from Candida albicans (CHK1), which is involved in a two-component signaling pathway that regulates cell wall mannan and glucan biosynthesis.

Name: 5MP1/2 HEAT domain
Description: This domain is found in the two human paralogs eIF5-mimic protein 1 and 2 (5MP1 and 5MP2) and related proteins. Both, 5MP1 and 5MP2, acts as competitors of eIF5 and can inhibit general translation by interaction with eIF2 and inhibiting the tertiary complex binding to the ribosome [[PMID:21745818],[PMID:27325740]]. These translation regulatory proteins exist in nearly all eukaryotes except in nematodes, yeasts and some protozoans. 5MP1/2 contain MA3- and W2-type HEAT domains. The domain represented by this entry is found N-terminal and it likely contributes to role of these proteins in translation regulation. It share some sequence, structural and functional similarities with the corresponding region of eIF4G.

Name: CPL3 ARM repeat domain
Description: This domain is found in RNA polymerase II C-terminal domain phosphatase-like 3 (CPL3), is a protein that plays important roles in plant development and defense mechanisms on Arabidopsis thaliana. CPL3 acts as a negative regulator of immune gene expression and immunity to pathogen infections [[PMID:25464831]]. It directly interacts with DELLA proteins, which are core suppressors in the gibberellic acid signaling pathway and promotes DELLA protein stability by inhibiting its degradation by the 26S proteasome [[PMID:38594216]]. The domain represented by this entry is predicted to contain ARM repeats. Its precise function is unknown.

Name: Plastid division regulator ARM repeat
Description: This entry represents the Armadillo/beta-catenin-like (ARM) repeat, a specific subclass of alpha-helical solenoid domains. ARM repeats are composed of tandem three-helix bundles that form a superhelical structure, primarily facilitating protein-protein interactions [[PMID:9770300]]. ARM repeats are predominantly found in plastid division regulators (PDR), where they play a crucial role in the coordination and regulation of plastid division in plants [[PMID:28248291]]. Proteins containing this domain include ARC6 and CDP1, both of which contribute to the proper assembly and stabilization of the FtsZ ring, a key component of the plastid division machinery. ARC6 facilitates FtsZ ring assembly and promotes the recruitment of MCD1, which is essential for forming the chloroplast Z-ring. CDP1, on the other hand, is required for the localisation of PDV1 and the correct formation of the FtsZ ring in non green plastids [[PMID:23936263]].

Name: Mitochondrial protein cyt-4, OB-fold domain
Description: This OB-fold domain is found in the Mitochondrial protein cyt-4 from Neurospora crassa and related proteins. cyt-4 is a nuclear-encoded protein that plays a crucial role in RNA processing and splicing within mitochondria [[PMID:1311848],[PMID:2478417]].

Name: Lysostaphin, N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents a domain found toward the N-terminal end of Lysostaphin from Staphylococcus simulans, an extracellular glycylglycine endopeptidase that lyses staphylococcal cells by hydrolysing the polyglycine interpeptide bridges of the peptidoglycan [[PMID:30018958],[PMID:31686030]]. This domain contains a repeated semi-conserved SHxxV[ED] sequence motif usually separated by 7-8 amino acid residues.

Name: BTB/POZ Ankyrin-like repeat
Description: This entry describes an ankyrin-like repeat found at the N-terminal of BTB/POZ containing proteins mostly found in plants.

Name: E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase synoviolin-like, TPR repeats
Description: This region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats is found at the N-terminal end of human E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase synoviolin (SYVN1) and similar proteins mainly found in animals. SYVN1 accepts ubiquitin specifically from endoplasmic reticulum -associated UBC7 E2 ligase and transfers it to substrates, promoting their degradation. It is a component of the endoplasmic reticulum quality control system.

Name: SYO1-like, TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found in Synchronised import protein 1 from Saccharomyces cerevisiae (SYO1) and similar eukaryotic proteins. SYO1 is a nuclear import adapter that specifically recruits the two functionally and topologically linked ribosomal proteins RPL5 and RPL11.

Name: ZFYVE26-like, TPR repeats
Description: This region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats is found in human Zinc finger FYVE domain-containing protein 26 (ZFYVE26) and similar proteins mainly found in animals. ZFYVE26 is a phosphatidylinositol 3-phosphate-binding protein required for the abscission step in cytokinesis. It is recruited to the midbody during cytokinesis and acts as a regulator of abscission.

Name: DENND3-like, TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found in human DENN domain-containing protein 3 (DENND3) and similar animal proteins. DENND3 is a guanine nucleotide exchange factor (GEF) that promotes the exchange of GDP to GTP, converting inactive GDP-bound RAB12 into its active GTP-bound form.

Name: CCP1-like, N-terminal TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found at the C-terminal end of human Cytosolic carboxypeptidase 1 (CCP1) and and similar proteins mainly found in animals. CCP1 catalyses the removal of polyglutamate side chains present on the gamma-carboxyl group of glutamate residues within the C-terminal tail of alpha- and beta-tubulin.

Name: ZSWIM8, TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found in human Zinc finger SWIM domain-containing protein 8 (ZSWIM8) and similar proteins mainly found in animals. ZSWIM8 is a substrate recognition component of a SCF-like E3 ubiquitin -protein ligase complex that promotes target-directed microRNA degradation.

Name: PSMD3-like, N-terminal TPR repeats
Description: This region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats is found at the N-terminal of human 26S proteasome non-ATPase regulatory subunit 3 (PSMD3) and similar eukaryotic sequences. PSMD3 is a component of the 26S proteasome, a multiprotein complex involved in the ATP -dependent degradation of ubiquitinated proteins.

Name: Importin subunit beta-1-like, TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found in human Importin subunit beta-1 (IMB1) and similar proteins. IMB1 functions in nuclear protein import, either in association with an adapter protein, or by acting as autonomous nuclear transport receptor.

Name: Serine/threonine-protein kinase BSK1-like, TPR repeats
Description: This region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats is found at the C-terminal end of Serine/threonine-protein kinase BSK1 from Arabidopsis thaliana and similar eukaryotic proteins. BSK1 acts as a positive regulator of brassinosteroid signalling downstream of the receptor kinase BRI1.

Name: RNF123-like, TPR repeat
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found in human E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase RNF123 and similar eukaryotic proteins. RNF123 is the catalytic subunit of the KPC complex that promotes the ubiquitination and proteasome-mediated degradation of CDKN1B, the cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitor at the G0-G1 transition of the cell cycle.

Name: TAF2-like, TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found towards the C-terminal end of human Transcription initiation factor TFIID subunit 2 (TAF2) and similar eukaryotic sequences. TFIID is a basal transcription factor complex that plays a major role in the initiation of RNA polymerase II-dependent transcription.

Name: E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase TRIP12-like, TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found at the N-terminal end of human E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase TRIP12 and similar eukaryotic proteins. TRIP12 is involved in ubiquitin fusion degradation pathway and regulation of DNA repair.

Name: Zinc finger protein Rlf-like, TPR repeats
Description: This region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats is found in human Zinc finger protein Rlf and similar proteins. Rlf may be involved in transcriptional regulation.

Name: Leucine Rich Repeat Variant domain
Description: This entry represents a likely small cytoplasmic domain found a a small set of bacterial integral membrane proteins. This domain is composed of a couple of leucine rich repeat variant repeats. The function of this domain is unknown.

Name: Plant U-box protein ARM repeat region
Description: This entry represents the ARM (Armadillo) repeat region found in plant U-box domain-containing E3 ubiquitin ligases (PUB proteins). These proteins contain multiple ARM-like repeats that are thought to be involved in protein-protein interactions. The ARM repeats are located C-terminal to the U-box domain. Plant U-box proteins play important roles in various cellular processes including stress responses and hormone signaling pathways. The family includes proteins such as PUB12, PUB22, PUB23, PUB24 and PUB25.

Name: WDR47 cross-over region
Description: This entry represents the cross-over region (COR) of the N-terminal domain of WD repeat-containing protein 47 (WDR47). The COR consists of three alpha-helices that intertwine to form a dimer interface and contains a non-canonical target-binding pocket. This pocket specifically recognizes a basic helical motif (WBR) in Calmodulin-regulated spectrin-associated proteins (Camsaps), which is essential for proper central-pair microtubule formation in motile cilia. Unlike typical LisH-CTLH-containing domains, the WDR47 COR is expanded and forms an acidic pocket that binds to a conserved basic motif in target proteins. The WDR47-Camsap interaction mediated by this domain is crucial for the assembly of non-centrosomal microtubules and for maintaining the proper planar beat pattern of mammalian motile multicilia.

Name: MUG190-like SMP domain
Description: This entry represents a synaptotagmin-like mitochondrial-lipid-binding (SMP) domain found in the Meiotically up-regulated gene 190 protein (MUG190), Tricalbin and related proteins. SMP domain is a lipid-binding module that belongs to the so-called TULIP (TUbular LIPid-binding) superfamily which includes other lipid-binding proteins like BPI-like and Takeout-like proteins [[PMID:21787343]]. SMP domains shuttle lipids between adjacent membranes without requiring membrane fusion or fission. This is achieved through their hydrophobic cavities that bind and transport lipid molecules [[PMID:26686281]].

Name: Bacteriophage nozzle protein
Description: This entry represents the nozzle protein family found in T7 bacteriophage and other phages. In phage T7, six copies of nozzle protein gp12 assemble into a hexameric tube attached to the adaptor protein ring (gp11) at the portal vertex. The nozzle forms part of the tail complex that completes phage assembly after DNA packaging. During phage infection, the nozzle serves as a tunnel for DNA delivery into the host cytoplasm. The tail assembly begins after the terminase complex cleaves the mature DNA genome and dissociates from the portal, allowing the tail proteins to assemble on the exposed portal clip domain.

Name: EBV TAP inhibitor BNLF2a
Description: This entry represents the BNLF2a protein family found in Epstein-Barr virus. BNLF2a is a membrane-associated protein that inhibits TAP-mediated peptide transport into the endoplasmic reticulum for binding to HLA class I molecules. By preventing peptide loading, BNLF2a helps the virus evade detection by cytotoxic T cells during the lytic phase of EBV infection. BNLF2a blocks the binding of both peptides and ATP to TAP, thereby inhibiting antigen presentation. The protein has a hydrophobic C-terminal domain that likely functions as a membrane anchor.

Name: INTS8, TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found towards the C-terminal end of human Integrator complex subunit 8 (INTS8), a component of the Integrator complex, a complex involved in the small nuclear RNAs (snRNA) U1 and U2 transcription and in their 3'-box-dependent processing.

Name: Dynein axonemal assembly factor 5-like, TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found towards the N-terminal end of human Dynein axonemal assembly factor 5 (DNAAF5) and similar eukaryotic proteins. DNAAF5 is a cytoplasmic protein involved in the delivery of the dynein machinery to the motile cilium. It is required for the assembly of the axonemal dynein inner and outer arms.

Name: Importin-11-like, TPR repeats
Description: This region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats is found at the C-terminal end of human Importin-11 (IPO11), which functions in nuclear protein import as nuclear transport receptor.

Name: PATROL1-like, TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found towards the C-terminal end of Protein unc-13 homolog from Arabidopsis thaliana (PATROL1), which is essential for stomatal opening in response to low concentration of carbon dioxide and light.

Name: RNA polymerase II-associated protein 1, TPR repeats
Description: This region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats is found in human RNA polymerase II-associated protein 1 (RPAP1) and similar eukaryotic proteins. RPAP1 is required for interaction of the RNA polymerase II complex with acetylated histone H3.

Name: Tubulin-specific chaperone D-like, ARM repeats
Description: This is a region of armadillo (ARM)-like repeats found in human Tubulin-specific chaperone D (TBCD), which is required for tubulin complex assembly and involved in the regulation of microtubule polymerisation or depolymerisation.

Name: IFT121-like, TPR repeats
Description: This region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats is found towards the C-terminal end of human WD repeat-containing protein 35 (also known as Intraflagellar transport protein 121 homolog, IFT121), a component of the IFT complex A (IFT-A), which is required for retrograde ciliary transport and entry into cilia of G protein-coupled receptors that is involved in ciliogenesis and ciliary protein trafficking.

Name: RRP12-like, N-terminal HEAT repeats
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal region of human RRP12-like protein, which consists of HEAT repeats. This domain is often found associated with [pfam:PF08161].

Name: ANAPC2-like, TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found in human Anaphase-promoting complex subunit 2 (ANAPC2) and similar eukaryotic sequences. ANAPC2 is part of the catalytic component of the anaphase promoting complex/cyclosome (APC/C), a cell cycle-regulated E3 ubiquitin ligase that controls progression through mitosis and the G1 phase of the cell cycle.

Name: IPO5-like, TPR repeats
Description: This region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats is found towards the N-terminal end of human Importin-5 (IPO5) and similar eukaryotic proteins. IPO5 serves as receptor for nuclear localisation signals in cargo substrates.

Name: TEX10-like, TPR repeats
Description: This is the C-terminal region of human Testis-expressed protein 10 (TEX10), which consists of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats. TEX10 functions as a component of the Five Friends of Methylated Chtop complex, which can only be recruited when Chtop is arginine-methylated leading to transactivation of ZNF148 target genes.

Name: CAND1-like, TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found in human Cullin-associated NEDD8-dissociated protein 1 (CAND1) and similar eukaryotic proteins. CAND1 plays a key role in the cellular repertoire of SCF (SKP1-CUL1-F-box protein) complexes. It functions as a F-box protein exchange factor.

Name: GCN1-like, C-terminal HEAT repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found towards the C-terminal end of human Stalled ribosome sensor GCN1 and similar sequences. GCN1 plays a key role in the RNF14-RNF25 translation quality control pathway, a pathway that takes place when a ribosome has stalled during translation, and which promotes ubiquitination and degradation of translation factors on stalled ribosomes.

Name: NAA35-like, TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found at the C-terminal end of human N-alpha-acetyltransferase 35, NatC auxiliary subunit (NAA35) and similar sequences. NAA35 is an auxiliary component of the N-terminal acetyltransferase C (NatC) complex which catalyses acetylation of N-terminal methionine residues.

Name: Exportin-7-like, TPR domain
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found in human Exportin-7 (XPO7), which mediates the nuclear export of proteins with broad substrate specificity.

Name: Stalled ribosome sensor GCN1-like, HEAT repeats
Description: This region of HEAT repeats is found at the C-terminal end of human Stalled ribosome sensor GCN1, which plays a key role in the RNF14-RNF25 translation quality control pathway, a pathway that takes place when a ribosome has stalled during translation, and which promotes ubiquitination and degradation of translation factors on stalled ribosomes.

Name: Nesprin-1, spectrin repeats
Description: This entry represents a region of spectrin repeats found in human Nesprin-1 (SYNE1), a multi-isomeric modular protein which forms a linking network between organelles and the actin cytoskeleton to maintain the subcellular spatial organisation. This protein has been associated with several severe disorders.

Name: LAA1-like, C-terminal TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found at the C-terminal end of AP-1 accessory protein LAA1 from Saccharomyces cerevisiae and similar sequences mainly found in fungi. LAA1 is involved in localisation of clathrin adapter protein complex-1 (AP-1) and subsequent AP-1-mediated clathrin-coated vesicle cargo loading.

Name: CTHRC1, C-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found at the C-terminal end of human Collagen triple helix repeat-containing protein 1 (CTHRC1) and similar animal sequences. CTHRC1 may act as a negative regulator of collagen matrix deposition. This domain is predicted to adopt an all-beta configuration.

Name: Mitochondrial transcription rescue factor 1, C-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found at the C-terminal end of human Mitochondrial transcription rescue factor 1 (MTRES1), which is involved in mitochondrial transcription regulation and functions as a protective factor to maintain proper mitochondrial RNA level during stress. This domain is predicted to fold as a beta-sheet with two alpha-helices on one side.

Name: Repeat of unknown function (DUF7951)
Description: This entry describes a repeat of unknown function found at the C-terminal of uncharacterised proteins mainly from animals. It displays a TPR fold.

Name: Tepsin VHS/ENTH-like domain
Description: This entry describes the VHS/ENTH-like domain found in AP-4 complex accessory subunit Tepsin proteins and related proteins in eukaryotes. The Epsin N-Terminal Homology (ENTH) domain is an evolutionarily conserved protein module found primarily in proteins that participate in clathrin-mediated endocytosis. ENTH is structurally similar to the VHS domain and is composed of a superhelix of eight alpha helices. ENTH domains bind both, inositol phospholipids and proteins. They contribute to the nucleation and formation of clathrin coats on membranes [[PMID:28691777]]. Tepsin associates with the Adapter-like complex 4 (AP-4) and may therefore play a role in vesicular trafficking of proteins at the trans-Golgi network [[PMID:26542808]]. This entry also includes the Modified transport to the vacuole 1 (MVT1) protein found in Arabidopsis thaliana. MTV1 represents a novel and unique member of the group of ENTH domain-containing proteins, which is potentially involved in vacuolar protein trafficking [[PMID:23771894]].

Name: ESX-1 scaffolding and assembly protein SaeA, N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of the ESX-1 scaffolding and assembly protein SaeA. The ESX-1 secretion system (T7SS) is required for secretion of ESAT-6 and CFP-10 proteins and is crucial for pathogenicity in Mycobacterium tuberculosis. In Mycobacterium smegmatis, SaeA is essential for proper localization of other ESX-1 components to the cell pole. This localization is required for ESX-1 secretion and function, including conjugative DNA transfer. SaeA appears to initiate an early step in the assembly of the ESX-1 apparatus at the pole. This domain shows structural similarity to the HRDC domain [pfam:PF00570].

Name: Ulvan lyase N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of the broad-spectrum ulvan lyase family. These enzymes are involved in the degradation of ulvan, a major cell wall polysaccharide from green seaweeds of the genus Ulva. The N-terminal domain is rich in alpha-helices and adopts an (alpha/alpha)6 toroid structure, which forms the catalytic core of the enzyme. In contrast to other known ulvan lyases (PL24, PL25, and PL28 families) that have predominantly beta-sheet structures, this domain has a helical-rich architecture. This domain is typically followed by a C-terminal beta-sandwich domain. Together, these domains enable the enzyme to catalyze the endolytic cleavage of the glycosidic bond between rhamnose-3-sulfate (Rha3S) and uronic acids (glucuronic acid or iduronic acid). The distinct structural fold of this domain contributes to the broad substrate specificity of these enzymes, which can degrade not only ulvan but also glycosaminoglycans like chondroitin sulfate and heparan sulfate.

Name: Pectate disaccharide-lyase N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal beta supersandwich domain of pectate disaccharide-lyase (EC 4.2.2.9), also known as exopolygalacturonate lyase. This enzyme catalyzes the exo-cleavage of pectate and contributes to pectate catabolism in bacteria. The enzyme requires calcium ions for activity and has an optimum pH of 7.5-8.0. Mutations in the gene encoding this protein result in reduced growth on pectate, but do not affect pathogenicity.

Name: DUF6242 beta propeller domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal beta propeller domain of this family.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF7957)
Description: This domain is found in uncharacterised protein from Halogeometricum borinquense (Hbor_03000) and related prokaryotic proteins. This protein is found next to a toxic gene Hbor_02990 and is likely a immunity protein which is able to prevent self-intoxication [[PMID:39546591]]. This domain is predicted to fold into three four-stranded beta-sheets that pack in a similar way as blades in beta-propellers.

Name: Winged Helix Domain in plant disease resistance proteins
Description: This entry represents a Winged Helix Domain (WHD) found in plant disease resistance proteins. The domain is typically found C-terminal and adjacent to the NB-ARC domain (PF00931) in proteins often annotated as disease resistance proteins in plants. These proteins are involved in the plant immune response against pathogens. This domain organization is characteristic of nucleotide-binding leucine-rich repeat (NLR) immune receptors in plants.

Name: Unconventional myosin-Va domain
Description: This domain is found in mouse Unconventional myosin-Va (Myo5a) and similar sequences mainly found in animals. Myo5a is a processive actin-based motor that can move in large steps approximating the 36-nm pseudo-repeat of the actin filament. This protein is involved in melanosome transport and mediates the transport of vesicles to the plasma membrane [[PMID:22908308]]. This domain shows an alpha helical configuration.

Name: NUDT9-like, N-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found towards the N-terminal end of human ADP-ribose pyrophosphatase, mitochondrial (NUDT9) and similar sequences. NUDT9 hydrolyses ADP-ribose (ADPR) to AMP and ribose 5'-phosphate. This domain, which has an alpha-beta configuration is often found associated to a catalytic C-terminal Nudix domain ([pfam:PF00293]) [[PMID:11385575],[PMID:12948489]].

Name: Peroxin 8 protein-like, N-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found toward the N-terminal end of the Peroxisomal biogenesis factor 8 protein (PEX8), Peroxisomal membrane protein PEX17 and related proteins from fungal species. PEX17 is involved in peroxisome biosynthesis. This protein is required for the import of a subset of matrix proteins into peroxisomes [[PMID:9111320]]. PEX8 is essential component of the machinery required for the import of both PTS1 and PTS2 (and perhaps all) peroxisomal matrix proteins [[PMID:7738036]]. Binding of PEX8 to the N-terminus of PEX5 cargo receptor induces a conformational change of the TPR domains and decrease their binding affinity to cargo, facilitating the release of the PTS1 proteins within the peroxisome [[PMID:23902771]].

Name: Leucine-rich repeat domain
Description: This domain is found in uncharacterised proteins mainly from order Trypanosomatida.

Name: Alanine--tRNA ligase domain
Description: This domain is found in Alanine--tRNA ligase, mitochondrial from Saccharomyces cerevisiae (ALA1), which catalyses the attachment of alanine to tRNA(Ala) in a two-step reaction: alanine is first activated by ATP to form Ala-AMP and then transferred to the acceptor end of tRNA(Ala). This domain is predicted to adopt an alpha-beta configuration.

Name: SMAUG-like PHAT domain
Description: This PHAT (a pseudo-HEAT-repeat analogous topology) domain is found in SMAUG and related proteins, including Zinc finger CCHC domain-containing protein 2 (ZCCHC2). SMAUG protein is a key regulator of posterior patterning and crucial for the maternal-to-zygotic transition of gene expression of the early Drosophila embryo. In animals, members of the SMAUG protein family exhibit various functions during development. Mammals express two SMAUG-related paralogs named SMAUG1 and SMAUG2. In Drosophila, the PHAT domain and the N-terminal dimerization domain interact and jointly form a groove that binds Smoothened and Oskar proteins [[PMID:37523566]].

Name: CLPX-like, zinc ribbon domain
Description: This zinc ribbon domain is found towards the N-terminal of human ATP-dependent clpX-like chaperone, mitochondrial (CLPX), which functions as an unfoldase. This protein is part of the ClpXP protease complex and is also involved in in haem biosynthesis and in the control of mtDNA nucleoid distribution [[PMID:11923310],[PMID:22841477],[PMID:22710082],[PMID:25957689],[PMID:28874591]]. This domain shows an all-beta structure.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF8034)
Description: This entry represents a family of probable enzymes found in a range of bacteria. Shows structural similarity to Mannosyl-oligosaccharide alpha-1,2-mannosidase.

Name: Epg5-like, TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found in Ectopic P granules protein 5 homolog from Drosophila melanogaster (Epg5) and similar proteins mainly from animals. Epg5 plays a role in late steps of autophagy [[PMID:26917586]].

Name: Epg5-like, C-terminal TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found at the C-terminal end of Ectopic P granules protein 5 homolog from Drosophila melanogaster (Epg5) and similar proteins mainly from animals. Epg5 plays a role in late steps of autophagy [[PMID:26917586]].

Name: TTC28, C-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found towards the C-terminal end of human Tetratricopeptide repeat protein 28 (TTC28) and similar sequences mainly found in animals. TTC28 may be involved in the condensation of spindle midzone microtubules during mitosis, leading to the formation of midbody [[PMID:23036704]]. This domain is predicted to show an alpha-beta configuration.

Name: Sporulation delaying protein family toxin
Description: This protein family contains the antimicrobial peptide SdpC, used in cannibalistic killing by Bacillus subtilis, and related sequences in species as distant as Myxococcus xanthus from the Deltaproteobacteria. A conserved gene neighborhood includes proteins associated with immunity.

Name: URB1, central HEAT repeat domain
Description: This domain is found in the Nucleolar pre-ribosomal-associated protein 1 (URB1) and related proteins. URB1 is a protein involved in the biogenesis of ribosomes, specifically in the early stages of ribosomal RNA processing [[PMID:15242642],[PMID:15226434],[PMID:15208443]]. It can associate with a subset of H/ACA and C/D small nucleolar RNPs (snoRNPs) involved in the chemical modification of residues in the vicinity of the peptidyl transferase center [[PMID:15226434]].

Name: DDX21/DDX50 dimerisation domain
Description: This entry represents the dimerisation domain in DEAD box helicase DDX21/DDX50 proteins. DDX21/DDX50 are involved in various aspects of RNA metabolism, including ribosomal RNA processing, pre-mRNA splicing, and RNA folding. These proteins act as sensors of transcriptional status for RNA polymerase I and II, promoting rRNA transcription and processing. They bind various RNAs, such as rRNAs, snoRNAs, and mRNAs, and are essential for ribosome biogenesis in chloroplasts. Additionally, they play a role in antiviral responses by acting as viral restriction factors, promoting the activation of signalling pathways following viral RNA stimulation. They are also involved in preventing R-loop-associated DNA damage and transcription-associated genomic instability. The Q motif, unique to these proteins, controls ATP binding and hydrolysis, which is crucial for their helicase activity [[PMID:22576849],[PMID:23227895],[PMID:25043599]]. The dimerisation domain has a LAAALA conserved sequence which is neccesary for dimerisation. This domain is essential for ATP dependet dsRNA unwinding and G-quadruplex remodeling [[PMID:9461305],[PMID:39764852]].

Name: ATRX C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain in ATRX proteins. ATRX proteins are involved in transcriptional regulation and chromatin remodelling. These proteins facilitate DNA replication across various cellular environments and are essential for the efficient replication of specific genomic loci. They bind to DNA tandem repeat sequences in telomeres and euchromatin and can interact with DNA quadruplex structures. They play a crucial role in stabilising G-rich regions into regular chromatin structures by remodelling G4 DNA and incorporating H3.3-containing nucleosomes. Additionally, they are implicated in DNA repair, telomere maintenance, and allele-specific gene expression [[PMID:21653732],[PMID:34162889]]. The function of the C-terminal has not yet been elucidated. It contains an all-alpha-helical structure. This domain appears to be specific to ATRX in animals, as it is not present in CHR20, the ATRX plant orthologue.

Name: Endo-beta-1,2-glucanase SGL
Description: This entry represents the endo-beta-1,2-glucanase (SGL) from Talaromyces funiculosus, a soil fungus, and related proteins. TfSGL specifically hydrolyses linear and cyclic beta-1,2-glucans to sophorose (Glc-beta-1,2-Glc) as a main product [[PMID:30926603]]. TfSGL is an inverting enzyme and belongs to GH162 family. This enzyme adopts an alpha-helical structure with (alpha/alpha)6 toroid fold which is related to the members of Six-hairpin glycosidase superfamily.

Name: Small ribosomal subunit protein mS26
Description: This entry represents the yeast Small ribosomal subunit protein mS26 and related proteins. mS26 is a component of the small subunit of mitochondrial ribosomes, specifically the 37S small subunit [[PMID:28154081]]. It contributes to the assembly and function of the ribosome. mS26 plays a critical role in mitochondrial protein synthesis, which is essential for cellular energy production. mS26, along with bS6m and mS43, has been shown to functionally interact with Pet122, a translational activator for cytochrome c oxidase subunit III [[PMID:2167435]].

Name: RNA-editing substrate-binding complex 7 protein
Description: This domain is found in the RNA-editing substrate-binding complex 7 protein (RESC7) from Trypanosoma brucei, a component of RESC which together with RECC forms the editosome that orchestrates guide RNA (gRNA)-programmed editing to recode cryptic mitochondrial transcripts into messenger RNAs [[PMID:37410820]]. RESC stabilises gRNAs and engages mRNAs. The RNA-editing catalytic complex (RECC) fulfills gRNA-programmed mRNA cleavage, uridine insertion or deletion, and religation reactions. RESC is a heterogenous assembly of ~18 proteins that enclose gRNAs and mRNAs. RESC7 is RESC-B-specific and it is involved with pre-edited mRNA binding [[PMID:37410820]].

Name: DDX60 TPR domain
Description: This entry represents the uncharacterised TPR repeat domain in DDX60 helicase proteins mostly found in vertebrates. DDX60-like helicase proteins are involved in the regulation of type I interferon and interferon-inducible gene expression in response to viral infections. In humans, DDX60 can bind single-stranded RNA, double-stranded RNA, and double-stranded DNA, facilitating the interaction of RIG-I with double-stranded RNA. This interaction is crucial for the antiviral response against viruses such as hepatitis C and vesicular stomatitis virus [[PMID:21791617],[PMID:21478870]]. This domain contains a TPR like fold with slightly longer helices.

Name: RNA-editing substrate-binding complex 5 protein
Description: This domain is found in the RNA-editing substrate-binding complex 5 protein (RESC5) from Trypanosoma brucei, a component of RESC which together with RECC forms the editosome that orchestrates guide RNA (gRNA)-programmed editing to recode cryptic mitochondrial transcripts into messenger RNAs [[PMID:37410820]]. RESC stabilises gRNAs and engages mRNAs. The RNA-editing catalytic complex (RECC) fulfills gRNA-programmed mRNA cleavage, uridine insertion or deletion, and religation reactions. RESC is a heterogenous assembly of ~18 proteins that enclose gRNAs and mRNAs. RESC5 protein is a key component of RESC and contributes to stabilizing gRNAs and facilitating their interaction with mRNAs during editing. This process involves remodeling events that expose editing sites to RECC for uridine insertion, deletion, or ligation. RESC5 adopts a dimethylarginine dimethylaminohydrolase-like (DDAH) fold. While DDAH enzymes typically hydrolyze methylated arginine residues, RESC5 lacks key catalytic residues necessary for this activity and does not bind DDAH substrates or products.

Name: RNA-editing substrate-binding complex 8 protein
Description: This domain is found in the RNA-editing substrate-binding complex 8 protein (RESC8) from Trypanosoma brucei, a component of RESC which together with RECC forms the editosome that orchestrates guide RNA (gRNA)-programmed editing to recode cryptic mitochondrial transcripts into messenger RNAs [[PMID:37410820]]. RESC stabilises gRNAs and engages mRNAs. The RNA-editing catalytic complex (RECC) fulfills gRNA-programmed mRNA cleavage, uridine insertion or deletion, and religation reactions. RESC is a heterogenous assembly of ~18 proteins that enclose gRNAs and mRNAs. RESC8 is a component of RESC-C and it is composed of HEAT repeats. In the RESC complex, the mRNA is sequentially shielded by the superhelical inner surface of the HEAT repeats of RESC9, RESC8, and RESC6 [[PMID:37410820]].

Name: RNA-editing substrate-binding complex 10 protein
Description: This domain is found in the RNA-editing substrate-binding complex 10 protein (RESC10) from Trypanosoma brucei, a component of RESC which together with RECC forms the editosome that orchestrates guide RNA (gRNA)-programmed editing to recode cryptic mitochondrial transcripts into messenger RNAs [[PMID:37410820]]. RESC stabilises gRNAs and engages mRNAs. The RNA-editing catalytic complex (RECC) fulfills gRNA-programmed mRNA cleavage, uridine insertion or deletion, and religation reactions. RESC is a heterogenous assembly of ~18 proteins that enclose gRNAs and mRNAs. RESC10 is composed of HEAT repeats. RESC10 is integral to maintaining the structural and functional integrity of RESC, acting upstream of other RESC proteins to orchestrate their organization and interactions. Besides, the gRNA 3' nucleotides interact with RESC10 [[PMID:37410820]].

Name: RNA-editing substrate-binding complex 6 protein
Description: This domain is found in the RNA-editing substrate-binding complex 6 protein (RESC6) from Trypanosoma brucei, a component of RESC which together with RECC forms the editosome that orchestrates guide RNA (gRNA)-programmed editing to recode cryptic mitochondrial transcripts into messenger RNAs [[PMID:37410820]]. RESC stabilises gRNAs and engages mRNAs. The RNA-editing catalytic complex (RECC) fulfills gRNA-programmed mRNA cleavage, uridine insertion or deletion, and religation reactions. RESC is a heterogenous assembly of ~18 proteins that enclose gRNAs and mRNAs. RESC6 is composed of HEAT repeats. RESC6 is part of the six-member RESC-A subcomplex, which stabilizes gRNAs by binding to their 3' ends [[PMID:37410820]]. RESC6 forms a heterodimer with RESC5 through hydrogen bonds and hydrophobic contacts, creating a positively charged crevice that likely facilitates RNA binding. RESC6 also interacts with other RESC proteins, such as RESC1, and RESC2, to stabilize the overall structure of the complex and mediate RNA editing processes.

Name: TYRAAT1-like, C-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found twice in Arogenate dehydrogenase 1, chloroplastic from Arabidopsis thaliana (TYRAAT1) and similar plant proteins. TYRAAT1, which is involved in the biosynthesis of tyrosine [[PMID:12354106]], consists of two highly similar and catalytically active peptide domains that do not undergo proteolytic cleavage. This entry represents a region found at the C-terminal end of each of these domains. This region is predicted to adopt an alpha helical structure.

Name: RESC9 N-terminal region
Description: This domain is found in the RNA-editing substrate-binding complex 9 protein (RESC9) from Trypanosoma brucei, a component of RESC which together with RECC forms the editosome that orchestrates guide RNA (gRNA)-programmed editing to recode cryptic mitochondrial transcripts into messenger RNAs [[PMID:37410820]]. RESC stabilises gRNAs and engages mRNAs. The RNA-editing catalytic complex (RECC) fulfills gRNA-programmed mRNA cleavage, uridine insertion or deletion, and religation reactions. RESC is a heterogenous assembly of ~18 proteins that enclose gRNAs and mRNAs. RESC9 as several other RESC components is composed of HEAT repeats. RESC9 interacts with RESC11 and with mRNA [[PMID:37410820]].

Name: RESC9 middle region
Description: This domain is found in the RNA-editing substrate-binding complex 9 protein (RESC9) from Trypanosoma brucei, a component of RESC which together with RECC forms the editosome that orchestrates guide RNA (gRNA)-programmed editing to recode cryptic mitochondrial transcripts into messenger RNAs [[PMID:37410820]]. RESC stabilises gRNAs and engages mRNAs. The RNA-editing catalytic complex (RECC) fulfills gRNA-programmed mRNA cleavage, uridine insertion or deletion, and religation reactions. RESC is a heterogenous assembly of ~18 proteins that enclose gRNAs and mRNAs. RESC9 as several other RESC components is composed of HEAT repeats. RESC9 interacts with RESC11 and with mRNA [[PMID:37410820]]. This entry represents the middle region of RESC9.

Name: RESC11
Description: This domain is found in the RNA-editing substrate-binding complex 11 protein (RESC11) from Trypanosoma brucei, a component of RESC which together with RECC forms the editosome that orchestrates guide RNA (gRNA)-programmed editing to recode cryptic mitochondrial transcripts into messenger RNAs [[PMID:37410820]]. RESC stabilises gRNAs and engages mRNAs. The RNA-editing catalytic complex (RECC) fulfills gRNA-programmed mRNA cleavage, uridine insertion or deletion, and religation reactions. RESC is a heterogenous assembly of ~18 proteins that enclose gRNAs and mRNAs. Like several other RESC componenets, RESC11 is composed of HEAT repeats. RESC11 interacts with RESC9 and likely interacts with mRNA.

Name: Yeast TEL2-interacting protein 1, second TPR domain
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found in yeast TEL2-interacting protein 1 (TTI1). In S.cerevisiae, this protein is a component of the ASTRA complex involved in chromatin remodeling.

Name: Trs120/TRAPPC9 TPR
Description: This region is found in Saccharomyces cerevisiae Trs120 protein ([swissprot:Q04183]) and in human Trafficking protein particle complex subunit 9 (TRAPPC9). It contains a tetratricopeptide repeat (TPR). Trs120 is a subunit of the multiprotein complex TRAPP (transport particle protein) which functions in ER to Golgi traffic [[PMID:10727015]]. Trs120 is specific to the larger TRAPP complex, TRAPP II, along with Trs65p and Trs130p(TRAPPC10). It is suggested that Trs120p is required for the stability of the Trs130p subunit, suggesting that these two proteins might interact in some way [[PMID:17041589]]. It is likely that there is a complex function for TRAPP II in multiple pathways [[PMID:18801063]].

Name: Histone domain in ABTB family protein
Description: This entry represents a histone-like domain found towards the N-terminus of Ankyrin repeat and BTB (POZ) domain containing proteins. The Ankyrin repeat and BTB/POZ domain-containing protein family is characterized by the presence of ankyrin repeats and a BTB/POZ domain. These proteins may play a role in cellular processes such as the initiation of hepatocyte growth, as suggested by some members of the family. The ankyrin repeats are known for mediating protein-protein interactions, while the BTB/POZ domain is involved in protein complex formation and transcriptional regulation. The combination of these domains suggests that the family may be involved in regulatory functions within the cell, potentially influencing cell growth and development.

Name: Repeat of unknown function (DUF8079)
Description: This domain is found in N-terminal in uncharacterised eukaryotic proteins. It is predicted to be composed of HEAT repeats. It is often found in combination with [pfam:PF25405].

Name: Sliding clamp-binding bacterial toxin SocB
Description: This entry represents the SocB toxin found in a toxin-antitoxin system in bacteria. SocB is a protein toxin that inhibits DNA replication by binding to the beta sliding clamp DnaN through a conserved clamp-binding motif. When SocB accumulates, it competes with DNA polymerase III for binding to DnaN, leading to replication fork collapse and activation of the SOS response. In Caulobacter crescentus, SocB is constitutively degraded by the ClpXP protease, which requires the antitoxin SocA to act as a proteolytic adaptor. SocA binds both SocB and the N-terminal domain of ClpX to promote SocB degradation. This toxin-antitoxin system is unusual because the toxin is unstable rather than the antitoxin. SocB homologs are found primarily in alpha-proteobacteria.

Name: Lipoprotein LpqS family
Description: The Lipoprotein LpqS family is implicated in the replication and survival of Mycobacterium tuberculosis within host cells. This suggests a potential role in the pathogen's ability to persist and proliferate during infection, which could be critical for its pathogenicity and the progression of tuberculosis.

Name: Periplakin-like, plectin repeat
Description: This plectin repeat is found five times in human Periplakin (PPL), a component of the cornified envelope of keratinocytes that may act as a localisation signal in PKB/AKT-mediated signalling [[PMID:9412476]]. It is predicted to adopt an helical fold.

Name: Pelota N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain found in Pelota family proteins. The Pelota protein family is involved in recognizing stalled ribosomes and facilitating the No-Go Decay (NGD) pathway. Members of this family can destabilize mRNA in the ribosome channel, promoting the disassembly of stalled ribosomes and subsequent degradation of damaged mRNAs. In eukaryotes, the Pelota-HBS1L complex, which includes Pelota proteins, plays a critical role in these processes. Additionally, Pelota proteins are implicated in mitochondrial quality control through mitophagy, where they are recruited to damaged mitochondria to facilitate the recruitment of autophagy receptors. The family is characterized by an RNA-binding Sm fold domain that harbors endoribonuclease activity, essential for its function in mRNA cleavage and ribosome rescue.

Name: Mycobacterial Pilin Family
Description: The mycobacterial pilin family comprises structural subunits of pili, which are thin, flexible, coiled-coil, aggregative fibers. These pili play a crucial role in mediating adhesion to the extracellular matrix, facilitating direct interaction with the host epithelium during infection, particularly in the lungs or other tissues. The pilins have a strong affinity for laminin, although they do not significantly bind to fibronectin or type IV collagen. The structural assembly of these proteins forms homomers composed of subunits that create a large, cohesive structure, contributing to the pathogenicity of mycobacterial species by enabling adherence and colonization of host tissues.

Name: Psb34 family
Description: The Psb34 family is involved in the assembly and repair of photosystem II (PSII), a crucial component of the photosynthetic machinery. This family likely plays a role in the conversion of late PSII assembly intermediates into mature dimeric PSII complexes. It may help mediate the optimal distribution of HliA and HliB proteins among intermediates containing CP47, thereby facilitating photoprotection during the assembly process. The Psb34 family is part of the PSII assembly intermediate RC47 complex, which includes several other components such as D1, D2, CP47, PsbE, PsbF, PsbH, Psb27, and Psb28. The interaction of Psb34 with PSII requires the presence of CP47 and PsbH, and it likely competes with HliA and HliB for binding to a similar site on CP47.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF8137)
Description: This entry represents a domain from a family of uncharacterised proteins found in archaea. The proteins in this family are predominantly found in the class Halobacteria, which includes genera such as Halorubrum, Natronorubrum, and Halovenus. The proteins containing this domain vary in length, with sequences ranging from approximately 59 to 347 amino acids. The structure of this domain adopts an alpha solenoid.

Name: Plant ARM repeat
Description: This entry represents a repeat from a family of uncharacterised proteins in the plant kingdom. The proteins are predominantly found in the eudicotyledons and monocots, specifically within the Magnoliopsida and Liliopsida classes. The reviewed protein Y1439_ARATH from Arabidopsis thaliana is an example of a protein containing this repeat. This protein may act as a substrate-specific adapter of an E3 ubiquitin- protein ligase complex (CUL3-RBX1-BTB) which mediates the ubiquitination and subsequent proteasomal degradation of target proteins. This domain is composed of an alpha solenoid structure composed of repeating pairs of alpha helices.

Name: Trm72 ARM-like repeat region
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal ARM repeat region of a protein family found in fungi, particularly within the Ascomycota phylum. It includes the tRNA (32-2'-O)- methyltransferase regulator trm732 from Schizosaccharomyces pombe ([swissprot:O60081]). Proteins in this family are typically around 1500 amino acids in length. This domain is composed of an alpha solenoid structure composed of repeating pairs of alpha helices.

Name: Plant ARM repeat
Description: This entry represents a family of uncharacterised plant proteins. The proteins in this family are found in a wide range of species. This domain is composed of an alpha solenoid structure composed of repeating pairs of alpha helices.

Name: REH2 DRSM domain
Description: This entry represents a DSRM domain found in REH2 family proteins. The RNA editing associated helicase 2 family is involved in mitochondrial mRNA editing, a crucial process that includes the addition and deletion of uridine nucleotides in pre-mRNA. This family functions as ATP-dependent RNA helicases that unwind RNA in a 3' to 5' direction. They play a significant role in the assembly of mRNA-gRNA hybrids and the recruitment of the RNA editing core complex. The family regulates various steps of mRNA editing, including the loading of unedited mRNA and editing progression across multiple sequence blocks.
