The domain shows similarity to pectin lyase fold structures. The specific function of this N-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: TP_0783 beta-propeller protein
Description: This entry represents TP_0783-like single-pass transmembrane proteins from Treponema pallidum and related spirochaete bacteria. The protein is 377 amino acids in length with a transmembrane helix spanning residues 21-43. Structural analysis indicates the presence of a beta-propeller fold, characteristic of the beta-propeller (CL0186). The protein exhibits remote structural similarity to quinoprotein amine dehydrogenase, though the specific biological function remains to be determined.

Name: VPS3 N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal CNH domain of vacuolar protein sorting-associated protein 3 (VPS3) from Saccharomyces cerevisiae. The domain is found in VPS3 family proteins involved in vacuolar protein sorting. The full-length VPS3 protein is required for sorting and processing of soluble vacuolar proteins, integrity of vacuolar morphology, efficient segregation of vacuolar material during the cell cycle, vacuolar acidification, and assembly of the vacuolar H(+)-ATPase [[PMID:2202738]]. VPS3 is a component of the CORVET tethering complex and localises to the cytoplasm. The specific function of this N-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: Protein y4wI C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of protein y4wI, found in bacteria. The full-length protein is annotated as a radical SAM family RIPP maturase (NCBIfam:TIGR04517, InterPro:IPR030950), involved in post-translational modification of ribosomally synthesised and post-translationally modified peptides. The specific function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: Uncharacterized mitochondrial protein AtMg01350
Description: This entry represents an uncharacterized protein encoded by the mitochondrial genome of Arabidopsis thaliana. The protein is predicted to be a single-pass transmembrane protein localised to the mitochondrial membrane. The protein contains a predicted transmembrane helical region. The function of this protein remains to be determined

Name: Unclassified hydrophobin D
Description: This entry represents unclassified hydrophobin D, a small amphipathic protein found in Penicillium expansum. Hydrophobins are secreted proteins that contribute to aerial growth, conidiation, and dispersal of filamentous fungi. The protein contains a signal peptide and three disulphide bonds. HfbD contributes to germination, tolerance to cold stress and mycotoxin production [[PMID:29371496]]. The protein affects hydrophobicity only when other hydrophobins are also deleted.

Name: TP_0598 N-terminal beta-propeller domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal beta-propeller domain of uncharacterised protein TP_0598 from Treponema pallidum. The full-length protein is 605 amino acids long. In some strains, this protein is fused with TP_0597 as a result of frameshift mutations. The function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: Uncharacterized protein C35A5.10
Description: This entry represents uncharacterized protein C35A5.10 from Caenorhabditis elegans. The protein contains two WR1 domains and cysteine repeat regions. The protein has a predicted signal peptide suggesting it may be secreted. Expression has been detected in embryo and other cell types, according to Bgee database. The function of this protein remains to be determined.

Name: C-terminal domain of TP_0123
Description: This entry represents a conserved C-terminal domain found in TP_0123, an 856-amino acid TPR repeat-containing protein from Treponema pallidum that is annotated as belonging to COG0457 and has a TPR-like helical domain superfamily structure.

Name: N-terminal domain of TP_0123
Description: This entry represents a conserved N-terminal domain found in TP_0123, an 856-amino acid TPR repeat-containing protein from Treponema pallidum.

Name: Serine rich endogenous peptide 22
Description: This entry represents the serine rich endogenous peptide 2 (PROSCOOP22) family found in Brassicaceae. These are plant endogenous peptides released into the apoplast and perceived by the MIK2 receptor in a BAK1/SERK3 and SERK4 coreceptor-dependent manner [[PMID:34535661]]. The peptides modulate physiological and antimicrobial processes including growth prevention and reactive oxygen species response regulation.

Name: Class I hydrophobin F
Description: This entry represents Class I hydrophobins F, small amphipathic proteins secreted by filamentous fungi. These proteins can self- assemble into an outermost layer of rodlet bundles on aerial cell surfaces, conferring cellular hydrophobicity that supports fungal growth, development and dispersal [[PMID:33022346]]. The proteins contain four disulphide bonds and are secreted to the cell wall. Hydrophobins are involved in aerial growth, conidiation and dispersal. The family includes proteins such as HYD3 and Hyd1F from entomopathogenic fungi.

Name: Cytochrome b termination protein 1
Description: This entry represents cytochrome b termination protein 1, a mitochondrial protein essential for cytochrome b formation in Saccharomyces cerevisiae. The protein is involved in 5'-end processing of mitochondrial COB, 15S rRNA, and RPM1 transcripts [[PMID:9339340]]. CBT1 may also have a role in 3'-end processing of the COB pre-mRNA and is required to stabilise the mature cytochrome b mRNA. The protein is essential for mitochondrial cytochrome b formation and cytochrome complex assembly.

Name: Domain of unknown function (DUF8586)
Description: This entry represents a TPR-like domain found at the C-terminal of a group of uncharacterised sequences from bacteria that contain a FtsH ternary system domain X7 at the N-terminal.

Name: Myxococcota tetratricopeptide repeat protein family
Description: This entry represents a tetratricopeptide repeat (TPR) protein family, found in Myxococcota including Pyxidicoccus, Hyalangium and Melittangium species. TPR domains are structural motifs involved in protein-protein interactions. The function of this protein remains to be determined.

Name: SOV1 mitochondrial middle domain
Description: This entry represents a TPR middle domain found in mitochondrial protein SOV1 and related proteins. SOV1 is a mitochondrial protein involved in translation activator activity and mitochondrial ribosome assembly [[PMID:14562095],[PMID:14576278]]. SOV1 localises to mitochondria and plays a role in mitochondrial translation. The specific function of this TPR middle domain remains to be determined.

Name: Putative defensin-like protein 263
Description: This entry represents putative defensin-like protein 263 and related proteins from the DEFL family in plants. The protein is a secreted protein from Arabidopsis thaliana that belongs to a large family of more than 300 defensin-like genes [[PMID:15955924]]. The mature protein contains multiple disulphide bonds and is predicted to have antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The specific function of this protein remains to be determined.

Name: SOV1 mitochondrial C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal TPR domain found in mitochondrial protein SOV1 and related proteins. SOV1 is a mitochondrial protein involved in translation activator activity and mitochondrial ribosome assembly [[PMID:14562095],[PMID:14576278]]. SOV1 localises to mitochondria and plays a role in mitochondrial translation. The specific function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: SOV1 mitochondrial N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal TPR domain found in mitochondrial protein SOV1 and related proteins. SOV1 is a mitochondrial protein involved in translation activator activity and mitochondrial ribosome assembly [[PMID:14562095],[PMID:14576278]]. SOV1 localises to mitochondria and plays a role in mitochondrial translation. The specific function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: Beta-catenin homolog sys-1 C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of beta-catenin homolog proteins from nematodes. The protein is a transcription coregulator that is part of the Wnt signalling asymmetry pathway [[PMID:18477457],[PMID:17296929],[PMID:26073017]]. The protein interacts with TCF transcription factor POP-1 and is involved in asymmetric cell divisions and cell fate specification. Despite low sequence similarity to canonical beta-catenins, the protein displays functional and structural similarities and is considered a highly divergent beta-catenin [[PMID:18477457],[PMID:17296929]]. The specific function of this C-terminal domain remains to be determined. This domain contains TPR-like fold.

Name: Beta-catenin homolog sys-1 N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the middle domain of beta-catenin homolog proteins from nematodes. The protein is a transcription coregulator that is part of the Wnt signalling asymmetry pathway [[PMID:18477457],[PMID:17296929],[PMID:26073017]]. The protein interacts with TCF transcription factor POP-1 and is involved in asymmetric cell divisions and cell fate specification. Despite low sequence similarity to canonical beta-catenins, the protein displays functional and structural similarities and is considered a highly divergent beta-catenin [[PMID:18477457],[PMID:17296929]]. The specific function of this domain remains to be determined. This domain contains a TPR-like fold.

Name: Beta-catenin homolog sys-1 middle terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the middle domain of beta-catenin homolog proteins from nematodes. The protein is a transcription coregulator that is part of the Wnt signalling asymmetry pathway [[PMID:18477457],[PMID:17296929],[PMID:26073017]]. The protein interacts with TCF transcription factor POP-1 and is involved in asymmetric cell divisions and cell fate specification. Despite low sequence similarity to canonical beta-catenins, the protein displays functional and structural similarities and is considered a highly divergent beta-catenin [[PMID:18477457],[PMID:17296929]]. The specific function of this middle domain remains to be determined.

Name: MRX5 C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents a C-terminal domain found in MRX5 and related proteins from fungi. MRX5 is a component of MIOREX complexes, which are large expressome-like assemblies of ribosomes with factors involved in all steps of post-transcriptional gene expression in mitochondria [[PMID:25683707]]. The protein associates with the mitochondrial ribosome and localises to mitochondria [[PMID:25683707]]. MRX5 is present at moderate levels in cells and plays a role in mitochondrial gene expression organisation [[PMID:25683707]]. The function of this C-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: MRX5 middle domain
Description: This entry represents a middle domain found in MRX5 and related proteins from fungi. MRX5 is a component of MIOREX complexes, which are large expressome-like assemblies of ribosomes with factors involved in all steps of post-transcriptional gene expression in mitochondria [[PMID:25683707]]. The protein associates with the mitochondrial ribosome and localises to mitochondria [[PMID:25683707]]. MRX5 is present at moderate levels in cells and plays a role in mitochondrial gene expression organisation [[PMID:25683707]]. The function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: Anionic antimicrobial peptide 2
Description: This entry represents an anionic antimicrobial peptide family found in Lepidoptera. These peptides are secreted into the hemolymph and are induced by bacterial infection [[PMID:17194500]]. The peptides exhibit selective antibacterial activity against certain Gram-positive bacteria including M.luteus, L.monocytogenes, and S.lutea, whilst lacking activity against B.circulans, S.aureus, and tested Gram-negative bacteria [[PMID:17194500]]. They also display antifungal activity against P.pastoris and P.stipitis but not against other tested fungal species [[PMID:17194500]]. These peptides function as part of the innate immune response in insects.

Name: Protein phosphatase 4 regulatory subunit 1 N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of protein phosphatase 4 regulatory subunit 1 (PPFR-1). The full-length protein is a regulatory subunit of serine/threonine-protein phosphatase PP4 that plays a role in meiosis and embryonic mitosis [[PMID:19087961],[PMID:23918937]]. PPFR-1 regulates microtubule severing during oocyte meiosis II by dephosphorylating and activating MEI-1, a component of the katanin microtubule severing complex [[PMID:19087961],[PMID:23918937]]. The protein associates with catalytic subunit PPH-4.1 and interacts with MEL-26 via its C-terminal region [[PMID:19087961],[PMID:23918937]]. PPFR-1 is found in nematodes and localises to the cytoplasm during meiosis and mitosis. The specific function of this N-terminal domain remains to be determined. PPFR-1 is longer than its animal orthologues. This domain contains a TPR-like fold.

Name: TGFB1-induced anti-apoptotic factor 1
Description: This entry represents TIAF1 (TGFB1-induced anti-apoptotic factor 1), a small protein originally identified from human mammary gland tissue [[PMID:9918798]]. TIAF1 inhibits the cytotoxic effects of TNF-alpha and overexpressed TNF receptor adapters TRADD, FADD, and RIPK1 [[PMID:9918798]]. The protein is involved in TGF-beta1 inhibition of IkappaB-alpha expression and suppression of TNF- mediated IkappaB-alpha degradation [[PMID:9918798]]. TIAF1 localises to the nucleus and is up-regulated in chronic and acute allograft rejection, being expressed in inflammatory infiltrate and tubular epithelial cells [[PMID:9918798]]. However, this protein is the product of a dubious gene prediction, being coded in the 3'-UTR region of MYO18A and does not appear to exist in orthologous sequences.

Name: Protein phosphatase 4 regulatory subunit 1 middle domain
Description: This entry represents the middle domain of protein phosphatase 4 regulatory subunit 1 (PPFR-1). The full-length protein is a regulatory subunit of serine/threonine-protein phosphatase PP4 that plays a role in meiosis and embryonic mitosis [[PMID:19087961],[PMID:23918937]]. PPFR-1 regulates microtubule severing during oocyte meiosis II by dephosphorylating and activating MEI-1, a component of the katanin microtubule severing complex [[PMID:19087961],[PMID:23918937]]. The protein associates with catalytic subunit PPH-4.1 and interacts with MEL-26 via its C-terminal region [[PMID:19087961],[PMID:23918937]]. PPFR-1 is found in nematodes and localises to the cytoplasm during meiosis and mitosis.

Name: Protein three rows C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of protein three rows (THR). The full-length protein is required specifically for chromosome disjunction during all mitoses and is involved in formation and maintenance of epithelial structures [[PMID:8270646]]. THR interacts with PIM and SSE and is proteolytically cleaved after the metaphase-to-anaphase transition [[PMID:11581162],[PMID:12231632]]. The protein localises to the cytoplasm during interphase and is distributed throughout the cell during early mitosis [[PMID:12231632]]. THR is found in Diptera and plays essential roles in mitotic sister chromatid segregation and epithelial morphogenesis. The specific function of this C-terminal domain remains to be determined. This domain contains a TPR-like fold.

Name: Protein three rows middle domain
Description: This entry represents the middle domain of protein three rows (THR). The full-length protein is required specifically for chromosome disjunction during all mitoses and is involved in formation and maintenance of epithelial structures [[PMID:8270646]]. THR interacts with PIM and SSE and is proteolytically cleaved after the metaphase-to-anaphase transition [[PMID:11581162],[PMID:12231632]]. The protein localises to the cytoplasm during interphase and is distributed throughout the cell during early mitosis [[PMID:12231632]]. THR is found in Diptera and plays essential roles in mitotic sister chromatid segregation and epithelial morphogenesis. The specific function of this middle domain remains to be determined. This domain contains a TPR-like fold.

Name: Protein three rows N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of protein three rows (THR). The full-length protein is required specifically for chromosome disjunction during all mitoses and is involved in formation and maintenance of epithelial structures [[PMID:8270646]]. THR interacts with PIM and SSE and is proteolytically cleaved after the metaphase-to-anaphase transition [[PMID:11581162],[PMID:12231632]]. The protein localises to the cytoplasm during interphase and is distributed throughout the cell during early mitosis [[PMID:12231632]]. THR is found in Diptera and plays essential roles in mitotic sister chromatid segregation and epithelial morphogenesis. The specific function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: Seroin C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents a C-terminal domain found in seroin, a silk protein from lepidopteran insects, particularly Galleria mellonella. Seroin is a secreted glycoprotein produced by silk glands and is involved in silk production [[PMID:9624126]]. The protein contains repeat regions and N-linked glycosylation sites. Seroin expression is regulated during development, reaching maximum levels during cocoon spinning [[PMID:9624126]]. The function of this C-terminal domain remains to be determined. This domain contains a beta-sheet fold.

Name: ATG41
Description: This entry represents a domain found in autophagy-related protein 41 (ATG41) from yeasts. ATG41 is involved in autophagosome formation and interacts with ATG9 [[PMID:26565778]]. ATG41 localises to preautophagosomal structure membranes and shows peri-mitochondrial distribution [[PMID:26565778]]. This domain contains helical and disordered regions.

Name: Autophagy-related protein 32 middle domain
Description: This entry represents a middle domain in autophagy-related protein 32 (ATG32) found in yeasts. ATG32 is a mitophagy-specific receptor that recruits the autophagic machinery to mitochondria and regulates selective degradation of mitochondria [[PMID:19619494]]. ATG32 is localised to the mitochondrial outer membrane and interacts with ATG8 and ATG11 [[PMID:19619494],[PMID:22308029]].  The protein is phosphorylated at serine residues which is critical for mitophagy and ATG11 interaction [[PMID:22308029]]. ATG32 also promotes autophagy-dependent peroxisome degradation.

Name: Fimbrial assembly protein FimV N-terminal TPR region
Description: This entry represents a small TPR region found in the fimbrial assembly protein FimV from Acinetobacter species. FimV proteins are involved in type IV pilus biogenesis and are predicted to be inner membrane proteins. In Acinetobacter baumannii, the protein encoded by locus A1S_0425 contributes to abiotic surface colonisation and biofilm formation.

Name: Peptide arginase FlmR/OhkR-like domain
Description: This entry represents a small domain found in FlmR from Flagellimonas meridianipacifica (Swiss A0A2T0MFS9), OhkR from Ohtaekwangia koreensis (Swiss A0A1T5M544) and similar uncharacterised proteins mainly found in bacteria and lower eukaryotes. FlmR and OhkR have been reported to be peptide arginases that modify Arg residues to ornithine in bioactive RiPP peptides [[PMID:41201059]]. This domain, which is found associated with [pfam:PF12640], is predicted to show an antiparallel three-strand beta-sheet with two alpha-helices on one side.

Name: Acylaminoacyl-peptidase, N-terminal beta-propeller domain
Description: This is the N-terminal beta-propeller domain of the putative Acylaminoacyl-peptidase ApeH-3 from Saccharolobus solfataricus, a stress-regulated protein that catalyses the removal of acetylated amino acid residues from the N terminus of oligopeptides [[PMID:22655081]].

Name: Cytochrome B pre-mRNA-processing protein middle domain
Description: This entry represents the middle domain of cytochrome B pre-mRNA- processing protein 1 (CBP1) found in fungi. CBP1 is responsible for conferring a stable 5'-end on cytochrome b mRNA and is localised to mitochondria [[PMID:6325407]]. The protein is involved in mRNA processing and binds mRNA. CBP1 contains multiple domains including this middle domain which may adopt a TPR-like fold. The specific function of this middle domain remains to be determined.

Name: Importin beta-like KAP122 middle domain
Description: This entry represents the middle domain of importin beta-like protein KAP122 found in yeasts. KAP122 is a nuclear transport factor involved in protein transport between the cytoplasm and nucleoplasm [[PMID:1882553]]. The protein is required for nuclear import of transcription factor IIA subunits (TOA1 and TOA2) and the ribonucleotide reductase small subunit (RNR2-RNR4 heterodimer) [[PMID:1882553],[PMID:10525531]]. KAP122 belongs to the importin beta family and associates with the nuclear pore complex. The specific function of this middle domain remains to be determined.

Name: Cytochrome B pre-mRNA-processing N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of cytochrome B pre-mRNA- processing protein 1 (CBP1) found in yeasts. CBP1 is responsible for conferring a stable 5'-end on cytochrome b mRNA and is localised to mitochondria [[PMID:6325407]]. The protein is involved in mRNA processing and binds mRNA. CBP1 contains multiple domains including this middle domain which may adopt a TPR-like fold. The specific function of this middle domain remains to be determined.

Name: Mu-thomitoxin
Description: This entry represents mu-thomitoxin, a neurotoxin found in the venom of the crab spider Heriaeus melloteei [[PMID:18606177]]. These toxins block specific voltage-gated sodium channels including Nav1.2, Nav1.4, and Nav1.6, reducing peak sodium current amplitude and negatively shifting steady-state inactivation [[PMID:18606177]]. The toxins contain three disulfide bonds and exhibit a knottin fold characterised by a disulfide through disulfide knot structure [[PMID:18606177]]. Family members belong to the neurotoxin 01 family and are secreted as venom components with C-terminal amidation [[PMID:18606177]].

Name: ATP synthase subunit p18 TPR-like domain
Description: This entry represents a tetratricopeptide repeat (TPR)-like domain found in the ATP synthase subunit p18 of trypanosomatids. The p18 subunit is an additional component of the F1-ATPase complex that is not present in metazoan ATP synthases [[PMID:19436713],[PMID:29247468]]. This subunit interacts directly with the alpha subunit in a 1:1 stoichiometry and is essential for proper assembly of the F1 component [[PMID:29440423]].

Name: CadC C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal TPR-like domain of CadC which forms the periplasmic doamin together with [pfam:PF18500] [[PMID:21308846]]. CadC is a transcriptional activator that regulates the lysine- and pH-dependent expression of the lysine decarboxylase CadA [[PMID:21216950]].

Name: MRX4 C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of MIOREX complex component 4 (MRX4). MRX4 is a component of MIOREX complexes, large expressome-like assemblies of ribosomes with factors involved in all the steps of post-transcriptional gene expression [[PMID:25683707]]. The protein associates with the mitochondrial ribosome and is localised to mitochondrial membranes [[PMID:25683707]]. MRX4 contains a mitochondrial transit peptide and a transmembrane helix. The protein is induced during the M phase of the cell cycle. This C-terminal domain is found in fungi and is part of the mitochondrial gene expression machinery.

Name: PET494 N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain in PET494 COX3 mRNA-specific translational activators. Members are required for the expression of the mitochondrial gene for cytochrome c oxidase subunit III (COX3) [[PMID:3010052],[PMID:8389363]]. The proteins are associated with the mitochondrial inner membrane as peripheral membrane proteins and regulate mitochondrial translation [[PMID:8389363]].

Name: PET494 C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain in PET494 COX3 mRNA-specific translational activators. Members are required for the expression of the mitochondrial gene for cytochrome c oxidase subunit III (COX3) [[PMID:3010052],[PMID:8389363]]. The proteins are associated with the mitochondrial inner membrane as peripheral membrane proteins and regulate mitochondrial translation [[PMID:8389363]]. The family is found in Saccharomycetaceae yeasts.

Name: Microplusin antimicrobial peptide
Description: This entry represents the microplusin family of antimicrobial peptides. Members have bacteriostatic activity against Gram-positive bacteria and fungistatic activity against some fungi. The peptides bind and sequester copper and iron ions, with copper-chelating being crucial for antimicrobial activity. The proteins are secreted and contain multiple disulfide bonds. The family is found in ticks (Ixodidae) [[PMID:22092765],[PMID:19828445]].

Name: Defensin-like protein 301 C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of defensin-like protein 301 and related proteins in plants. The protein belongs to the DEFL family and is predicted to be secreted [[PMID:15955924]]. Defensin-like proteins are involved in plant defence responses against fungi and have antimicrobial activity. This protein contains six disulfide bonds instead of the four typically conserved in the family. The function of this C-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: SaeB, TPR-like domain
Description: This entry represents a TPR-like domain found at the C-terminal of the Putative ESX-1 scaffolding and assembly protein SaeB. SaeB may be involved in assembly of the ESX-1/type VII specialized secretion system (TSS7), which exports several proteins including EsxA and EsxB. It is involved in DNA conjugation in recipient (MKD8) but not donor (mc2155) strain [[PMID:18554329]].

Name: Defensin-like protein 293
Description: This entry represents defensin-like protein 293 (DEF293), a member of the DEFL family found in Brassicaceae, particularly in Arabidopsis, Brassica and Eutrema species [[PMID:17565583]]. DEF293 is a secreted antimicrobial peptide involved in plant defence responses, particularly against fungal pathogens [[PMID:17565583]]. The protein contains disulphide bonds that are essential for its structure and activity. DEF293 lacks one of the four disulphide bonds that are typically conserved in the family. More than 300 defensin-like genes are present in the Arabidopsis genome [[PMID:15955924]].q

Name: CTF13 N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain in centromere DNA-binding protein complex CBF3 subunit C, also known as CTF13 (chromosome transmission fidelity protein 13). This protein is found in Saccharomycetaceae family yeasts. CTF13 acts as a central component of the CBF3 complex, which is essential for chromosome segregation and movement of centromeres along microtubules [[PMID:8500169]]. The CBF3 complex is required for the recruitment of other kinetochore complexes to centromeric DNA and binds selectively to the highly conserved CDEIII DNA sequence found in centromeres [[PMID:8500169]]. The association of CBF3C with CBF3D and SGT1 is required for CBF3C activation and CBF3 assembly [[PMID:15090617]]. CTF13 contains leucine-rich repeat domains and is localised to the nucleus and centromere.

Name: CTF13 C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain in centromere DNA-binding protein complex CBF3 subunit C, also known as CTF13 (chromosome transmission fidelity protein 13). This protein is found in Saccharomycetaceae family yeasts. CTF13 acts as a central component of the CBF3 complex, which is essential for chromosome segregation and movement of centromeres along microtubules [[PMID:8500169]]. The CBF3 complex is required for the recruitment of other kinetochore complexes to centromeric DNA and binds selectively to the highly conserved CDEIII DNA sequence found in centromeres [[PMID:8500169]]. The association of CBF3C with CBF3D and SGT1 is required for CBF3C activation and CBF3 assembly [[PMID:15090617]]. CTF13 contains leucine-rich repeat domains and is localised to the nucleus and centromere. This C-terminal contains an alpha-beta fold formed by insertions within the latter LRRs of Ctf13 (LRR insertion domain).

Name: Daisho antimicrobial peptide
Description: This entry represents the Daisho antimicrobial peptide family found in Drosophila species. These small secreted peptides play a role in the humoral immune response against a subset of filamentous fungi, including Fusarium oxysporum and Fusarium verticillioides [[PMID:32038657]]. The peptides are expressed as precursors with signal sequences and are processed to mature forms. They are induced by bacterial and fungal infection and are found in the hemolymph [[PMID:9736738],[PMID:32038657]]. The name 'Daisho' refers to the Japanese term for a matched pair of samurai swords and reflects the paired role of Dso1 and Dso2 peptides in antifungal defence [[PMID:32038657]]. The family is found in Diptera, particularly within the Drosophilidae.

Name: Small ribosomal subunit protein mS46 C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of the small ribosomal subunit protein mS46 (RSM28), found in fungal mitochondrial ribosomes. RSM28 is a component of the mitochondrial small ribosomal subunit (mt- SSU) which forms part of the mitoribosome, the dedicated translation machinery responsible for synthesis of mitochondrial genome-encoded proteins [[PMID:15701796],[PMID:28154081]]. The mature yeast 37S small subunit contains 15S ribosomal RNA and 34 different proteins [[PMID:28154081]]. The mitoribosomes are attached to the mitochondrial inner membrane and translation products are cotranslationally integrated into the membrane [[PMID:28154081]]. The specific function of this C-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: Small ribosomal subunit protein mS46 middle domain
Description: This entry represents the middle domain of the small ribosomal subunit protein mS46 (RSM28), found in fungal mitochondrial ribosomes. RSM28 is a component of the mitochondrial small ribosomal subunit (mt- SSU) which forms part of the mitoribosome, the dedicated translation machinery responsible for synthesis of mitochondrial genome-encoded proteins [[PMID:15701796],[PMID:28154081]]. The mature yeast 37S small subunit contains 15S ribosomal RNA and 34 different proteins [[PMID:28154081]]. The mitoribosomes are attached to the mitochondrial inner membrane and translation products are cotranslationally integrated into the membrane [[PMID:28154081]]. The specific function of this middle domain remains to be determined.

Name: Putative defensin-like protein 39
Description: This entry represents defensin 39 found in plants. Defensins are characterised by multiple disulphide bonds that provide structural stability. These proteins are secreted and are involved in plant defence responses against fungi [[PMID:15955924]]. The proteins contain antimicrobial and fungicidal activities. Some members are precursor proteins with signal peptides for secretion to the extracellular region.

Name: ATPase expression protein 2, mitochondrial N-terminal
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain in ATPase expression protein 2 (AEP2) found in fungi. These proteins are required for translation of the mitochondrial OLI1 transcript coding for the mitochondrial ATP synthase subunit 9 [[PMID:1825065],[PMID:1718609]]. This protein contains a TPR-like fold.

Name: Mitochondrial outer membrane protein OM45
Description: This entry represents mitochondrial outer membrane protein OM45 found in fungi. This protein is localised to the mitochondrial outer membrane and contains a single transmembrane helix [[PMID:2687271]]. The protein interacts with AIM5, FCJ1 and MOS1 [[PMID:21987634]]. Despite being a major outer membrane protein, OM45 is not essential for cell growth or mitochondrial function [[PMID:2687271]]. The protein contains disordered regions and is oriented with its C-terminal domain in the cytoplasm and a small N-terminal portion in the mitochondrial intermembrane space.

Name: AtMg00590 C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of the uncharacterised mitochondrial cytochrome b-like protein AtMg00590 from plants. The full-length protein belongs to the cytochrome b family and is a multi- pass membrane protein localised to the mitochondrial membrane [[PMID:8988169]]. The protein is encoded by the mitochondrial genome and may be involved in respiratory electron transport chain based on sequence similarity. A 270 kb stretch of the mitochondrial genome containing this gene is duplicated within the centromere of chromosome 2, and expression of both copies has been demonstrated [[PMID:8988169]]. This C-terminal domain is found downstream of the transmembrane helices that characterise the cytochrome b-like proteins. While the complete protein is predicted to function in electron transport, the specific function of this C- terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: TBCD C-terminal domain
Description: This is the C-terminal region of armadillo (ARM)-like repeats found in yeast Tubulin-specific chaperone D (TBCD) and its homologues in eukaryotes. Tubulin-folding cofactor D (TBCD) can interact with beta tubulin, which is regulated via its interaction with ARL2 (ADP ribosylation factor-like protein 2), a small monomeric G protein [[PMID:26208336]].

Name: VID28/GID5 N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of VID28/GID5, a component of the GID (glucose-induced degradation) E3 ubiquitin ligase complex. VID28 is found in fungi, particularly within the Saccharomycetaceae family [[PMID:12686616],[PMID:31708416]]. The GID complex mediates proteasome-dependent catabolite degradation of gluconeogenic enzymes such as fructose-1,6- bisphosphatase when yeast cells are shifted to glucose-containing medium [[PMID:12686616],[PMID:31708416],[PMID:22645139]]. Within the GID complex, VID28 interacts directly with RMD5/GID2 and FYV10/GID9, and recruits the substrate receptor VID24/ GID4 to form the active E3 ligase complex [[PMID:31708416],[PMID:22645139]].

Name: Stationary phase gene 1 protein
Description: This entry represents the SPG1 (stationary phase gene 1) protein. SPG1 is a small transmembrane protein found in fungi [[PMID:15456898],[PMID:14576278]]. The protein is localised to the mitochondrial membrane and contains a single transmembrane helix [[PMID:14576278]]. SPG1 is abundantly expressed during stationary phase (G0) in yeast cells, and its mRNA levels rapidly decrease upon exit from this growth phase [[PMID:15456898]]. The protein is associated with the cellular response to nutrient limitation and stationary phase conditions. The specific function of this protein family remains to be determined.

Name: VID28/GID5 C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of VID28/GID5, a component of the GID (glucose-induced degradation) E3 ubiquitin ligase complex. VID28 is found in fungi, particularly within the Saccharomycetaceae family [[PMID:12686616],[PMID:31708416]]. The GID complex mediates proteasome-dependent catabolite degradation of gluconeogenic enzymes such as fructose-1,6- bisphosphatase when yeast cells are shifted to glucose-containing medium [[PMID:12686616],[PMID:31708416],[PMID:22645139]]. Within the GID complex, VID28 interacts directly with RMD5/GID2 and FYV10/GID9, and recruits the substrate receptor VID24/ GID4 to form the active E3 ligase complex [[PMID:31708416],[PMID:22645139]].

Name: YCF15 A/B chloroplast
Description: This entry represents the YCF15 (ycf15) protein family found in chloroplast genomes. YCF15 is a small protein found in plants, particularly within the Viridiplantae lineage including eudicotyledons and monocotyledons. The protein is encoded by chloroplast DNA and is localised to the chloroplast. YCF15 is present in various plant families including Asteraceae, Malvaceae, and Fabaceae. The protein may exist as duplicated copies (ycf15-A and ycf15-B) in some species. In some lineages, ycf15 may represent a pseudogene that has lost function during evolution. The specific function of this chloroplast protein family remains to be determined [[PMID:11292076]].

Name: MSC6 C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal tetratricopeptide repeat (TPR) domain of MSC6 (Meiotic sister-chromatid recombination protein 6). MSC6 is a mitochondrial protein involved in the control of meiotic sister- chromatid recombination [[PMID:10511544]]. The protein is found in fungi and localises to the mitochondrial matrix.

Name: GID12 WD40 beta-propeller domain
Description: This entry represents the WD40 beta-propeller domain of GID12, a regulator of the GID E3 ubiquitin ligase complex. GID12 is found in fungi, particularly within the Saccharomycetaceae family [[PMID:35650207],[PMID:32064787]]. This beta-propeller domain is responsible for protein-protein interactions within the GID complex, binding both the substrate receptor GID4 and the tip of GID5 in the scaffolding module [[PMID:35650207]]. The domain enables GID12 to seal GID4 onto the scaffold and remodel the N-degron binding pocket in the GID complex [[PMID:35650207]]. GID12 modulates the assembly and activity of the GID E3 ligase complex, potentially limiting substrate accessibility to ubiquitination sites and thereby stabilising gluconeogenic enzyme substrates [[PMID:35650207]]. The protein is also involved in actin patch formation [[PMID:32064787]].

Name: R-phycoerythrin gamma chain
Description: This entry represents the gamma chain of R-phycoerythrin, found in red algae (Rhodophyta) [[PMID:8344905]]. R-phycoerythrin is a phycobiliprotein component of the phycobilisome complex, which functions as the major light- harvesting antenna complex in red algal photosynthesis [[PMID:8344905]]. The gamma chain is critical for the incorporation of phycoerythrin into the phycobilisome complex and forms part of a heteromeric structure composed of one alpha, one beta, and two gamma chains [[PMID:8344905]]. The protein is localised to the chloroplast thylakoid membrane and forms the periphery of the phycobilisome rod [[PMID:8344905]]. The gamma chain contains four covalently linked bilin chromophores including phycourobilin and phycoerythrobilin which are essential for light absorption and energy transfer during photosynthesis. The protein is synthesised with a chloroplast transit peptide that directs its import into the plastid.

Name: PAC11 dynein intermediate chain
Description: This entry represents the PAC11 family, found in fungi, particularly within the Saccharomycetaceae [[PMID:11266443]]. PAC11 is a WD repeat-containing protein that functions as a dynein intermediate chain component of the cytoplasmic dynein complex [[PMID:11266443]]. The protein is required for viability in the absence of the kinesin-related CIN8 mitotic motor and is essential for proper dynein function [[PMID:11266443]]. PAC11 interacts with NUM1 when DYN1 is present and localises to the cytoplasm and cytoskeleton [[PMID:11266443]]. The protein plays important roles in nuclear migration along microtubules, establishment of mitotic spindle localisation and orientation, and transport along microtubules [[PMID:11266443]]. PAC11 contains WD repeats that form a beta-propeller structure typical of this protein family. The protein is essential for proper dynein heavy chain and light chain binding and is a critical component of the dynein motor complex in fungal cells.

Name: YpiA-like, C-terminal tetratricopeptide repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found at the C-terminal end of YpiA from Bacillus subtilis, EF_1553 from Enterococcus faecalis and similar proteins mainly found in firmicutes.

Name: YpiA-like, tetratricopeptide repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found in YpiA from Bacillus subtilis, EF_1553 from Enterococcus faecalis and similar proteins mainly found in firmicutes.

Name: Deinococcus radiodurans protein DR_A0282, C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of DR_A0282, a minor subunit of the S-layer deinoxanthin-binding complex (SDBC) from Deinococcus radiodurans. The SDBC is a porin-like channel complex involved in selective ion permeability across the bacterial cell surface layer [[PMID:32071085]]. DR_A0282 shows sequence similarity to peptidases and is proposed to participate in protein substrate processing [[PMID:32071085]]. The specific function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: Par, wHTH
Description: This entry represents the wHTH domain of Par protein from multi-resistance plasmid pSK1 [[PMID:35907571]]. pSK1 Par binds a centromere consisting of seven repeat elements. It enhances the separation of plasmids from clusters, driving effective segregation upon cell division [[PMID:35907571]].

Name: MRX9 transmembrane helix
Description: This entry represents the transmembrane helix of MRX9 (MIOREX complex component 9), a mitochondrial protein found in fungi. MRX9 is a component of MIOREX complexes, which are large expressome-like assemblies of ribosomes with factors involved in post- transcriptional gene expression [[PMID:25683707]]. The full-length protein is a multi-pass transmembrane protein localised to mitochondrial membranes and associates with the mitochondrial ribosome.

Name: LAG2 cullin-binding protein N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of LAG2, a cullin- associated NEDD8-dissociated protein homologue. This domain is found in proteins involved in SCF E3 ubiquitin ligase complex regulation from fungi. LAG2 acts as an assembly factor and F-box protein exchange factor for SCF complexes and interacts with unneddylated cullin CDC53 [[PMID:19763088],[PMID:19942853]]. The domain contains a neddylation site and is involved in longevity assurance. The function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: LAG2 cullin-binding protein C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of LAG2, a cullin- associated NEDD8-dissociated protein homologue. This domain is found in proteins involved in SCF E3 ubiquitin ligase complex regulation from fungi. LAG2 acts as an assembly factor and F-box protein exchange factor for SCF complexes and interacts with unneddylated cullin CDC53 [[PMID:19763088],[PMID:19942853]]. The domain contains a neddylation site and is involved in longevity assurance. The function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: WRM-1 armadillo protein N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal TPR-like region of WRM-1 armadillo proteins. This domain is found in beta-catenin-like proteins from Caenorhabditis nematodes involved in Wnt signalling pathways. The domain directly interacts with LIT-1 kinase and activates its kinase activity, leading to phosphorylation of transcription factor POP-1 [[PMID:9288750],[PMID:10380924]]. WRM-1 proteins are components of the beta-catenin-lit-1 complex and play roles in asymmetric cell division and embryogenesis. The function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: WRM-1 armadillo protein C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of WRM-1 armadillo proteins. This domain is found in beta-catenin-like proteins from Caenorhabditis nematodes involved in Wnt signalling pathways. The protein directly interacts with LIT-1 kinase and activates its kinase activity, leading to phosphorylation of transcription factor POP-1 [[PMID:9288750],[PMID:10380924]]. WRM-1 proteins are components of the beta-catenin-lit-1 complex and play roles in asymmetric cell division and embryogenesis. The function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: MSS18
Description: This entry represents the MSS18 protein family. These proteins are found in fungi, particularly in the Saccharomycetaceae family. The proteins are involved in splicing of intron aI5-beta of the mitochondrial COX1 transcript [[PMID:2842150]]. MSS18 proteins localise to mitochondria and are involved in mRNA processing and splicing. The function of this family remains to be determined.

Name: DEF265
Description: This entry represents defensin 265 from plants. These domains are found in secreted antimicrobial proteins that function in plant defense responses against fungi. The mature domain contains three conserved disulfide bonds, though some family members lack one of the typical four bonds. The domain is involved in defense response to fungus and killing of cells of other organisms [[PMID:15955924]]. Over 300 defensin-like genes are present in the Arabidopsis genome [[PMID:15955924]].

Name: PIF8 N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain found in PIF8 and related ATP-dependent DNA helicase-like proteins. The domain is found in trypanosomatids and related kinetoplastid parasites. PIF8 proteins are required for the maintenance of mitochondrial kinetoplast genome stability and localise to the mitochondrion matrix. Despite being helicase-like, these proteins probably lack helicase activity as they are missing at least two of the seven conserved helicase motifs [[PMID:22220754]]. The proteins belong to the helicase family, PIF1 subfamily, and are essential for cell viability.

Name: PRD1-like, TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found in Protein PUTATIVE RECOMBINATION INITIATION DEFECT 1 from Arabidopsis thaliana (PRD1) and similar sequences. PRD1 is involved in DNA cleavage that forms the double-strand breaks (DSB) that initiate meiotic recombination [[PMID:17762870],[PMID:19763177]].

Name: C30G12.6 N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain found in C30G12.6 and related uncharacterised proteins from nematodes, including Caenorhabditis and Ancylostoma species. The proteins contain ARM-type fold. The proteins undergo alternative splicing and contain multiple disordered regions. The function of this N-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: Mitochondrial HMG-box protein CIM1
Description: This entry represents the CIM1 (Copy number influence on Mtdna protein 1) family of mitochondrial HMG-box proteins found in fungi. CIM1 limits the copy number of mitochondrial DNA by antagonising ABF2, a mtDNA packaging factor [[PMID:37850632]]. The protein contains two HMG-box regions (A and B) and localises to the mitochondrial matrix. CIM1 is synthesised as a precursor with a mitochondrial transit peptide and is involved in mitochondrial genome maintenance. Disruption of CIM1 leads to increased mitochondrial DNA copy number.

Name: PRD1-like, TPR repeats
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found in Protein PUTATIVE RECOMBINATION INITIATION DEFECT 1 from Arabidopsis thaliana (PRD1) and similar sequences. PRD1 is involved in DNA cleavage that forms the double-strand breaks (DSB) that initiate meiotic recombination [[PMID:17762870],[PMID:19763177]].

Name: Arminin antimicrobial peptide precursor
Description: This entry represents the arminin family of antimicrobial peptide precursors found in freshwater cnidarians of the genus Hydra. The precursor proteins contain a signal peptide, a propeptide region, and a C-terminal mature antimicrobial peptide that undergoes amidation. The mature arminin peptides display broad-spectrum bactericidal activity against both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria, including multiresistant pathogens such as methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA) and vancomycin-resistant enterococci [[PMID:19770277]]. The peptides maintain antibacterial activity under physiological salt conditions [[PMID:19770277]]. Arminins are expressed in the entodermal epithelium and are secreted as part of the innate immune response [[PMID:19770277],[PMID:24003149]].

Name: Uncharacterised protein YJR039W N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of the uncharacterised protein YJR039W from Saccharomyces cerevisiae. The full-length protein has been detected in the mitochondrion by high-throughput studies. This domain is found in Saccharomycetaceae fungi. The function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: Uncharacterised protein YJR039W middle domain
Description: This entry represents the middle domain of the uncharacterised protein YJR039W from Saccharomyces cerevisiae. The full-length protein has been detected in the mitochondrion by high-throughput studies. This domain is found in Saccharomycetaceae fungi. The function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: Uncharacterised protein YJR039W C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of the uncharacterised protein YJR039W from Saccharomyces cerevisiae. The full-length protein has been detected in the mitochondrion by high-throughput studies. This domain is found in Saccharomycetaceae fungi. The function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: Mitochondrial 3' processome subunit 2 N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of mitochondrial 3' processome subunit 2 (MPSS2), found in kinetoplastid parasites of the family Trypanosomatidae including Trypanosoma and Leishmania species. MPSS2 is a component of the mitochondrial 3' processome (MPsome) complex, which also includes terminal uridylyltransferase KRET1, 3'-5' exonuclease DSS1, MPSS1 and MPSS3 [[PMID:26833087]]. The MPsome is involved in the maturation of guide RNA (gRNA) precursors in the mitochondrion [[PMID:26833087]]. MPSS2 interacts directly with DSS1 within the complex [[PMID:26833087]]. The specific function of this N-terminal domain remains to be determined. It contains a TPR-like fold.

Name: Mitochondrial 3' processome subunit 2 middle domain
Description: This entry represents the middle domain of mitochondrial 3' processome subunit 2 (MPSS2), found in kinetoplastid parasites of the family Trypanosomatidae including Trypanosoma and Leishmania species.  MPSS2 is a component of the mitochondrial 3' processome (MPsome) complex, which also includes terminal uridylyltransferase KRET1, 3'-5' exonuclease DSS1, MPSS1 and MPSS3 [[PMID:26833087]]. The MPsome is involved in the maturation of guide RNA (gRNA) precursors in the mitochondrion [[PMID:26833087]]. MPSS2 interacts directly with DSS1 within the complex [[PMID:26833087]]. The specific function of this middle domain remains to be determined. It contains a TPR-like fold.

Name: Mitochondrial 3' processome subunit 2 C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of mitochondrial 3' processome subunit 2 (MPSS2), found in kinetoplastid parasites of the family Trypanosomatidae including Trypanosoma and Leishmania species. MPSS2 is a component of the mitochondrial 3' processome (MPsome) complex, which also includes terminal uridylyltransferase KRET1, 3'-5' exonuclease DSS1, MPSS1 and MPSS3 [[PMID:26833087]]. The MPsome is involved in the maturation of guide RNA (gRNA) precursors in the mitochondrion [[PMID:26833087]]. MPSS2 interacts directly with DSS1 within the complex [[PMID:26833087]]. The specific function of this C-terminal domain remains to be determined. It contains a TPR-like fold.

Name: TRAPPC10/Trs130, NTS-like domain
Description: This entry represents a TPR domain found in TRAPPC10 from animals and its homologues from plants, Trs130. This domain is the analogue of [pfam:PF24967] from yeast. These proteins are subunits of the TRAPP II complex and function in late Golgi trafficking as a membrane tether [[PMID:20972447],[PMID:23078654]].

Name: Mitochondrial 3' processome subunit 1 N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of mitochondrial 3' processome subunit 1 (MPSS1), found in kinetoplastid parasites of the family Trypanosomatidae including Trypanosoma and Leishmania species. MPSS1 is a component of the mitochondrial 3' processome (MPsome) complex, which also includes terminal uridylyltransferase KRET1, 3'-5' exonuclease DSS1, MPSS2 and MPSS3 [[PMID:26833087]]. The MPsome is involved in the maturation of guide RNA (gRNA) precursors in the mitochondrion [[PMID:26833087]]. MPSS1 interacts directly with KRET1 within the complex [[PMID:26833087]]. The specific function of this N-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: Mitochondrial 3' processome subunit 1 middle domain
Description: This entry represents the middle domain of mitochondrial 3' processome subunit 1 (MPSS1), found in kinetoplastid parasites of the family Trypanosomatidae including Trypanosoma and Leishmania species.  MPSS1 is a component of the mitochondrial 3' processome (MPsome) complex, which also includes terminal uridylyltransferase KRET1, 3'-5' exonuclease DSS1, MPSS2 and MPSS3 [[PMID:26833087]]. The MPsome is involved in the maturation of guide RNA (gRNA) precursors in the mitochondrion [[PMID:26833087]]. MPSS1 interacts directly with KRET1 within the complex [[PMID:26833087]].

Name: Mitochondrial 3' processome subunit 1 C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of mitochondrial 3' processome subunit 1 (MPSS1), found in kinetoplastid parasites of the family Trypanosomatidae including Trypanosoma and Leishmania species.  MPSS1 is a component of the mitochondrial 3' processome (MPsome) complex, which also includes terminal uridylyltransferase KRET1, 3'-5' exonuclease DSS1, MPSS2 and MPSS3 [[PMID:26833087]]. The MPsome is involved in the maturation of guide RNA (gRNA) precursors in the mitochondrion [[PMID:26833087]]. MPSS1 interacts directly with KRET1 within the complex [[PMID:26833087]].

Name: Minor histocompatibility protein HMSD
Description: This entry represents the minor histocompatibility protein HMSD, found in mammals including primates and artiodactyls. In humans, an alternatively spliced variant of HMSD serves as the precursor of the minor histocompatibility antigen ACC-6 [[PMID:17409267]]. ACC-6 is presented at the cell surface by MHC HLA-B*4403 and can elicit cytotoxic T-lymphocyte reactivity [[PMID:17409267]]. The protein is highly expressed in dendritic cells and primary leukaemia cells, particularly those of myeloid lineage [[PMID:17409267]]. Minor histocompatibility antigens can trigger graft-versus-host disease after haematopoietic stem cell transplantation from HLA-identical donors [[PMID:17409267]].

Name: MICOS complex subunit MIC27
Description: This entry represents MICOS complex subunit MIC27, found in Saccharomycetaceae yeasts. MIC27 is a component of the mitochondrial contact site and cristae organising system (MICOS) complex, which includes MIC10, MIC12, MIC19, MIC26, MIC27 and MIC60 [[PMID:21944719],[PMID:22009199]]. The MICOS complex plays crucial roles in maintenance of crista junctions, inner membrane architecture, and formation of contact sites to the outer membrane [[PMID:21944719]]. MIC27 is a multi-pass membrane protein localised to the mitochondrial inner membrane and enriched at crista junctions [[PMID:22009199]].

Name: TP013X lipoprotein family
Description: This entry represents the TP013X lipoprotein family, a group of uncharacterised lipoproteins of approximately 376-485 amino acids found in Treponema species. Members include TP_0133, TP_0134, TP_0136, and TP_0462/TP_0463 from Treponema pallidum. The proteins are predicted to be lipid-anchored to the cell membrane. The function of this protein family remains to be determined.

Name: AtMg00530
Description: This entry represents an uncharacterised protein found in the mitochondria of land plants. Family members are found in eudicots including Brassicaceae, Fabaceae and Rhamnaceae. In Arabidopsis thaliana, the gene encoding this protein (AtMg00530/ORF109) is located in the mitochondrial genome, with an additional copy (At2g07776) present in the nuclear genome resulting from a duplication of mitochondrial DNA into chromosome 2. The function of this protein remains to be determined.

Name: Defensin-like protein 269
Description: This entry represents defensin-like DEFL269 protein found in Arabidopsis thaliana. The protein contains a signal peptide and a mature cysteine-rich domain with four predicted disulfide bonds. Defensin- like proteins belong to a large gene family in Arabidopsis with over 300 members [[PMID:15955924]]. These small cysteine-rich peptides resemble antimicrobial peptides and have been suggested to function in plant defence [[PMID:17565583]]. The protein is predicted to be secreted.

Name: YJL133C-A C-terminal region
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal region in uncharacterised protein YJL133C-A found in Saccharomycetaceae yeasts including Saccharomyces, Lachancea, Torulaspora and Naumovozyma species. The full-length protein localises to the mitochondrial outer membrane and contains a single transmembrane helix at the N-terminus [[PMID:16823961],[PMID:16407407]]. This C-terminal region is located after the transmembrane region and is predicted to be exposed to the cytoplasm or intermembrane space. The function of this protein and domain remains to be determined.

Name: Tbc2 translation factor, N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of the Tbc2 translation factor found in Chlorophyceae green algae including Chlamydomonas, Volvox, Monoraphidium and Haematococcus. The full-length Tbc2 protein is a nucleus-encoded chloroplast translation factor required for expression of the psbC mRNA, which encodes the CP43 subunit of photosystem II [[PMID:12045185]]. Tbc2 interacts with the 5'-UTR of psbC and is part of a 400 kDa complex in the chloroplast stroma [[PMID:12045185]]. The specific function of this N-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: Tbc2 translation factor, C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of the Tbc2 translation factor found in Chlorophyceae green algae including Chlamydomonas, Volvox, Monoraphidium and Haematococcus. The full-length Tbc2 protein is a nucleus-encoded chloroplast translation factor required for expression of the psbC mRNA, which encodes the CP43 subunit of photosystem II [[PMID:12045185]]. Tbc2 interacts with the 5'-UTR of psbC and is part of a 400 kDa complex in the chloroplast stroma [[PMID:12045185]]. The specific function of this C-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: YdhS-like middle domain
Description: This entry represents a middle domain of approximately 120 residues found in YdhS-like proteins. The domain is located between an N-terminal NAD-binding domain ([pfam:PF13454]) and a small C-terminal region. Family members are classified as COG4529 and some are annotated as FAD-dependent urate hydroxylases in the InterPro resource (IPR052189). In Escherichia coli K12, YdhS is encoded within a region corresponding to Salmonella pathogenicity island 2. The family is found predominantly in Gammaproteobacteria. The function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: Don juan sperm protein
Description: This entry represents Don juan (dj) sperm-specific proteins in Drosophilidae. Don juan is expressed exclusively in post-meiotic male germ cells and localises predominantly to the nucleus, with a fraction associated with mitochondria [[PMID:9232593],[PMID:9788872]]. The full-length protein may be involved in mitochondrial differentiation within the flagellum. Don juan contains an unusual repetitive amino acid motif consisting of tandem DPCK(K/R)K repeats [[PMID:9232593]]. The specific function of this coiled-coil region remains to be determined.

Name: YjjJ-like toxin, middle domain
Description: This entry represents the middle domain of YjjJ-like toxins, approximately 110 residues in length. The domain is found between a small N-terminal region and a C-terminal HipA-like domain ([pfam:PF07804]). In Escherichia coli K12, YjjJ is a toxin that causes filamentous cell morphology when overexpressed and binds DNA [[PMID:28430938]]. The toxic effect of YjjJ is neutralised by the non-cognate antitoxin HipB [[PMID:28430938]]. The family is found predominantly in Proteobacteria.

Name: Mitochondrial membrane protein FMP33
Description: This entry represents the mitochondrial membrane protein FMP33, found in budding yeasts (Saccharomycetaceae). FMP33 is a multi-pass membrane protein of approximately 180 residues that localises to the mitochondrial membrane [[PMID:14562095],[PMID:14576278]]. The protein contains three predicted transmembrane helices. FMP33 expression is induced by cell wall perturbation. The function of this protein remains to be determined.

Name: RAB11-binding protein RELCH N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of the RAB11-binding protein RELCH (also known as KIAA1468). RELCH is found in metazoans and plants and contains a LisH domain, coiled-coil regions, and HEAT repeats. The full-length RELCH protein regulates intracellular cholesterol distribution from recycling endosomes to the trans-Golgi network through interactions with RAB11 and OSBP [[PMID:29514919]]. The specific function of this N-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: RAB11-binding protein RELCH C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of the RAB11-binding protein RELCH (also known as KIAA1468). RELCH is found in metazoans and plants and contains a LisH domain, coiled-coil regions, and HEAT repeats. The full-length RELCH protein regulates intracellular cholesterol distribution from recycling endosomes to the trans-Golgi network through interactions with RAB11 and OSBP [[PMID:29514919]]. The specific function of this C-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: tRNA methyltransferase TRM82 WD40 domain
Description: This entry represents the WD40 beta-propeller domain found in the tRNA (guanine-N(7)-)-methyltransferase non-catalytic subunit TRM82 in fungi. TRM82 forms a heterodimer with the catalytic subunit TRM8 and is required for the formation of N(7)-methylguanine at position 46 (m7G46) in tRNA. In the complex, TRM82 is required to stabilise and induce conformational changes of the catalytic subunit. The domain contains WD40 repeats that form a beta-propeller structure. This family is found in fungi.

Name: Intraflagellar transport protein 70 C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of intraflagellar transport protein 70 (IFT70), also known as tetratricopeptide repeat protein 30 (TTC30). The domain contains TPR repeats. IFT70 is a component of the intraflagellar transport complex B (IFT-B) and is required for polyglutamylation of axonemal tubulin. The protein plays a role in anterograde intraflagellar transport, the process by which cilia precursors are transported from the base of the cilium to the tip. In C. elegans, the orthologue DYF-1 is specifically required for kinesin OSM-3 to dock onto and move IFT particles. This family is found in metazoans.

Name: VPS35L C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of VPS35L (VPS35 endosomal protein-sorting factor-like) proteins found in metazoans. VPS35L is a component of the retriever complex, a heterotrimeric complex formed by VPS26C, VPS29 and VPS35L that is involved in endocytic recycling of cargo proteins [[PMID:25355947]]. Mutations in human VPS35L cause Ritscher-Schinzel syndrome [[PMID:25355947]]. The specific function of this C-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: AIM6-like phosphodiesterase domain
Description: This entry represents a TIM-barrel like domain found in the AIM6 (altered inheritance of mitochondria protein 6) in fungi. This is fold is characteristic of the PLC-like phosphodiesterase superfamily. AIM6 proteins contain an N-terminal signal peptide and are secreted. In Saccharomyces cerevisiae, disruption of AIM6 impairs respiratory growth, suggesting a role in mitochondrial biogenesis [[PMID:19300474]]. The precise enzymatic substrate and biochemical function of this domain remain to be determined.

Name: MP81-like, OB fold domain
Description: This entry represents the OB fold domain found at the C-termina of RNA-editing complex protein MP81 ([swissprot:Q586L9], also known as KREPA1) from Trypanosoma brucei brucei and related sequences from Trypanosomatida. The editing process is performed by the editosome, a multi-protein complex that catalyses the insertion and deletion of uridylates (U) that occurs during RNA editing. Uridylate insertion/deletion editing, which appears to be unique to these organisms, processes most mitochondrial pre-mRNAs to produce mature mRNAs. Editosomes consist of several components and in Trypanosoma that include: REL1/MP52 and REL2/Mp48 RNA ligases; MP18, MP42, MP63 and MP81 that contain zinc fingers and an OB fold domain at the C-terminal [[PMID:12649499]] This entry also includes KREPA5 ([swissprot:Q57YG2]), which is a monodomain protein.

Name: PAB N-terminal
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of the PAB (protein IN CHLOROPLAST ATPASE BIOGENESIS). PAB is a chloroplast stromal protein that functions as an assembly chaperone involved in the assembly of the F1 ATP synthase in chloroplast thylakoid membranes. The protein functions downstream of the CPN60 chaperones to promote assembly of the catalytically active core of the chloroplast ATP synthase and assists the assembly of the ATP synthase gamma subunit into the active F1 core. PAB interacts with ATPC1 and is critical for the biogenesis of the chloroplast ATP synthase [[PMID:25775508]]. The domain represented by this entry is predicted to adopt a structure which share similarity with domains members of SUKH superfamily.

Name: Mitochondrial helicase Irc3, C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of mitochondrial ATP- dependent helicases related to Irc3 (Increased Recombination Centers 3) from Schizosaccharomyces pombe. These proteins contain an N-terminal restriction endonuclease domain ([pfam:PF04851]), DEAD box helicase domain ([pfam:PF00270]), helicase C-terminal domain ([pfam:PF00271]), UPF0547 domain ([pfam:PF10571]), and this C-terminal domain. The proteins are predicted to function as ATP-dependent helicases involved in mitochondrial genome maintenance. The family is restricted to fungi, found in both Ascomycota and Basidiomycota. The specific function of this C-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: HilA/YgeH-like transcriptional regulator, second domain
Description: This entry represents the second domain of HilA, YgeH and related transcriptional regulators. It follows the N-terminal OmpR/PhoB- type DNA-binding domain ([pfam:PF00486]). In Salmonella, HilA is the main transcriptional regulator of Salmonella pathogenicity island 1 (SPI1) and activates the expression of invasion genes [[PMID:8817493]]. The Escherichia coli homologue YgeH can functionally replace HilA in Salmonella [[PMID:25343852]]. The family is widely distributed in bacteria, including Pseudomonadota and Bacteroidota. The specific function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: Autotransporter YaiT, N-terminal passenger repeat
Description: This entry represents a repeat region found in the N-terminal passenger domain of the autotransporter protein YaiT and related proteins. YaiT is an outer membrane autotransporter found in Enterobacterales, including Escherichia coli and Salmonella enterica. The passenger domain precedes the C-terminal autotransporter beta-barrel domain ([pfam:PF03797]). In E. coli K12, the yaiT gene is interrupted by an IS3B insertion element, making it a pseudogene. Related autotransporters have been implicated in adhesion and virulence functions. The specific function of this repeat region remains to be determined.

Name: CLU5d N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of CLU5d (pathogenicity cluster 5 protein d), a secreted fungal protein. CLU5d is required for appressorial penetration of intact host epidermal cells and for pathogenicity in the maize anthracnose fungus Colletotrichum graminicola [[PMID:31599055]]. Proteins containing this domain are found in Dikarya fungi including Ascomycota and Basidiomycota. This domain is typically found in combination with the CLU5d C-terminal domain ([pfam:PF27672]). The specific function of this N-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: Intraflagellar transport protein 56
Description: This entry entry represents intraflagellar transport protein 56 (IFT56), also known as TTC26 or DYF-13. IFT56 is a component of the intraflagellar transport (IFT) complex B that is required for transport of proteins in motile cilia [[PMID:25340710],[PMID:28264835]]. IFT56 localises to the ciliary basal body and transition zone [[PMID:25340710]]. The full-length protein plays a key role in maintaining IFT complex B integrity and proper ciliary microtubule organisation [[PMID:31595528]]. Mutations in IFT56 cause biliary, renal, neurologic, and skeletal syndrome (BRENS), a ciliopathy with multisystemic manifestations [[PMID:22718903]]. In Trypanosoma brucei, the orthologue PIFTC3 interacts with established IFT components [[PMID:20923419]].

Name: CGGBP1 N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain found in CGG triplet repeat-binding protein 1 (CGGBP1). CGGBP1 binds to nonmethylated 5'-d(CGG)(n)-3' trinucleotide repeats in the FMR1 gene promoter and may play a role in regulating FMR1 expression [[PMID:9201980]]. Binding is severely inhibited by cytosine-specific DNA methylation of the binding motif. CGGBP1 localises to the nucleus and is ubiquitously expressed, with high levels in placenta, thymus, lymph nodes and brain [[PMID:14667814]].

Name: CGGBP1 helical domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal helical domain found in CGG triplet repeat-binding protein 1 (CGGBP1). CGGBP1 binds to nonmethylated 5'-d(CGG)(n)-3' trinucleotide repeats in the FMR1 gene promoter and may play a role in regulating FMR1 expression [[PMID:9201980]]. Binding is severely inhibited by cytosine-specific DNA methylation of the binding motif. CGGBP1 localises to the nucleus and is ubiquitously expressed, with high levels in placenta, thymus, lymph nodes and brain [[PMID:14667814]].

Name: ETO1 N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain found in ETO1 (Ethylene Overproducer 1) and ETO1-like (EOL) proteins from land plants. These proteins function as substrate-specific adaptors that connect type-2 1-aminocyclopropane-1-carboxylate synthase (ACS) enzymes to CUL3- based ubiquitin ligase complexes, leading to proteasomal degradation of ACS and regulation of ethylene biosynthesis [[PMID:15118728],[PMID:16091151]]. The specific function of this N-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: ETO1 C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain found in ETO1 (Ethylene Overproducer 1) and ETO1-like (EOL) proteins from land plants. These proteins function as substrate-specific adaptors that connect type-2 1-aminocyclopropane-1-carboxylate synthase (ACS) enzymes to CUL3- based ubiquitin ligase complexes, leading to proteasomal degradation of ACS and regulation of ethylene biosynthesis [[PMID:18808454],[PMID:15118728]]. The specific function of this C-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: NDH complex assembly factor CRR9
Description: This entry represents CHLORORESPIRATORY REDUCTION 9 (CRR9), a stromal protein required for the assembly of the chloroplast NADH dehydrogenase-like (NDH) complex [[PMID:27481895]]. In Arabidopsis thaliana, CRR9 is specifically required for the formation of NDH subcomplex A, which corresponds to the Q module of respiratory Complex I. CRR9 facilitates the accumulation of the NdhK subunit in NDH assembly intermediates in the stroma and the incorporation of NdhM into the assembly intermediate complex. The chloroplast NDH complex mediates cyclic electron transport around photosystem I in the thylakoid membrane. The family is conserved in cyanobacteria and land plants but absent from green algae, which lack chloroplast NDH.

Name: Epg5 middle TPR-like domain
Description: This entry represents a middle TPR-like domain in EPG5 (Ectopic P granules protein 5 homolog). EPG5 is involved in autophagy and is a large 2282 amino acid protein found in Aedes aegypti. The domain shares structural similarity with TPR motifs but may have diverged sequence characteristics. This domain is found across eukaryotes including vertebrates, arthropods and nematodes. The function of this domain remains to be determined [[PMID:20550938]].

Name: MIP2, beta-propeller domain
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller domain found at the N-terminal of MAG2-interacting protein 2 (MIP2) from Arabidopsis thaliana and related plant sequences. MIP2 is essential for the proper maturation of seed storage proteins in plants [[PMID:24118572]]. It forms a complex in the endoplasmic reticulum with MAG2, ZW10/MIP1, and MIP3. This complex is likely involved in the efficient transport of seed storage proteins, ensuring their correct processing and delivery within the cell. The coordinated action of these proteins is crucial for the development and storage of nutrients in seeds, which are vital for plant reproduction and growth [[PMID:24118572]].

Name: TPM phosphatase-associated C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents a C-terminal domain of approximately 90 residues found in cyanobacteria and photosynthetic eukaryotes. The domain follows an N-terminal TPM phosphatase domain ([pfam:PF04536]). In Synechocystis, sll1071 is localised to the thylakoid membrane and is regulated by the redox-responsive transcription factor RpaB. In Synechococcus elongatus, Synpcc7942_1090 clusters with photosystem proteins, suggesting an association with photosynthesis. Homologues are conserved throughout the photosynthetic lineage including Chlamydomonas and Arabidopsis. The specific function of this C-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: Defensin-like protein 268
Description: This protein family includes the 103 amino-acid sequence Defensin-like protein 268 from Arabidopsis thaliana, a defensin-like (DEFL) protein [[PMID:28064246]].

Name: Defensin-like protein 308 domain
Description: This protein family includes Defensin-like protein 308 from Arabidopsis thaliana, a defensin-like (DEFL) protein.

Name: Apidermin 2
Description: This protein family includes honeybee Apidermin 2, an antimicrobial peptide that binds cell wall carbohydrates of microbial symbionts and induces structural damage [[PMID:36292906]].

Name: CFAP251 N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain found in cilia- and flagella-associated protein 251 (CFAP251, also known as WDR66). This domain is found in metazoans and ciliates. The full-length protein CFAP251 is a component of the radial spoke complex and is involved in ciliary and flagellar motility [[PMID:30122541],[PMID:25694453]]. In Tetrahymena thermophila, CFAP251 is required for assembly of radial spoke 3 [[PMID:25694453]]. In humans, mutations in CFAP251 cause spermatogenic failure due to defective sperm flagella [[PMID:30122541]].

Name: LRC39 N-terminal domin
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal helix of Leucine-rich repeat-containing protein 39 (LRC39), a component of the sarcomeric M-band which plays a role in myocyte response to biomechanical stress. It may regulate expression of other M-band proteins via an SRF-dependent pathway. Important for normal contractile function in heart [[PMID:20847312]].

Name: Complex I 9.5 kDa ubiquinone-binding subunit
Description: This entry represents the 9.5 kDa ubiquinone-binding subunit of mitochondrial Complex I (NADH:ubiquinone oxidoreductase), found in fungi (Dikarya). Complex I functions in the transfer of electrons from NADH to ubiquinone in the respiratory chain. This subunit is an accessory component that is not directly involved in catalysis but has been shown to bind ubiquinone [[PMID:1445878],[PMID:1445879]]. The protein contains a single transmembrane helix and is localised to the mitochondrial inner membrane.

Name: KICSTOR complex protein kaptin
Description: This entry represents kaptin (KPTN), a component of the KICSTOR complex found in vertebrates. KPTN functions as part of the four-protein KICSTOR complex (KPTN, ITFG2, KICS2, SZT2) which recruits the GATOR1 complex to lysosomal membranes and is essential for amino acid-dependent regulation of mTORC1 signalling [[PMID:28199306]]. In the absence of amino acids, KICSTOR negatively regulates mTORC1 activity, while loss of KICSTOR function results in constitutive mTORC1 activation [[PMID:28199306]]. KPTN also has actin-binding properties and localises to lamellipodia and stereocilia [[PMID:24239382]]. The protein is approximately 430-436 amino acids in length. Mutations in human KPTN cause intellectual developmental disorder with macrocephaly [[PMID:28199306]].

Name: GAC PH domain
Description: This PH domain is found in the Toxoplasma gondii glideosome-associated connector (GAC) which is a giant armadillo-repeat protein, essential for parasite motility and conserved across Apicomplexa. It connects actin filaments to the plasma membrane via interactions with phosphatidic acid and membrane-spanning adhesins. This PH domain contains conserved lipid-binding residues, which are exposed to solvent (PDB:8BT6), suggesting a role in membrane recognition/interactions.

Name: GAC ARM domain
Description: This ARM domain is found in the Toxoplasma gondii glideosome-associated connector (GAC) which is a giant armadillo-repeat protein, essential for parasite motility and conserved across Apicomplexa. It connects actin filaments to the plasma membrane via interactions with phosphatidic acid and membrane-spanning adhesins.

Name: GAC ARM domain
Description: This ARM domain is found in the Toxoplasma gondii glideosome-associated connector (GAC) which is a giant armadillo-repeat protein, essential for parasite motility and conserved across Apicomplexa. It connects actin filaments to the plasma membrane via interactions with phosphatidic acid and membrane-spanning adhesins.

Name: ARMC6
Description: This entry represents the human Armadillo repeat-containing protein 6 (ARMC6) and related proteins. This protein is characterized by tandem armadillo repeats. It is a member of the so-called ARMC subfamily of proteins, of which ARMC1-10, ARMC12, and ARMCX1-6, has received increasing attention as they play a critical role in various diseases [[PMID:34912852]]. ARMC6 is the target gene of MYCN and indicates a poor prognosis for patients with neuroblastoma.

Name: Tel1 pincer domain
Description: This domain is found in the Serine/threonine-protein kinase tel1 and related proteins. Tel1 is a large kinase that resides in the cell in an autoinhibited dimeric state and upon activation orchestrates the cellular response to DNA damage. The domain represented by this entry is composed of HEAT repeats and was shown to bind DNA together with the spiral domain [[PMID:31740028]].

Name: Tel1 spiral domain
Description: This domain is found in the Serine/threonine-protein kinase tel1 and related proteins. Tel1 is a large kinase that resides in the cell in an autoinhibited dimeric state and upon activation orchestrates the cellular response to DNA damage.
