The domain represented by this entry is composed of HEAT repeats and together with the pincer domain was shown to bind DNA [[PMID:31740028]].

Name: TMCO6
Description: This entry represents TMCO6 (transmembrane and coiled-coil domain- containing protein 6) proteins mostly found in vertebrates. The full-length TMCO6 protein contains an N-terminal coiled-coil region and Armadillo- like repeats. In humans, TMCO6 is predicted to interact with UBQLN1 (ubiquilin-1) and ZNF511, according to IntAct database. The function of both TMCO6 and this C-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: ORANGE protein middle domain
Description: This entry represents the middle domain of ORANGE (OR) proteins, which are found in land plants (Embryophyta). ORANGE proteins are chloroplast-localised regulators of carotenoid biosynthesis that function through post-transcriptional regulation of phytoene synthase (PSY) [[PMID:24267591]]. A conserved arginine residue within this domain region is critical for carotenoid accumulation; substitution to histidine promotes carotenoid overaccumulation in both Arabidopsis and melon [[PMID:26224804]]. The proteins are related to the DnaJ family but lack the J domain, and possess a C-terminal CR-type-like zinc finger region. The specific function of this middle domain remains to be determined.

Name: ORANGE protein N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of ORANGE (OR) proteins, which are found in land plants (Embryophyta). ORANGE proteins are chloroplast-localised regulators of carotenoid biosynthesis that function through post-transcriptional regulation of phytoene synthase (PSY) [[PMID:25754094]]. A conserved arginine residue within the middle region is critical for carotenoid accumulation; substitution to histidine promotes carotenoid overaccumulation in both Arabidopsis and melon [[PMID:26224804]]. The proteins are related to the DnaJ family but lack the J domain, and possess a C-terminal CR-type-like zinc finger region.

Name: ORANGE protein C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of ORANGE (OR) proteins, which are found in land plants (Embryophyta). ORANGE proteins are chloroplast-localised regulators of carotenoid biosynthesis that function through post-transcriptional regulation of phytoene synthase (PSY) [[PMID:24267591]]. A conserved arginine residue within this domain region is critical for carotenoid accumulation; substitution to histidine promotes carotenoid overaccumulation in both Arabidopsis and melon [[PMID:26224804]]. The proteins are related to the DnaJ family but lack the J domain, and possess a C-terminal CR-type-like zinc finger region. This entry also includes Protein PHOTOSYSTEM I ASSEMBLY 2, chloroplastic.

Name: GtfB third domain
Description: GtfB is required for polymorphic O-glycosylation of serine-rich repeat proteins. GtfB, together with GtfA, forms a two-protein enzyme complex that is involved in glycosylation. GtfB stabilises the glycosylation activity of GtfA and forms a heterotetramer complex with GtfA [[PMID:24936067],[PMID:28246170]]. GtfB is a peripheral membrane protein localised to the cell membrane and is part of the accessory SecA2/SecY2 protein translocation system [[PMID:24936067],[PMID:28246170],[PMID:20971868],[PMID:18083807],[PMID:21862581]]. This entry represents the third domain which may adopt an alpha/beta fold.

Name: GtfB N-terminal domain
Description: GtfB is required for polymorphic O-glycosylation of serine-rich repeat proteins. GtfB, together with GtfA, forms a two-protein enzyme complex that is involved in glycosylation. GtfB stabilises the glycosylation activity of GtfA and forms a heterotetramer complex with GtfA [[PMID:24936067],[PMID:28246170]]. GtfB is a peripheral membrane protein localised to the cell membrane and is part of the accessory SecA2/SecY2 protein translocation system [[PMID:24936067],[PMID:28246170],[PMID:18083807],[PMID:20971868]]. This is the N-terminal domain which may adopt a beta/alpha/beta/alpha/beta fold.

Name: GftB/GlyA-F C-terminal domain
Description: GtfB is required for polymorphic O-glycosylation of serine-rich repeat proteins. GtfB, together with GtfA, forms a two-protein enzyme complex that catalyses the first step of PsrP glycosylation and provides the primary binding site for the acceptor. GtfB stabilises the glycosylation activity of GtfA and forms a heterotetramer complex with GtfA [[PMID:24936067],[PMID:28246170]]. GtfB is a peripheral membrane protein localised to the cell membrane and is part of the accessory SecA2/SecY2 protein translocation system [[PMID:24936067],[PMID:28246170],[PMID:20971868],[PMID:18083807],[PMID:21862581]]. This entry represents the glycosyltransferase domain found at the C-terminal of GftB. This domain is also found in glycosyltransferases GlyA/B/D/E/F from Streptococcus pneumoniae, which catalyse the third step in glycosylation of PsrP [[PMID:21862581]]. In Glycosyltransferase GlyD, this domain apprears twice, one after the other.

Name: UBR5 first HEAT repeat domain
Description: This domain is found in the human E3 ubiquitin ligase UBR5 and related proteins. UBR5 is a nuclear E3 ligase that ubiquitinates a vast range of substrates for proteasomal degradation and has been identified as an important regulator of oncogenes. This domain is located central and it plays a role of a scaffold that holds protein interaction domains [[PMID:37409633]].

Name: UBR5 second HEAT repeat domain
Description: This domain is found in the human E3 ubiquitin ligase UBR5 and related proteins. UBR5 is a nuclear E3 ligase that ubiquitinates a vast range of substrates for proteasomal degradation and has been identified as an important regulator of oncogenes. This domain is located central and it plays a role of a scaffold that holds protein interaction domains [[PMID:37409633]]. It is the second of the two HEAT repeat domains.

Name: Rhs1 C-terminal toxin domain
Description: This domain is found C-terminal in the T6SS-dependent Rhs effector (Rhs1) from Serratia marcescens and related proteins. This domain possesses NAD(P)+ glycohydrolase activity and is a cytoplasmic-acting anti-bacterial toxin [[PMID:37770429]]. Rhs1CTD is neutralised by the immunity protein, RhsI1, encoded immediately downstream of rhs1.

Name: E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase UBR4 TPR-like domain
Description: This entry represents a small TPR-like alpha solenoid domain found in a family of UBR4 E3 ubiquitin ligase enzymes.

Name: HIR3 TPR domain
Description: This entry represents a tetratricopeptide repeat (TPR) domain found in HIR3 proteins. This domain is present in proteins such as HIR3 from Candida albicans, a histone transcription regulator involved in nucleosome assembly pathways required for heterochromatin integrity and chromosome segregation [[PMID:38925115]]. While the full-length HIR3 protein functions in chromatin organisation and transcription regulation, the specific function of this TPR domain remains to be determined.

Name: UBR1/2/3-like ARM repeat domain
Description: This domain is found in a number of E3 ubiquitin-protein ligases such as UBR1, UBR2, UBR3, UBR11 etc. It is composed of alpha-helical ARM repeats and defines the helical scaffold of these proteins [[PMID:34789879]].

Name: UBR4 N-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found at the N terminus of the E4 ubiquitin-protein ligase UBR4 from Caenorhabditis elegans and related proteins. This domain is composed of alpha-helical ARM repeats and is involved in a dimer formation.

Name: SFI1 first Coiled-coil region
Description: This entry represents the first coiled-coil region SFI1, a centrosomal protein found in vertebrates and other metazoans. SFI1 plays a role in the dynamic structure of centrosome-associated contractile fibres through its interaction with centrin-2 (CETN2) [[PMID:16956364],[PMID:14504268]]. The full-length protein contains multiple HAT (Half-A-TPR) repeats and several CETN2-binding regions distributed along its length. The C-terminal domain contains one HAT repeat and forms part of the region that interacts with CETN2. The CETN2-binding regions contain a conserved tryptophan residue at their C-terminal ends that is critical for centrin binding [[PMID:16956364]]. SFI1 localises to the distal end of centrioles.

Name: Proline-rich antimicrobial peptide 1
Description: This protein family includes Proline-rich antimicrobial peptide 1 from Galleria mellonella (PROP1), an antimicrobial small protein [[PMID:17194500]].

Name: DF190-like, C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of the putative defensin-like protein 190 from Arabidopsis thaliana (DF190) and similar plant proteins. This domain is predicted to adopt an alpha-beta configuration.

Name: Nucleoporin NUP82 beta-propeller
Description: This entry represents the beta-propeller domain of nucleoporin NUP82, found in fungi of the Ascomycota phylum. NUP82 is a component of the nuclear pore complex (NPC), which mediates nucleocytoplasmic transport [[PMID:21784248]].

Name: Lebocin-like anionic peptide 1
Description: This protein family includes Lebocin-like anionic peptide 1 from Galleria mellonella (LEB1), a short antimicrobial protein [[PMID:17194500]].

Name: Nucleoporin NUP53 middle domain
Description: This entry represents the middle domain of fungal nucleoporin NUP53, a component of the nuclear pore complex (NPC). The domain adopts an RRM-like alpha/beta plait fold [[PMID:27081075]]. NUP53 is part of a tetrameric module with NUP192 or NUP188, NUP170, and NIC96. This domain is found in Ascomycota fungi and can be found with the C-terminal domain ([pfam:PF28376]).

Name: Nucleoporin NUP53 N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of fungal nucleoporin NUP53, a component of the nuclear pore complex (NPC). The domain adopts an RRM-like alpha/beta plait fold [[PMID:27081075]]. NUP53 is part of a tetrameric module with NUP192 or NUP188, NUP170, and NIC96. This domain is found in Ascomycota fungi and is distinct from the C-terminal domain ([pfam:PF28376]).

Name: At4g37920-like C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents a C-terminal domain found in uncharacterised plant proteins. In Arabidopsis thaliana, the protein At4g37920 localises to the chloroplast envelope and thylakoid membrane. This domain is found C-terminal to the DUF8246 domain ([pfam:PF26740]). The family is restricted to land plants (Embryophyta). The function of this domain remains to be determined. This domain can also be found in other positions in uncharacterised plant proteins.

Name: At4g37920-like middle domain
Description: This entry represents a middle domain found in uncharacterised plant proteins. In Arabidopsis thaliana, the protein At4g37920 localises to the chloroplast envelope and thylakoid membrane. This domain is found C-terminal to the DUF8246 domain ([pfam:PF26740]). The family is restricted to land plants (Embryophyta). The function of this domain remains to be determined. This domain can also be found in other positions in uncharacterised plant proteins.

Name: Neutral sphingomyelinase N-terminal transmembrane region
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal transmembrane region of neutral sphingomyelinases (nSMases), including SMPD3 and SMPD5. This region contains intramembrane helices that anchor these enzymes to cellular membranes. In SMPD3, this region contains anionic phospholipid binding domains that bind phosphatidylserine and phosphatidic acid, which modulate enzymatic activity and subcellular localisation [[PMID:14720208]]. SMPD3 localises to the Golgi and plasma membrane, while SMPD5 localises to the mitochondrial inner membrane [[PMID:15059969]]. This domain is found N-terminal to the catalytic Exo_endo_phos domain ([pfam:PF03372]) and is restricted to vertebrates.

Name: Light-harvesting-like protein
Description: This entry represents a domain found in light-harvesting complex-like protein 3 (LIL3) family members. This domain is found in land plants (Embryophyta). LIL3 proteins are chloroplast thylakoid membrane proteins that are required for biosynthesis of phytylated chlorophylls and alpha-tocopherol [[PMID:20823244]]. They function by anchoring geranylgeranyl reductase (GGR) in the thylakoid membrane, stabilising GGR activity [[PMID:20823244],[PMID:24275650]]. LIL3 proteins bind chlorophyll a and form homo- and heterodimers [[PMID:26320415]]. This domain contains GGR-interacting residues. The specific function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: Uncharacterized protein At4g14450, chloroplastic
Description: This entry represents the proline/serine-rich protein (PRP) family, also known as PH1 and PH2 (Proline/serine-rich protein Homolog 1 and 2). This family is found in land plants (Embryophyta), particularly in dicotyledonous species. In Arabidopsis thaliana, the family is represented by At4g14450 (PH1) and At1g04330 (PH2). These proteins are phospho-targets of stress-related mitogen-activated protein kinases (MAPKs) and differentially regulate growth and pathogen defence [[PMID:28755319]]. The proteins contain a chloroplast transit peptide and have been detected in the chloroplast, cytoplasm and nucleus. It contain disordered regions.

Name: Sll0103-like, C-terminal alpha-helical domain
Description: This entry represents a C-terminal alpha-helical domain of approximately 80 residues found in Sll0103-like proteins. This domain is typically found C-terminal to von Willebrand factor type A (VWA) domains ([pfam:PF00092]). This family is found in bacteria, predominantly Cyanobacteriota and Chloroflexota. The function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: PGRL1 C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain in PGRL1 (PGR5-like protein 1) proteins. The domain is found in plants (Viridiplantae). PGRL1 is a chloroplast thylakoid membrane protein that functions as a ferredoxin-plastoquinone reductase involved in cyclic electron flow around photosystem I [[PMID:18243102],[PMID:23290914]]. The C-terminal region is required for ferredoxin binding and contains conserved cysteine residues involved in disulfide bond formation with PGR5 and binding of an iron-containing cofactor [[PMID:23290914]].

Name: PGRL1 N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain in PGRL1 (PGR5-like protein 1) proteins. The domain is found in plants (Viridiplantae). PGRL1 is a chloroplast thylakoid membrane protein that functions as a ferredoxin-plastoquinone reductase involved in cyclic electron flow around photosystem I [[PMID:18243102],[PMID:23290914]]. The C-terminal region is required for ferredoxin binding and contains conserved cysteine residues involved in disulfide bond formation with PGR5 and binding of an iron-containing cofactor [[PMID:23290914]].ED A0A0L9UC26.1/159-195;

Name: TIM14 C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal DnaJ/J domain in TIM14 (mitochondrial import inner membrane translocase subunit 14) proteins. The domain is found in plants (Viridiplantae).TIM14 is a component of the PAM complex required for translocation of proteins into the mitochondrial matrix.

Name: Mitochondrial protein TAR1
Description: This entry represents the TAR1 (transcript antisense to ribosomal RNA) protein found in fungi and plants. In Saccharomyces cerevisiae, TAR1 is encoded on the antisense strand of the nuclear 25S rDNA and localises to mitochondria [[PMID:12414727]]. The protein may be involved in mitochondrial DNA stability or mitochondrial gene expression regulation at the post-transcriptional level [[PMID:12414727]]. In plants, the domain is found in senescence-associated proteins.

Name: TIM14 N-terminal transmembrane region
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal transmembrane region in plant TIM14 proteins and related proteins. The region is found in eukaryotes. This region contains a single transmembrane helix that anchors the protein to the mitochondrial inner membrane [[PMID:17098851]]. TIM14 proteins also contain a C-terminal DnaJ/J domain. TIM14 is a component of the PAM complex required for translocation of proteins into the mitochondrial matrix.

Name: GIGANTEA middle domain
Description: This entry represents a middle domain of GIGANTEA proteins found in plants. The full-length GIGANTEA protein is involved in regulation of circadian rhythm and photoperiodic flowering [[PMID:10920210],[PMID:17890372]]. GIGANTEA belongs to a distinct protein family and is localised to both the nucleus and cytoplasm [[PMID:17890372]]. The protein stabilises photoreceptors ADO1/ZTL and ADO3, and interacts with multiple transcription factors and regulatory proteins via its N-terminal region [[PMID:19619493]]. GIGANTEA shows circadian expression patterns with peak levels occurring 8-10 hours into the light period [[PMID:19619493]]. A crystal structure is available for a large portion of the Arabidopsis protein (7WA4) showing predominantly helical architecture [[PMID:10920210]]. This structure does not include the region represented by this entry. While the full-length protein has well-characterised roles in circadian clock regulation and flowering control, the specific function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: GIGANTEA N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of GIGANTEA proteins found in plants. The full-length GIGANTEA protein is involved in regulation of circadian rhythm and photoperiodic flowering [[PMID:10920210],[PMID:17890372]]. GIGANTEA belongs to a distinct protein family and is localised to both the nucleus and cytoplasm [[PMID:17890372]]. The protein stabilises photoreceptors ADO1/ZTL and ADO3, and interacts with multiple transcription factors and regulatory proteins via its N-terminal region [[PMID:19619493]]. GIGANTEA shows circadian expression patterns with peak levels occurring 8-10 hours into the light period [[PMID:19619493]]. A crystal structure is available for a large portion of the Arabidopsis protein (7WA4) showing predominantly helical architecture [[PMID:10920210]]. This structure does not include the region represented by this entry. While the full-length protein has well-characterised roles in circadian clock regulation and flowering control, the specific function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: OSB PDF repeat
Description: This entry represents a repeated region of approximately 50 residues found in OSB (organellar single-stranded DNA-binding) proteins from plants (Viridiplantae). The repeat occurs in two to four copies C-terminal to the SSB domain ([pfam:PF00436]). OSB proteins localise to chloroplasts and mitochondria where they bind preferentially to single-stranded DNA [[PMID:17189341]]. While the SSB domain is responsible for DNA binding, the specific function of this repeat remains to be determined.

Name: AAL-toxin resistant protein 7
Description: This entry represents the ATR7 (AAL-toxin resistant 7) protein family found in seed plants (Spermatophyta). Members localise to the nucleus [[PMID:31250033]]. In Arabidopsis thaliana, ATR7 is required to regulate responses to abiotic stress including drought and oxidative stress leading to cell death [[PMID:22710144],[PMID:31250033]]. The protein confers sensitivity to paraquat, the catalase inhibitor aminotriazole, and the fungal Alternaria alternata f.sp. lycopersici toxin (AAL toxin) [[PMID:22710144]]. Expression is induced by oxidative and abiotic stresses such as hydrogen peroxide, cadmium chloride, and osmotic stress [[PMID:31250033]].

Name: OSB SSB domain
Description: This entry represents the SSB domain in OSB (organellar single-stranded DNA-binding) proteins from plants (Viridiplantae).. OSB proteins localise to chloroplasts and mitochondria where they bind preferentially to single-stranded DNA [[PMID:17189341]]. The SSB domain is responsible for DNA binding and contains an OB-like fold.

Name: Cytochrome c oxidase subunit 5C
Description: This entry represents cytochrome c oxidase subunit 5C (COX5C), a nuclear-encoded component of cytochrome c oxidase (complex IV) found in plants. Cytochrome c oxidase is the terminal oxidase in mitochondrial electron transport. This subunit is localised to the mitochondrion inner membrane and contains a single transmembrane helix. The structure of plant complex IV, including this subunit, has been determined by cryo-electron microscopy [[PMID:33463523]].

Name: Mediator complex subunit 16 N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain found in plant MED16 (Mediator of RNA polymerase II transcription subunit 16), also known as SFR6 (SENSITIVE TO FREEZING 6). MED16 is a component of the Mediator complex, a coactivator involved in regulated transcription of RNA polymerase II-dependent genes [[PMID:19067974],[PMID:12753580]]. In Arabidopsis thaliana, MED16 is involved in regulation of the circadian clock, control of flowering time, freezing and osmotic stress tolerance, and both salicylic acid and jasmonate-mediated defence gene expression [[PMID:19067974],[PMID:12753580]]. While the full-length MED16 protein functions in transcriptional regulation, the specific function of this N-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: TSET complex tstB/TSAUCER-like N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents a domain found in tstB (also known as TSAUCER), a component of the TSET complex [[PMID:24529374]]. The TSET complex is a heterohexameric membrane trafficking complex that may function in plasma membrane turnover [[PMID:24529374]]. This domain adopts an ARM-type fold. In plants, homologous proteins are known as TASH3 (TPLATE-associated SH3 domain-containing protein 3) and are components of the related TPLATE complex. These proteins typically contain a C-terminal SH3 domain ([pfam:PF00018]). This family is found across diverse eukaryotes including amoebozoa and plants.

Name: Bucky ball germ plasm organiser
Description: This entry represents the Bucky ball protein family found in vertebrates, predominantly in teleost fish. Bucky ball is a prion-like protein required for the formation of the Balbiani body and germ plasm assembly, and for the establishment of oocyte polarity during early oogenesis [[PMID:19249209],[PMID:18582455],[PMID:24496621]]. The protein localises to the cytoplasm, nuage, and the Balbiani body during oogenesis, and accumulates at embryonic cleavage furrows [[PMID:26253407]]. Bucky ball interacts with several proteins including TDRD6, RBPMS2, DAZL, and Kinesin-1/KIF5BA [[PMID:24496621],[PMID:26253407],[PMID:30086300]]. The C-terminal region contains RG motifs that undergo symmetric dimethylarginine modification, which promotes interaction with TDRD6 [[PMID:30086300]].

Name: Mitochondrial ATP synthase 6 kDa subunit
Description: This entry represents the ATP synthase 6 kDa subunit (MtATP6) found in land plants (Embryophyta). The protein is approximately 55 residues in length including an N-terminal mitochondrial transit peptide. The mature protein contains a single transmembrane helix and is a component of the F0 domain of the mitochondrial F1F0-ATP synthase (Complex V). The protein localises to the mitochondrial inner membrane. In Arabidopsis thaliana, overexpression of this protein confers tolerance to abiotic stresses including salt, mannitol, drought, and cold [[PMID:23096681],[PMID:18338219]]. The potato homologue has been characterised by direct protein sequencing [[PMID:8919912]].

Name: Senescence-associated protein OSA15 C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of the OSA15/AAF senescence-associated protein family found in land plants (Embryophyta). This domain is found in combination with an N-terminal OSA15_N domain ([pfam:PF28580]). The full-length protein localises to the chloroplast and is upregulated during leaf senescence [[PMID:21940719]]. In Arabidopsis thaliana, AAF is involved in modulating redox homeostasis to regulate leaf senescence, and its function is dependent on EIN2, a central factor of ethylene signalling [[PMID:21940719]]. The specific function of this C-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: STR4 middle domain
Description: This entry represents the rhodanese domain in the middle region of STR4/TROL (thylakoid rhodanese-like) proteins in land plants (Embryophyta). This domain is located between the N-terminal transit peptide and the rhodanese domain. The full-length STR4/TROL protein localises to the chloroplast thylakoid membrane and contains two transmembrane helices, a rhodanese domain, and a C-terminal domain ([pfam:PF28616]). The STR4/TROL protein anchors ferredoxin-NADP reductase to thylakoid membranes and is required for efficient linear electron flow [[PMID:19682289]]. The specific function of this middle domain remains to be determined.

Name: Chloroplast outer envelope pore protein OEP21
Description: This entry represents the outer envelope pore protein 21 (OEP21) family found in land plants (Embryophyta). OEP21 is a beta-barrel porin containing eight transmembrane beta strands. The protein localises to the chloroplast outer membrane and etioplast membrane. OEP21 is predicted to function as a voltage-dependent rectifying anion channel that facilitates the translocation of phosphorylated carbohydrates between the chloroplast and cytoplasm [[PMID:12649433]].

Name: Y3IP1/CEST N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of the Y3IP1/CEST protein family found in land plants (Embryophyta). This domain is found in combination with a C-terminal Y3IP1_CEST_C domain ([pfam:PF28637]). The full-length protein contains an N-terminal chloroplast transit peptide and a C-terminal transmembrane helix, and localises to the chloroplast thylakoid membrane. Y3IP1 interacts with the plastid-encoded Ycf3 protein and participates in photosystem I assembly [[PMID:20807881]]. The specific function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: TTC22 C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal region of tetratricopeptide repeat protein 22 (TTC22). TTC22 proteins contain multiple TPR repeats and are found in vertebrates including mammals and ray-finned fish.

Name: TTC22 N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal region of tetratricopeptide repeat protein 22 (TTC22). TTC22 proteins contain multiple TPR repeats and are found in vertebrates including mammals and ray-finned fish.

Name: CABIN1 C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal TPR-containing region of calcineurin-binding protein 1 (CABIN1) found in plants. CABIN1 is a component of the HIRA complex, which also includes UBN1, UBN2, ASF1A and HIRA [[PMID:25086063]]. The HIRA complex is involved in replication-independent chromatin assembly and deposition of histone H3.3. CABIN1 proteins contain multiple TPR repeats and an HIR3_bundle domain ([pfam:PF27595]).

Name: CABIN1 N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal TPR-containing region of calcineurin-binding protein 1 (CABIN1) and their homologues in mammals. CABIN1 is a component of the HIRA complex, which also includes UBN1, UBN2, ASF1A and HIRA [[PMID:25086063]]. The HIRA complex is involved in replication-independent chromatin assembly and deposition of histone H3.3. CABIN1 proteins contain multiple TPR repeats and an HIR3_bundle domain ([pfam:PF27595]).

Name: CABIN1 middle domain
Description: This entry represents the middle TPR-containing region of calcineurin-binding protein 1 (CABIN1) in plants. CABIN1 is a component of the HIRA complex, which also includes UBN1, UBN2, ASF1A and HIRA [[PMID:25086063]]. The HIRA complex is involved in replication-independent chromatin assembly and deposition of histone H3.3. CABIN1 proteins contain multiple TPR repeats and an HIR3_bundle domain ([pfam:PF27595]).

Name: Maintenance of PSII under high light protein 1
Description: This entry represents the MPH1 (maintenance of PSII under high light 1) protein found in land plants. MPH1 is a single-pass thylakoid membrane protein that localises to chloroplasts [[PMID:25846821]]. In Arabidopsis thaliana, MPH1 interacts with photosystem II (PSII) core complex proteins including psbA, psbB, psbC and psbD, and participates in the maintenance of normal PSII activity under photoinhibitory stress [[PMID:25846821]]. MPH1 protects PSII from photooxidative damage under high-light conditions [[PMID:26337456]]. Loss of MPH1 results in elevated photodamage to PSII reaction centre proteins after high-light treatment.

Name: Ankyrin repeat and fibronectin type-III domain protein middle region
Description: This entry represents a middle region of approximately 170 residues found in ANKFN1 (ankyrin repeat and fibronectin type-III domain-containing protein 1) in vertebrates. ANKFN1 contains N-terminal ankyrin repeats ([pfam:PF12796]) and a fn3 domain ([pfam:PF00041]). ANKFN1 plays a role in neuronal function and vestibular-related functions, and mutations cause neurological abnormalities including deficits in fear learning and circadian behavior.

Name: Eukaryotic translation initiation factor 2A C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents a C-terminal domain of approximately 50 residues found in eukaryotic translation initiation factor 2A (eIF2A) proteins in eukaryotes including metazoans and fungi. The domain forms a small helical hairpin and follows the main eIF2A WD-repeat beta-propeller domain ([pfam:PF08662]). eIF2A functions in translation initiation by directing binding of methionyl-tRNAi to 40S ribosomal subunits in a codon-dependent manner [[PMID:12133843],[PMID:15718232]]. The specific function of this C-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: Fungal NDUFC2 subunit
Description: This entry represents the NDUFC2 accessory subunit of mitochondrial complex I (CI) from fungi. Mitochondrial complex I is a redox-driven proton pump that generates proton-motive force across the inner mitochondrial membrane, powering oxidative phosphorylation and ATP synthesis in eukaryotes. Mitochondrial complex I has a characteristic L-shape, with a membrane arm embedded in the lipid bilayer of the inner mitochondrial membrane, joined at one end to the peripheral arm that extends into the mitochondrial matrix. NDUFC2 is a single-pass protein which is located at the membrane arm of CI and makes contacts with other accessory subunits contributing to the proper assembly and structural stability of the enzyme [[PMID:36427319]].

Name: NDUFC2 subunit
Description: This entry represents the NDUFC2 accessory subunit of mitochondrial complex I (CI) from Tetrahymena thermophila and related proteins. Mitochondrial complex I is a redox-driven proton pump that generates proton-motive force across the inner mitochondrial membrane, powering oxidative phosphorylation and ATP synthesis in eukaryotes. Mitochondrial complex I has a characteristic L-shape, with a membrane arm embedded in the lipid bilayer of the inner mitochondrial membrane, joined at one end to the peripheral arm that extends into the mitochondrial matrix. NDUFC2 is a single-pass protein which is located at the membrane arm of CI and makes contacts with other accessory subunits contributing to the proper assembly and structural stability of the enzyme [[PMID:35357889],[PMID:37248254]].

Name: NDUC2 subunit
Description: This entry represents the NDUC2 accessory subunit of mitochondrial complex I (CI) primarily found in Viridiplantae. Mitochondrial complex I is a redox-driven proton pump that generates proton-motive force across the inner mitochondrial membrane, powering oxidative phosphorylation and ATP synthesis in eukaryotes. Mitochondrial complex I has a characteristic L-shape, with a membrane arm embedded in the lipid bilayer of the inner mitochondrial membrane, joined at one end to the peripheral arm that extends into the mitochondrial matrix. NDUC2 is a single-pass protein which is located at the membrane arm of CI and makes contacts with other accessory subunits contributing to the proper assembly and structural stability of the enzyme [[PMID:32840211],[PMID:33060577]].

Name: Drosocin antimicrobial peptides
Description: This protein family includes fruit fly Drosocin antimicrobial peptides (Dro), antibacterial peptides with strong anti-Gram -negative bacteria activity. They bind within the nascent peptide exit tunnel of the bacterial large ribosomal subunit, potentially interfering with nascent chain translocation that occurs post-peptide bond formation [[PMID:8325867],[PMID:35730150],[PMID:36997647],[PMID:36997646]].

Name: ANKFN1 alpha/beta domain
Description: This entry represents a conserved domain found in ANKFN1 (ankyrin repeat and fibronectin type-III domain-containing 1) proteins in metazoans. This domain is located downstream of the ankyrin repeat ([pfam:PF12796]), fibronectin type-III ([pfam:PF00041]) domains and a conserved domain of unknown function ([pfam:PF31008]). ANKFN1 is required for neurological function including vestibular function, circadian behaviour and fear learning [[PMID:26131556],[PMID:35100349]]. The domain contains the highly conserved GLYLGYLK peptide motif; a single glycine to alanine substitution within this motif is sufficient to abolish ANKFN1 function in mice [[PMID:26131556]]. The specific function of this domain remains to be determined.

Name: Pentamidine resistance factor domain
Description: This domain is found in yeast Pentamidine resistance factor (PNT1), which is likely to be involved in mitochondrial export. It confers resistance to the anti-pneumocystis carinii drug pentamidine. This domain is predicted to adopt a mostly alpha- helical structure.

Name: Dermonecrotic toxin SPH
Description: This domain is found in Dermonecrotic toxin SPH from the deer tick. SPH cleaves the phosphodiester linkage between the phosphate and headgroup of certain phospholipids, forming an alcohol and a cyclic phosphate. This domain is predicted to show a TIM-barrel fold.

Name: Defensin
Description: This protein family includes Defensin from the moth Galleria mellonella (DEF1), which has antifungal activity against several species [[PMID:17194500]].

Name: Diptericin A
Description: This protein family includes Diptericin A from Drosophila melanogaster (DptA), an antimicrobial peptide required to resist Gram-negative bacterial infections, regulated by Dredd [[PMID:2125051],[PMID:11269502]].

Name: Genetic interactor of prohibitins 3, C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of the genetic interactor of prohibitins 3 (GEP3), a mitochondrial protein found in fungi of the Saccharomycotina subphylum. The full-length GEP3 protein contains an N-terminal mitochondrial targeting peptide, a CP-type G GTPase domain ([pfam:PF01926]), and this C-terminal domain of approximately 200 amino acids. GEP3 interacts genetically with prohibitins and is involved in mitochondrial lipid metabolism [[PMID:19751518]]. Disruption of GEP3 increases the frequency of mitochondrial genome loss [[PMID:19751518]]. The specific function of this C-terminal domain remains to be determined.

Name: Chloroplast protein ycf73
Description: This entry represents the ycf73 family, a group of uncharacterised proteins encoded in the chloroplast genome of grasses (Poaceae). The proteins are approximately 170-250 amino acids in length and are localised to the chloroplast. Members are found predominantly in the BOP clade (Bambusoideae, Oryzoideae, Pooideae) and PACMAD clade (Panicoideae, Arundinoideae, Chloridoideae, Micrairoideae, Aristidoideae, Danthonioideae) of the Poaceae. The function of this protein family remains to be determined.

Name: Kinetoplast-associated protein, N-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal domain of kinetoplast- associated proteins (KAPs), found in trypanosomatid protozoa. KAPs are histone H1-like DNA-binding proteins involved in the organisation and segregation of kinetoplast DNA (kDNA) [[PMID:8446592]]. The mitochondrial DNA of kinetoplastid protozoa consists of approximately 5,000 minicircles and 20-30 maxicircles that are held together by catenation into a highly organised compact disc structure. KAPs bind preferentially to specific fragments of minicircle DNA and compact kDNA networks through DNA charge neutralisation [[PMID:8446592]].

Name: rbcL RNA S1-binding domain protein N-terminal S1 domain
Description: This entry represents an N-terminal S1 domain region found in RLSB/PBR1/BSF-like proteins in plants. This domain is found N-terminal to other S1 motifs ([pfam:PF00575]). These chloroplastic RNA-binding proteins act as RNA chaperones that modulate translation and stability of plastid-encoded mRNAs including rbcL, petA, petB, petD and Ycf1, regulating biogenesis of photosynthetic complexes.

Name: Rhizobiales growth-associated tandem repeat domain
Description: This entry represents a tandemly repeated domain found in Alphaproteobacteria (Rhizobiales and Burkholderiales). The domain has structural similarity to carboxypeptidase regulatory domains (clan CL0287). It is typically found in 2-3 copies in proteins. In Sinorhizobium meliloti, family members include RgsD, which is part of the Rgs (Rhizobiales-specific growth and septation) protein system that interacts with the Tol-Pal complex to promote unipolar growth and cell division. These proteins are essential for viability in several Rhizobiales species.

Name: High light-inducible protein HliA
Description: This protein family includes High light-inducible protein HliA from Synechocystis sp., which is involved in photosystem II (PSII) assembly and/or repair under high light stress. It is likely to be important during long-term acclimation to stress [[PMID:35218444],[PMID:35279779]].

Name: CdiA C-terminal tRNase toxin domain
Description: This entry represents a tRNase toxin domain found at the C-terminus of CdiA proteins in contact-dependent growth inhibition (CDI) systems. The domain adopts a crescent-shaped fold comprising three alpha-helices and a four-stranded antiparallel beta-sheet, and is structurally homologous to the C-terminal RNase domain of Colicin D [[PMID:32156726]]. A conserved histidine residue is essential for catalytic activity. The domain cleaves tRNA molecules, with apparent preference for proline and glutamine tRNAs [[PMID:32156726]]. It functions both in interbacterial competition and as a virulence factor against mammalian hosts [[PMID:32156726]]. This domain is found in polymorphic toxin systems, often associated with RHS repeats, PAAR motifs and SpvB-like domains.

Name: DNA repair protein brc-2, central domain
Description: This entry represents the central domain of BRC-2 (DNA repair protein brc-2), found in Caenorhabditis species. BRC-2 is a BRCA2- related protein required for homologous recombination repair of DNA double-strand breaks and chromosome integrity. The protein contains an N-terminal BRCA2 repeat-like region (residues 28-62 in G5EG86) that mediates interaction with RAD-51 and stabilisation of RAD-51-DNA filaments, this central domain, and a C-terminal region required for single-stranded DNA binding. BRC-2 functions by targeting RAD-51 to DNA damage sites and blocking ATP hydrolysis by RAD-51, thereby promoting homologous recombination [[PMID:17483448],[PMID:16843491]]. The specific function of this central domain remains to be determined. This domain me adopt an OB-fold structure.

Name: DNA repair protein brc-2, N-terminal region
Description: This entry represents the N-terminal region of BRC-2 (DNA repair protein brc-2), found in Caenorhabditis species. BRC-2 is a BRCA2- related protein required for homologous recombination repair of DNA double-strand breaks and chromosome integrity [[PMID:17483448],[PMID:18779660],[PMID:16843491]]. This region is mostly disordered and contains a BRCA2 repeat-like segment (residues 28-62) that mediates interaction with RAD-51, BRC-2 functions by targeting RAD-51 to DNA damage sites and blocking ATP hydrolysis by RAD-51, thereby promoting homologous recombination.

Name: DNA repair protein brc-2, C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of BRC-2 (DNA repair protein brc-2), found in Caenorhabditis species. BRC-2 is a BRCA2- related protein required for homologous recombination repair of DNA double-strand breaks and chromosome integrity. The protein contains an N-terminal BRCA2 repeat-like region ([pfam:PF31226]) that mediates interaction with RAD-51 and stabilisation of RAD-51-DNA filaments, a central domain ([pfam:PF31223]), and this C-terminal domain that is required for single-stranded DNA binding [[PMID:17483448],[PMID:16843491]]. BRC-2 functions by targeting RAD-51 to DNA damage sites and blocking ATP hydrolysis by RAD-51, thereby promoting homologous recombination [[PMID:17483448],[PMID:18779660],[PMID:16843491]].

Name: Intron-encoded DNA endonuclease aI3, C-terminal domain
Description: This entry represents the C-terminal domain of intron-encoded DNA endonuclease aI3, found in fungi. The aI3 protein is a mitochondrial DNA endonuclease involved in intron homing. The protein is encoded within intron 3 of the mitochondrial COX1 gene. Some members contain an N-terminal GIY-YIG endonuclease domain ([pfam:PF01541]). The specific function of this domain remains to be determined. This domain may have a beta/beta/alpha/alpha/beta fold.

Name: Psidin, N-terminal TPR repeats region
Description: This is a region of tetratricopeptide (TPR)-like repeats found at the N-terminal end of Phagocyte signaling-impaired protein from Drosophila melanogaster (Psidin), a non-catalytic subunit of the NatB complex which catalyses acetylation of the N-terminal methionine residues of proteins beginning with Met-Asp or Met-Glu. This protein is required both for the phagocytic degradation of internalized bacteria and for the induction of Defensin in the fat body. Within the phagocytic blood cells, it has a role in detection of infection and activation of the humoral immune response [[PMID:17208189]].

Name: Psidin-like, C-terminal domain
Description: This domain is found at the C-terminal end of Phagocyte signaling-impaired protein from Drosophila melanogaster (Psidin), a non-catalytic subunit of the NatB complex which catalyses acetylation of the N-terminal methionine residues of proteins beginning with Met-Asp or Met-Glu. This protein is required both for the phagocytic degradation of internalized bacteria and for the induction of Defensin in the fat body. Within the phagocytic blood cells, it plays a role in detection of infection and activation of the humoral immune response [[PMID:17208189]]. This domain shows an all-alpha configuration.

Name: Cuben fold calcium-binding domain
Description: This domain is found at the C-terminus of noncanonical aromatic amino acid decarboxylases (ncAAADs) in fungi. The domain adopts a novel double-beta-barrel fold, termed the Cuben fold, consisting of two perpendicularly stacked six-stranded beta-barrel repeats with Greek-key topology [[PMID:30484626]]. Each repeat contains six beta-strands followed by a helix-containing loop. The domain contains two metal-binding sites formed cooperatively between the repeats and between this domain and the catalytic domain. Calcium binding at these sites increases enzyme catalytic rate over 300-fold and enhances thermal stability [[PMID:30484626]]. This domain is essential for ncAAAD activity; the catalytic domain alone shows little decarboxylase activity.

Name: Plant antimicrobial peptide
Description: This family includes plant antimicrobial peptides. This domain, usually found associated with [pfam:PF14861], is predicted to adopt an alpha-helical hairpin fold.

Name: Cuben fold calcium-binding domain
Description: This domain is found at the C-terminus of noncanonical aromatic amino acid decarboxylases (ncAAADs) in fungi. The domain composes half of a double-beta-barrel fold, termed the Cuben fold, consisting of two perpendicularly stacked six-stranded beta-barrel repeats with Greek-key topology [[PMID:30484626]]. Each repeat contains six beta-strands followed by a helix-containing loop. The domain contains two metal-binding sites formed cooperatively between the repeats and between this domain and the catalytic domain. Calcium binding at these sites increases enzyme catalytic rate over 300-fold and enhances thermal stability [[PMID:30484626]]. This domain is essential for ncAAAD activity; the catalytic domain alone shows little decarboxylase activity.

Name: Antimicrobial peptide damicornin
Description: This protein family includes the antimicrobial peptide damicornin from Pocillopora damicornis (the cauliflower coral), a cationic peptide with probable antimicrobial activity against coral pathogens [[PMID:21536670]]. It is predicted to show some alpha helices around a central disordered region.

Name: Pinf_Pram_Psoj_Pcap_DWL
Description: Phytophthora DWL domain HMM profile (https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0202305)

Name: Pinf_Pram_Psoj_Pcap_LFLAK
Description: Phytophthora N-terminal CRN effector LFLAK motif HMM profile (https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0202305)

Name: ZiF_tribe_I
Description: HMM profile built from multiple sequence alignment of tribeI sequences (https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.ppat.1012277)

Name: ZiF_tribe_II
Description: HMM profile built from multiple sequence alignment of tribeII sequences (https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.ppat.1012277)

Name: ZiF_tribe_III
Description: HMM profile built from multiple sequence alignment of tribeIII sequences (https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.ppat.1012277)

Name: RNA polymerase Rpb1, domain 3
Description: RNA polymerases catalyse the DNA dependent polymerisation of RNA. Prokaryotes contain a single RNA polymerase compared to three in eukaryotes (not including mitochondrial. and chloroplast polymerases).  This domain, domain 3, represents the pore domain. The 3' end of RNA is positioned close to this domain. The pore delimited by this domain is thought to act as a channel through which nucleotides enter the active site and/or where the 3' end of the RNA may be extruded during back-tracking [[PMID:8910400],[PMID:11313498]].

Name: Cyanobacterial and plant NDH-1 subunit O
Description: The proton-pumping NADH:ubiquinone oxidoreductase catalyzes the electron transfer from NADH to ubiquinone linked with proton translocation across the membrane. It is the largest, most complex and least understood of the respiratory chain enzymes and is referred to as Complex I. The subunit composition of the enzyme varies between groups of organisms. Complex I originating from mammalian mitochondria contains 45 different proteins, whereas in bacteria, the corresponding complex NDH-1 consists of 14 different polypeptides.  Homologues of these 14 proteins are found among subunits of the mitochondrial complex I, and therefore bacterial NDH-1 might be considered a model proton-pumping NADH dehydrogenase with a minimal set of subunits. Escherichia coli NDH-1 readily disintegrates into 3 subcomplexes: a water-soluble NADH dehydrogenase fragment (NuoE, -F, and -G),the connecting fragment (NuoB, -C, -D, and -I), and the membrane fragment (NuoA, -H, -J, -K, -L, -M, -N). In cyanobacteria and their descendants, the chloroplasts of green plants, the subunit composition of NDH-1 remains obscure. The genes for eleven subunits NdhA-NdhK, homologous to the NuoA-NuoD and NuoH-NuoN of the E. coli complex, have been found in the genome of Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803 which has a family of 6 ndhD genes and a family of 3 ndhF genes. Two reported multisubunit complexes, NDH-1L and NDH-1M, represent distinct NDH-1 complexes in the thylakoid membrane of Synechocystis 6803 -cyanobacterium. NDH-1L was shown to be essential for photoheterotrophic cell growth, whereas expression of NDH-1M was a prerequisite for CO2 uptake and played an important role in growth of cells at low CO2. Here we report the subunit composition of these two complexes. Fifteen proteins were discovered in NDH-1L including NdhL, a new component of the membrane fragment, and Ssl1690 (designated as NdhO), a novel peripheral subunit [[PMID:12460669],[PMID:15548534]]. The three nuclear-encoded subunits NdhM,NdhN and NdhO are vital for the functional integrity of the plastidial complex [[PMID:15608332]].

Name: RNA polymerase beta subunit external 1 domain
Description: RNA polymerases catalyse the DNA-dependent polymerisation of RNA. Prokaryotes contain a single RNA polymerase compared with three in eukaryotes (not including mitochondrial or chloroplast polymerases). This domain in prokaryotes spans the gap between domains 4 and 5 of the yeast protein. It is also known as the external 1 region of the polymerase and is bound in association with the external 2 region [[PMID:11313498]].

Name: RNA polymerase Rpb1, domain 2
Description: RNA polymerases catalyse the DNA dependent polymerisation of RNA. Prokaryotes contain a single RNA polymerase compared to three in eukaryotes (not including mitochondrial. and chloroplast polymerases).  This domain, domain 2, contains the active site. The invariant motif -NADFDGD- binds the active site magnesium ion [[PMID:8910400],[PMID:11313498]].

Name: RNA polymerase Rpb1, domain 1
Description: RNA polymerases catalyse the DNA dependent polymerisation of RNA. Prokaryotes contain a single RNA polymerase compared to three in eukaryotes (not including mitochondrial. and chloroplast polymerases).  This domain, domain 1, represents the clamp domain, which a mobile domain involved in positioning the DNA, maintenance of the transcription bubble and positioning of the nascent RNA strand [[PMID:8910400],[PMID:11313498]].

Name: RNA polymerase Rpb2, domain 3
Description: RNA polymerases catalyse the DNA dependent polymerisation of RNA. Prokaryotes contain a single RNA polymerase compared to three in eukaryotes (not including mitochondrial. and chloroplast polymerases). Domain 3, s also known as the fork domain and is proximal to catalytic site [[PMID:11313498]].

Name: RNA polymerase Rpb1, domain 5
Description: RNA polymerases catalyse the DNA dependent polymerisation of RNA. Prokaryotes contain a single RNA polymerase compared to three in eukaryotes (not including mitochondrial. and chloroplast polymerases).  This domain, domain 5, represents the discontinuous cleft domain that is required to from the central cleft or channel where the DNA is bound [[PMID:8910400],[PMID:11313498]].

Name: RNA polymerase Rpb2, domain 4
Description: RNA polymerases catalyse the DNA dependent polymerisation of RNA. Prokaryotes contain a single RNA polymerase compared to three in eukaryotes (not including mitochondrial. and chloroplast polymerases). Domain 4, is also known as the external 2 domain [[PMID:11313498]].

Name: DYW family of nucleic acid deaminases
Description: This is the DYW domain found in nucleic acid  deaminases prototyped by the plant PPR DYW proteins that are implicated in chloroplast and mitochondrial RNA transcript maturation by numerous C to U editing events [[PMID:17707818],[PMID:25512379],[PMID:23288601]]. The name derives from the DYW motif present at the C-terminus of the classical plant PPR DYW deaminases. Members containing this domain are present in bacteria, plants, Naegleria, and fungi [[PMID:21890906]]. Plants and Naegleria show lineage-specific expansions of this family. This domain contains a characteristic zinc-binding motif (CXXC, HXE) which has been shown to bind zinc ions. This domain is often fused to PPR repeats. Ascomycete versions, which are independent lateral transfers, contain a large insert within the domain and are often fused to ankyrin repeats. Bacterial versions are predicted to function as toxins in polymorphic toxin systems [[PMID:21890906]].

Name: RNA polymerase Rpb2, domain 2
Description: RNA polymerases catalyse the DNA dependent polymerisation of RNA. Prokaryotes contain a single RNA polymerase compared to three in eukaryotes (not including mitochondrial. and chloroplast polymerases). Rpb2 is the second largest subunit of the RNA polymerase. This domain forms one of the two distinctive lobes of the Rpb2 structure.  This domain is also known as the lobe domain [[PMID:11313498]].  DNA has been demonstrated to bind to the concave surface of the lobe domain, and plays a role in maintaining the transcription bubble [[PMID:11313498]].  Many of the bacterial members contain large insertions within this domain, as region known as dispensable region 1 (DRI).

Name: RNA polymerase Rpb2, domain 6
Description: RNA polymerases catalyse the DNA dependent polymerisation of RNA. Prokaryotes contain a single RNA polymerase compared to three in eukaryotes (not including mitochondrial. and chloroplast polymerases). This domain represents the hybrid binding domain and the wall domain [[PMID:11313498]].  The hybrid binding domain binds the nascent RNA strand / template DNA strand in the Pol II transcription elongation complex.  This domain contains the important structural motifs, switch 3 and the flap loop and binds an active site metal ion[[PMID:11313498]].  This domain is also involved in binding to Rpb1 and Rpb3 [[PMID:11313498]].  Many of the bacterial members contain large insertions within this domain, as region known as dispensable region 2 (DRII).

Name: RNA polymerase Rpb1, domain 7
Description: RNA polymerases catalyse the DNA dependent polymerisation of RNA. Prokaryotes contain a single RNA polymerase compared to three in eukaryotes (not including mitochondrial. and chloroplast polymerases).  This domain, domain 7, represents a mobile module of the RNA polymerase. Domain 7 forms a substantial interaction with the lobe domain of Rpb2 ([pfam:PF04561]) [[PMID:8910400],[PMID:11313498]].

Name: Ribosomal proteins 50S-L15, 50S-L18e, 60S-L27A
Description: This family includes higher eukaryotic ribosomal 60S L27A, archaeal 50S L18e, prokaryotic 50S L15, fungal mitochondrial L10, plant L27A, mitochondrial L15 and chloroplast L18-3 proteins.

Name: NADH-quinone oxidoreductase cyanobacterial subunit N
Description: The proton-pumping NADH:ubiquinone oxidoreductase catalyzes the electron transfer from NADH to ubiquinone linked with proton translocation across the membrane. It is the largest, most complex and least understood of the respiratory chain enzymes and is referred to as Complex I. The subunit composition of the enzyme varies between groups of organisms. Complex I originating from mammalian mitochondria contains 45 different proteins, whereas in bacteria, the corresponding complex NDH-1 consists of 14 different polypeptides. Homologues of these 14 proteins are found among subunits of the mitochondrial complex I, and therefore bacterial NDH-1 might be considered a model proton-pumping NADH dehydrogenase with a minimal set of subunits. Escherichia coli NDH-1 readily disintegrates into 3 subcomplexes: a water-soluble NADH dehydrogenase fragment (NuoE, -F, and -G),the connecting fragment (NuoB, -C, -D, and -I), and the membrane fragment (NuoA, -H, -J, -K, -L, -M, -N). In cyanobacteria and their descendants, the chloroplasts of green plants, the subunit composition of NDH-1 remains obscure. The genes for eleven subunits NdhA-NdhK, homologous to the NuoA-NuoD and NuoH-NuoN of the E. coli complex, have been found in the genome of Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803 which has a family of 6 ndhD genes and a family of 3 ndhF genes. Two reported multisubunit complexes, NDH-1L and NDH-1M, represent distinct NDH-1 complexes in the thylakoid membrane of Synechocystis 6803 -cyanobacterium. NDH-1L was shown to be essential for photoheterotrophic cell growth, whereas expression of NDH-1M was a prerequisite for CO2 uptake and played an important role in growth of cells at low CO2. Here we report the subunit composition of these two complexes. Fifteen proteins were discovered in NDH-1L including NdhL, a new component of the membrane fragment, and Ssl1690 (designated as NdhO), a novel peripheral subunit [[PMID:12460669]]. The cyanobacterial NDH-1 complex contains additional subunits, NdhM and NdhN, compared with the minimal set of the bacterial enzyme and these seem to be specific for thylakoid-located NDH-1 of photosynthetic organisms [[PMID:15548534]].

Name: Protein-only RNase P
Description: PRORPs (protein-only RNase P) are a class of RNA processing enzymes that catalyze maturation of the 5' end of precursor tRNAs in Eukaryotes [[PMID:26655022]]. Arabidopsis thaliana contains PRORP enzymes (PRORP1, PRORP2 and PRORP3) where PRORP1 localizes to mitochondria as well as chloroplasts, while PRORP2 and PRORP3 are found in the nucleus. In humans and most other metazoans, mt-RNase P is composed of three protein subunits (mitochondrial RNase P proteins 1-3; MRPP1-3), homologs to the Arabidopsis thaliana PRORP1-3. This domain corresponds to the metallonuclease domain of PRORPs. PRORP1 has 22% sequence identity to the human homologue MRPP3. PRORP1 crystal structure shows a V-shaped tripartite structure with a C-terminal metallonuclease domain of the NYN (N4BL1, YacP-like nuclease) family, with a typical and functional two-metal-ion catalytic site that has conserved aspartate residues [[PMID:25953853]].

Name: RNA polymerase Rpb2, domain 5
Description: RNA polymerases catalyse the DNA dependent polymerisation of RNA. Prokaryotes contain a single RNA polymerase compared to three in eukaryotes (not including mitochondrial. and chloroplast polymerases). Domain 5, is also known as the external 2 domain [[PMID:11313498]].

Name: RNA polymerase beta subunit
Description: RNA polymerases catalyse the DNA dependent polymerisation of RNA. Prokaryotes contain a single RNA polymerase compared to three in eukaryotes (not including mitochondrial. and chloroplast polymerases). This domain forms one of the two distinctive lobes of the Rpb2 structure.  This domain is also known as the protrusion domain [[PMID:3116266]].  The other lobe ([pfam:PF04561]) is nested within this domain.

Name: Protein FERTILITY RESTORER RF2, mitochondrial helical domain
Description: This entry represents a small coiled-coil domain found in RF2-like proteins from grasses and related plants. The domain is present in fertility restorer proteins such as RF2, which is involved in restoring male fertility in rice with cytoplasmic male sterility, and in YL1 (Yellow Leaf 1), which plays a role in chloroplast development and photosynthetic protein complex assembly [[PMID:27585744],[PMID:21265890]]. These proteins are targeted to organelles (mitochondria or chloroplasts) and contain glycine-rich regions that may form coiled-coil structures. The domain is found predominantly in the Poaceae family, particularly in Oryza, Triticum, and related grass genera. The specific function of this helical domain remains to be determined.

Name: RNA polymerase Rpb1, domain 4
Description: RNA polymerases catalyse the DNA dependent polymerisation of RNA. Prokaryotes contain a single RNA polymerase compared to three in eukaryotes (not including mitochondrial. and chloroplast polymerases).  This domain, domain 4, represents the funnel domain.  The funnel contain the binding site for some elongation factors [[PMID:8910400],[PMID:11313498]].

Name: RNA polymerase Rpb1, domain 6
Description: RNA polymerases catalyse the DNA dependent polymerisation of RNA. Prokaryotes contain a single RNA polymerase compared to three in eukaryotes (not including mitochondrial. and chloroplast polymerases).  This domain, domain 6, represents a mobile module of the RNA polymerase. Domain 6 forms part of the shelf module [[PMID:8910400],[PMID:11313498]].  This family appears to be specific to the largest subunit of RNA polymerase II.

Name: ATP synthase D chain, mitochondrial (ATP5H)
Description: This family consists of several ATP synthase D chain, mitochondrial (ATP5H) proteins. Subunit d has no extensive hydrophobic sequences, and is not apparently related to any subunit described in the simpler ATP synthases in bacteria and chloroplasts [[PMID:2890767],[PMID:7509337]].

Name: RNA polymerase Rpb2, domain 7
Description: RNA polymerases catalyse the DNA dependent polymerisation of RNA. Prokaryotes contain a single RNA polymerase compared to three in eukaryotes (not including mitochondrial. and chloroplast polymerases).  Rpb2 is the second largest subunit of the RNA polymerase.  This domain comprised of the structural domains anchor and clamp [[PMID:11313498]]. The clamp region (C-terminal) contains a zinc-binding motif [[PMID:11313498]]. The clamp region is named due to its interaction with the clamp domain found in Rpb1. The domain also contains a region termed "switch 4". The switches within the polymerase are thought to signal different stages of transcription [[PMID:11313498]].
